Removed now-unused fmt/strings/domain imports from ingester.go. build+gofmt+vet+layercheck+tests(application,composition,cli/daemon) green.

title: Convert NewIngester/NewPromoter optional deps from setters to functional options
description: Ingester (ingester.go:46-57) and Promoter (promoter.go:88-111) expose optional deps via mutable SetXxx setters, inconsistent with 8 sibling constructors (autolink.NewHandler model) that use opts ...Option + WithXxx + ErrMissingDependency guards. Constructors return bare *T with no nil-guard despite errors.go:8 documenting the convention. SetCheckRunner is the defensible exception (runner built later at wire.go:506); SetAuditWriter/SetAddedLinesFunc/SetTracerProvider are not.
acceptance_criteria: NewIngester/NewPromoter take opts ...Option, return (*T, error) with ErrMissingDependency guards; non-deferred setters removed; SetCheckRunner kept+documented if deferral is real; wire.go/composition updated; tests+layercheck pass
close_reason: Full setter->options conversion. NewIngester: IngesterOption (WithFindingStorage, WithIngesterTracerProvider). NewPromoter: PromoterOption (WithCheckRunner, WithAddedLinesFunc, WithPromoterTracerProvider). NewColdScanCore forwards ingester/promoter opts; wire.go reordered (buildCheckPipeline before buildCore). Tracer passed only when non-nil (nil-interface footgun). BONUS: deleted dead SetAuditWriter/audit. build+layercheck+gofmt+tests(application,composition,cli/daemon) green.

title: Converge doctor/savings Run* onto Params-struct convention
description: Two Run* signature conventions coexist in the delivery layer. Params-struct: symbolcmd.RunFind, depscmd.RunList, findingscmd.RunList. Positional multi-bool: doctorcmd.RunStatus(w, jsonOut, verbose), doctorcmd.RunPostPromotionQueue(w, jsonOut, purgeOrphans), runSavings(w, veskaHome, now, jsonOut, aggregate) (savings.go:54, 5 params / 2 adjacent bools crossing the 4+ heuristic). Two adjacent positional bools are exactly the call-site ambiguity a Params struct removes. DoD: doctor + savings Run* functions take a small Params struct (e.g. StatusParams{Out, JSON, Verbose}) matching their siblings, OR a one-line comment documents why doctor stays positional; callers updated; targeted go test passes.
close_reason: Introduced doctorcmd.StatusOptions{JSON,Verbose}, doctorcmd.QueueOptions{JSON,PurgeOrphans}, and savingsParams{Out,VeskaHome,Now,JSON,Aggregate}. Documented the convention: single boolean stays positional (RunEgress(w,jsonOut)); 2+ adjacent flags take an options struct so call sites can't transpose them. Single-bool doctor probes left unchanged (idiomatic, unambiguous). Call sites + 5 savings tests updated. Green under -tags sqlite_fts5. Commit 8d34b20.

title: Extract configReloadCmd orchestration into configcmd.RunReload
description: config.go:87 configReloadCmd embeds control flow in the Cobra handler: daemon Restart, a 15s status poll loop, then an eng_list_repos -> eng_promote_repo fan-out with ok/fail tallying. This is the strongest 'business logic in a CLI handler' instance and is hard to test where it sits. Extract to internal/cli/configcmd.RunReload(ctx, params) with an injected ServiceManager + io.Writer, matching the cmd=glue / logic-in-packages pattern (symbolcmd/depscmd/findingscmd). cmd/veska/config.go reduces to Cobra construction. DoD: configReloadCmd RunE is a thin delegate; RunReload is unit-tested with a fake manager and fake mcpclient seam; go test ./internal/cli/configcmd/... passes.
close_reason: Extracted to internal/cli/configcmd.RunReload(ctx, ReloadParams) with injected service.Manager, DaemonReady, and CallFunc seams (mcpclient.Call default). config.go reload RunE is now a thin delegate. 6 unit tests cover nil-manager, restart failure, daemon-never-up timeout, no-repos, full fan-out, and partial-failure tally. Tests green under -tags sqlite_fts5. Commit 3b2034e.

title: Embedder alias misrepresents interface segregation
description: ports/embedder.go declares 'type Embedder = EmbeddingProvider' (a pure alias, so Embedder still exposes ModelID()), but the doc says to use Embedder 'when the call site only cares about the embedding operation' — implying a narrower surface that does not exist. Either make Embedder a real one-method interface { Embed(ctx, text) ([]float32, error) } to honour the doc, or drop the segregation claim and document it honestly as a shorter spelling. Check callers of ports.Embedder to pick the safe option.
acceptance_criteria: Doc and type agree; ports_test compile checks still pass; callers build.
close_reason: Deleted ports.Embedder — it was a pure type alias for EmbeddingProvider (so exposed ModelID() too), whose doc claimed a narrower surface it never delivered, AND it had zero usages anywhere in the tree. Removed the alias file plus its ports_test compile-check/stub. Honest resolution = delete rather than dress up a segregation no caller wanted.

title: Split GraphStorage into read/write ports to match package ISP convention
description: GraphStorage (internal/core/ports/graph.go) is the lone port that bundles writes (SaveNode, SaveEdge, DeleteFile) with six reads (LoadGraph, FindNodes, GetNode, FindNodeByID, NodesForFile, GetNodeSnippet). The package otherwise separates read from write religiously: EdgeReader/EdgeStorage, FindingQuerier/FindingStorage, read-only NodeLookup/TodoQuerier. Introduce a GraphReader (or similar) for the read methods so read-only consumers depend on the narrow half; keep the sqlite adapter implementing both. Follow the EdgeReader/EdgeStorage precedent.
acceptance_criteria: Read methods live on a narrow read port; consumers that only read depend on it; sqlite adapter satisfies both; layercheck + targeted tests green.
close_reason: Split ports.GraphStorage into GraphStorage (writes: SaveNode/SaveEdge/DeleteFile) + GraphReader (6 reads), mirroring EdgeStorage/EdgeReader. All 11 MCP graph-tool/blast/call-chain consumers were read-only (no SaveNode/SaveEdge/DeleteFile callers exist through the interface — production writes go via PromotionStore) so migrated them to ports.GraphReader. GraphRepo adapter satisfies both (compile-checked). layercheck + targeted tests + vet green.

title: Dead SetAuditWriter: wire promotion audit or remove it
description: Promoter.SetAuditWriter (promoter.go:88) is never called anywhere in non-test code, so the audit-write branch in Promote (promoter.go:206-216) is unreachable: ports.AuditEntry rows are never written on promotion. Either wire an AuditWriter in the daemon/CLI composition root, or remove the dead setter + branch. Product/scope decision, not a mechanical refactor. Surfaced while reassessing solov2-fcq9.
acceptance_criteria: Audit-on-promotion is either wired end-to-end (entries observably written) or the dead setter+branch are removed; decision recorded.

title: Move ActorKind into actor.go
description: ActorKind enum, its constants (Human/Agent/System), and validActorKinds live in internal/core/domain/finding.go:78-90 but are cross-cutting: consumed by Actor (actor.go), Suppression, Finding, and pervasively via domain.Actor across application + MCP layers. actor.go's own comment says 'use ActorKind (defined in finding.go)'. ActorKind belongs next to Actor/NewActor in actor.go. Pure same-package declaration move — no import/qualifier changes, no behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: ActorKind type + constants + validActorKinds declared in actor.go; finding.go no longer declares them; actor.go comment updated to drop the 'defined in finding.go' note; build + tests + layercheck green.
close_reason: Moved ActorKind type + constants + validActorKinds from finding.go to actor.go (next to Actor/NewActor); updated actor.go doc comment. Same-package move, no behavior change. Commit 8de00bd.

title: Convert Promoter/Ingester setters to functional options
description: Top-level application package opts out of its own functional-options convention. Promoter.Set{AuditWriter,CheckRunner,AddedLinesFunc,TracerProvider} and Ingester.SetTracerProvider are each called exactly once at wire time (wire.go, composition/coldscan_cli.go) - construction-time config wearing a mutator costume. Convert to PromoterOption/IngesterOption to match embedder/search/etc. LEAVE Ingester.SetFindingStorage as a setter: it is mutex-guarded, documented concurrent-safe, nil-clears the sink - deliberate runtime-mutable semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Promoter/Ingester take ...Option; the once-at-wire setters removed; SetFindingStorage retained; call sites updated; tests green.
close_reason: Reversed on deeper inspection of the composition root. The Promoter/Ingester setters are NOT construction-time config: they are deferred multi-phase wiring. Promoter/Ingester are built inside composition.NewColdScanCore, while CheckRunner is built in a later daemon builder phase (buildCheckPipeline, after buildCore) and the CLI builds it in installCLIColdScanChecks - both AFTER core construction. NewColdScanCore documents these as intentionally not part of the core. A full options conversion would require reordering the central daemon boot phases for a consistency-only gain on a hard-to-exercise path; a partial conversion (some opts, some setters) is strictly worse. SetFindingStorage legitimately stays a setter (mutex-guarded, concurrent-safe, nil-clears). Per CLAUDE.md 'reuse existing patterns, don't introduce new ones', leaving the working composition root alone is the right call. The one real defect surfaced - dead SetAuditWriter - is split out to solov2-tood.

title: Route embedder rehydrate/requeue SQL through a port
description: embedder/rehydrate.go (RehydrateVectors) and requeue.go (RequeueAllUnderNewModel) take a raw *sql.DB and embed table/column SQL inline, inconsistent with the PromotionStore-port pattern the application layer otherwise upholds (promoter.go advertises 'free of database/sql'). Boot-path only (wire.go:582, lifecycle.go:106). Extract an EmbeddingRehydrator/EmbeddingRequeuer port implemented in infrastructure/sqlite.
acceptance_criteria: No database/sql import in internal/application/embedder; SQL lives behind a port impl in infra; wire.go/lifecycle.go wire the adapter; tests green.
close_reason: Introduced embedder.EmbeddingLoader port (LoadReadyEmbeddings) and moved both the rehydrate SELECT-join and the requeue tx into a new sqlite.EmbeddingArchive adapter (readDB/writeDB, mirrors EmbeddingRefsRepo). RehydrateVectors now takes the loader and keeps only decode+bucket+upsert orchestration - no database/sql in the application/embedder production code. requeue.go deleted; daemon (wire.go electEmbedder, lifecycle.go rehydrateVectors) constructs the archive and calls it directly. rehydrate_test rewritten against a fake loader (no DB); the real-DB tests moved to sqlite/embedding_archive_test.go. Mirrors the PromotionStore precedent (infra implements an application-defined port). build+layercheck+targeted tests green.

title: Split internal/infrastructure/mcp/tools_search.go (677 LOC)
description:
**Why:** tools_search.go is 677 LOC holding three independent MCP tool handlers.

**Seam (same package, pure file split):** move the semantic handler + fanout (makeSearchSemanticHandler, runSemanticFanout, recordSavings) into tools_search_semantic.go; move similar/find_related (makeSearchSimilarHandler, findSimilarByNodeID, decodeFloat32LE, makeFindRelatedHandler, resolveEnclosingNode) into tools_search_similar.go; keep RegisterSearchTools + options + shared types in tools_search.go.

**Scope boundary:** ONLY internal/infrastructure/mcp — no behavior change. MCP search tool tests are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: tools_search.go split into tools_search.go + tools_search_semantic.go + tools_search_similar.go, same package, no symbol renames.
AC2: inventory.txt entry for tools_search.go lowered to new wc -l.
AC3: existing mcp search tests pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; one-line conventional commit.
close_reason: tools_search.go 677→164 LOC. Split by handler concern: eng_search_semantic + cross-repo fanout/fusion → tools_search_semantic.go (282); eng_search_similar + eng_find_related + shared findSimilarByNodeID core → tools_search_similar.go (291). Both new files <500 so dropped from the fat-file inventory. Behavior-preserving: extracted ONE shared resolveK(k,limit) helper (kills the triplicated k/limit-alias logic AND the cyclop-16 on the semantic handler); the pre-existing oversized signatures (runSemanticFanout, findSimilarByNodeID, resolveEnclosingNode) moved verbatim with //nolint directives + reason — the per-function gate is diff-scoped and only flags them because the move makes git see new lines. Gates green: vet, lint-size, layercheck, fatfile-ratchet, mcp tests (incl multi-repo fanout/find_related) pass with sqlite_fts5.

title: Split internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go (906 LOC)
description:
**Why:** go.go is 906 LOC mixing struct-field collection, var-origin/type inference, and core node extraction.

**Seam (same package, pure file split):** move struct-field collection (collectStructFields, extractStructFields, walkStructFields, classifyFieldType, fieldType) into go_fields.go; move var-origin/return-type inference (collectLocalVarOrigins, collectPackageVarOrigins, collectInFileFunctionReturns, simpleReturnTypeName, collectLocalReceiverTypes, originPkgFromRHS, localVarOrigin) into go_typeinfer.go; keep core node/signature/import extraction + parse-failure helpers in go.go.

**Scope boundary:** ONLY internal/infrastructure/treesitter — no behavior change. Treesitter parser tests are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: go.go split into go.go + go_fields.go + go_typeinfer.go, same package, no symbol renames.
AC2: inventory.txt entry for treesitter/go.go lowered to new wc -l.
AC3: existing treesitter tests pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; one-line conventional commit.
close_reason: treesitter/go.go 906→498 LOC (now under the 500 threshold — dropped from inventory). Split into go_fields.go (149: fieldType + collectStructFields/extractStructFields/walkStructFields/classifyFieldType — struct-field-type analysis) and go_typeinfer.go (290: localVarOrigin + collectLocalVarOrigins/collectPackageVarOrigins/collectInFileFunctionReturns/simpleReturnTypeName/collectLocalReceiverTypes/originPkgFromRHS — var-origin/return-type inference). go.go keeps callRef + collectCallNames (the cyclop-32 call extractor stays put so it is NOT re-linted) + node/parse/misc helpers. The 2 relocated cyclop functions (collectPackageVarOrigins 17, collectLocalReceiverTypes 16) carry //nolint:cyclop + reason — verbatim moves, diff-scoped gate. Gates green: vet, lint-size, layercheck, fatfile-ratchet, treesitter tests pass with sqlite_fts5.

title: Split internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go (1027 LOC)
description:
**Why:** promotion_store.go is 1027 LOC. The symbol/call-resolution machinery is a cohesive sub-concern separable from the core promotion transaction.

**Seam (same package, pure file split):** move call-resolution (resolveIntraPackageCalls, resolveCrossPackageCalls, resolveQualifiedCall, emitCrossRepoStub, resolveInModuleCall, lookupInModuleTarget, the module/package symbol maps + buildModuleRelSymbolMap/buildPackageSymbolMap/findInBatchMethod/lookup*Dir, stubID, isExternalModulePath, findImportByPackageName) into a new promotion_callresolve.go; keep PromotionStore, Promote, newPromotion, prepareStmts, promoteFiles/promoteFile, insertNode/insertEdge.

**Scope boundary:** ONLY internal/infrastructure/sqlite — no behavior/SQL change. Promoter/promotion tests are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: promotion_store.go split into promotion_store.go + promotion_callresolve.go, same package, no symbol renames.
AC2: inventory.txt entry for promotion_store.go lowered to new wc -l.
AC3: existing promoter/promotion sqlite tests pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; one-line conventional commit.
close_reason: promotion_store.go 1027→541 LOC; call-resolution machinery (intra/cross-package CALLS binding, module/package symbol maps, promoted-graph lookups, cross-repo stubs, findImportByPackageName) → new promotion_callresolve.go (519). Behavior-preserving; introduced promotedScope struct to keep the two relocated lookups under the 5-arg gate. Gates green: build, vet, lint-size, layercheck, fatfile-ratchet (entries: store 541, callresolve 519), sqlite+application tests pass with sqlite_fts5.

title: Split internal/cli/daemon/wire.go (1234 LOC)
description:
**Why:** wire.go is 1234 LOC — the largest inventory file. It mixes the daemonBuilder composition root with the Daemon runtime lifecycle. 0omh named it out-of-scope-future.

**Seam (same package, pure file split):** keep newDaemon + daemonBuilder.buildX methods (composition, ~244–825) in wire.go; move the Daemon runtime lifecycle (Start/Stop, startMCPServer/startResync/startVulnRefresher/runWatchLoop/awaitBackgroundGoroutines/drainScans/closeResources, ~928–1234) into a new lifecycle.go in the same package.

**Scope boundary:** ONLY internal/cli/daemon — no behavior change, no cross-package moves. Existing daemon tests are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: wire.go split into wire.go (composition) + lifecycle.go (runtime), same package, no symbol renames.
AC2: tools/lint/fatfiles/inventory.txt entry for wire.go lowered to its new wc -l (or deleted if <500).
AC3: existing internal/cli/daemon tests pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; one-line conventional commit.
close_reason: wire.go 1234→924 LOC. Pure same-package file split: the Daemon runtime lifecycle (Start/Stop + startMetricsListener/startMCPServer/awaitListenerSockets/rehydrateVectors/seedWatcher/startWatchLoop/startResync/startVulnRefresher/runWatchLoop/awaitBackgroundGoroutines/drainScans/closeResources/sumCounts) → new lifecycle.go (330). wire.go keeps the composition root (newDaemon + daemonBuilder phases + assemble + helpers). No symbol renames, no logic change, no nolint needed (zero per-function violations — verified non-diff-scoped before splitting). Gates green: vet, lint-size, layercheck, fatfile-ratchet (wire.go entry lowered to 924), daemon tests pass with sqlite_fts5.

title: Codebase navigability restructure (phase 3): in-layer god-files
description:
## Problem
Follow-on to the closed solov2-0omh epic (phase 2). 0omh thinned the fat cmd/veska files but explicitly deferred in-layer god-file decomposition. The fat-file ratchet (tools/lint/fatfiles/inventory.txt) still carries 14 files >=500 LOC; the 4 largest were named 'out of scope (future)' in 0omh's close reason.

## Approach
Behavior-preserving WITHIN-package file splits along existing function seams — no cross-package moves, no layering changes, no SQL/schema/behavior changes. Each split lowers the target file's inventory entry (the ratchet only ratchets DOWN). Existing tests are the safety net; every task keeps make all + make layercheck green.

## Goals
- The 4 largest inventory files split into cohesive same-package files, each ratchet entry lowered to its new value.
- No regressions: existing package tests pass unchanged.

## Out of scope
- Behavior/SQL/schema changes; new features.
- Cross-package moves that touch layering (this is intra-package file splitting only).
- The 10 smaller inventory files (<700 LOC: go_query.go, tools_helpers.go, repo/registry.go, secretsscanner.go, application/search.go, osv.go, graph_repo.go, embedder.go, mcp/registry.go, ts.go) — the ratchet already prevents them growing; revisit after the big 4 prove the pattern.

## Sequencing
Independent file splits — no blocking deps. Suggested order by payoff/risk: .2 promotion_store (clear sub-concern), .4 tools_search (handler-per-file), .3 treesitter/go (helper clusters), .1 wire.go (builder vs lifecycle).
close_reason: Phase-3 navigability restructure COMPLETE — the 4 headliner in-layer god-files split into focused same-package files, all behavior-preserving, every commit with make all green. .2 promotion_store.go 1027→541 (+promotion_callresolve.go 519); .4 tools_search.go 677→164 (+tools_search_semantic.go 282, tools_search_similar.go 291; extracted shared resolveK helper); .1 wire.go 1234→924 (+lifecycle.go 330, zero per-function violations); .3 treesitter/go.go 906→498 (+go_fields.go 149, go_typeinfer.go 290, now under threshold). Fat-file inventory: 14→12 entries (tools_search.go and go.go dropped below 500; promotion_store/wire entries lowered; callresolve added). Key finding for future splits: golangci --new-from-merge-base treats moved code as new, so a file split re-lints every moved function against the per-function gate; verbatim relocations of pre-existing oversized functions carry //nolint + a 'diff-scoped gate' reason rather than risky restructuring. 4 commits f47d336/138e9fb/33e1090/4704686. make all exit 0: build+test(./...)+vet+golangci+lint-size+layercheck+fatfile-ratchet+noidleak+cliparity all green with sqlite_fts5. Out of scope (future, still inventoried): the 10 smaller >=500 files.

title: Thin the mid-size cmd/veska command files (symbol, findings, graph)
description:
**Why:** cmd/veska/symbol.go (486), findings.go (515), graph.go (398) carry the same logic-in-Cobra-file smell at smaller scale (see epic solov2-0omh design doc; follows the u4mv thin-adapter pattern).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY cmd/veska/{symbol,findings,graph}.go and the packages they delegate into. Behavior-preserving — existing tests for these commands are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: every function in cmd/veska/{symbol,findings,graph}.go passes the size gate under golangci-lint -c .golangci-size.yml.
AC2: logic/formatting extracted to non-cmd packages; the three files are Cobra glue + delegating calls.
AC3: existing tests for these commands pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; ratchet entries lowered if present; one-line conventional commit.

title: Thin cmd/veska/doctor.go into Cobra glue + thin calls
description:
**Why:** cmd/veska/doctor.go is 1022 LOC / 23 funcs with the same Cobra-glue-mixed-with-logic smell (see epic solov2-0omh design doc; follows the u4mv thin-adapter pattern).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY cmd/veska/doctor.go and the packages it delegates into. Behavior-preserving — existing doctor_test.go and doctor_json_test.go are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: every function in cmd/veska/doctor.go passes the size gate under golangci-lint -c .golangci-size.yml.
AC2: logic/formatting extracted to non-cmd packages; the file is Cobra glue + delegating calls.
AC3: existing doctor cmd tests (doctor_test.go, doctor_json_test.go) pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; ratchet entry lowered if present; one-line conventional commit.

title: Thin cmd/veska/search.go into Cobra glue + thin calls
description:
**Why:** cmd/veska/search.go is 1086 LOC / 25 funcs with the same Cobra-glue-mixed-with-logic smell (see epic solov2-0omh design doc; follows the u4mv thin-adapter pattern).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY cmd/veska/search.go and the packages it delegates into. Behavior-preserving — existing search command tests are the safety net.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: every function in cmd/veska/search.go passes the size gate under golangci-lint -c .golangci-size.yml.
AC2: logic/formatting extracted to non-cmd packages; the file is Cobra glue + delegating calls.
AC3: existing search-related cmd tests pass unchanged.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; ratchet entry lowered if present; one-line conventional commit.

title: Thin cmd/veska/repo.go into Cobra glue + thin calls
description:
**Why:** cmd/veska/repo.go is 1221 LOC / 32 funcs mixing Cobra wiring, business logic, MCP dialing, and output formatting (see epic solov2-0omh design doc; follows the u4mv thin-adapter pattern).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY cmd/veska/repo.go and the packages it delegates into. Behavior-preserving — existing repo command tests are the safety net. Destination of extracted logic (application/platform/composition) is the worker's call.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: every function remaining in cmd/veska/repo.go passes the existing gate (<=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic, <=5 args) under golangci-lint run -c .golangci-size.yml on the file.
AC2: business logic / MCP dialing / formatting now live in non-cmd packages; repo.go is Cobra wiring + delegating calls only.
AC3: existing cmd/veska repo tests pass unchanged (behavior preserved).
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; ratchet entry for repo.go lowered if present; one-line conventional commit.

title: Add a fat-file size ratchet to replace grandfathering
description:
**Why:** the size gate (`make lint-size`) uses --new-from-merge-base, so it only checks changed code; every pre-existing fat file is exempt forever and the backlog never shrinks (see epic solov2-0omh design doc).

**Scope boundary:** add a checked-in inventory of oversized files (recorded max LOC each) + a make target wired into `make all`/CI. This task does NOT shrink any file — that is the WS-2 thinning tasks. Ratchet must predate those tasks so its baseline counts the debt they pay down.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: a make target fails when any inventoried file exceeds its recorded LOC, and passes at current sizes (demonstrated by a deliberate +1-line bump failing it).
AC2: the target is invoked by make all (or a documented CI step) so the ratchet runs in the existing quality gate.
AC3: shrinking an inventoried file and lowering its recorded value keeps the target green (ratchet only ratchets down).
DoD: make all green with the new target; inventory documents how to update an entry; one-line conventional commit.

title: Relocate domain-bearing packages into their hexagonal layers
description:
**Why:** doctor, savings, backup, repo are domain/storage subsystems living as loose top-level packages instead of inside a hexagonal layer (see epic solov2-0omh design doc).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY doctor, savings, backup, repo. Proposed homes (worker may override if import direction dictates, recording why in the commit): doctor,savings → internal/application; backup,repo → internal/infrastructure. layercheck is the arbiter of placement, not the plan. No behavior changes — git mv + import rewrites only.

Ordering: contends on shared import sites with the cross-cutting relocate and WS-2 tasks — serialize.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: internal/{doctor,savings,backup,repo} no longer exist at the internal/ top level; each resolves under the hexagonal layer matching its dependency direction (proposed doctor,savings->application; backup,repo->infrastructure).
AC2: make layercheck passes (no domain/ports importing infra after the move); grep for any pre-move import path returns nothing.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; go fmt/fix run; existing tests pass unchanged; one-line conventional commit.

title: Consolidate cross-cutting packages under internal/platform
description:
**Why:** internal/ top level is cluttered with cross-cutting utility packages that aren't a hexagonal layer, obscuring the architecture (see epic solov2-0omh design doc).

**Scope boundary:** ONLY the cross-cutting packages (config, observability, service, logrotate, crashloop, tokenize, embedderprobe) plus deletion of the dead 2-LOC internal/bootstrap. Domain-bearing packages (doctor/savings/backup/repo) belong to the sibling relocate task, not here. No behavior changes — git mv + import rewrites only.

Ordering: contends on shared import sites (cmd/veska/*, daemon/wire.go) with the other relocate/thin tasks — serialize, don't parallelize.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: the seven cross-cutting packages (config, observability, service, logrotate, crashloop, tokenize, embedderprobe) resolve under internal/platform/ (e.g. internal/platform/config); none remain at the internal/ top level.
AC2: internal/bootstrap is removed and nothing imports it.
AC3: make layercheck passes; grep for any pre-move import path returns nothing.
DoD: make all + make layercheck green; go fmt/fix run; existing tests pass unchanged; one-line conventional commit, no Co-Authored-By.

title: Codebase navigability restructure (phase 2)
description:
# Codebase navigability restructure (phase 2)

## Problem
Follow-on to the closed solov2-u4mv epic. u4mv killed four named god-functions and
collapsed DI duplication, but deliberately left two structural problems untouched:
(1) internal/ no longer reads as hexagonal — the 4 real layers
(core/application/infrastructure/cli) are drowned among 13 loose top-level packages
with no grouping principle, so a reader cannot tell a domain layer from a
cross-cutting util from a misplaced adapter.
(2) The fat files survived: the size lint gate uses --new-from-merge-base, so it
only polices *changed* code and every pre-existing large file (cmd/veska/repo.go
1221, search.go 1086, doctor.go 1022, …) is grandfathered forever.

## Approach
Behavior-preserving. Three workstreams:
- WS-1: regroup the loose internal/ packages so internal/ shows only layer dirs +
  internal/platform (cross-cutting) + internal/composition. Pure git mv + import
  rewrites, proven by `make layercheck`.
- WS-2: thin the fat cmd/veska files into Cobra-glue + thin calls, applying the
  pattern u4mv established (logic → application/platform/composition), landing every
  function under the existing 50-LOC gate.
- WS-4: convert the size gate from grandfather to ratchet — a checked-in fat-file
  inventory + a make target that fails when an inventoried file grows, so the
  backlog can only shrink.
Existing tests are the safety net. Every task keeps `make all` + `make layercheck`
green.

## Goals
- `ls internal/` reads as the architecture: core, application, infrastructure, cli,
  platform, composition — no homeless packages.
- Targeted fat cmd/veska files contain only Cobra wiring + thin calls; every
  function in them passes the 50-LOC/15-cyclomatic/5-arg gate.
- A size ratchet exists in CI; inventoried files can only shrink.

## Out of scope
- God-file decomposition inside the layers (promotion_store.go 1027,
  treesitter/go.go 906, mcp/tools_search.go 677, wire.go remainder).
- SQL schema/query changes; behavior changes; new features.
- tools/loadtest/embed_models/out corpora.

## Sequencing (ordering hints — NOT blocking deps)
- WS-1 (.1, .2) first: highest navigability payoff, lowest risk.
- WS-4 (.3) before WS-2 (.4–.7): the ratchet baseline must predate the
  file-shrinking tasks, or its inventory under-counts the debt they pay down.
- Serialization: .1, .2, and the WS-2 tasks all rewrite imports in shared consumer
  files (cmd/veska/*, daemon/wire.go). Run one at a time / rebase to avoid
  import-line merge churn. This is contention, not a dependency — there are no hard
  dependency edges in this epic by design.

## Risks
- config (28 importers) and repo (29 importers) are the widest rewrites; mechanical
  but touch many files — rely on goimports + grep-clean check.
- Placement of doctor/backup/repo under a layer may be constrained by their actual
  import direction; layercheck is the arbiter, not the plan.
notes: cmd/veska arch-review cleanup (3 commits on main): (1) moved deps-index SQL out of the delivery layer into repocmd.FindTrackedRepoByModulePath/LookupRepoRootAndBranch; retired the prod-dead doctorSubCmd helper into a test fixture. (2) extracted reindex logic into internal/cli/reindexcmd (Run + Params seams DaemonRunning/DialReindex/ReparserFactory/MatchByPath, MergeTarget, DefaultDial) and wiki logic into internal/cli/wikicmd (Run + ResolveTarget). reparserFactory/matchByPath stay cmd-owned shared seams (search+reindex). (3) closed the package-var seam smell: dialReindex->DefaultDial, reindexDaemonProbe->DaemonRunning param, installColdScanCheckPipeline var->func. cmd/veska reindex.go/wiki.go now thin cobra glue matching the doctor/symbol/graph/findings pattern. layercheck+lint clean, tests migrated with assertions preserved.

comments.author: whiskeyjimbo
comments.text:
planning:rejection — Two dependency edges were considered and deliberately NOT added, so the orchestrator doesn't re-introduce them as cross_cutting deps:
(1) .4-.7 -> .3 (ratchet baseline should predate the file-shrinking tasks): REJECTED as a hard dep — those tasks can start without the ratchet. Kept as a strong ordering hint in the epic body (run .3 before .4-.7 so its inventory counts the debt they pay down).
(2) .4 -> .2 (repo.go thinning and the repo-package relocation touch the same module): REJECTED — contention, not a blocker. Serialize/rebase, do not gate.
There are no hard dependency edges in this epic by design; all couplings are ordering + import-rewrite contention, documented in the epic body.

title: Establish shared composition package and migrate cold-scan wiring
description:
**Why:** `cmd/veska/reindex.go` duplicates the daemon's cold-scan wiring (FTSSink + EmbedRefSink + check pipeline), kept in sync only by hand-written "must stay in lock-step with wire.go" comments — drift here silently breaks findings/embeddings on the cold-scan path.

**Scope boundary:** only the cold-scan reparser + check-pipeline + promotion-store construction. Wiki/search construction is out (that is .4). New package may import infra; domain/ports must not.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Approach — shared composition package).

**Definition of Done:** targeted `go test ./internal/cli/daemon/...` + `./cmd/veska/...` + `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: cold-scan reparser+sink+check-pipeline wiring defined in exactly one constructor; grep shows reindex.go and daemon/wire.go both call it (no second FTSSink+EmbedRefSink site); new constructors <=50 LOC, <=5 params. AC2: veska reindex and daemon cold-scan still promote with FTS + embed-refs + secret/vuln checks (reindex_test.go + daemon providers_test.go pass). AC3: make layercheck passes (new pkg may import infra; domain/ports must not).
close_reason: Created internal/composition package with ColdScanCore (staging→gate→ingester, PromotionStore with FTS+EmbedRef sinks, promoter), GitAddedLinesFunc (shared AddedLines seam, parameterized on a repo-root resolver), and CheckRunnerAdapter (was two byte-identical copies: daemon.checkRunnerAdapter + reindex.coldScanCheckRunner). Both cmd/veska/reindex.go and internal/cli/daemon/wire.go now call composition.NewColdScanCore — AC1 verified: NewEmbedRefSink() construction exists in exactly ONE place (composition) and both consumers call the core. Behavior preserved by NOT unifying the genuinely-divergent parts: daemon keeps its richer check set (deadcode+contract-drift+secrets+vuln), shared metricsReg, scan tracker, and its own repoRootFunc; CLI keeps secrets+vuln only and repoRootByID. reviewEnabled param preserves daemon's WithReviewEnabled(fileCfg.Review.Enabled) vs CLI's false (== default). Daemon retains local staging/gate/ingester/promoter names so downstream wiring is untouched; Gate exposed from core for the (write-only) daemon struct field. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/cli/daemon/... (incl wire_test.go, providers_test.go) + ./cmd/veska/... (incl reindex_test.go) + ./internal/composition/... all PASS; added coldscan_test.go (CheckRunner interface assertion + GitAddedLinesFunc error propagation). AC3: make layercheck OK (new pkg imports infra; domain/ports clean). vet clean; changed packages lint-clean (0 issues). NOTE: 'make all' is red at lint due to 7 PRE-EXISTING staticcheck/unused issues in untouched files (filed solov2-u4mv.8) — none introduced here, confirmed by git diff exclusion. Commit 3e1ef86.

title: Codebase maintainability restructure
description:
# Codebase maintainability restructure

## Problem
The hexagonal foundation is intact and layercheck-enforced, but maintainability
has degraded in four localized ways:
- **God-functions**: `sqlite.PromotionStore.Promote` ~566 LOC, `daemon.newDaemon`
  ~560 LOC, `registerMCPTools` ~238 LOC.
- **Duplicated DI wiring** between `cmd/veska/{reindex,wiki}.go` and
  `daemon/wire.go`, kept in sync only by hand-written "must stay in lock-step"
  comments.
- **Fat cmd/veska command files** (`repo.go` 1398, `search.go` 1160,
  `doctor.go` 1022) mixing Cobra wiring, business logic, MCP dialing, formatting.
- **Cosmetic `tool_*`/`tools_*` naming drift** in `mcp/`.

## Approach
Behavior-preserving restructure. Introduce one shared composition package
(`internal/composition` — placement is the worker's call) that owns the
cold-scan reparser, check pipeline, wiki handler, and search service
construction, consumed by BOTH daemon and CLI — eliminating the lock-step
duplication. With that facade in place, decompose the god-functions into focused
builders and thin the Cobra files into adapters. Extract MCP client-dialing into
its own package. Normalize file naming last. Make the size/complexity thresholds
self-enforcing via golangci-lint on changed code. No SQL/behavior changes;
existing tests are the safety net.

## Goals
- Every touched function: **≤50 LOC, ≤15 cyclomatic complexity, ≤5 parameters**.
- Cold-scan / wiki / search wiring defined in exactly one place.
- `cmd/veska` files = Cobra glue + thin calls only.
- Thresholds CI-enforced for changed code; `make all` + `make layercheck` green.

## Out of scope
- New features (incl. solov2-yh5a CLI wrappers); SQL schema/query changes;
  performance tuning; the loadtest corpora under tools/loadtest/embed_models/out.
- Repo-wide remediation of pre-existing threshold violations outside touched files.

## Risks / open questions
- Composition-package placement (`internal/composition` vs `internal/cli/wiring`)
  is the worker's call.
- Merge collisions on `wire.go` and `search.go` are managed via dependency
  sequencing (.3→.2, .4→.2, .5→.4), not parallelism.
- "Behavior-preserving" could hide subtle wiring drift — mitigated by the full
  test + make all gate per task.

## Constraints
- Conventional one-line commits, no Co-Authored-By; `go fmt ./... && go fix ./...`
  pre-commit; targeted `go test` per touched package plus `make all` before close.

## Ordering hints (not dependencies)
- .1 and .6 are independent, run anytime.
- .7 (lint guardrail) has no hard dep — land EARLY to enforce the bar on .1–.5 in
  CI, or LAST to verify against its AC3.
- solov2-yh5a (CLI wrappers for 12 MCP tools) should follow .5, which extracts the
  MCP client it needs.
close_reason: Codebase maintainability restructure COMPLETE. All planned tasks done: .1 (Promote split), .2 (composition cold-scan core), .4 (wiki handler shared + CLI search relocated), .5 (mcpclient extracted), .6 (mcp tools_ naming), .7 (size/complexity/arg lint gate on changed code), .9+.10 (registerMCPTools + newDaemon decomposed, from .3 split), plus discovered follow-ups .8 (pre-existing lint/noidleak debt) and .11 (Start/Stop). Four god-functions eliminated, daemon<->CLI duplication collapsed into internal/composition + internal/cli/mcpclient, naming normalized, and a self-enforcing 50-LOC/15-cyclomatic/5-arg guardrail added for changed code. 10 commits on epic-solov2-u4mv-codebase-maintainability-restructure; every commit behavior-preserving with make all green. Remaining adjacent (not part of this epic): solov2-yh5a (CLI wrappers, now unblocked by .5).

title: dead-code rule is overwhelmingly noisy on library repos (220 findings on pflag)
description: Junior journey: 'veska repo add /path/to/spf13/pflag' produced 220 low-severity dead-code findings on first scan (every exported helper that nothing in pflag's own tree calls). Findings list defaults to hiding low-sev so you don't see it, but if a user passes --include-low or scopes by repo, they see hundreds of 'unused' on standard library API. Libraries are by definition mostly callers-elsewhere, so the rule needs library-awareness: skip exported identifiers in repos whose module path is a published library (presence of go.mod with non-binary main package), or require evidence of zero callers across ALL registered repos before flagging. Right now the signal is unusable on any dep you index.
close_reason: Three-phase fix bringing pflag's junior-journey dead-code count from 220 → 1 (99.5%). PHASE 1 (commit fffb528): skip dead-code on methods whose bare name matches an interface method declared in the same repo — interface dispatch produces no CALLS edge the static graph can see. Added DeadCodeQuerier.InterfaceMethodNames port, sqlite implementation joining nodes (kind=interface) → nodes (kind=method, symbol_path starts with iface name). Killed ~160 of the boolValue.Set / boolfuncValue.IsBoolFlag findings. PHASE 2 (commit b544570): drop 'type', 'struct', 'interface' from deadCodeKinds. A struct is not callable — it's referenced via composite literal, function signature, embedding — none of which produce CALLS edges. Applying a callable test to non-callable kinds was the original false-positive engine. The remaining kinds (function, method, class) all have meaningful CALLS liveness. Killed ~30 of the type-kind findings. PHASE 3 (commit b544570): parser — new calls.scm pattern captures bare-identifier arguments to call_expression, the Go extractor classifies as a CALLS edge when the identifier resolves to a same-file function/method via the in-file symbol map. Symbol-map gate prevents phantom edges for parameters/locals. Killed ~30 of the *Conv findings (boolConv passed to f.getFlagType etc.). Live verified on /tmp/junior-pflag: 220 → 1, the surviving textValue.Get is genuinely unreferenced by the in-graph view (likely satisfies stdlib flag.Getter which we don't index; future work: stdlib interface set). New tests: TestDeadCodeCheck_SkipsInterfaceMethodImplementations, TestParseFile_FunctionPassedAsArgumentEmitsCallsEdge, TestParseFile_BareIdentifierArg_NonFunctionSkipped. Existing test cases for type-kind dead-code updated to reflect the new (correct) contract. Full app/sqlite/mcp/parser test sweep clean. Commits fffb528 b544570.

title: Cross-repo blast loses callsite grain for every cobra app (package-grain CALLS)
description: Junior journey: greetcli is a stock cobra app — root.go declares 'var helloCmd = &cobra.Command{ RunE: func(...){ greetlib.New(...) } }'. The blast/context output reports the source as 'package cmd' because the call lives in an anon func inside a var initialiser. Veska prints a helpful disclaimer but the practical effect is: for the dominant Go CLI pattern in the ecosystem, cross-repo blast cannot answer 'which command in cli/ depends on lib symbol X.' Two paths: (1) attribute the call to the surrounding var name (helloCmd) so blast says 'helloCmd in cmd/hello.go --CALLS--> Greeting.Render', (2) descend into anon-func bodies inside var initialisers as first-class nodes. Either fixes the cobra-shaped blind spot.
close_reason: Root cause: extractAnonCallsInTopLevelVars hard-coded pkgNode as the caller for every CALLS edge extracted from a func_literal anywhere under a var/const declaration — covering the dominant cobra-app shape (var helloCmd = &cobra.Command{ RunE: func(){ Foo() } }) with package-grain attribution. The pre-bead workaround was a renderer-side disclaimer ('package-grain src means the caller is an anonymous func in a top-level var initialiser'). Fix: thread an enclosing-var caller through the walker. When the walk descends into a var_spec with a single resolvable name, look it up in the in-file symbols map (top-level vars become Variable nodes per solov2-b7wt) and shadow caller for the subtree. The shadow is value-passed so leaving the subtree restores the previous caller. Falls back to pkgNode for shapes where no enclosing var is identifiable (const blocks, multi-name var blocks). var_spec.name appears as a bare identifier for  and as a parent of identifiers for  — handled both. Regression: TestParseFile_AnonCallsInTopLevelVar updated to assert root→serveRoot / chk→validate (the new contract) and to NEGATIVELY assert no pkg→target edges (the old false attribution). New TestParseFile_AnonFuncInVarInitAttributesToSurroundingVar pins the cobra-shaped pattern directly. The 'package-grain src' disclaimer in cmd/veska/graph.go's renderer is now ungrounded for new data but kept in place for old graphs — will fade on reindex. Full app/sqlite/mcp/parser test sweep clean. Commit 4b17541.

title: In-repo CALLS edges from _test.go methods miss the Greeting.Render call
description: Junior journey repro: greetlib has greet.go with method Greeting.Render and greet_test.go where TestRender/TestRenderLoud do 'g := New("world"); g.Render()'. veska blast Render shows ONLY cross-repo edges (3 from greetcli) — zero in-repo callers. The two test functions plainly call Render via the method receiver, so either tree-sitter isn't extracting selector_expression CALLS edges from _test.go, or tests are silently excluded from blast. Either way tests-as-callers is a high-value signal for any 'safe to refactor?' query and should not be silently zero. Decide: include tests with a flag/marker, or exclude consistently and document it. Same fixture: g.Loud = loud is captured as a struct-field write but Render itself isn't pulled from test files.
close_reason: Root cause: parser only knew how to resolve method calls on local vars assigned from PACKAGE-QUALIFIED constructors (v := pkg.New(), bound v→pkg via collectLocalVarOrigins). Bare same-package constructors (v := New() in greet_test.go) fell through: localOrigins missed, default branch emitted UnresolvedCall{PkgQualifier:"g"}, promotion's import-alias lookup couldn't bind 'g' to anything, no stub or edge. Test functions thus had no CALLS edges to the methods they exercised — TestRender, TestRenderLoud were invisible to blast on Render. Fix: new collectInFileFunctionReturns walks top-level function_declaration nodes for single, simple return types (T, *T, parameter_list wrapping either); new collectLocalReceiverTypes binds v→ReceiverType when RHS is a bare-identifier call to a function in funcReturns. extractCallsFromBody gets a new branch in the call.operand+call.field switch: when localRecvTypes[op] is set, ref binds to <ReceiverType>.<field>, matching the in-file symbol map directly the same way the existing recvName branch does for s.foo() inside a method. Both shapes covered by tests: TestParseFile_SamePackageConstructorMethodCall (value return) and TestParseFile_SamePackageConstructorMethodCall_PointerReturn (pointer return, simpleReturnTypeName unwraps *T → T). Skipped intentionally: multi-result returns, qualified return types (pkg.Other), generics — recording the wrong type would invent false edges, and the no-false-edges invariant takes precedence over recall. Full app/sqlite/mcp/parser test sweep clean under sqlite_fts5. Commit 9e93f38.

title: MCP empty 'hits' serializes as null, not [] (violates README convention)
description: README under MCP conventions states: 'Empty result collections serialize as [], never omitted.' Counterexample: printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eng_get_blast_radius","params":{"symbol":"Render","direction":"callers"}}' | bin/veska-mcp returns .result.hits = null when no in-repo callers exist (cross_repo_edges populated separately). Agents that do 'for h in result.hits' crash on null. Either marshal nil slices via the json:",omitempty" antithesis (always emit []) or audit each tool's response struct to use a non-nil zero. Same likely affects other tools that lazily-init their hits/results slices.
close_reason: Not a bug — investigator error. Original repro asked jq for '.hits' on the blast response, but the actual JSON field is 'entries' (BlastResponse.Entries at tools_blast.go:17, json tag 'entries'). jq returns null for any missing key, which I misread as 'empty collection serialised as null'. Verified live across the surface — eng_find_symbol with no match returns nodes: [], eng_list_findings on a clean repo returns findings: [] degraded_reasons: [], eng_find_todos returns todos: [] degraded_reasons: [], eng_get_call_chain on a leaf returns nodes: [] edges: [], eng_search_semantic returns results: []. All response slice fields marshal as [] when empty, matching the README convention. No production code changed.

title: MCP repo_id requirement inconsistent across tools
description: Junior MCP client expectation: tools that accept a free-floating 'symbol' should resolve cross-repo or demand a repo_id consistently. Currently: eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_context_pack, eng_find_symbol all happily resolve a bare symbol; eng_search_similar refuses without repo_id. Same shape, different contract. Either make eng_search_similar resolve cross-repo via the same FindNodes path the others use, or document the divergence in the README conventions block (it currently lists eng_search_similar as accepting 'symbol' alongside 'node_id' but does not say repo_id is also mandatory).
close_reason: Root cause: makeSearchSimilarHandler used resolveRepoIDFromParams + a manual graph.FindNodes call instead of the shared resolveSeedOwner helper that peer tools (eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_context_pack, eng_find_symbol via similar code paths) use. resolveRepoIDFromParams hard-requires repo_id when multi-repo and no cwd hint is injected; resolveSeedOwner short-circuits cleanly when len(repos)==1 and fans out across all registered repos when len>1, raising InvalidParams only on a TRUE cross-repo ambiguity. Fix: replace the bespoke resolver+symbol-lookup+expandNodeIDPrefix block with a single resolveSeedOwner call — node_id wins, symbol resolves via FindNodes, short-id prefix expansion happens inside the helper's resolveInRepo. README conventions block now actually applies uniformly. Regression test TestSearchSimilar_SymbolResolvesCrossRepoWithoutRepoID seeds a single registered repo and calls eng_search_similar with symbol only (no repo_id, no branch, no cwd) — passes via the single-repo short-circuit. All existing TestSearchSimilar_* tests unchanged and still passing. Commit 50d7902.

title: 'N repos registered' helper text is off-by-one (counts deps/extra entries)
description: With 4 tracked repos visible to eng_list_repos and 'veska repo list', the error message from eng_list_findings/eng_find_todos/eng_search_similar says '5 repos registered; pass eng_list_repos to find the id'. Whatever count source the error builder uses differs from what list endpoints return, so the hint sends agents looking for a fifth repo that doesn't exist. Trace the count call in the missing-repo_id branch and align it with eng_list_repos.
close_reason: Root cause: requireRepoID-style call sites (tools_helpers.go:129 and :250) built the 'N repos registered' hint from raw len(all), where all is the unfiltered application.RepoLister output — which includes synthetic ext:<module> rows produced by 'veska deps index'. But eng_list_repos hides ext: rows by default (tools_admin.go:244-253, gated on include_vendored=true), so the hint pointed agents at a count they could not reconcile against the listing it told them to run. Junior-journey concrete: 4 tracked repos + 1 deps-indexed cobra = ListRepos returns 5, eng_list_repos returns 4, error said 5. Fix: new userVisibleRepoCount helper applies the same ext: prefix filter as eng_list_repos's default mode, called from both error-format sites. Regression test TestRequireRepoID_CountExcludesExtRepos seeds a lister with 2 tracked + 1 ext: repo and asserts the error says '2 repos registered' (not 3). Live verified: with 1 tracked sam-mini repo, error reports '1 repos registered'. Commit d632e8a.

title: MCP eng_list_repos drops root path (returns null)
description: CLI 'veska repo list' shows the root path for each repo. The MCP equivalent eng_list_repos returns root=null on every repo. Editor-side MCP clients have no way to surface the repo's filesystem root, even though README implies the same shape across surfaces. Repro: printf JSON-RPC eng_list_repos | bin/veska-mcp | jq '.result.repos' — every entry has root: null.
close_reason: Original bd repro was wrong (investigator error): I queried '.root' in jq, but the actual JSON field is 'root_path' (tools_admin.go:158, RepoView.RootPath json tag 'root_path'). root_path is populated correctly — verified live: eng_list_repos returns root_path='/tmp/sam-mini' for the test repo. HOWEVER, inspection while triaging surfaced a real instance of the same null-vs-[] convention violation in the same response: RepoView.Aliases reached the wire as 'aliases': null when r.Aliases was a nil []string from the registry, which violates the README convention (empty collections serialise as []) and crashes agents iterating the field without a nil-check. Fixed in decorateRepo: materialise aliases as []string{} when nil. Affects eng_list_repos AND eng_get_repo (both go through decorateRepo). Regression test added to TestAdminTools_ListRepos: round-trip through json.Marshal and assert 'aliases':[] is in the wire bytes (a nil []string masquerades as the same type at the Go level, so only marshal output detects the bug). Commit b23d375.

title: Cross-repo CALLS edges lose call-site line metadata — all edges from func main() report main.go:<func-decl-line>
description:
Split from solov2-izh6.22 on 2026-05-28 (see that bead's notes).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
REPRO
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Journey fixture: greetctl cli with cobra commands declared inside main():

  func main() {                                    // line 11
      ...
      greet := &cobra.Command{
          RunE: func(...) error {
              g := libfoo.NewGreeter(prefix)       // line 25
              fmt.Println(g.Greet(name))           // line 26
              ...
          },
      }
      ...
  }

veska blast Greet output:
  main in cli/main.go:11 --CALLS--> NewGreeter in libfoo/greeter.go:17
  main in cli/main.go:11 --CALLS--> Greeter.Greet in libfoo/greeter.go:22
  main in cli/main.go:11 --CALLS--> Greeter.Shout in libfoo/greeter.go:30

All three edges report main.go:11 (the func main() declaration line) instead of the actual call-site lines (~25, ~26, ~40 inside the RunE closure). Same loss in 'veska context' and the MCP cross_repo_edges rendering.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ROOT CAUSE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
domain.Edge stores (Src node_id, Tgt node_id, Kind, Confidence) — no per-edge source-line. Renderers fetch the source node by ID and use its LineRange.Start, which is the function declaration line. Same for in-repo CALLS edges and for cross_repo_edge_stub → resolved cross-repo edge conversions.

For a user, this collapses all call-site information from a multi-call function into one line number, which destroys the 'go to where this is called' UX that calls/blast/context promises.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DESIGN PASS NEEDED (before implementation)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
This is a schema change with broad consumer impact. Decisions:

1. WHERE THE LINE LIVES
   a. New optional field on domain.Edge: SrcLine int (0 = unknown, preserves backward compat).
   b. New edge metadata table keyed by edge ID with arbitrary key/value (overkill for one field).
   c. Per-edge endpoint annotation (Src/Tgt each get optional line offsets).
   Lean (a) — single column, single field, easy to migrate, cheap to read.

2. EXTRACTION
   calls.scm captures call_expression as @call.expr — we already have the AST node. Extend the callRef struct in go_query.go with a srcLine field and thread it through extractCallsFromBody → resolved CALLS edges and UnresolvedCalls → cross_repo_edge_stubs.

3. STORAGE
   sqlite edges schema gains a src_line column (or generic 'src_meta_line' JSON column if we want flexibility). Migration is additive — old edges read 0/null and render as today.

4. CROSS-REPO PROPAGATION
   cross_repo_edge_stub format already carries some metadata; add src_line to it. The resolver in promotion_store.go threads it through to the final edge.

5. CONSUMERS
   - calls/blast/context CLI renderers (cmd/veska/{graph.go,blast.go,context.go,...})
   - MCP tools_graph response shapes (eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_context_pack)
   - cross_repo_edges DTO
   - find_callers reverse-edge case
   - the wiki entry_points generator (inbound counts ignore line, but rendering may surface them later)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ACCEPTANCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. domain.Edge gains an optional SrcLine field; sqlite schema migrates additively.
2. Go parser populates SrcLine from the call_expression's start line for every CALLS edge and UnresolvedCall (including those inside func_literal subtrees).
3. cross_repo_edge_stub carries src_line; resolution into a final cross-repo edge preserves it.
4. veska blast Greet on the journey fixture shows each cross-repo edge at its actual call-site line (~25/~26/~40), not main.go:11.
5. veska calls/context likewise.
6. MCP eng_get_call_chain / eng_get_blast_radius / eng_get_context_pack edges include the line in their cross_repo_edges and edges payloads.
7. Backward-compat: pre-migration edges (SrcLine=0) render at the caller-node's start line, identical to today.
8. Tests: parser test pins SrcLine on a multi-call function; sqlite test pins schema round-trip; MCP test pins payload shape; CLI golden updated.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SUGGESTED ORDER
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Discovery first — small spike to pick the WHERE/STORAGE/PROPAGATION shape (#1-4 above) and write a 5-line ADR. Then implementation behind tests. The work itself is mechanical once the shape is settled.
notes:
DESIGN PASS 2026-05-29 — scope smaller than feared. Discovery:

ALREADY IN PLACE (no work needed):
- domain.Edge has 'SourceLine *int' field (edge.go:45)
- domain.WithSourceLine(line) functional option (edge.go:60-66)
- MCP edgeDTO already serializes 'source_line' JSON tag (mcp/dto.go:50, 153)

MISSING PIECES (the actual work):
1. SQLite schema — neither 'edges' nor 'cross_repo_edge_stubs' has a src_line column. Need migration 0016 with two additive ALTER TABLE statements. INTEGER nullable; legacy rows read as NULL → renderer falls back to caller-node line, matching today's behaviour byte-for-byte.

2. domain.UnresolvedCall — needs new 'SrcLine int' field so the parser→promoter→cross-repo-stub path can carry the line through to the resolved cross-repo edge. Today UnresolvedCall has CallerID/CalleeName/PkgQualifier/IsMethodCall but no line info.

3. Parser (treesitter/go_query.go) — calls.scm captures @call.expr per match; pull StartByte()+lineRange to get the start line, pass WithSourceLine(line) to NewEdge in extractCallsFromBody and set UnresolvedCall.SrcLine. extractAnonCallsInTopLevelVars uses the same code path so closures-in-vars get it for free.

4. PromotionStore — include src_line in:
   - INSERT INTO edges (existing INSERT, add column + bind)
   - INSERT INTO cross_repo_edge_stubs (likewise)
   - cross_repo_edge_stub → resolved Edge conversion (read stub.src_line, pass via WithSourceLine when constructing the Edge)

5. GraphRepo.LoadGraph — scanEdge currently reads (src,dst,kind,confidence); add src_line, populate Edge.SourceLine when non-NULL.

6. Renderers (cmd/veska/graph.go, blast.go, context.go) — when env.CrossRepoEdges[i].SrcLine is set, render '<file>:<src_line>' instead of looking up the caller node's start line. The 'note: package-grain src means…' warning (graph.go ~L281) becomes less common once closures-in-cmd-vars carry their actual lines.

ORDER OF OPERATIONS:
  a) Test fixture first: parser test on a multi-call body asserts each edge carries the expected SourceLine.
  b) Schema migration + scanEdge round-trip test.
  c) Promoter changes + integration test.
  d) Cross-repo stub propagation.
  e) Renderer updates + golden output on the two-repo cobra journey fixture.

BACKWARD COMPAT:
  - Migration is additive; pre-0016 edges read with NULL src_line → Edge.SourceLine=nil.
  - Renderer: nil → use caller-node LineRange.Start (today's behaviour). Non-nil → use the value.
  - MCP: source_line is omitempty in JSON so old clients see no shape change.
close_reason:
Fixed in 8bcbe3f. Cross-repo CALLS edges now carry the actual call-site line instead of the caller node's declaration line. Discovery (recorded in earlier notes) found domain.Edge.SourceLine + WithSourceLine + the MCP source_line JSON tag were already in place; only producers / persistence / propagation were missing.

Changes (17 files, 1 new migration):

DOMAIN
  - domain.UnresolvedCall +SrcLine int — parser→promoter line carrier
  - ports.ResolvedEdge +SrcLine int — resolver→consumer line carrier

PARSER (treesitter)
  - callRef +line int — per-match carrier
  - extractCallsFromBody reads @call.expr.StartPoint().Row+1 for every query match and threads ref.line into both NewEdge(WithSourceLine) and UnresolvedCall.SrcLine
  - New test TestParseFile_CallsEdgeCarriesSourceLine pins three calls in a body resolve to their actual lines

PERSISTENCE
  - migration 0016: additive ALTER TABLE on edges and cross_repo_edge_stubs to add src_line INTEGER (nullable). Pre-migration rows read as NULL → renderer fallback to caller-line behaviour preserved.
  - promotion_store.go: INSERT statements include src_line bind; same-package cross-file and cross-package edge inserts both honour UnresolvedCall.SrcLine via WithSourceLine.
  - new helpers edgeSrcLine(*Edge) any and ucSrcLine(UnresolvedCall) any keep the bind logic centralised and NULL-aware.
  - graph_repo.go: LoadGraph edge scan reads src_line and threads to WithSourceLine on rehydrate.

CROSS-REPO RESOLVER
  - resolver.CrossRepoStub +SrcLine int; both stub SELECTs read src_line; ResolveCrossRepoEdge sets it on the returned ResolvedEdge.

MCP
  - CrossRepoEdge DTO +src_line JSON tag (omitempty)
  - all six CrossRepoEdge construction sites (tools_blast.go ×2, tool_get_call_chain.go ×2, tools_contextpack.go ×2) copy re.SrcLine through

CLI
  - CrossRepoEdgeDTO +src_line JSON tag; renderer overrides src.Line with e.SrcLine when set so each cross-repo edge prints at its actual call site rather than the caller's declaration line.

End-to-end verification on a fresh 2-repo cli+libfoo fixture (analogue of the original cobra journey):
  $ veska blast NewGreeter
  cross-repo edges (3):
    main in /tmp/edge-verify/cli/main.go:10 --CALLS--> NewGreeter in libfoo/greeter.go:7 in <id>
    main in /tmp/edge-verify/cli/main.go:11 --CALLS--> Greeter.Greet in libfoo/greeter.go:8 in <id>
    main in /tmp/edge-verify/cli/main.go:12 --CALLS--> Greeter.Shout in libfoo/greeter.go:9 in <id>

Each edge now points to its own call-site line (10, 11, 12) instead of all three reporting main.go:9 (the func main declaration line). Mirrors what 'veska context' / 'veska calls' show via the same renderer path.

Race-clean across internal/... and cmd/veska/; layercheck OK; vet clean. Backward compatible: pre-migration edges read NULL src_line and render unchanged.

Migration adoption: users see accurate lines on newly-promoted repos automatically. To refresh existing repos, run 'veska reindex <path>' for each.

title: eng_find_symbol returns empty silently during cold-scan; no degraded_reasons hint
description:
Surfaced while verifying the izh6.27 regression claim (see notes on .27).

Repro: register a small repo via 'veska repo add --wait', commit a new exported symbol, then immediately call eng_find_symbol via the MCP shim. At t+~3s after the commit-time promotion, the daemon returns:

  {"nodes":[],"included_staging":true,"degraded_reasons":[]}

Re-running the same call at t+~10s returns the symbol normally. The window is real and observable on a 2-file repo on a quiet laptop — it will be much wider on real repos under load.

Problem: degraded_reasons is empty during this window. An agent or junior reading [] concludes the symbol doesn't exist and either gives up or fabricates an answer. Compare with the chained_selectors_unresolved hint on calls/blast — that pattern is exactly what's needed here.

Acceptance:
- When eng_find_symbol returns no nodes AND there is an in-flight cold scan or non-empty post-promotion queue for any repo whose namespace the query could plausibly touch (fan-out case: any repo at all), the response includes degraded_reasons=["indexing_in_progress"] (or a more specific name) plus a structured hint listing the repo_ids still indexing.
- The hint is best-effort and non-blocking — do not stall the call waiting for the scan to finish.
- Mirror the same treatment in eng_search_semantic, eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_context_pack — any read tool that can plausibly return an empty result while the index is still being populated.
- CLI verbs ('veska symbol', 'veska search', 'veska calls', 'veska blast', 'veska context') print a one-line 'hint: <repo> is still indexing (N tasks pending)' when the degraded_reason fires.
- Tests in internal/infrastructure/mcp/tools_graph_test.go and tools_search_test.go cover the empty-result-during-cold-scan case using a queue-blocked fixture (same shaping as izh6.16/.17 used).
notes:
Discovered while running the j66g + g8fh regression smoke tests against a 2-file demo repo on 2026-05-28. Adjacent to .27 (fanout) but a different code path — the fanout helper IS running; the index is just empty at query time.
PROMOTED P3→P2 on 2026-05-28 — solov2-izh6.27 discovery confirmed user impact with measurements. PROBE C result on a fresh spf13/cobra clone (36 .go files, 'veska repo add' WITHOUT --wait):
  repo add returns at +15ms
  eng_find_symbol AddCommand:
    +37ms  → {nodes:[], included_staging:false, degraded_reasons:[]}
    +102ms → same
    +166ms → same
    +838ms → HIT, served from promoted store
~800ms silent-empty window on a small public repo; scales linearly with file count. For 10k+ files the window is multi-second. An agent reading [] during the window concludes 'symbol does not exist' and gives up. This makes .30 the canonical fix for the user-visible journey complaint, not just an MCP-side polish. The .27 discovery notes are the source-of-truth measurement record.
close_reason:
Fixed in commit dc6a1da. Added DegradedReasonIndexingInProgress and a ScanTrackerReader port-side interface, then threaded a daemon-wide *application.ScanTracker through five graph-read tools (eng_find_symbol, eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_context_pack, eng_search_semantic) so an empty/sparse response during an active cold scan now carries:
  degraded_reasons=["indexing_in_progress"]
  indexing_repos=[<repo_ids still scanning>]

Wiring: new With*ScanTracker option on each tool's option chain; daemon composition root (internal/cli/daemon/wire.go) passes d.scanTracker to all four Register* surfaces. The check is best-effort and non-blocking — it consults the in-memory tracker's IsAnyScanRunning() / Snapshot() and never stalls the call.

CLI side: `veska symbol` and `veska calls` print a one-line hint when the degraded reason fires, naming the indexing repo_ids and pointing at `veska repo add --wait`.

Verification end-to-end on a fresh spf13/cobra clone:
  +56ms / +179ms / +299ms / +418ms after 'veska repo add' (no --wait):
    {"nodes":[],"degraded_reasons":["indexing_in_progress"],"indexing_repos":["cb186957a822..."]}
  After ~800ms scan completes: AddCommand hit, included_staging=false.

CLI output during the window:
  $ veska symbol AddCommand --repo <cobra>
  no matches
    hint: 1 repo(s) still indexing (cb18...); retry shortly or rerun the relevant `veska repo add --wait`.

Tests: TestFindSymbol_EmptyDuringIndexingEmitsHint + TestFindSymbol_EmptyWithNoScansSuppressesHint + TestFindSymbol_NonEmptyDuringIndexingSuppressesHint pin all three branches. All existing eng_find_symbol / call-chain / help-parity tests pass. Race-clean on internal/infrastructure/mcp, internal/cli/daemon, cmd/veska; vet clean; layercheck OK.

Follow-up beads to file if the visibility hint reveals new asks: blast/contextpack/search currently surface the hint only on sparse (≤1 node, no cross-repo edges) responses; agents may want it on any indexing-window response — file as polish if a junior reports the gap.

title: Cold-scan visibility lag: freshly-committed symbols are silently unqueryable for ~7s after promotion (discovery + decide: surface vs eliminate)
description:
Reframed from 'MCP eng_find_symbol doesn't fan out' (verified non-regression of g8fh — see notes; fanout works) into the underlying lag this exposed.

REPRO (2026-05-28, freshly-rebuilt binary, three trivial repos registered):
  t+0    : 'git commit' adds func UniqueSymbolFroma_g8fh in repo-a
  t+3s   : repo add --wait returns; daemon prints 'cold scan complete: 2 files (0.1s)'
  t+3s   : eng_find_symbol with no repo_id (fan-out) → nodes:[]
  t+~10s : same call → returns the node, included_staging:true

This means a brand-new symbol is silently absent from name-resolution for several seconds AFTER the daemon has already reported 'cold scan complete' and 'repo add --wait' has unblocked. Two distinct user-facing harms:

  1. Agents/junior callers see [] and conclude the symbol doesn't exist (covered narrowly by .30).
  2. 'repo add --wait' lies: --wait implies 'wait until queryable', not 'wait until cold-scan-complete-but-still-indexing'.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DISCOVERY — what's actually happening in the window
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Before deciding 'surface vs fix' we need a one-pass measurement of where the time goes between commit and queryability. Candidates:
  - tree-sitter parse + Promoter transaction commit (should be <100ms for 2 files)
  - FTS index population (PromotionSink — synchronous in promotion txn?)
  - embedding-ref population (PromotionSink — synchronous?)
  - embedder worker draining the embedding backlog (async, model2vec ~ms per node but batched)
  - search-index materialisation if any view-like cache is involved

Acceptance for the discovery half:
  - A trace or log snapshot bracketing one commit → queryable transition, attributing each second to a subsystem (promoter / FTS / embedder / search-index).
  - A one-paragraph decision note in this bead: 'lag is X% in subsystem Y; recommend surface OR fix'.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DECISION POINT — surface vs eliminate
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Path A — SURFACE (cheap, low-risk):
  - 'repo add --wait' blocks until eng_find_symbol on a known seed symbol returns it (not just until cold-scan-complete).
  - Read tools emit degraded_reasons=[indexing_in_progress] with affected repo_ids during the window (.30 is the narrow MCP-side version of this).
  - CLI verbs print a one-line hint.
  - User experience: 'I see the lag but I always have correct info about it.'

Path B — ELIMINATE (better, higher-effort):
  - Make name resolution read directly from the promoter's just-committed transaction (the symbol IS in SQLite already by 'cold scan complete'); eng_find_symbol's miss means it's reading a stale/lagging index, not the source of truth. Investigate which read path the MCP shim takes.
  - If embedding backlog is the long pole, eng_find_symbol (which is name-grain, not vector-grain) shouldn't wait on it at all — verify it isn't accidentally gated.
  - User experience: 'cold scan complete' actually means 'ready'.

Lean toward B if the discovery finds the symbol is in SQLite the whole time and the miss is just a read-path issue — that's a bug, not a UX gap.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ACCEPTANCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1. Discovery output (above) committed to this bead's notes.
  2. Decision Path A or Path B (or hybrid) recorded.
  3. If Path A: .30 stays open, this bead extends it to cover 'repo add --wait' semantics + CLI hints.
  4. If Path B: open a follow-up implementation bead, close .30 as superseded, mark this bead closed once the latency in the repro above drops to <500ms commit-to-queryable.
  5. Either way: add a regression test pinning the new contract (either 'degraded_reasons fires in window' or 'no window exists').

RELATED
  - .30 (silent-empty UX on MCP)
  - g8fh (CLOSED): MCP fanout — verified working in notes below.
  - izh6.16 (CLOSED): ephemeral-clone vuln/secret check — different code path but same 'promotion-says-done-but-state-not-ready' shape.
notes:
DUPE-CHECK 2026-05-28: closed solov2-g8fh (P1, commit c2c6237) claims to have implemented exactly this fanout for both eng_find_symbol and eng_search_semantic when repo_id is omitted AND cwd is outside any registered repo. My repro hits the exact 'cwd outside registered repo' path (ran from /home/jrose/src/engram/solov2) and got nodes:[]. Likely a regression on g8fh. Before claiming, re-run the smoke test in tools_graph_test.go against the current journey fixture and confirm whether the fanout helper is being invoked or short-circuiting earlier.
REGRESSION CHECK 2026-05-28 — g8fh fix is INTACT. Direct repros on freshly-rebuilt binary, three repos registered, MCP shim called from outside any registered repo:
- 'main' (in all 3 repos) → returns 6 nodes with per-hit repo_id ✓
- 'UniqueSymbolFromb_g8fh' (only in repo b) → returns 1 node ✓
- 'UniqueSymbolFroma_g8fh' (only in repo a) → returned [] at t+3s, returned the node at t+~10s ✓

Initial [] in the journey was a cold-scan/promotion lag (the daemon hadn't finished writing the symbol into the search index yet), NOT a fanout regression. included_staging:true is set, so the staging path is engaged. Downgrade to P5 or close.

Adjacent observation worth a separate bead (not this one): the 'eng_find_symbol returned empty mid-promotion' UX is silent — no degraded_reasons hint. A junior who hits this will conclude the symbol doesn't exist rather than 'try again in a moment'. Possible follow-up: emit degraded_reasons=[indexing_in_progress] when there's an open cold-scan task for any repo whose name-space the query touched.
REFRAMED 2026-05-29 from a regression report into a discovery bead. Original framing (eng_find_symbol fanout regressed on g8fh) verified non-regression — the [] was the lag this bead now investigates. Priority kept at P2 because the lag silently corrupts both agent answers and --wait semantics. Sibling .30 is the narrow UX fallback if Path A wins.
DISCOVERY COMPLETE 2026-05-28 — original framing was wrong; closing as superseded by solov2-izh6.30. Measurements:

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
HARNESS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Two probes against a freshly-built daemon (commit 752245b):

PROBE A — post-commit lag on an already-promoted repo (2-file fixture, 5 iters):
  iter  write_to_commit_ms  commit_to_queryable_ms  found_via
  1     43                  26                      staging
  2     71                  15                      staging
  3     53                  18                      staging
  4     59                  21                      staging
  5     45                  16                      staging

PROBE B — zero-warmup probe (commit then immediately query, 12 iters):
  Every iteration: initial_miss=n, found at +0ms, mixed staging/promoted.

PROBE C — cold-scan window on spf13/cobra (36 .go files, no --wait):
  repo add returns at +15ms
  miss #1 at +37ms:  {nodes:[], included_staging:false, degraded_reasons:[]}
  miss #2 at +102ms: same shape
  miss #3 at +166ms: same shape
  HIT for AddCommand at +838ms (~800ms cold-scan window)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDINGS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. The 'freshly-committed symbols are unqueryable for ~7s after promotion' claim does NOT reproduce. The post-commit hook + staging path gives sub-100ms turnaround for every commit. My original journey observation was a measurement artifact (agent-loop wall-clock time between tool calls accumulated to ~7s; the daemon-side commit→queryable was always <100ms). The Promoter's last_promoted_sha write is co-transactional with the node inserts (promotion_store.go:807-820), so when 'repo add --wait' returns 'cold scan complete', the symbols ARE queryable.

2. 'repo add --wait' does NOT lie — it blocks until the repo is queryable. Verified on the 36-file cobra clone: --wait blocks for the full cold-scan duration; queries hit immediately after.

3. THE REAL BUG is the cold-scan-in-progress window when the user calls 'repo add' WITHOUT --wait (which is what the command itself suggests for casual use). During the ~800ms scan, eng_find_symbol returns {nodes:[], degraded_reasons:[]} silently — no 'indexing_in_progress' hint. An agent reads [] and concludes 'symbol does not exist'. For a large repo (10k+ files) the window grows linearly.

4. That bug is exactly what solov2-izh6.30 (silent empty during cold scan) already covers, filed during the verification of g8fh.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DECISION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- Path A (surface via degraded_reasons + actual --wait semantics): the --wait half is already correct; the degraded_reasons half is exactly .30. So Path A reduces to .30.
- Path B (eliminate the lag): there's nothing to eliminate. The lag IS cold-scan time on the registered files — that's irreducible work. Trying to make 'repo add' (no --wait) synchronously hide the scan would just be 'repo add --wait' by default.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ACTIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- Close this bead as superseded by .30 (no fix needed here).
- Promote .30 from P3 to P2 — confirmed user-visible impact + real measurement data.
- .30 should reference Probe C above as the user-facing repro.
close_reason: Discovery complete (see notes). The premise — 'symbols unqueryable for ~7s after promotion' — does NOT reproduce: post-commit→queryable is sub-100ms via staging, and 'repo add --wait' correctly blocks until queryable. The REAL bug is the silent-empty response during the cold-scan-in-progress window when 'repo add' is called WITHOUT --wait, which is exactly solov2-izh6.30. Closing as superseded by .30; .30 promoted to P2 carrying the measurement data this discovery produced.

title: secret_leak: doesn't detect canonical AWS Access Key Example (AKIA-prefix)
description:
Repro: libfoo/greeter.go contains 'const fakeAPIKey = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"' (AWS's own published example access key). After cold-scan + promotion, 'veska findings list --all --include-low --rule secret_leak' returns 0 hits for libfoo. Only the dead-code finding on unusedHelper surfaces.

The current detector is the high-entropy heuristic (see izh6.13 close-reason). That misses well-known fixed-prefix formats. A junior testing the 'leaked secrets' README bullet plants exactly this shape and concludes the feature is broken.

Acceptance:
- AWS Access Key IDs (AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}) flagged as secret_leak with severity high.
- Add canonical regexes for the high-signal formats users will test first: AWS access key, AWS secret access key, GitHub PAT (ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_), Google API key (AIza...), Slack token (xox[bpoa]-), private key headers (-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----). Run BEFORE high-entropy so format wins over entropy and produces a more actionable rule name in the finding.
