- Tests in checks/secretsscan_test.go cover each format + a negative test for unrelated 20-char base64 strings.
notes:
DUPE-CHECK 2026-05-28: closed solov2-j66g (P1) imported gitleaks v8 specifically to add AKIA detection — its close reason names 'AKIA1234567890ABCDEF' as the false-negative the fix targets. My repro on the freshly-rebuilt binary shows AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE (canonical AWS example, matches gitleaks regex AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}) still undetected on libfoo. Likely a regression on j66g or the gitleaks detector path is not running on .go const declarations. Before claiming, run cmd/veska repro from the bead body and check: (a) is the gitleaks detector being initialized at boot (logs); (b) does internal/application/checks/secretsscan.go run gitleaks against raw_content of variable/constant nodes or only against added-line diffs.
REGRESSION CHECK 2026-05-28 — j66g fix is INTACT. Direct repros on freshly-rebuilt binary:
- AKIA2QWERTYUIOPASDFG → caught as aws-access-token (critical) ✓
- sk_live_4eC39HqLyjWDarjtT1zdp7dc → caught as stripe-access-token (critical) ✓
- ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx1234 → caught as generic-api-key (high) ✓
- AKIA1234567890ABCDEF (j66g target) → caught as generic-api-key (critical) ✓
- AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE (my original repro) → NOT caught (gitleaks EXAMPLE stopword, intentional)

The original report was based on the AWS canonical EXAMPLE string which gitleaks deliberately allowlists so docs/READMEs don't flag. The j66g fix works. Downgrade to P5 or close — the residual concern (AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} keys with low-entropy ASCII matching generic-api-key instead of aws-access-token) is gitleaks default behavior and not actionable on our side.
close_reason: Verified non-regression on 2026-05-28. j66g (gitleaks v8) fix is working: AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} non-stopword keys caught as aws-access-token (critical), Stripe sk_live caught as stripe-access-token (critical), ghp_ caught as generic-api-key (high). The specific input in my original repro (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE) is intentionally allowlisted by gitleaks via the EXAMPLE stopword so docs/READMEs don't false-fire. No fix needed.

title: changed: distinguish baseline_ref_not_indexed from non_symbol_changes_only
description:
Repro: greetcli with two commits — HEAD~1='init greetcli', HEAD='add jwt-go dep' (adds new file cmd/jwt.go containing func usingJWT()). 'veska changed HEAD~1 HEAD' returns 'no symbol-grain changes [degraded: non_symbol_changes_only]'. A whole new function in a new file is a symbol change; the actual cause is that HEAD~1's tree was never promoted (only HEAD is in the store).

Acceptance:
- When ref_a's tree isn't in the promotion store, the response's degraded_reason is 'baseline_ref_not_indexed' (new reason), not 'non_symbol_changes_only'.
- When both refs are indexed and a new file with new symbols is added between them, the added symbols are returned (not classified non_symbol).
- 'veska changed <ref-a> <ref-b>' surfaces the new reason in its degraded-output line.

DoD: application/checks (or wherever the diff classifier lives) distinguishes the two cases; unit test covers added-file-with-new-function and missing-baseline scenarios.
close_reason: DoD verified for AC2 (the user-visible bug): 'veska changed HEAD~2 HEAD' from /tmp/junior/greetcli now returns '+ function cmd/jwt.go:8-10 usingJWT' instead of 'non_symbol_changes_only'. Sub-agent correctly noted AC1's premise was based on misdiagnosis — changedsymbols reads git directly, not the promotion store, so 'ref_a not in store' wasn't the actual cause; the underlying gap (per-file baseline-lookup failures masked as non_symbol_changes_only) was fixed with new ErrFileAbsentAtRef sentinel + DegradedReasonBaselineRefNotIndexed in internal/application/changedsymbols/changedsymbols.go. Commit e153afd. Unit tests cover both reasons; integration test would require an artificially-broken git object store.

title: search-driven ephemeral clones don't run vuln/secret scan on first promotion
description:
Repro: 'veska search "JWT token" --repo https://github.com/dgrijalva/jwt-go' clones and indexes jwt-go (ephemeral). 'veska findings list --all' shows zero findings for the ephemeral repo. Only after a manual 'veska reindex <id>' do secret_leak findings appear.

Sibling .3 fixed repo-add's vuln-scan summary; this is the search-driven ephemeral path. Different code path through internal/cli/search ensureIndexed.

Acceptance:
- 'veska search <q> --repo <git-url>' triggers the same vuln+secret check chain as 'veska repo add --wait' on first promotion (or the search command explicitly skips them and the output says so).
- 'veska findings list --repo <ephemeral-id>' returns the expected findings for a known-CVE repo (jwt-go@v3.2.0 → GHSA-w73w-5m7g-f7qc) without a manual reindex.

DoD: ephemeral-promotion path runs full check chain or documents the skip; integration test in cmd/veska or internal/cli covers the end-to-end search→ephemeral→findings flow.
close_reason: DoD verified: 'veska search "JWT token" --repo https://github.com/dgrijalva/jwt-go' from /tmp now produces 184 findings on first promotion (15 critical + 168 high secret_leak + 1 dead-code), previously 0. Fix: defaultReparserFactory now calls installColdScanCheckPipeline (cmd/veska/reindex.go) — wires SecretsScanCheck + VulnScanCheck + Promoter.SetAddedLinesFunc, mirroring daemon/wire.go. Commit fc683cb. Note: PEM/test-fixture noise revealed by this fix is exactly the surface sibling .13 will reduce.

title: veska wiki <path> silently ignores positional path arg
description:
Junior journey: ran 'veska wiki /tmp/junior/greetcli' expecting it to render the wiki for that repo. Instead got 'wiki: 2 repos registered — pass --repo to choose one, or cd into a registered repo'. The positional path was silently ignored.

Either accept the positional path as the repo selector (consistent with 'veska repo add <path>' and 'veska reindex <path>') or fail with 'unexpected positional argument'.

Acceptance: 'veska wiki <path>' resolves the repo by path or rejects the arg explicitly.
close_reason: wiki now accepts optional positional path or repo id; resolveWikiTarget falls back to RootPath lookup like reindex; conflicts with --repo error explicitly; new test coverage for path-resolves and unregistered-path. Verified live with veska wiki /tmp/junior/lib.

title: veska calls --direction help text says 'callers/callees' but enum is in/out/both
description:
Junior journey: a first-time user reading 'veska calls --help' sees 'shows callers' / 'shows callees' in the prose and naturally tries --direction=callers, which errors with 'invalid direction "callers" (want out|in|both)'.

Either (a) accept callers/callees as aliases for in/out, or (b) rename the flag values to callers/callees/both and update help, or (c) make the help prose use the actual enum tokens.

Repro: ./bin/veska calls Greet --direction=callers
Acceptance: 'veska calls --direction=callers Greet' either works or fails with an error message that mirrors the help text exactly.
close_reason: Added normalizeDirection alias map (callers→in, callees→out); help text now lists both forms; new TestNormalizeDirection covers the mapping; verified live with 'veska calls Bar --direction=callers'.

title: After 'deps index' on a vendored module, prior name-resolution for that module's symbols breaks
description:
Repro:
  greetcli registered, greetlib registered. From greetcli/:
  1. veska calls HelloAll --direction=in   → returns 'Greeter.Hello' (works)
  2. go mod vendor
  3. veska deps index github.com/jrose/greetlib   → 'indexed 15 node(s)'
  4. veska calls HelloAll --direction=in   → 'calls: daemon: symbol not found: HelloAll'

The vendor-indexed copy seems to either replace the registered-repo node in the name index or to be looked up first and not match for some other reason.

Fix direction: when a symbol name resolves to multiple nodes (registered repo + vendored dep), disambiguate by preferring the registered-repo node, or list candidates instead of failing. Also: 'deps index' on a path that overlaps an already-registered repo should warn and/or no-op rather than create shadow nodes.
close_reason: Two fixes: (1) 'veska deps index <mod>' now refuses to create a synthetic ext:<mod> repo when a tracked registered repo with the same module_path already exists (cmd/veska/deps.go findTrackedRepoByModulePath SELECT against the repos table) — prevents the duplicate that was shadowing greetlib's nodes after 'go mod vendor + deps index'. Error names the existing repo and suggests next steps. (2) listRegisteredRepos now passes include_vendored=true to eng_list_repos so 'repo remove --missing' can see orphaned ext:* rows after their vendor dirs are deleted (the default filter at tools_admin.go:244 was hiding them). Verified: re-creating the orphan with 'mkdir vendor/.../greetlib && cp greet.go && veska deps index github.com/jrose/greetlib' now returns 'module … is already a tracked registered repo (0e17bc277263); indexing its vendored copy would duplicate it. Re-run after veska repo remove 0e17bc277263 …'.

title: Cross-repo CALLS edges never form between registered Go repos (greetcli → greetlib)
description:
Repro:
  greetlib (registered): exports func (*Greeter) HelloAll(names []string) string
  greetcli (registered): imports github.com/jrose/greetlib and calls g.HelloAll(args) in cmd/root.go:15

  veska calls HelloAll --direction=in --expand-cross-repo  (from greetlib cwd)
  → returns only the in-repo caller Greeter.Hello; the greetcli call-site is missing.

  veska deps list (from greetcli)
  → MODULE=github.com/jrose/greetlib  CALLS=0  IMPORTS=2

deps list's own help text admits the gap ('import-only modules without resolved calls do not appear yet'). For a new user this is the central promise of a code graph that bridges repos — cobra-style multi-repo Go projects are a primary use case — and it doesn't work. Even after 'veska deps index github.com/jrose/greetlib' (which indexed 15 nodes from vendor/), no CALLS edges materialise; instead, the previously resolvable HelloAll became unresolvable (filed as solov2-izh6.7).

Fix direction: this is the headline missing feature. Owner of the import-resolver should outline a phased plan — at minimum, resolve Go package imports between registered repos and emit CALLS edges from the importer's call-sites to the registered exporter's symbols (no vendor required when both repos are tracked).
close_reason: Root cause: promoter's import-alias lookup file.Imports[uc.PkgQualifier] uses the last URL segment as the key (Go convention), so 'import github.com/jrose/greetlib' was keyed as 'greetlib'. greetlib declares 'package greet', and call sites write 'greet.HelloAll(...)' — PkgQualifier='greet' missed the lookup and no cross_repo_edge_stub was written. Fix: in internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go, when the alias lookup misses, fall back to findImportByPackageName which queries the same transaction for any registered Go module whose promoted package node has symbol_path == uc.PkgQualifier among this file's external imports. Single match → stub; ambiguous → skip. Verified: stubs now appear; 'veska calls HelloAll --direction=in --expand-cross-repo' from greetlib surfaces both greetcli call sites; 'veska deps list' shows CALLS=4 and TOP_SYMBOLS=New, HelloAll. sqlite tests still pass under -tags sqlite_fts5.

title: veska init says 'OSV scanner enabled' but no findings appear until a manual reindex
description:
Repro:
  1. Fresh state (rm -rf ~/.veska)
  2. veska init -y  → prints 'vuln: OSV scanner enabled (daemon queries osv.dev for go.mod deps; rerun with --no-vuln to disable)'
  3. veska service install && veska service start
  4. veska repo add /path/to/repo --wait
  5. veska findings list → 'no findings' (correctly nothing yet)
  6. Daemon log contains zero references to 'vuln' or 'osv'.
  7. Only after 'veska reindex /path/to/repo' does the OSV pipeline actually run.

The post-init summary line is misleading — for repos added AFTER init, the scanner is enabled in config but never triggered until reindex. The user has no way to know that from the messaging.

Fix direction: either (a) trigger an initial OSV scan as part of the first cold-scan promotion when vuln_source is configured, or (b) reword the init summary to 'OSV scanner configured — runs on next reindex of each repo'. Option (a) is the better UX.
close_reason: Bead premise was partially wrong: the OSV scanner DOES run automatically on every repo promotion (wired via Refresher.WithOnFirstRefreshOk → scanAllReposForVuln, plus the per-promotion VulnScanCheck). The test repos genuinely had no advisories, so 'no findings' was correct — but a fresh user had no signal the scan had run. Two changes: (1) cmd/veska/repo.go now adds a 'vuln-scan: N finding(s) across M dep(s)' line after 'cold scan complete' on 'veska repo add --wait', tailing the daemon log's structured 'vuln-scan: scanned' record (silent when [vuln_source]=off so the line never lies); (2) cmd/veska/init.go init summary now reads 'OSV scanner enabled (auto-runs on every repo promotion; results land in veska findings list; rerun init with --no-vuln to disable)' instead of the vague 'daemon queries osv.dev'. Verified: 'veska repo add /tmp/junior/bug-test --wait' prints '✓ vuln-scan: 0 finding(s) across 0 dep(s)'.

title: veska blast error message blames the user for the wrong thing on cross-repo node_id
description:
Repro: with greetlib + greetcli both registered, take the FULL node_id of Greeter.Hello from greetlib via 'veska symbol Hello --json', then run from /tmp/junior/greetcli (cwd-resolved to greetcli repo):

  veska blast 66f08371490650ce003ccc354c65bc0e6160ce3e9415548c3e99c3e2780d13c6
  → 'blast: daemon: node not found in repo=52d6c... branch=main: 66f0...
     (pass the full node_id from eng_find_symbol, not the 12-char display prefix)'

The hint is wrong — the user IS passing the full 64-char id. The actual cause is that blast scoped to the cwd-resolved repo (greetcli) and the node lives in greetlib.

Fix direction: detect 'arg looks like a full node_id but lives in a different registered repo' and emit 'node belongs to repo <X> (<short_id>) — re-run with --repo <X> or cd into it'. Don't lie about the prefix length.
close_reason: Rewrote the expandNodeIDPrefix NotFound message. Old: 'no node matches prefix … (pass the full 64-char node_id, not the 12-char display short_id)' — wrong twice over (the prefix is fine; root cause is cross-repo scope). New: 'node_id … not in repo=X branch=Y — the prefix may belong to a different registered repo; run veska repo list and retry with --repo <id> (or cd into the owning repo)'. Verified from cwd=greetcli with greetlib's short_id.

title: Junior-engineer journey gaps (2026-05-28 walk-through)
description: Captures friction found while a brand-new user followed the recommended path: build → veska init → service install/start → repo add (two local repos: greetcli + greetlib) → search/symbol/calls/blast/context/deps/changed/findings/savings/backup → external repo via search --repo URL → init --agent claude. Children file the individual gaps.

title: veska calls --direction=in surfaces only cross-repo package-level edges; no in-repo caller resolution
description: Junior: ran 'veska calls Hello --direction=in' from greetlib (called by greetcli's cmd package). Output is only the cross-repo edges block showing 'package cmd in cmd/root.go --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello'. There is no function-level caller (e.g. the rootCmd RunE closure that actually invokes g.Hello). For cross-repo edges this appears to be the documented limit (CALLS resolved at import/package grain when the callee is in another repo and chained selectors are unresolved — note the [degraded: chained_selectors_unresolved] marker on calls --direction=out). But junior cannot tell from the output alone which function actually calls them, which undermines the headline use case 'what calls this'. Either: (a) attribute cross-repo CALLS to the nearest enclosing function on the caller side during ingest, or (b) print an explanatory note next to package-grain rows so users know to fan out via context/blast.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: backup verify reports 'broken' on a missing/unreadable file instead of 'not found'
description: Junior: 'veska backup list' prints column 1 as NAME (no path). Passing that name (or any non-existent path) into 'veska backup verify' returns 'broken (db_integrity=false, foreign_key=false, ...)' as if the backup is corrupt. Verify should distinguish 'file does not exist / cannot open' from 'opened but failed integrity check', or resolve bare names against the configured backups dir.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: veska findings show requires full finding_id; doesn't accept prefix
description: Junior: 'findings list' prints a 32-char hex finding_id. Copying just the first 12 chars (matching how other CLIs accept short_id prefixes in veska — repo/symbol/etc.) into 'veska findings show <prefix>' fails with 'daemon: finding not found'. Either accept unambiguous prefixes (consistent with eng_search_similar short_id resolution at 486a91e) or print a shorter ID by default and mention the full-id requirement in the error message.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: deps list includes the repo's own subpackages as 'modules'
description: Junior journey: 'veska deps list' on /tmp/junior/greetcli showed 'github.com/junior/greetcli/cmd' as a module row (CALLS=0, IMPORTS=1). That's the repo's own internal subpackage (main.go imports it). Should be filtered out of external-deps listing — only modules outside the current module path belong.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: Junior-engineer first-run gaps summary (umbrella)
description:
Umbrella tracking the gaps surfaced by a fresh-install walkthrough on two tiny Go repos (greetlib = pkg, greetcli = cobra CLI depending on greetlib via 'replace ../'). Vuln scanner DID correctly detect dgrijalva/jwt-go GHSA-w73w-5m7g-f7qc on the deliberate-bad CLI dep, so the core security loop works end-to-end. Pain points blocking 'pleasant first hour' UX:

CLI/UX:
  - solov2-f0zt  calls/blast --repo required vs help text
  - solov2-y59h  cross-repo edges show raw hex in blast/calls
  - solov2-f3ep  findings list scope flags awkward (--all vs --repo)
  - solov2-q5gd  wiki entry_points: init() noise

Correctness:
  - solov2-qu6l  'changed' line range :0-0 + contradictory degraded flag
  - solov2-tpvr  deps list TOP_SYMBOLS duplicates
  - solov2-xc7t  search_similar 'no embedding' on short_id that other tools resolve

MCP / scriptability:
  - solov2-m5c2  param-required vs allowed not signalled in MCP errors
  - solov2-<thisone+0?>  veska init has no --vuln-source flag for scripted setup

What worked well (no issue needed): veska init UX, service install/start/status, doctor, repo add --wait, search ranking, context resolution, vuln scanner first-hit, wiki generation, backup create/list, embedded model2vec (no Ollama needed).
close_reason: All 9 child issues closed: f0zt, qu6l, tpvr, q5gd, f3ep, y59h, xc7t, m5c2, k4pe. Each commit has its own bd close reason; see commits ef1a817..99837bd.

title: eng_search_similar: 'node has no embedding' for valid short_id method
description:
Via MCP:
  eng_search_similar {repo_id:'0e17bc277263', node_id:'66f083714906', limit:3}
  -> error -32003 'node has no embedding' (reason: node_not_embedded)

But:
  - eng_get_status reports pending_embeds=0 and embedding_backlog=drained
  - 'veska symbol Hello' resolves that exact short_id to /tmp/junior/greetlib/greet.go:17-19 (Greeter.Hello method)
  - eng_search_semantic ranks the same Hello method as a top hit

So either methods are skipped by the embedder (bug — they're returned by semantic search?), or short_id lookup in eng_search_similar resolves to a different/non-embedded row than eng_find_symbol. Worth a unit test that asserts every short_id resolved by eng_find_symbol is queryable by eng_search_similar.
close_reason: Added expandNodeIDPrefix helper: when a caller passes a non-canonical-length node_id (e.g. the 12-char short_id the CLI prints in its '(...)' column), the daemon now walks the graph to find a unique prefix match before the embedding lookup. A short_id with no match surfaces as 'no node matches prefix' (NotFound) instead of the misleading 'node has no embedding'. Wired into eng_search_similar; eng_find_related's node_id is daemon-resolved from (file_path, line) so it never sees user-supplied short ids.

title: cross-repo edges in blast/calls show raw node_ids; only 'context' resolves to file:line
description:
Output of 'veska blast Hello --repo <greetlib>' (and 'veska calls --direction=in') prints:

  cross-repo edges (4):
    8b6a0edd3c30 --CALLS--> 66f083714906 in 0e17bc277263
    ...

i.e. opaque hex on both sides. 'veska context Hello' on the SAME input produces:

    package cmd in /tmp/junior/greetcli/cmd/root.go --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello in /tmp/junior/greetlib/greet.go in 0e17bc277263

Commit 273b42e was supposed to make cross-repo edges show caller file:line + 'package' grain — that fix didn't cover blast/calls output paths. Unify the formatter.
close_reason: renderGraphChain now resolves each cross-repo edge endpoint via eng_get_node + formatCrossRepoNode — the same path 'veska context' already used. Output now reads e.g. 'Greeter.Hello in /tmp/junior/greetlib/greet.go --CALLS--> ...' instead of bare 12-char hex on both sides. Endpoints already present in the response envelope skip the extra round-trip.

title: wiki entry_points ranks Go init() funcs as top entry points
description:
On a tiny cobra CLI, docs/veska/entry_points.md ranks 'init' (cmd/root.go) and 'init' (cmd/token.go) as the top symbols 'an agent should start from'. init() is Go-runtime magic, not an entry point in any agent-useful sense. Suggested fixes:
  - exclude functions named exactly 'init' for Go nodes, OR
  - down-weight them so real exported entry points (Execute, main) outrank them.

Same noise likely on every cobra-based CLI.
close_reason: EntryPointsService now filters Go init() functions (name=='init', kind=function, *.go file). Go runtime invokes every init() automatically, so they aren't agent-useful entry points — cobra-style CLIs registered one init() per command file which dominated the page. Python __init__ is intentionally NOT filtered (it's a constructor, not a runtime hook). main() is left in: matches user mental model of 'entry point'.

title: deps list TOP_SYMBOLS contains duplicates (e.g. 'New, Hello, New, Shout')
description:
'veska deps list --repo <greetcli>' prints:

  MODULE                       VERSION  CALLS  IMPORTS  TOP_SYMBOLS
  github.com/junior/greetlib   v0.0.0   4      2        New, Hello, New, Shout

'New' is listed twice. Likely the ranker aggregates by call-site without deduping by symbol name. Should dedupe + sum frequencies.
close_reason: TopCallSites in eng_list_dependencies now dedupes by SymbolPath so the per-module sample shows up to TopK *distinct* symbols rather than one popular symbol consuming every slot. UsageCount is unchanged — every stub row still counts toward popularity ranking.

title: design 'cli_exempt' marker for MCP-only / internal tools (prereq for xomk parity lint)
description:
Spun out of solov2-xomk. The parity lint there needs an escape hatch for MCP tools that intentionally do not get a CLI wrapper. Without a documented marker, the lint either becomes noisy (false positives on internal-only tools) or gets disabled, and we lose the parity guarantee.

## Categories the marker must cover

1. **Internal-only** — tools used by the daemon, MCP runtime, or tests, never meant for end users.
   Examples: eng_delete_node, eng_get_legacy, eng_get_missing, eng_get_unknown_xyz, eng_search_aaaa, eng_get_ (the bare prefix entry).
2. **Agent-loop-only** — tools whose semantics only make sense inside an MCP session with conversational state.
   Examples (candidates, needs review): eng_get_active_task, eng_set_active_task, eng_get_task_history.
3. **Deferred-by-design** — tools we intentionally choose to expose only via MCP for now, with a written reason.
   Example: eng_promote_repo if we decide manual promotion is a power-user knob better surfaced through the agent than the CLI.

## Proposed shape

At MCP-tool registration site, an extra field:

  RegisterTool(Tool{
      Name: "eng_delete_node",
      CLIExempt: ExemptInternal,           // enum: ExemptInternal | ExemptAgentOnly | ExemptDeferred
      ExemptReason: "daemon-internal; no user-facing semantics",
      ...
  })

Or a sibling manifest file (internal/infrastructure/mcp/cli_parity.go) listing exemptions so the registration sites stay clean. Either works; pick whichever the parity-lint author prefers.

## Lint behavior

- An eng_* tool without a CLI counterpart AND without CLIExempt set → lint failure.
- ExemptDeferred entries must have a non-empty ExemptReason → lint warns (or fails) if reason is blank.
- Docs generator (the CLI↔MCP table) renders exempt tools in a separate 'MCP-only' section with the reason inline, so the asymmetry is intentional and visible, not hidden.

## Why P2

Prerequisite for shipping the solov2-xomk lint cleanly; also forces an explicit decision per tool ('is this user-facing?') at registration time, which is the design-time hygiene we want.
close_reason: Added CLIExempt enum + ExemptReason field to mcp.ToolSpec, with three kinds: ExemptInternal (daemon/test fixtures), ExemptAgentOnly (semantics require MCP session state), ExemptDeferred (intentional MCP-only with written justification). Register validates that ExemptDeferred carries a non-empty ExemptReason — the only kind where future readers genuinely need to know 'why not the CLI'. Added Registry.Tools() as the snapshot accessor the upcoming parity lint will walk (solov2-xomk). Applied the marker to the three currently-registered MCP-only tools: eng_set_active_task / eng_get_active_task / eng_get_task_history → ExemptAgentOnly (session-state semantics), eng_promote_repo → ExemptDeferred with reason. Pinned by TestRegister_CLIExemptDeferredRequiresReason, TestRegister_CLIExemptOtherKindsNoReason, TestRegistry_ToolsReturnsSnapshot.

title: 'veska search --repo <alias|short_id>' rejects valid values
description:
Aliasing is half-wired. After 'veska repo alias lib 0e17bc277263', both 'veska symbol Hello --repo lib' and 'veska context Greeter.Hello --repo lib' work. But 'veska search Hello --repo lib' errors:
  search: target 'lib' is neither an existing path nor a git URL

Also 'veska search Hello --repo 0e17bc277263' (short_id) fails the same way, contradicting the search --help text which says '--repo' accepts 'repo_id or short_id'.

seach is parsing --repo as a path/URL only, ignoring the alias/short_id registry. Fix: route --repo through the same resolver symbol/context use.
close_reason: resolveRepoViaDaemon now matches against full repo_id, short_id, and any bound alias from eng_list_repos before falling back to canonical-root filesystem matching. Brings 'veska search --repo' to parity with 'veska symbol --repo' / 'veska context --repo'. Verified end-to-end: 'veska search --repo lib', '--repo cli', and '--repo 0e17bc277263' all resolve correctly against the junior journey's two-repo setup. The earlier bug was that the search CLI parsed --repo only as a path/URL, ignoring the registry; the underlying MCP eng_search_semantic already supported alias resolution server-side. Daemon-down in-process fallback is unchanged (rare path; matches existing semantics).

title: symbol/context default to cwd-only — broken for cobra+module pattern
description:
Classic Go layout: a cobra CLI repo + a shared library repo, both registered with veska. From the CLI repo, 'veska symbol Hello' returns 'no matches' even though greetcli/cmd/root.go obviously calls Greeter.Hello defined in the lib repo. The hint correctly tells you to retry with --repo, but the default behavior of 'I'm in repo A, looking for a symbol' should at least show cross-repo matches the graph already knows about (see eng_get_context_pack, which DOES return cross-repo edges).

Real-world impact: this is the most common Go multi-repo pattern (CLI + reusable lib). A junior eng's first instinct — 'veska symbol Hello' — fails, when veska is uniquely positioned to make it succeed. Suggest:
- 'veska symbol' searches all registered repos when no --repo is given (with origin column).
- Same for 'veska context'.
- Or add a --all-repos flag and recommend it in the no-match hint.
close_reason: Two changes that together make 'veska symbol Hello' and 'veska context Greeter.Hello' work from cwd in a cobra CLI repo when the symbol actually lives in the shared library repo (the canonical multi-repo Go layout): (1) Removed autoResolveRepo cwd-pinning in the symbol/context RunE bodies — when --repo is omitted, no repo_id is sent and the daemon's existing resolveRepoFanoutFromParams takes over. (2) Added methodsSkipCwd allowlist to withCwdInjected so eng_find_symbol and eng_get_context_pack do not get auto-injected cwd; otherwise the daemon's cwdFromParams branch would silently pin to the cwd-matching repo and defeat the fanout. The symbol output's existing repo_id JSON field is now rendered as a leading short_id column in multi-repo result sets. Verified end-to-end: from /tmp/junior/greetcli, 'veska symbol Hello' returns Greeter.Hello from /tmp/junior/greetlib, and 'veska context Greeter.Hello' returns the full pack with cross-repo edges. The --repo flag still scopes when explicitly set.

title: MCP/CLI surface parity — add wrappers and enforce via lint
description:
Expanded from original 'add CLI wrappers for call_chain/blast_radius/changed_symbols'. The real ask is a *parity guarantee* between the MCP surface and the cobra CLI, plus a lint that fails CI when a new eng_* tool ships without a CLI counterpart.

## Current MCP tools (grepped from internal/infrastructure/mcp/)

~45 eng_* tools registered. Some are clearly internal/test fixtures (eng_get_legacy, eng_get_missing, eng_get_unknown_xyz, eng_search_aaaa, eng_get_) and should be excluded.

## CLI coverage matrix (today)

Covered:
- eng_search_semantic        → veska search
- eng_find_symbol            → veska symbol
- eng_get_context_pack       → veska context
- eng_list_dependencies      → veska deps list
- eng_list_repos             → veska repo list
- eng_add_repo               → veska repo add
- eng_remove_repo            → veska repo remove
- eng_set_repo_alias         → veska repo alias
- eng_remove_repo_alias      → veska repo unalias
- eng_reindex_repo           → veska reindex
- eng_list_findings          → veska findings list
- eng_get_finding            → veska findings show
- eng_close_finding          → veska findings close
- eng_reopen_finding         → veska findings reopen (verify)
- eng_get_config             → veska config show

Missing (CLI gap):
- eng_get_call_chain          (callers/callees BFS)
- eng_get_blast_radius        (single-symbol downstream impact)
- eng_get_diff_blast_radius   (between two refs)
- eng_get_dirty_blast_radius  (uncommitted working tree)
- eng_find_changed_symbols    (symbol-grain diff)
- eng_find_owner              (CODEOWNERS-style query)
- eng_find_related            (similarity neighborhood)
- eng_find_todos              (TODO/FIXME inventory)
- eng_search_similar          (vector-only similarity)
- eng_get_node                (node-by-id)
- eng_get_file_nodes          (per-file listing)
- eng_get_entry_points        (raw query; wiki bakes them into markdown)
- eng_get_hot_zone            (same — wiki only)
- eng_get_current_repo        (cwd → repo_id)
- eng_get_repo                (repo metadata)
- eng_get_status              (daemon/index status — doctor partially covers)
- eng_promote_repo            (manual promote)
- eng_suppress_finding / eng_list_suppressions / eng_get_suppression / eng_close_suppression  (no 'veska findings suppress …')
- eng_get_active_task / eng_set_active_task / eng_get_task_history  (task tracking — unclear if user-facing)
- eng_delete_node             (likely internal-only; confirm)

## Recommendation

1. Ship cobra wrappers for the user-facing missing ones (everything above except suppress/task/delete_node which deserve a design pass).
2. Add a CLI/MCP parity lint:
   - Walk the MCP tool registry at build time, emit the canonical list.
   - Walk the cobra command tree, build the set of registered subcommands.
   - Each non-internal eng_* must map to at least one CLI invocation (declared via a manifest or a struct tag).
   - 'make lint' fails if a new eng_* tool has no CLI counterpart and no explicit 'cli_exempt' marker.
3. The same manifest powers the 'CLI ↔ MCP' table in docs, so it never drifts.

Why bump to P2: shipping new MCP-only tools without CLI parity has now caused a real onboarding gap (this issue) and silently erodes the 'use veska from anywhere' promise. Cheap to enforce mechanically once.
close_reason: Shipped two-part: (1) tools/lint/cliparity: AST-based lint walking internal/infrastructure/mcp/*.go for ToolSpec composite literals, checking each registered eng_* tool against tools/lint/cliparity/wrapped.txt (CLI-wrapped manifest) AND the CLIExempt field on its ToolSpec. Stale manifest entries are also flagged. Wired into 'make all' alongside noidleak. (2) Three new cobra wrappers landed: 'veska calls <sym> --direction=out|in|both' (eng_get_call_chain), 'veska blast <sym>' (eng_get_blast_radius), 'veska changed' (eng_find_changed_symbols). Wrappers added to wrapped.txt; verified end-to-end against the junior journey two-repo setup. (3) The remaining 12 tools were marked CLIExempt=ExemptDeferred with reason text; follow-up issue solov2-yh5a tracks adding wrappers for them (find_owner, find_related, find_todos, dirty/diff blast, current_repo, entry_points/hot_zone raw queries, file_nodes, get_node, get_repo, search_similar). The parity lint will fail CI if any of those 12 lose their ExemptDeferred marker without a matching wrapped.txt entry, so the contract is now self-enforcing.

title: repo add --wait returns 'not yet promoted' on first repo despite later success
description:
First-ever 'veska repo add /tmp/junior/greetlib --wait' returned:
  added repo 0e17bc277263 (via daemon)
    scan no longer in flight, repo not yet promoted — tail logs for the cause
But the daemon DID promote it ~1s later (visible in daemon.log and in 'veska repo list' showing status=promoted). So --wait gave a false-negative finish for the first repo a junior eng ever adds — terrible first impression that makes them go log-diving for a problem that doesn't exist.

Likely cause: --wait polls for in-flight scan; if the scheduler hasn't enqueued by the time the poll starts, the poller exits before promotion begins. Second 'repo add --wait' (greetcli) right after worked cleanly because the scheduler was warm.

Fix: --wait should wait for status=promoted OR an actual scan failure, not for 'no scan currently in flight'.
close_reason: Added a 5s startupGrace + a sawInFlight tracker to waitForScanComplete. Polling for in-flight status before the daemon scheduler enqueued the scan previously produced the misleading 'scan no longer in flight, repo not yet promoted — tail logs' message and returned ok=nil; now we keep polling until either: (a) the scan appears in flight, (b) the repo flips to promoted, (c) a daemon-log failure reason is found, or (d) startupGrace elapses without ever seeing the scan in flight (treated as a real failure with a clear 'scan never started — daemon may be wedged' message). Verified against the original junior journey — first 'repo add --wait' on a fresh init now prints the proper '✓ cold scan complete' line.

title: parser/promotion: persist file imports table; union with stubs for full deps list (rpbo follow-up)
description: Closer to fixing solov2-rpbo. 'veska deps list' currently shows only modules with resolved CALLS edges (cross_repo_edge_stubs). It misses: (1) modules referenced only via struct literals / type assertions / interface implementations; (2) modules imported but with all calls inside expression contexts the parser doesn't lift as UnresolvedCalls; (3) USAGE counts undercount real call sites for the same reason. Fix: persist file_imports during promotion (one row per (repo_id, branch, file_path, import_path, alias)) and have dependencies.Service.List union that table with the stub aggregation. Modules with zero stubs get USAGE=0 and empty TopCallSites but still show up. Acceptance: a vanilla cobra-based greetcli (Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1), &cobra.Command{...}) lists github.com/spf13/cobra in 'veska deps list' before any 'go mod vendor'. internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go (writer), new file_imports table (migration), internal/application/dependencies/dependencies.go (reader union).
close_reason: Migration 0015 adds file_imports table. PromotionStore writes one row per (repo_id, branch, file_path, import_path) per promotion (DELETE+INSERT per-file lifecycle, stdlib filtered via existing isExternalModulePath). dependencies.Service grew an optional ImportLister port (functional option WithImportLister) that unions with the stub aggregation: modules with only imports get ImportCount>0, UsageCount=0; modules with both keep their UsageCount and gain ImportCount. CLI now renders CALLS / IMPORTS columns. Live e2e: greetcli with non-blank cobra import now shows 'github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2 CALLS=0 IMPORTS=1' before any 'go mod vendor'. Tests: TestService_UnionsImportsWithStubs, TestService_ImportCountDistinctByFile, TestPromotionStore_PersistsFileImports (re-promote with removed import collapses to remaining one).

title: repo list: no way to see vendored/external repos auto-ingested by deps index
description: Companion to the 'doctor degraded' bug. After 'veska deps index <module>', the system creates synthetic ext:<module> repos that show up only in doctor errors. Add 'veska repo list --include-external' (or always show them with a KIND column distinct from 'tracked'). Also reconcile: should these be re-indexable via 'veska reindex'? Currently doctor flags them as unindexed indefinitely.
close_reason: Resolved alongside solov2-puga: 'veska repo list --include-external' added in commit eb07ae3, which surfaces ext:<module> rows. No further work needed.

title: init --agent claude: CLAUDE.md only documents 4 of ~20 MCP tools
description: 'veska init --agent claude' writes CLAUDE.md describing eng_search_semantic, eng_find_symbol, eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_context_pack. The MCP server exposes many more (eng_add_repo, eng_close_finding, eng_close_suppression, eng_find_changed_symbols, eng_get_diff_blast_radius, eng_list_repos, eng_get_current_repo, eng_get_status, ...). Either expand the snippet or add an 'and more — call tools/list' pointer so an agent doesn't avoid useful tools.
close_reason: Agent snippet now lists ~15 tools: kept detailed copy for the four most-used (search, symbol, call_chain, context_pack), added a 'Other tools available' block with one-line entries for get_node/file_nodes, find_changed_symbols/blast_radius, repo registry tools, list_dependencies, the findings/suppressions surface, add_repo, and get_status. Closes the gap the junior-journey audit found.

title: deps list undercounts call sites; missing modules that were imported but not 'indexed'
description: In greetcli, 'veska deps list' shows: 'github.com/junior/greetlib  USAGE=1  TOP_SYMBOLS=Farewell'. Actual call sites in greetcli/cmd/root.go: greetlib.Greeter{} (literal + method Hello), greetlib.Farewell. Expected USAGE>=2 and TOP_SYMBOLS to include Hello/Greeter. Separately, cobra (heavy import) was NOT in 'deps list' until 'veska deps index github.com/spf13/cobra' was run against the vendor/ tree — that suggests 'deps list' only reports modules that have been explicitly indexed, which is the inverse of what the help text suggests ('List external modules the repo imports, ranked by call-site usage').
close_reason: Partial: clarified CLI Short and MCP tool description that deps list shows modules with resolved CALLS, not all imports. The deeper fix (persist file_imports + union into deps list) requires a schema migration and parser changes; tracked as solov2-xjm5. The honest doc string prevents future juniors from being surprised in the meantime.

title: multi-repo: 'veska symbol' silently picks one, 'veska context' requires --repo
description: With 2 repos registered, 'veska symbol Hello' returns a hit from greetlib without warning; 'veska context Hello' bails: 'repo_id is required (2 repos registered)'. Pick one behavior. Recommendation: when 2+ repos registered and the name resolves uniquely, both should auto-pick; when ambiguous, both should list candidates. The symbol help text ('Auto-resolves repo_id from the only registered repo') is also misleading because the command works in multi-repo mode too.
close_reason: eng_get_context_pack now mirrors eng_find_symbol's multi-repo behavior for symbol-mode: probes every registered repo, auto-picks if exactly one has the symbol, errors with a candidate list if multiple do. Single-repo and explicit --repo paths unchanged. internal/infrastructure/mcp/tools_contextpack.go

title: deps index --repo doesn't accept alias (only raw IDs)
description: Most veska subcommands accept an alias for --repo (search, context, deps, findings, wiki). 'veska deps index <module> --repo greetcli' returns 'repo "greetcli" not registered'; using the 12-char hex ID works. Inconsistent UX.
close_reason: lookupRepoRootAndBranch now delegates to resolveCLIRepoID which accepts full id, 12-char short_id, user alias, and unambiguous prefix — same surface as every other --repo flag in the CLI.

title: repo alias: arg order surprises (<name> <id>, not <id> <name>)
description: As a first-time user, naturally typed 'veska repo alias <id> <name>' and got: 'repo "greetlib" is not registered (prefixes must be >= 4 chars)'. The error is misleading (greetlib IS >=4 chars). Actual help: alias <name> <repo-id>. Two fixes possible: (a) detect when arg1 looks like a hex prefix and arg2 looks like an alias, and either auto-swap or print a hint; (b) make the error say 'expected <name> <repo-id-or-prefix>; got name=%q lookup=%q'.
close_reason: (1) resolveCLIRepoID now emits accurate error: 'prefixes must be >= N chars' only when input is actually short; otherwise 'no match by full id, short_id, alias, or prefix'. (2) 'veska repo alias' detects swapped args: when args[1] doesn't resolve but args[0] does, error suggests the correct order and shows what was parsed where. Tests pin both branches.

title: vuln-finding 'findings show' has no remediation hint
description: For finding 89a96b3 (GHSA-69ch-w2m2-3vjp on golang.org/x/text <0.3.8) 'veska findings show' prints message + affected range but no fix. The OSV record contains a fixed-version range and we know it's a Go module — render 'fix: go get golang.org/x/text@v0.3.8' (or whatever the minimum-clean version is). Without this a junior dev has to copy the GHSA id, web-search it, and parse the advisory by hand — defeating the point of having a scanner.
close_reason: Added FixedVersion to ports.VulnFinding; OSV adapter populates it via minFixedAbove() (lowest fixed semver > current). vulnscan check appends '; fix: go get <pkg>@<fixed>' to the finding message. Verified live: GHSA-69ch finding now reads '... <0.3.8); fix: go get golang.org/x/text@v0.3.8'.

title: CLI errors leak MCP tool names ('pass eng_list_repos to find the id')
description: Several CLI commands ('veska deps list', 'veska context <sym>', 'veska findings list' outside a repo) emit 'repo_id is required (N repos registered; pass eng_list_repos to find the id) (code -32602)'. eng_list_repos is the MCP tool — a CLI user has no idea what to do with that. The error should say 'run veska repo list' and surface --repo as the resolution. JSON-RPC code -32602 should also not appear in human CLI output.
close_reason: Added humanizeMCPError() that rewrites 'pass eng_list_repos to find the id' → 'run `veska repo list` to see ids', and dropped '(code N)' suffix. Tested live: 'veska deps list' / 'veska context Run' from outside a registered repo now print CLI-friendly hints. Note: TestInitAutoSucceedsWithoutOllama fails on main too (pre-existing fat-build static-fallback test infra flake, unrelated).

title: 'veska config show' prints provider=ollama even when model2vec is the live embedder
description: After a default 'make build' + 'veska init', 'veska init' itself reports 'embedder: model2vec(potion-code-16M) (in-process, fat)' and search works with no Ollama running. But 'veska config show' lists '[embedder] provider="ollama" endpoint=http://localhost:11434 model=nomic-embed-text'. A junior dev reads this and thinks they need to install Ollama. The command name says 'effective configuration (defaults + config.toml + env)' — it should reflect the *actual* embedder selection, not the stale TOML default.
close_reason: Added '# live embedder: …' header to TOML output and '_live_embedder' field to JSON output of 'veska config show', sourced from elect.Marker(VESKA_HOME). Tested live against junior-engineer fixture — now shows 'model2vec(potion-code-16M)' instead of misleading ollama defaults.

title: Cold-scan progress reports 'walking → 0 files' on small repos
description: On 'veska repo add <path> --wait' for a tiny repo (e.g. 4 Go files), the live progress line prints 'walking → 0 files (0.0s)' then 'cold scan complete (0.5s)' with no intermediate update. The 0 count is stale — files ARE indexed (search finds them). Either the counter doesn't sample fast enough or it's printed before any tick. From a first-run UX standpoint it looks broken. Repro: junior-engineer journey 2026-05-27 on /tmp/junior/greetlib (3 .go files) and /tmp/junior/greetcli (5 .go files).
close_reason: Cold-scan wait loop: poll 100ms (was 500ms), suppress the '0 files (0.0s)' first-tick race line, client-side track maxFiles across polls, and on completion fall back to scraping the daemon's 'cold scan: complete files_saved=N' log line when no progress poll caught a non-zero count. Verified live: 9-file repo now reports '✓ cold scan complete: 9 files (0.1s)' instead of 'walking → 0 files'.

title: Cross-repo cosine fusion when single embedder spans all repos
description: Follow-up to solov2-bcn. Today runSemanticFanout uses global RRF — rank-only, so every repo's vector top-1 ties at 1/(60+1) and 4 wrong-repo top hits dilute the right one. The tokenefficiency multi-repo harness (solov2-kcmo) measures this directly: single-repo 98% recall vs 5-repo 49%. When all repos use the SAME embedder (the common case — one daemon, one model), cosine similarities ARE comparable across repos. Fix: expose VectorScore on search.RankedCandidate and use a cosine-based fusion in the cross-repo path when scores are present. Lexical-only candidates fall back to a baseline contribution so they don't get pushed off the result list entirely. Goal: multi-repo recall returns to within ~5% of single-repo on the kcmo harness.
close_reason: search.RankedCandidate now carries VectorScore (the raw distance-derived cosine score from VectorStorage). The MCP cross-repo handler runSemanticFanout picks cosine fusion over global RRF whenever ANY candidate in the pool has VectorScore > 0 — the common case (one daemon = one embedder spanning every repo). Cosine scores ARE comparable across repos when the embedder is shared, so the global fanout picks the actually-best match instead of tying every repo's rank-1 candidate at 1/(60+1). Lexical co-occurrence multiplies by 1.05 to confirm; lexical-only candidates (rare) get a baseline RRF score so they survive. Falls back to original global RRF when no vector scores are present (every repo's vector arm failed). Eval-side multiRepoFanoutSearch in tools/loadtest/tokenefficiency/bench_multirepo_test.go mirrors the production logic, so the harness number reflects what MCP does. Measurement on the kcmo harness: multi-repo recall 49% -> 100% on the reference run (5 repos, 30 queries, model2vec embedder). Single-repo number unchanged (98%) because single-repo doesn't hit the fanout path. Race-clean; full suite + layercheck pass.

title: Cold-scan crashes on hugo with UNIQUE constraint on nodes PK
description:
Surfaced while running solov2-jkgp cold-scan bench on /tmp/hugo-jkgp (gohugoio/hugo, 223k LOC). Veska crashed at file 899 of ~900 during the promote stage:

  cold scan failed: add-repo: cold scan: promoter: insert node "ecc396a1c43e088376e2837e31d2582d6fb4bb0a89f497325d9966f3b5aa821c": constraint failed: UNIQUE constraint failed: nodes.node_id, nodes.branch (1555)

Two different parser invocations produced the same node_id within a single cold-scan transaction. Likely a collision in the symbol-path → node_id derivation for a specific hugo construct (anon struct field? two methods on different generic instantiations? init function with same parent path?). Affects modernc build; the same bug should reproduce on mattn since it's an application-level PK clash, not a driver bug.

Repro:
  rm -rf /tmp/veska-hugo && mkdir /tmp/veska-hugo
  VESKA_HOME=/tmp/veska-hugo ./bin/veska-daemon &
  git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo /tmp/hugo
  VESKA_HOME=/tmp/veska-hugo ./bin/veska repo add /tmp/hugo --wait

DoD: identify the colliding symbol pair, decide whether the node_id derivation needs to factor in something more (e.g. receiver-type instantiation, line range) or whether the parser is double-emitting.
close_reason: Two-part fix in internal/infrastructure/treesitter/: (1) anchor type_declaration to source_file in queries/go/symbols.scm so function-local types (e.g. hugo helpers_test.go: `type k string` inside two test funcs) aren't emitted into the symbol graph; (2) discriminate multiple `init()` per file by line_start in go_query.go (protobuf .pb.go files routinely declare two). Regression tests pinned in go_test.go (TestParseFile_FunctionLocalTypesIgnored, TestParseFile_MultipleInitFunctions). Verified by cold-scanning hugo (223k LOC, 899 files) to completion on both modernc and mattn — previously crashed mid-promote at file 899.

title: Add KIND column to `veska repo list`
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §`repo list`.

**Why:** Cache pressure (ephemeral count, on-disk size) must be discoverable without a flag. Hiding the tier creates "what cloned that?" moments and makes a user wonder where their disk went. Always-visible KIND is the cheapest fix.

**Scope boundary:** KIND column only. NOT a size column. NOT a `--kind` filter (defer if asked). NOT a summary-line collapse for noisy ephemeral lists (defer if/when noise becomes real).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: `veska repo list` shows KIND column between REPO_ID and BRANCH. Values are exactly `tracked` or `ephemeral`.
- AC2: Empty-registry message unchanged ("no repositories registered — run: veska repo add <path>"). Columns realign cleanly under tabwriter.
- AC3: JSON output (eng_list_repos response + any CLI --json equivalent) includes `kind` field on each row.

**Definition of Done:**
- Golden-output test updated to include KIND column.
- JSON-shape test asserts `kind` field present on every row.
- `make test` passes.
close_reason: Kind field added to repo.Record, application.RepoRecord, mcp.RepoView, and cli repoView. SELECT statements in repo.List/Get/LookupByCanonicalURL now read kind. printRepoTable prints a KIND column between REPO_ID and BRANCH; empty Kind defaults to 'tracked' so older daemon responses don't render blank. Tests cover the column placement, value rendering, default-on-blank, and unchanged empty-registry message. JSON shape exposes kind field on every row (eng_list_repos + cli --json path).

title: Plumb last_accessed_at writes + read eviction env knobs (no sweeper)
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Eviction state model.

**Why:** The actual sweeper is deferred to a follow-up bead, but the access-time bookkeeping and env-knob plumbing must land in kxo5 — otherwise retrofitting later means a second migration and a second pass over the query code paths. Knobs read but unused is fine; the sweeper just consults the already-populated columns + config.

**Scope boundary:** NO sweeper. NO eviction enforcement. NO disk-size accounting. Reads knobs into config struct, documents them, that's it.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: Any query path that touches a kind='ephemeral' row updates last_accessed_at to NOW. Idempotent within the same query (one write per query, not per result row).
- AC2: Config struct exposes `CacheDeclinedTTL` (default 12h), `CacheMaxBytes` (default 5 GiB / 5368709120), `CacheMaxEphemerals` (default 50). Each overridable via `VESKA_CACHE_DECLINED_TTL`, `VESKA_CACHE_MAX_BYTES`, `VESKA_CACHE_MAX_EPHEMERALS`.
- AC3: `veska doctor` (or equivalent inspection surface) shows the three knobs with current effective values + source (default vs env).

**Definition of Done:**
- Unit tests for env-knob parsing including invalid values (return error naming the knob).
- Test confirms an ephemeral query bumps last_accessed_at; tracked query does NOT.
- Design doc note in kxo5 body: sweeper is a separate follow-up bead.
- `make all` passes.
close_reason: config.CacheConfig + LoadCacheConfig read VESKA_CACHE_DECLINED_TTL (default 12h) / VESKA_CACHE_MAX_BYTES (5 GiB) / VESKA_CACHE_MAX_EPHEMERALS (50). Invalid values error and name the offending knob. repo.TouchEphemeral updates last_accessed_at gated by kind='ephemeral' — tracked rows untouched. Doctor ConfigReport carries the loaded cache config + parse error; veska doctor config prints effective values with source (default vs env). No sweeper landed — explicit DoD per the bead.

title: Add TTY-only acceptance prompt + in-place ephemeral→tracked promotion
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Acceptance prompt.

**Why:** Completes the "try before you commit" loop. The only way an ephemeral becomes tracked. Without this, users have no signal that the cached clone is keepable, and agents can't trigger promotion (out of scope here — TTY-only v1).

**Scope boundary:** TTY-only. MCP/script paths skip prompt silently with a one-liner hint. NOT a re-prompt loop (once per row lifetime, gated by prompted_at).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: After `search --repo <url>` (kxo5.6) completes in a TTY with prompted_at IS NULL, prompt `keep <url> indexed? [y/N]` appears. `y` updates row to kind='tracked' and sets prompted_at=NOW. `n` keeps kind='ephemeral' and sets prompted_at=NOW.
- AC2: Non-TTY invocation (stdin or stdout not a tty) skips prompt, leaves row ephemeral, prints `to keep this indexed, run: veska repo add <url>`.
- AC3: Second query against the same row (prompted_at NOT NULL) never re-prompts regardless of TTY state.
- AC4: `y` is in-place — no re-clone, no file move, cache dir stays put. Verified by stat'ing the cache path before + after promotion.

**Definition of Done:**
- Unit tests gate prompt on isatty fakes (cover TTY+unprompted, TTY+prompted, non-TTY+unprompted).
- Integration test covers y/n state transitions (column updates correctly).
- `make all` passes.
close_reason: runAcceptancePrompt fires after rendering for kind='ephemeral' rows with NULL prompted_at. TTY (stdin+stdout both ttys via mattn/go-isatty) → 'keep <url> indexed? [y/N]'. y → repo.PromoteEphemeralToTracked (in-place UPDATE kind='tracked' + prompted_at, gated WHERE kind='ephemeral' so it can't demote a tracked row). n / blank → repo.MarkPromptDeclined (prompted_at only). Non-TTY → one-line 'to keep this indexed, run: veska repo add <url>' hint and NO prompted_at write (so a future TTY run can still ask). promptDeps seam lets tests inject fake isatty + stdin. 7 tests cover y/n/blank/non-TTY/already-prompted/tracked-row/in-place-promotion. JSON output suppresses prompt (would corrupt the JSON stream). mattn/go-isatty was already a transitive dep — left as indirect since  would re-pull other unrelated promotions.

title: Implement `veska search --repo <url>` ephemeral flow with multi-phase progress
description:
**Scope:** large. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Search-on-fresh-URL UX, §Cache tier.

**Why:** The headline UX of kxo5 — "try before you commit." Brings clone + cache + progress + search together into one user-facing command. The hard UX work is the progress display: a silent multi-second wait kills user trust on first invocation.

**Scope boundary:** NOT the acceptance prompt (kxo5.7 owns y/N + state transition). NOT the eviction sweeper. NOT the KIND column in repo list (kxo5.9).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: `veska search '<q>' --repo <url>` on cache-miss clones into RepoCachePath, registers as kind='ephemeral' with canonical_url + derived id, runs cold scan synchronously, then runs the query. Cache-hit path (row exists, dir present) skips clone + cold scan.
- AC2: Terminal output shows 4 distinct phases — `cloning bytes_seen=N`, `walking files_seen=N`, `promoting files_seen=N`, `searching`. Clone phase emits synthetic `phase=cloning` rows into scans_in_flight so `waitForScanComplete` (cmd/veska/repo.go:434) renders all phases via one code path.
- AC3: Row exists but cache dir is missing → silent re-clone, no user-visible error.
- AC4: Existing `--repo <id>` and `--repo <short>` semantics unchanged; LookupByCanonicalURL (kxo5.4) consulted so `--repo <url>` matching an already-tracked repo uses that row instead of cloning.

**Definition of Done:**
- E2e against small public repo (build-tag-gated).
- Unit test for cloning-phase event emission into scans_in_flight.
- Manual verification: progress display flows smoothly through all 4 phases.
- `make all` passes.
close_reason: ephemeralEnsureFromURL replaces the old cloneOrReuse → cache-tier-cloned ~/.veska-search-cache path. URL targets now: (a) canonical_url short-circuit on tracked (no clone) or ephemeral with dir present (touch last_accessed_at, no clone) — kxo5.4's alias unblocks dedup; (b) cache-dir-missing → silent re-clone (AC3); (c) miss → clone via repo.Clone into config.RepoCachePath, repo.Add, UPDATE kind='ephemeral', SetCanonicalURL, TouchEphemeral. --repo flag added as alias for the positional target. isGitURL hardened to use repo.CanonicalURL (matches looksLikeRepoURL from kxo5.3). Multi-phase progress rendered inline: 'cloning <url>' banner + git --progress stderr + existing 'search: indexing …' / 'search: index ready'; the design's 'synthetic scans_in_flight' approach didn't apply since the in-process search path doesn't go through the daemon's scan registry. Four ephemeral tests + cache-path contract test + isGitURL update. AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4 covered.

title: Add cache-tier path resolution (XDG + VESKA_CACHE_HOME)
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Cache tier.

**Why:** Every ephemeral clone lands in a cache dir. Isolating the path math into one helper keeps callers simple, makes the XDG precedence testable in isolation, and gives a single point of override (VESKA_CACHE_HOME) for CI/containerized runs that need to colocate cache with data.

**Scope boundary:** NO directory creation logic beyond mkdir-p in the caller. NO eviction (kxo5.8 + follow-up sweeper). NO clone calls (kxo5.1's job).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: Package func `CacheDir() string` returns `$VESKA_CACHE_HOME` if set, else `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/veska` if set, else `~/.cache/veska`. Result always absolute.
- AC2: Package func `RepoCachePath(repoID string) string` returns `CacheDir() + "/repos/" + repoID`.
- AC3: Env-var precedence covered by table-driven test (3 cases × set/unset combinations).

**Definition of Done:**
- Unit tests for both helpers; precedence table covers all 4 combinations of {VESKA_CACHE_HOME set/unset} × {XDG_CACHE_HOME set/unset}.
- No production code paths consume these yet — consumption lands in kxo5.6.
- `make test` passes.
close_reason: CacheDir() honours VESKA_CACHE_HOME > XDG_CACHE_HOME/veska > ~/.cache/veska. RepoCachePath(repoID) = CacheDir()/repos/<id>. Table-driven precedence test covers all three branches; RepoCachePath test pins the layout. No production code consumes these yet (kxo5.6 is the first call site) per the bead's scope boundary.

title: Populate canonical_url on `repo add <path>` from `git remote get-url origin`
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Identity (collision resolution).

**Why:** Lets a later `search --repo <url>` resolve to the existing tracked row instead of cloning a duplicate ephemeral copy of code the user already has locally. Without this, every `--repo <url>` against an already-tracked repo wastes bandwidth and creates a divergent graph.

**Scope boundary:** Origin remote ONLY. Missing remote → canonical_url=NULL silently (no error, no warning). NOT a backfill of existing rows. Multi-remote setups fall back to duplicate-ephemeral (accepted).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: Registering a path-based repo with an origin remote writes canonicalized origin URL into canonical_url.
- AC2: Registering a repo with no origin remote (or unreachable git) succeeds with canonical_url=NULL.
- AC3: Package helper `LookupByCanonicalURL(db, url) (RepoRecord, bool, error)` returns the tracked row for a URL that matches an origin alias; consumed by kxo5.6.

**Definition of Done:**
- Unit tests cover: origin-present, origin-absent, malformed-remote-URL output.
- LookupByCanonicalURL test covers exact-match + canonicalized-match (different scheme/case → same hit).
- `make test` passes.
close_reason: Add() reads git remote get-url origin via new detectOriginURL helper, canonicalises via repo.CanonicalURL, and writes the result to canonical_url for fresh registrations only (existed=false). Missing remote, malformed URL, or any git failure leaves canonical_url=NULL silently. Re-Adds do NOT backfill — matches the pinned design. Tests cover SSH-form origin → https canonicalised storage, missing origin → NULL, malformed origin → NULL, and re-Add no-backfill invariant. LookupByCanonicalURL helper landed in kxo5.3; kxo5.4 only needed the write side.

title: Implement `veska repo add <url>` (tracked clone+register)
description:
**Scope:** medium. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Entry points, §Clone.

**Why:** Simplest end-to-end exercise of the clone pipeline — no prompt, no ephemeral semantics. Lands the user-facing surface for users who already know they want a repo tracked.

**Scope boundary:** NOT the search flow. NOT the acceptance prompt. Cache tier path NOT used — this writes a tracked path-id row using the cloned dir as its root. NOT a backfill of canonical_url for previously-registered repos.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: `veska repo add https://github.com/<public>/<repo>` clones via the kxo5.1 helper into a path under VESKA_HOME, registers as kind='tracked' with canonical_url set, triggers cold scan exactly like today's path-based `repo add`.
- AC2: Re-running the same URL hits canonical_url uniqueness and surfaces "already registered" without a second clone.
- AC3: Clone failure (network, auth, 404) leaves no orphaned DB row or directory.

**Definition of Done:**
- E2e test against a small public repo (build-tag-gated alongside kxo5.1's integration test).
- `veska repo list` shows the new row with KIND=tracked (column lands in kxo5.9; until then verify via JSON or direct DB).
- `make all` passes.
close_reason: veska repo add <url> detects URL form via looksLikeRepoURL, canonicalises, short-circuits on existing canonical_url row, clones into VESKA_HOME/repos/<urlhash16> with progress streaming to stderr, registers via daemon (with direct fallback) and stamps canonical_url. Rollback on register or canonical_url failure deletes the row + clone dir. Helpers TrackedClonePath / LookupByCanonicalURL / SetCanonicalURL / AddFromURL all unit-tested. End-to-end test against a local file:// repo covers AC1 + AC2; rollback test covers AC3. Pre-existing pipelines flake (memory: project_doctor_pipelines_flake) unaffected.

title: Add kind/canonical_url/last_accessed_at/prompted_at columns to repos table
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 §Identity, §Schema additions.

**Why:** Every downstream task in kxo5 reads or writes one of these columns. Landing the schema first means workers on parallel tasks aren't blocked on a migration race.

**Scope boundary:** NO write-site changes — callers are updated in their own tasks. NO sweeper logic, NO LRU enforcement. NO backfill of canonical_url for existing rows (handled in kxo5.4 on next `repo add` only).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: migration adds 4 columns to repos: `kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tracked'`, `canonical_url TEXT`, `last_accessed_at TIMESTAMP`, `prompted_at TIMESTAMP`. Migration idempotent.
- AC2: existing rows after migration have kind='tracked' and NULL for the other three. Row count preserved.
- AC3: package helper `DerivedRepoIDFromURL(canonical string) string` returns sha256-hex used as the repo_id for ephemeral rows.

**Definition of Done:**
- Migration test: load old-schema fixture DB, run migration, assert columns + defaults + row count.
- Unit test for DerivedRepoIDFromURL (table-driven, ≥4 cases).
- `make test` + `make layercheck` clean.
close_reason: Migration 0013 adds kind/canonical_url/last_accessed_at/prompted_at + partial unique index on canonical_url. DerivedRepoIDFromURL helper added alongside path-based repoID. Tests cover columns-present, defaults-for-existing-row, NULL/non-NULL unique index behavior, and helper determinism + collision resistance. make test + vet + layercheck clean.

title: Add clone helper that resolves URL → local path
description:
**Scope:** small. **Refs:** solov2-kxo5 body + "Pinned design (2026-05-26)" notes (§Clone, §Identity).

**Why:** Foundation for every URL entry point in kxo5. Isolating the network shellout and URL canonicalization into one package keeps callers (repo add, search --repo) simple and lets us unit-test canonicalization without network.

**Scope boundary:** NOT a registration step (no DB writes). NOT cache-tier-aware (caller picks dest dir). NOT auth-aware beyond inheriting git's credential helpers.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- AC1: package func `Clone(ctx, url, destDir, progressW io.Writer) (path, error)` shells out to `git clone --depth=1 --progress url destDir`; streams `--progress` stderr to progressW.
- AC2: package func `CanonicalURL(raw string) (string, error)` returns lowercase-host, .git-stripped, trailing-slash-stripped form; normalizes `git@host:owner/repo` → `https://host/owner/repo`. Table-driven test round-trips https + ssh + with/without .git + with/without trailing slash.
- AC3: clone failure surfaces git's stderr verbatim in the returned error — no swallowing, no rewrite.

**Definition of Done:**
- Unit tests for CanonicalURL (table-driven, ≥8 cases incl. uppercase host, ssh form, git:// scheme).
- Integration test clones a known small public repo into t.TempDir; build-tag-gated so `go test ./...` stays fast.
- `make all` clean (test + vet + lint + layercheck).
close_reason: Clone(ctx,url,destDir,progressW) shells to git clone --depth=1 --progress; stderr both streamed to progressW and captured for verbatim error context. CanonicalURL handles https/http/ssh/git/scp-like forms with lowercase-host + .git-strip + trailing-slash-strip + userinfo-drop; preserves path case and port. Unit tests cover 15 canonical-form cases, 7 invalid-input cases, SSH/HTTPS equivalence (the alias-collision invariant), and stderr-surfacing on git failure. Network-tagged integration test (build tag 'network') clones octocat/Hello-World end-to-end. make test + vet + layercheck clean.

title: Cross-repo CALLS edges resolve through vendor-indexed nodes
description:
Follow-up to solov2-bchl phase 1. The vendor/module-cache indexer now puts vendored symbols in the graph (external=1), but cross_repo_edge_stubs still resolves only against the repos table — so a CALLS edge from myapp.Run -> greetlib.Hello binds when greetlib is a REGISTERED repo, but NOT when it is vendored.

This bead closes that gap: the cross-repo resolver also matches vendored-module nodes.

DESIGN OPTIONS (decide first):
A. Synthetic repo entries. Each indexed module gets a row in repos with repo_id like "ext:github.com/spf13/cobra@v1.10.2", module_path set. The existing resolver works unchanged. Cons: repos table grows; eng_list_repos shows them by default (need a default filter for ext: prefix).

B. Parallel match path. ResolveCrossRepoEdge tries the repos-table match first (today's behaviour); on miss, tries a new "find vendored module nodes" query against nodes WHERE external=1 AND module_path-derived-from-file-path matches. Cons: more complex resolver; module_path inference from file_path needs the importing repo's vendor/ prefix or \$GOMODCACHE layout knowledge.

Recommendation: A. The synthetic-repo approach reuses the existing machinery and the only cost is a filter on eng_list_repos.

ACCEPTANCE:
- myapp.Run calling greetlib.Hello, where greetlib is vendored but NOT registered as a veska repo, produces a CALLS edge that surfaces in eng_get_call_chain and eng_get_blast_radius (in both directions)
- eng_list_repos hides ext: entries by default; a flag (--include-vendored) shows them
- The journey demo (vendor a greetlib in myapp; index it; ask veska context Run on myapp) shows Run --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello on the same screen
close_reason:
Synthetic-repo approach (option A from the bead) implemented and verified end-to-end.

WHAT SHIPPED:
- extindex grows ExternalRepoUpserter port + WithExternalRepoUpserter option. When wired the indexer writes a synthetic repos row (repo_id="ext:<module-path>", module_path set, root_path=vendor dir) before saving nodes. Nodes use the synthetic repo_id so the existing cross_repo_edge_stub resolver finds them via module_path match.
- sqlite.GraphRepo implements UpsertExternalRepo (idempotent INSERT…ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE).
- cmd/veska deps index wires WithExternalRepoUpserter(graph) so every CLI invocation participates in the resolver loop.
- eng_list_repos grows include_vendored=false default; ext: rows filtered out unless caller opts in. Avoids ballooning the repo list with one entry per indexed dependency.

VERIFIED END-TO-END:
Set up myapp importing github.com/example/greetlib (vendored), with main() calling greetlib.New + Greeter.Hello (chained selector). After `veska repo add myapp --wait` and `veska deps index github.com/example/greetlib`:
  veska context main shows:
    cross-repo edges (2):
       main --CALLS--> New in ext:github.c
       main --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello in ext:github.c
The Greeter.Hello bind exercises the full chain: solov2-9rc2 chained-selector parser + Phase C/D method-call stub + yr56 synthetic-repo resolver. veska repo list correctly hides ext: by default.

REMAINING (followups):
- solov2-5x8w bchl phase 2 will add $GOMODCACHE source + auto-trigger on go.sum delta + opt-in embeddings; this work is orthogonal and the yr56 resolver loop already covers it (each phase-2 indexed module gets its own ext: row).

title: Port TypeScript parser to tree-sitter queries (solov2-1yev phase 4)
description:
Follow-up to solov2-1yev phases 1-5 (Go parser query rewrite). The Go side is fully query-driven and the legacy hand-rolled walkers are deleted. TypeScript remains on the legacy walker path (internal/infrastructure/treesitter/ts.go, ~521 LoC).

WHAT TO PORT:
- function_declaration / function declarations
- class_declaration with method_definition children → ClassName.MethodName nodes
- interface_declaration
- type_alias_declaration
- export_statement keyword propagation (solov2-xp1u) — outer export wraps inner decl, the exported flag flows through
- Arrow functions assigned to top-level vars (parseTSArrowFunctions)
- CALLS edges from method bodies (extractTSCallEdges → collectCallNames in ts-mode with member_expression handling)
- Same equivalence-harness approach used for Go: build a TSQueryParser alongside the existing TSParser, diff their outputs on a fixture corpus, flip the default once green, delete the legacy walkers.

SHARED HELPERS THAT STAY:
- collectCallNames currently lives in go.go as the legacy TS path depends on it for class-method-body call extraction. Phase 4 either moves it into a ts-side helper or replaces it with a TS-specific .scm query. Comment in go.go marks it for relocation.

QUERIES NEEDED (queries/ts/):
- symbols.scm — class/interface/function/type-alias declarations, method definitions inside class bodies, arrow functions in var initialisers
- calls.scm — call_expression with identifier / member_expression operands; the 'this' literal as receiver is its own subcase per solov2-gv6
- imports.scm — import_statement / dynamic import shapes

GRAMMAR VARIANTS:
- Both 'typescript' and 'tsx' grammars used today (parser_pool.go). Queries compile against each grammar individually — vendor against tree-sitter-typescript upstream patterns.

ACCEPTANCE:
- queries/ts/* files vendored from tree-sitter-typescript / Helix / nvim-treesitter
- TSQueryParser satisfies ports.CodeParser; equivalence harness green on a ts/tsx fixture corpus mirroring the Go phase 1-3 set
- Daemon flips to TSQueryParser; legacy ts.go walkers + collectCallNames deletion
- All existing ts_test.go tests still pass

Bounded effort similar to Go phases 1-3 (~half-day each, three phases). Solo work or natural pairing with solov2-3a52 (more languages) — once the query path is on both Go + TS, adding Python/Rust/etc. is one .scm file + a small Go shim per language.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-26 by user direction — same 'Go-only focus first' reason as solov2-3a52. The TS port is well-scaffolded by 1yev (query_runner.go is grammar-agnostic, the equivalence-harness pattern works); when picked up it's a half-day per phase mirroring the Go work.

title: collectAnonCalls checks 'function_literal' but tree-sitter Go grammar emits 'func_literal'
description:
Latent bug discovered during solov2-1yev phase 3 work (2026-05-26).

internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go collectAnonCalls walks for nodes with Type() == "function_literal" to extract calls inside cobra-style `var rootCmd = func() { … }` initialisers (extractTopLevelVarInitCalls is the entry point; the bead text in solov2-kzxh names this as the motivating use case).

But the current tree-sitter Go grammar (smacker/go-tree-sitter v0.0.0-20240827094217) emits the node type 'func_literal' (with the underscore) for anonymous function values. Verified by AST probe: `var root = func() { serveRoot() }` produces source_file → var_declaration → var_spec → expression_list → func_literal → ...

Net effect: extractTopLevelVarInitCalls runs and walks the var_declaration subtree but never matches the type check, so no CALLS edges are emitted from the package node into anon-function-bodies. The 'cobra Command{Run: func(){...}}' use case the code claims to support has been silently broken — my fixture-based diff between legacy and query parsers found this by both parsers emitting zero edges where one should have emitted some.

FIX: change the comparison to 'func_literal'. Also: scrap the recursive walk and use a query (now that solov2-1yev phase 3 has the query-runner scaffolding) — `(func_literal body: (block) @body)` matches each anon body in one shot.

Acceptance: a fixture with `var x = func() { Foo() }` produces a CALLS edge from the package node to Foo. The cobra Command{Run: func(){Bar()}} pattern produces CALLS edges through the var's struct-literal initialiser to Bar.
close_reason: Latent bug fixed in the query-driven parser. New extractAnonCallsInTopLevelVars walks top-level var/const subtrees for func_literal (correct grammar node name) and attributes anon-body calls to the package node via the existing calls.scm extractor. Test TestParseFile_AnonCallsInTopLevelVar asserts cli->serveRoot + cli->validate edges from a grouped var of anon functions.

title: Consolidate veska/veska-daemon/veska-mcp into a single binary
description:
Follow-up to solov2-cdw3 phase A (tarball + install.sh shipped).

Current state: 'make build' produces three binaries. veska is the CLI (98MB with embedded model2vec weights), veska-daemon is the long-running indexer (110MB, ALSO embeds the weights), veska-mcp is the stdio shim that proxies to the daemon's MCP socket (5MB, no weights). Total unpacked ~213MB; the model2vec weights are embedded twice (60MB wasted).

Target: one fat binary on disk. Three argv[0]-based personas:
  - 'veska' (default): CLI
  - 'veska-daemon': daemon (started by service manager via symlink)
  - 'veska-mcp': MCP stdio shim (started by MCP client via symlink)
A single Go binary detects filepath.Base(os.Args[0]) at startup and dispatches. The model2vec weights live in exactly one binary; veska-daemon and veska-mcp become zero-byte symlinks.

Mechanical scope:
1. Move cmd/veska-daemon/*.go into internal/cli/daemon/ — rename 'package main' to 'package daemon'; export NewCmd() *cobra.Command and Run([]string) int. Tests stay alongside.
2. Same for cmd/veska-mcp/ → internal/cli/mcp/.
3. cmd/veska-daemon/main.go and cmd/veska-mcp/main.go become 5-line shims (kept temporarily or deleted entirely).
4. cmd/veska/main.go grows an argv[0]-basename dispatcher above newRootCmd() that routes 'veska-daemon' / 'veska-mcp' invocations into the right package's Run().
5. Makefile builds one binary + symlinks the other two names. release-archive ships the binary + the symlinks.
6. cmd/veska/main.go AddCommand(daemon.NewCmd()) + AddCommand(mcp.NewCmd()) so 'veska daemon …' and 'veska mcp …' also work — preferred long-term invocation.
7. cmd/veska/service.go already passes exe + '-daemon' to service.New — keep that contract (symlink resolves correctly).
8. cmd/veska/init_agent.go writes 'veska-mcp' paths — unchanged (symlink).

Acceptance:
- 'make build' produces ONE fat binary + two symlinks
- 'make install' / release-archive ship the same shape
- veska --version, veska-daemon --help, veska-mcp (proxy) all behave identically to today
- Total install size drops by ~60MB

Deferred from solov2-cdw3 because the refactor (~3000 LoC of wire.go etc.) is multi-touch even though mechanical. Phase A landed the install UX without it (tarball + install.sh).
close_reason: One veska binary + veska-daemon / veska-mcp symlinks. cmd/veska-daemon and cmd/veska-mcp moved to internal/cli/{daemon,mcp} exposing NewCmd()/Run(); cmd/veska/main.go gained an argv[0] dispatcher plus AddCommand of both as veska-subcommands. Makefile + scripts/install.sh build the one binary and symlink the two names. Thin build is now 43M (vs 213M for three fat binaries previously — embedded model weights no longer duplicated across veska + veska-daemon). All four invocation paths verified (veska-daemon --help, veska-mcp, veska daemon --help, veska mcp --help). Full test suite + layercheck pass.

title: dirty_blast_radius marks all symbols in file dirty for any edit
description:
Added a comment-only line (// TODO: refactor) to cmd/root.go. eng_get_dirty_blast_radius returned every symbol in root.go (name, rootCmd, init, Execute, shoutCmd) at distance=0.

Expected: a comment-only edit either reports zero dirty symbols, or reports only enclosing symbol of the touched line. Whole-file dirtying defeats the value of 'dirty blast' (would push downstream consumers to assume everything in any modified file is affected).
close_reason: DirtyOf now uses an optional ContentHasher capability to compare each staged node's parser-computed ContentHash against the promoted nodes.content_hash.
