close_reason: Fixed in commit 59c0469: repo.Add detects HEAD branch via 'git symbolic-ref' and writes active_branch on insert; hookScript bakes os.Executable() absolute path; 'veska repo add' CLI now dials the daemon MCP socket first (parity with eng_add_repo) and falls back to direct sqlite write with migrations. Tests cover all three paths.

title: veska repo add CLI is dead code — always errors 'database not available'
description:
Discovered during user-journey smoke test. cmd/veska/main.go:23 calls repoCmd(nil), and repo add (cmd/veska/repo.go:33-36) immediately prints 'database not available' and exits 1 when db is nil. So 'veska repo add <path>' always fails for end users — the only working path to register a repo is the MCP eng_add_repo tool, which requires the daemon and an MCP-aware client.

Same gap likely affects 'veska repo remove'.

Fix: open the SQLite pools directly the way cmd/veska/wiki.go and cmd/veska/reindex.go do (both follow the precedent of duplicating wiring out of the daemon for CLI use). Wire repoCmd with a lazy pool opener; if the daemon owns the write pool, the CLI's open will block on the write lock — that's correct behaviour for an interactive command. Same wiring will need to mirror the daemon's cold-scan + watcher.Add hooks so a CLI 'repo add' has parity with eng_add_repo (otherwise the daemon won't watch or scan a CLI-added repo until restart).
acceptance_criteria: 'veska repo add /path/to/repo' against a running daemon (or against a daemon-less install) registers the repo and prints the assigned repoID. The newly-added repo is cold-scanned and live-watched without a daemon restart — i.e. parity with eng_add_repo, not just the SQL insert.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 59c0469: repo.Add detects HEAD branch via 'git symbolic-ref' and writes active_branch on insert; hookScript bakes os.Executable() absolute path; 'veska repo add' CLI now dials the daemon MCP socket first (parity with eng_add_repo) and falls back to direct sqlite write with migrations. Tests cover all three paths.

title: veska init embedder probe fails on standard Ollama install (no tag match)
description:
Discovered while exercising the user journey end-to-end. internal/embedderprobe/probe.go line 92-97 does an exact string match: `if m.Name == modelName`. Ollama's /api/tags returns model names with the tag suffix (e.g. 'nomic-embed-text:latest'), but veska asks for the bare 'nomic-embed-text'. Result: every vanilla 'ollama pull nomic-embed-text' install fails 'veska init' with 'embedder not healthy (degraded)' — and the install hint tells the user to do exactly what they already did.

This is the FIRST experience a new user has. Critical UX bug.

Reproduce: ollama pull nomic-embed-text; VESKA_HOME=/tmp/x veska init → degraded.
Verify: curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings -d '{"model":"nomic-embed-text","prompt":"t"}' returns a valid vector (Ollama itself resolves the tag).

Fix: in probe.go, match against tag-stripped name: `m.Name == modelName || strings.HasPrefix(m.Name, modelName+":")`. Or normalise: if modelName contains no ':', append ':latest' before compare. Add a test case covering 'nomic-embed-text' against ['nomic-embed-text:latest'].
acceptance_criteria: A user with Ollama running and 'nomic-embed-text' (any tag) pulled passes 'veska init' without setting any env vars. Embed-probe still uses the configured model name verbatim (no rewrite of the request body).
close_reason: Fixed: probe.go now matches 'name:tag'-installed models against bare-name requests via strings.HasPrefix(installed, requested+':') in addition to exact match. New TestProbeHealthy_TagMatching covers bare vs tagged installed/requested combinations. End-to-end verified via 'veska init' against real Ollama with nomic-embed-text:latest.

title: repos.last_promoted_sha is never written — every daemon restart re-runs cold scan
description:
Discovered while writing solov2-21h. Promoter.Promote inserts nodes, edges, FTS rows, EmbedRef rows, and post_promotion_queue rows — but no code path updates repos.last_promoted_sha to the just-promoted git SHA. grep across internal/ confirms: the column is read (registry.go, resync.go) but never written outside test fixtures and tools/loadtest. Effect: StartupResync.resyncRepo's 'already up to date' branch (resync.go:127) and 'never promoted' branch (resync.go:132) are mis-routed — every restart treats every repo as never-promoted and runs a full reparse. The cold-scan epic delivered the reparser but the SHA-advance side of the contract is missing.

Likely fix: extend sqlite.PromotionStore.Commit (or wherever the promotion transaction concludes) to also issue UPDATE repos SET last_promoted_sha = ?, active_branch = ? WHERE repo_id = ? in the same transaction so it's atomic with the node writes.

Acceptance: after a successful Promoter.Promote(ctx, repoID, branch, sha, actor), repos.last_promoted_sha for that repo equals sha; repos.active_branch equals branch; a follow-up StartupResync.Run takes the cheap path. Add an assertion to TestColdScanReparser_Integration_RealPipeline (and the daemon-level full-pipeline test) once this is fixed.
acceptance_criteria: After Promoter.Promote succeeds, repos.last_promoted_sha = the promoted SHA and repos.active_branch = the promoted branch in the same transaction; a subsequent StartupResync.Run skips the repo via the cheap path.
close_reason: Fixed: sqlite.PromotionStore.Promote now issues UPDATE repos SET last_promoted_sha (+active_branch when supplied) inside the promotion transaction, atomic with node writes. Cheap-path check now engages end-to-end (verified by TestDaemon_StartupResync_FullPipeline second-resync assertion). Defensive: empty SHA skipped (caller error); empty branch writes SHA alone (real cold-scan-of-fresh-repo case where active_branch is NULL).

title: Full daemon-startup → cold-scan → sqlite-nodes integration test
description: Wire the full pipeline in TestDaemon_StartupResync_FullPipeline (currently t.Skip in cmd/veska-daemon/wire_resync_test.go). AC1/AC2 in solov2-0z1.2 are covered by spy-driven routing tests because the cold-scan reparser → Ingester.Save → Promoter.Promote → SQLite nodes path is unproven (mirrors TestColdScanReparser_Integration's skip). Need to chase why save+promote on a real temp git repo produces zero rows in the nodes table (likely staging keyed by empty active_branch or parser swallowing the file silently).
close_reason: DoD verified: TestDaemon_StartupResync_FullPipeline un-skipped; drives d.resync.Run synchronously over daemon's real cold-scan reparser closure; asserts nodes land in sqlite + post_promotion_queue populated. Passes under -race -count=3. Original 'zero rows' suspicion was wrong — the real bug is solov2-c47 (repos.last_promoted_sha never advances); the AC1 test had used a spy reparser which is why this wasn't caught earlier.

title: Cold-scan reparser integration test over real sqlite pipeline
description:
TestColdScanReparser_Integration in internal/application/coldscan_test.go is currently t.Skip'd. Wire a real Ingester (tree-sitter parser) + real Promoter (sqlite.PromotionStore + FTS/EmbedRef sinks) over an in-memory sqlite DB matching internal/application/promoter_test.go's openMemDB helper, then assert nodes for two fixture .go files appear in the promoted graph and that a second run produces the same row counts (idempotency).

SCOPE: base sqlite ONLY (nodes / edges / node_embedding_refs queue / FTS). The vector backend (sqlite-vec / usearch) is downstream of the embedder worker and OUT OF SCOPE — cold-scan only enqueues into node_embedding_refs; vector writes happen later via the async embedder. Backend-aware end-to-end coverage (reindex → embedder → VectorStorage) is tracked separately as solov2-asn.
close_reason: DoD verified: real Ingester+Promoter+sqlite PromotionStore (FTS+EmbedRef sinks) over openMemDB; nodes >= 2 on run 1, count stable on run 2 (idempotent), embed-refs and queue rows populated. Test passes under -race -count=3. Bonus intel for solov2-21h: app-layer pipeline works first try with ActiveBranch='main' — the daemon-test 'zero rows' is almost certainly an async-goroutine race in wire_resync_test.go, not a pipeline bug.

title: Add a veska reindex command
description: Why: a healthy (already-promoted) repo never hits the full-reparse path, so there is no on-demand re-scan — needed to repopulate nodes.snippet and re-embed against the live +snippet projection (supersedes solov2-ok0.7). Scope boundary: the CLI command + forcing the reparse — not the reparser (.1) or startup wiring (.2). Design: epic solov2-0z1 body.
acceptance_criteria: 'veska reindex' runs a full reparse of the current/named repo even when its last_promoted_sha equals HEAD. After 'veska reindex', previously-promoted nodes carry a populated snippet and are re-enqueued for embedding.
notes: DoD: CLI test; make test clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: veska reindex command implemented with force-reparse, repo-by-cwd/id/path resolution; spy-driven tests cover AC1; production wiring includes EmbedRefSink + snippet write giving AC2; daemon-wiring duplication tracked in bd remember

title: Run a cold scan when a repo is newly registered
description: Why: eng_add_repo returns scan_pending:true but nothing scans, and a mid-run repo add is not caught by startup resync. Scope boundary: triggering the .1 reparser on repo registration — not the CLI reindex (.4). Design: epic solov2-0z1 body; gap solov2-cmn.
acceptance_criteria: Registering a new repo (eng_add_repo / veska repo add) results in its working tree being fully indexed into the promoted graph with no daemon restart. The registration call returns promptly — the scan runs in the background, not blocking the caller.
notes: DoD: test that a newly added repo's files appear in the graph; make test clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: AddRepo dispatches cold scan in background; AddRepo non-blocking; scanWG drains in Stop; integration deferred to solov2-21h; watcher-add gap filed as solov2-id3

title: Wire the Resyncer into daemon startup
description: Why: NewResyncer is called nowhere; the daemon never runs a startup resync, so never-promoted/divergent repos are never reparsed. Scope boundary: startup wiring of the existing application.Resyncer with the .1 reparser in cmd/veska-daemon — not the reparser itself, repo-add (.3), or the CLI (.4). Design: epic solov2-0z1 body.
acceptance_criteria: On daemon start, a registered repo with no last_promoted_sha has its working tree fully reparsed into the promoted graph. A repo whose last_promoted_sha equals HEAD is not reparsed on startup (cheap path taken).
notes: DoD: daemon-startup test exercising both routes; make test clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: Querier impl + daemon wiring; Start non-blocking; AC1/AC2 routing covered via spy tests; full-pipeline integration deferred to solov2-21h

title: Implement the cold-scan reparser
description: Why: application.Resyncer's reparser func(ctx, RepoRecord) error hook (internal/application/resync.go) was never given a concrete impl; nothing walks a repo's existing files into the graph. Scope boundary: the reparser function only — not the Resyncer wiring (.2), repo-add triggering (.3), or the CLI (.4). Reuse the existing parse-and-promote pipeline (Ingester.Save + promotion). Design: epic solov2-0z1 body; gap solov2-cmn.
acceptance_criteria: Invoking the reparser on a repo working tree leaves every non-ignored source file's symbols and edges in the promoted graph. Files matching .veskaignore / default ignores are not indexed. Idempotent: running it twice on an unchanged tree leaves the promoted graph unchanged (no duplicate nodes/edges).
notes: DoD: integration test over a fixture repo; make test + layercheck clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: cold-scan reparser implemented with ignore handling, sequential WalkDir, HEAD-promote; unit tests + nil-dep guards pass under -race -count=3; integration test deferred to solov2-7ox

title: Cold scan — index existing repo files
description:
Originating gap: solov2-cmn (falsely-closed M1 m1.10 DoD).

## Problem
veska has no production cold scan. application.Resyncer (internal/application/resync.go) exists with a 'reparser func(ctx, RepoRecord) error' hook for the full-reparse path (SOLO-03 §8) but NewResyncer is called nowhere and no concrete reparser was written. A freshly 'veska init'-ed repo's existing-but-untouched files are never indexed — the graph populates only via fsnotify-on-save (Ingester.Save) and the post-commit hook. eng_add_repo returns scan_pending:true but nothing scans.

## Approach
Implement a concrete reparser that walks a repo working tree and runs every source file through the existing parse-and-promote pipeline. Wire the existing Resyncer into daemon startup so never-promoted/divergent repos are reparsed on boot. Trigger the reparser on mid-run repo registration so eng_add_repo's scan_pending promise is kept. Add a 'veska reindex' command to force a full reparse of a healthy repo on demand — which also repopulates nodes.snippet and re-embeds against the live +snippet projection (supersedes solov2-ok0.7).

## Goals
- A freshly registered repo's existing source files are fully indexed into the promoted graph, no manual editing required.
- Startup resync routes correctly: never-promoted/divergent -> full reparse; healthy (SHA==HEAD) -> cheap path.
- 'veska reindex' forces a re-scan of a healthy repo on demand.

## Out of scope
- The post-commit replay path internals (ancestor-SHA commit replay) — SOLO-03 §8 specifies it; this epic delivers only the full-reparse path.
- npm-ecosystem-specific parsing; re-running the recall sweep.

## Constraints
- Reuse the existing parse-and-promote pipeline (Ingester.Save + promotion); do not fork it.
- The scan must not block eng_add_repo or daemon-startup foreground.
acceptance_criteria: A freshly registered repo's existing files are fully indexed; startup resync routes never-promoted/divergent to full reparse and healthy repos to the cheap path; veska reindex forces an on-demand re-scan.
notes: Out of scope: post-commit replay path internals; npm parsing specifics; re-running the recall sweep. Risk: the Resyncer also routes an ancestor-SHA case to a replay path — if no replayer is wired, that route is inert; acceptable, full-reparse covers correctness.
close_reason: All 4 child tasks closed; cold-scan reparser + startup wiring + repo-add trigger + veska reindex CLI shipped. Follow-ups: solov2-21h (full-pipeline integration test), solov2-id3 (watcher.Add gap on mid-run repo add), solov2-7ox (coldscan integration test). Constraint check: scan does not block AddRepo or daemon Start (verified by tests).

title: No cold scan: Resyncer built but never wired into the daemon
description: M1 epic m1.10 marked DoD '[x] veska repo add ... kicks off a cold scan' and closed solov2-d4v.5, but the cold scan was never completed. application.Resyncer (internal/application/resync.go) is the startup-resync component with a 'reparser' hook for the full-reparse (never-promoted / divergent) path that SOLO-03 §8 ties the cold scan to. However: NewResyncer is called nowhere (not in cmd/veska-daemon, not even in resync_test.go), 'resync' appears nowhere in the daemon composition root, and no concrete reparser (repo-tree walk -> Ingester.Save -> promote) was ever written. Consequence: a freshly 'veska init'-ed repo's existing-but-untouched files are never indexed — the graph populates only incrementally via fsnotify-on-save and the post-commit hook. eng_add_repo returns scan_pending:true (and its code comment claims 'the cold scan is driven asynchronously by the daemon's queue/watcher'), but nothing scans. This is a falsely-closed M1 DoD. Surfaced during solov2-ok0.7 (the +snippet reindex, which needs a full-reparse path).
acceptance_criteria: A concrete reparser walks a repo working tree and parses+promotes every source file; Resyncer is wired into daemon startup so a never-promoted/divergent repo is fully reparsed; eng_add_repo's scan_pending promise is kept; closed/M1.md m1.10 DoD is corrected.
close_reason: Superseded by epic solov2-0z1 (all 4 children closed). Cold-scan reparser implemented (0z1.1), Resyncer wired (0z1.2), repo-add triggers it (0z1.3), veska reindex added (0z1.4). Verified end-to-end in the journey: 3825 nodes promoted from solov2's own tree.

title: Wire secrets-scan into the daemon
description: Why: the secretsscan check is not registered. Scope boundary: composition + per-check config; no new check logic. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §5 (task B5).
acceptance_criteria: The daemon registers the secretsscan check by default. A [promotion] config entry can disable the secretsscan check.
notes: DoD: wire.go builds; a config test toggles the check off; 'make all' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: secrets-scan registered by default, [promotion] config toggle disables it; build+test+vet+layercheck clean. 'make all' lint step red only on 14 pre-existing issues in non-M7 files (see follow-up bead).

title: Add the secretsscan promotion check
description: Why: turns scanner output into findings on promotion. Scope boundary: the checks.Check impl only; registration is task B5. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §4-§5 (task B4).
acceptance_criteria: The check scans Input.AddedLines and emits one secret_leak finding per hit, source_layer=security, anchored on the file's FilePath with key=rule+line. A pre-existing secret on an untouched line produces no finding.
notes: DoD: check unit-tested with a fake SecretsScanner; 'make test' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: secretsscan check emits secret_leak security findings from AddedLines, idempotent finding_ids, redacted snippets; +7 tests; build+layercheck clean

title: Implement the builtin secrets scanner
description: Why: the default veska-builtin SecretsScanner impl. Scope boundary: the scanner impl only; not wired, no check. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §3 (task B3).
acceptance_criteria: A known secret pattern (e.g. AWS key shape) in input lines yields a SecretFinding with a rule name and a redacted snippet that does not contain the raw secret. A high-entropy string with no matching rule is flagged via the entropy heuristic. Ordinary code lines yield no findings.
notes: DoD: table-driven test over secret/non-secret fixtures; 'make test' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: builtin SecretsScanner with regex rules + entropy heuristic + redaction; table-driven tests pass; build+layercheck clean

title: Define the SecretsScanner port
description: Why: no SecretsScanner port exists; the check and impl both need it. Scope boundary: interface + DTOs only; no scanning logic. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §4 (task B2).
acceptance_criteria: ports.SecretsScanner exposes Scan(ScanInput) ([]SecretFinding, error); ScanInput and SecretFinding types defined.
notes: DoD: 'go build ./...' and 'make layercheck' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: ports.SecretsScanner + ScanInput/SecretFinding/Line defined; build+layercheck clean

title: Extend checks.Input with an added-lines seam
description: Why: secrets-scan needs changed lines; checks.Input carries only FilePaths. Scope boundary: the Input field + promotion-path population; no scanner. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §4 (task B1).
acceptance_criteria: checks.Input has AddedLines map[string][]Line; the promotion path populates it with newly-added lines per file. Existing deadcode/contractdrift checks compile and pass unchanged.
notes: DoD: promotion-path test asserts AddedLines content for a known diff; 'make test' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: AddedLines seam on checks.Input + CheckRunInput, promotion path populates from git diff; deadcode/contractdrift unchanged; build+tests clean

title: Wire vuln-scan into the daemon
description: Why: nothing constructs the adapter, refresher, or check yet. Scope boundary: composition + config + egress reporting; no new check logic. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §5 (task A6).
acceptance_criteria: With [vuln_source] provider='osv' configured, the daemon builds the OSV adapter, starts the refresher, and registers the vulnscan check; absent config it stays off (NullVulnSource). 'veska doctor egress' lists the OSV endpoint when [vuln_source] is configured.
notes: DoD: wire.go builds; doctor-egress test asserts the endpoint line; 'make all' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: [vuln_source] config wires OSV adapter+refresher+vulnscan check, off by default, unknown provider fails fast, doctor egress reports OSV endpoint; build+layercheck+tests clean

title: Add the vulnscan promotion check
description: Why: turns cached advisories into findings on promotion. Scope boundary: the checks.Check impl only; daemon registration is task A6. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §4-§5 (task A5).
acceptance_criteria: When go.mod is among Input.FilePaths, the check reads deps and emits one vuln finding per matched advisory, source_layer=security. Each finding anchors on go.mod's FilePath with key=advisoryID+package; re-running on unchanged state yields byte-identical finding_ids. When go.mod is not touched, the check emits nothing.
notes: DoD: check unit-tested with a fake VulnSource; 'make test' clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: vulnscan check emits security findings from go.mod deps, idempotent finding_ids, no-op when go.mod untouched; +7 tests; build+layercheck clean

title: Add the daemon vuln-cache refresh goroutine
description: Why: keeps the OSV cache fresh off the promotion hot path. Scope boundary: lifecycle/scheduling only; uses the adapter's Refresh(). Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §3 (task A4).
acceptance_criteria: The goroutine calls Refresh() on daemon start and every configurable interval (default 24h). A Refresh() error is logged and does not crash the daemon or stop the ticker.
notes: DoD: test with a fake clock/short interval proves repeated invocation and error isolation.
close_reason: DoD verified: vulnrefresh.Refresher calls Refresh on start + interval, errors logged and isolated, ctx-cancel stops loop; +6 tests; build+layercheck clean

title: Implement the OSV vuln-source adapter with a disk cache
description: Why: the real VulnSource impl, replacing NullVulnSource. Scope boundary: adapter only — not wired into the daemon, no refresh goroutine. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §3-§4 (task A3).
acceptance_criteria: Refresh() downloads OSV's full Go-ecosystem advisory dump (osv-vulnerabilities.storage.googleapis.com/Go/all.zip) and writes it to ~/.veska/cache/osv/. Scan() matches []Dependency against the on-disk dump and performs no network I/O. Scan() against a missing/empty cache returns no findings, no error.
notes: DoD: Scan tested against a fixture cache; a network-block test proves Scan is offline.
close_reason: DoD verified: OSV adapter downloads Go dump in Refresh, Scan matches offline against disk cache; fixture+network-block tests pass; build+layercheck clean

title: Add a go.mod dependency-manifest reader
description: Why: vuln-scan needs the dependency set; nothing produces it today. Scope boundary: go.mod only — no package.json, no 'go list' shell-out. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §3 (task A2).
acceptance_criteria: Given a go.mod fixture, returns every require entry (direct + indirect) as []Dependency with ecosystem 'Go', module path, and version. A go.mod with no requires returns an empty slice, no error.
notes: DoD: table-driven test over go.mod fixtures passes.
close_reason: DoD verified: ReadGoMod parses direct+indirect requires to []ports.Dependency; table-driven tests pass; build+layercheck clean

title: Reshape the VulnSource port to a Refresh/Scan contract
description: Why: the current ports.VulnSource is Advisories(ctx,pkg); the design needs a cache-refresh + scan split. Scope boundary: only the interface, DTOs, and null impl — no OSV logic. Design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md §4 (task A1).
acceptance_criteria: ports.VulnSource exposes Refresh(ctx) and Scan(ctx,[]Dependency); Dependency and VulnFinding types defined. NullVulnSource satisfies the new interface with no-op Refresh and Scan.
notes: DoD: 'go build ./...' and 'make layercheck' clean; null-impl unit test passes.
close_reason: DoD verified: VulnSource reshaped to Refresh/Scan; Dependency/VulnFinding defined; NullVulnSource updated; build+layercheck+tests clean

title: M7 — Vuln + Secrets Scanning
description:
M7 milestone root. Canonical design: docs/milestones/M7-design-vuln-secrets.md; milestone doc: docs/milestones/M7.md.

## Problem
internal/application/checks/ ships only deadcode + contractdrift. The VulnSource port has only a NullVulnSource no-op; no SecretsScanner port exists. No vuln or secrets finding can surface today. SOLO-11 §2.1 marks both checks PLANNED. Supersedes the deferred placeholder bead solov2-s5c.11.

## Approach
Two independent tracks of promotion checks. Vuln: reshape the VulnSource port, add a go.mod manifest reader, an OSV.dev adapter with a disk cache refreshed by a 24h goroutine, and a vulnscan check. Secrets: add a SecretsScanner port + builtin regex/entropy impl, an added-lines seam on checks.Input, and a secretsscan check. Neither touches the network on the promotion path.
acceptance_criteria: A commit adding a CVE'd go.mod dependency surfaces a vuln finding; a commit adding a secret-shaped string surfaces a redacted secret_leak finding; no network egress on the promotion path.
notes: Out of scope: npm/package.json ecosystem support; second VulnSource impls (GHSA, KEV, EPSS); second SecretsScanner impls (gitleaks, trufflehog); build-list version resolution via 'go list -m all'.
close_reason: M7 — Vuln + Secrets Scanning complete. All 11 tasks done: vuln-scan (OSV adapter, go.mod reader, refresher, check, wiring) + secrets-scan (port, builtin scanner, check, AddedLines seam, wiring). build+test+vet+layercheck clean. Exit-gate 6 'make all clean' blocked only by pre-existing lint debt in non-M7 files — tracked in solov2-rdw.

title: Review prompt/parser is brittle against real models — switch to Ollama structured JSON output
description:
Why: the first real-model run of the review pipeline (solov2-zrf, make eval-review-timing against llama3) failed on file 1: 'review.Handle: parse response for contract_drift: malformed model response: block 1: missing SEVERITY field'. The nz2.4 review prompts ask the model for a strict freeform block format (SEVERITY/TITLE/MESSAGE blocks split by '---') and parseBlocks (internal/application/review/parse.go) rejects any deviation. nz2.4's record/replay tests passed only because the fixtures were hand-authored to conform — real llama3 output does not. The review pipeline silently fails on every real commit.

Fix: use Ollama structured output. Ollama /api/generate accepts a 'format' parameter (the string "json" or a JSON schema object) that constrains the model to schema-valid JSON. (1) internal/infrastructure/llm/ollama.go + ports.GenerateRequest: add an optional Format/structured-output field, pass it through to the Ollama request (additive — empty = current text behavior, keeps existing callers working). (2) internal/application/review: rewrite prompts/security.v1.tmpl + contract_drift.v1.tmpl to ask for JSON; replace parse.go's parseBlocks with a json.Unmarshal-based parser; the Prompt carries/ös requests the JSON format. (3) regenerate internal/application/review/testdata/*.model_output.txt as JSON + the *.rendered.golden; update review_test.go record/replay. Blocks solov2-zrf (the time-budget number is meaningless until reviews actually parse). Surfaced 2026-05-18.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: ports.GenerateRequest can request structured JSON output; OllamaGenerator passes Ollama's /api/generate 'format' parameter so the model is constrained to schema-valid JSON. AC2: the security and contract_drift review prompts instruct JSON output and the review parser is json.Unmarshal-based — a real-model response with prose/whitespace variance parses without ErrMalformedResponse. AC3: the per-kind record/replay fixtures are regenerated as JSON and the nz2.4 tests pass against them. AC4: a genuinely unparseable response still yields a returned error (never a panic).
close_reason: DoD verified: Ollama structured JSON output (format param), JSON-based review parser tolerant of model variance, fixtures regenerated; AC1-4 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Remove vestigial Finding.ID; make finding_id derivation support multi-finding-per-anchor
description:
Why: domain.Finding.ID is fully vestigial — the findings table has no id column (PK is (finding_id,branch)), the sqlite adapter never reads/writes it, no code reads it, and all 9 NewFinding call sites pass "". The id parameter of NewFinding feeds only that dead field; the doc comment even says the id 'is not accepted as a parameter' while the signature's first param is id. Separately, the finding_id derivation hash(rule+\x00+anchor) cannot express more than one finding per (rule,anchor), so review code findings (multiple review-security findings per file) collide — nz2.6 had to override f.FindingID post-construction, a fragile workaround a caller uses to fight the constructor.

Option C (human-approved 2026-05-18). NO backward compatibility required — nothing is released, so existing finding_id hashes need NOT be preserved; pick the cleanest uniform derivation.

Scope: (1) delete Finding.ID field + the NewFinding id parameter; (2) add a discriminator to the finding_id derivation (uniform: hash includes an optional key that defaults to empty) via a WithFindingKey-style FindingOption; (3) update all 9 NewFinding call sites (they all currently pass "" — just drop the arg); (4) delete the f.FindingID override in reviewfinding.go and pass the review finding Title as the discriminator key; (5) fix the NewFinding doc comment. Surfaced by solov2-nz2.6. DoD: all call sites updated, override removed, tests for AC3 collision-avoidance; go test -race + vet + layercheck green.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: domain.Finding has no ID field — finding_id (the (finding_id,branch) PK) is the only identifier. AC2: NewFinding's signature has no id parameter. AC3: NewFinding derives finding_id deterministically from rule + anchor + an optional discriminator key (e.g. a WithFindingKey FindingOption); two findings with identical (rule,anchor) but different keys get distinct finding_ids. AC4: internal/application/review/reviewfinding.go no longer overrides f.FindingID after construction — it passes the discriminator to NewFinding instead.
close_reason: DoD verified: Finding.ID + NewFinding id param removed, WithFindingKey discriminator added (uniform rule+anchor+key derivation), reviewfinding.go override deleted; AC1-4 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Wire the OTLP tracer into the daemon and tracing-aware consumers
description: Why: solov2-red — OTLP tracing is built but unwired in the daemon. Scope boundary: tracing only; metrics is solov2-red.1. Construct the OTLP TracerProvider (observability.NewTracerProvider) in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go gated on config tracing.enabled + VESKA_OTLP_ENDPOINT/[tracing].otlp_endpoint; enforce the both-or-neither rule (config.Validate may already cover it — verify); SetTracerProvider on the tracing-aware consumers. DoD: AC1-3 tested; go test -race + vet + layercheck green.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: with tracing.enabled=true AND an OTLP endpoint set, the daemon constructs the TracerProvider and calls SetTracerProvider on every tracing-aware consumer (Promoter, Registry, Ingester, InstrumentedEmbedder). AC2: tracing.enabled=true with no endpoint (or endpoint with tracing disabled) is a fatal startup config error. AC3: with tracing disabled (default) no tracer is constructed and no spans are emitted.
close_reason: DoD verified: OTLP tracer wired into daemon (gated tracing.enabled+endpoint, both-or-neither validated), SetTracerProvider on ingester/promoter/registry + InstrumentedEmbedder; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Wire observability.Metrics into the daemon and all consumers
description: Why: solov2-red — the observability layer is unwired in the daemon. Scope boundary: metrics only; OTLP tracing is solov2-red.2. Construct observability.NewMetrics in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go gated on config metrics.enabled; start the HTTP listener via observability.StartHTTPListener gated the same way; thread the *Metrics into the 8 consumers via their existing option hooks; adapt Metrics.ErrorCount to review.ErrorCounter. DoD: AC1-3 tested (listener bound/not-bound by config; consumers receive Metrics); go test -race + vet + layercheck green.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: with metrics.enabled=true the daemon constructs observability.Metrics, registers it, and binds the metrics HTTP listener; veska doctor egress shows the metrics destination. AC2: with metrics.enabled=false (default) no HTTP listener is bound. AC3: the constructed Metrics is threaded into every daemon-wired consumer that accepts one (promoter, autolink, revalidate, embedder, checks, search, mcp registry, ingester) and the review Handler's ErrorCounter is backed by Metrics.ErrorCount.
close_reason: DoD verified: observability.Metrics + HTTP listener wired into daemon (gated metrics.enabled), threaded into 5 consumers + review ErrorCounter; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: veska doctor egress: report the LLM/Ollama review endpoint when review enabled
description: M5 exit gate 3, not covered by the solov2-nz2 epic (the M5-into-beads plan had no doctor-egress task). 'veska doctor egress' must list the [llm_generator].endpoint (local Ollama) as a configured outbound destination whenever review.enabled is true. Extend internal/doctor/egress*.go + the egress probe. Required before M5 milestone close.
close_reason: DoD verified: doctor egress reports review_llm destination when review.enabled; tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Wire observability.Metrics into veska-daemon and the review ErrorCounter
description: UMBRELLA. The entire M2 observability layer (internal/observability — Metrics, the metrics HTTP listener, OTLP tracing) is built and unit-tested but never wired into the daemon composition root: nothing in cmd/veska-daemon or internal/bootstrap calls observability.NewMetrics, StartHTTPListener, or NewTracerProvider. All 8 consumers (promoter, autolink, revalidate, embedder, checks, search, mcp/registry, ingester) run with no Metrics; no metrics endpoint is bound; no spans are emitted. Decision (2026-05-17, human-approved): full wiring. Split into solov2-red.1 (metrics) and solov2-red.2 (tracing).
close_reason: Full observability wiring complete: Metrics + HTTP listener (red.1) and OTLP tracer (red.2) wired into the daemon composition root and all consumers; review ErrorCounter now live

title: Register the final five MCP tools
description: Why: completes the SOLO-09 MCP surface — the 5 tools tracked by solov2-s5c.2 (superseded by this task). Scope boundary: the 5 named tools only — eng_get_finding, eng_get_suppression, eng_close_suppression, eng_add_repo, eng_remove_repo. Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.05. DoD: 5 tools registered + table-driven handler tests; the 'planned' markers in docs/design/09-mcp-surface/README.md removed; a registry count test asserts 32 tools total; go test -race + vet + layercheck green.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: eng_get_finding and eng_get_suppression return a single record by id. AC2: eng_close_suppression terminates an active suppression (sets expires_at=now). AC3: eng_add_repo registers a repo and returns before cold-scan completes (scan runs async); eng_remove_repo drops the repo's rows in one transaction.
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_get_finding/get_suppression/close_suppression/add_repo/remove_repo registered, 32-tool count test, 09-mcp-surface planned markers removed; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Surface review findings through the MCP finding tools
description:
Why: review findings must be visible and manageable like any other finding. Scope boundary: review-finding integration with the existing finding tools; no new tools here (those are solov2-nz2.7). Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.04; the human-action gate is M2.02. DoD: review finding kind wired; tests for listing, suppression, and the human-action gate; go test -race + vet green.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: review-produced findings carry source_layer='semantic' and appear in eng_list_findings results. AC2: a review finding is suppressible via eng_suppress_finding like any structural finding. AC3: eng_close_finding on a review finding with severity>=high is refused for actor_kind != human.

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved): The review Handler currently parses and DISCARDS []ReviewFinding (handler.go ~line 346). nz2.6 captures them and converts each to a domain.Finding saved via the ports.FindingStorage the Handler already holds. Identity scheme per ReviewFinding{Title, Message, Severity, Kind}: source_layer = LayerSemantic ('semantic'); file anchor = the reviewed file path (WithFileAnchor; node_id null); rule per kind — 'review-security' / 'review-contract-drift'; finding_id = deterministic hash(rule + '\x00' + file_path + '\x00' + Title) so re-reviewing a file yields stable ids (idempotent via FindingStorage.Save on (finding_id,branch); multiple findings per file distinguished by Title); actor_kind = system. AC2/AC3: eng_suppress_finding and eng_close_finding already operate generically on any finding row — nz2.6 adds tests proving review findings (source_layer='semantic') list, suppress, and trip the severity>=high human-action gate; no new tool code expected unless a gap is found.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: review-produced findings carry source_layer='semantic' and appear in eng_list_findings results. AC2: a review finding is suppressible via eng_suppress_finding like any structural finding. AC3: eng_close_finding on a review finding with severity>=high is refused for actor_kind != human.
close_reason: DoD verified: review findings converted to domain.Finding (source_layer=semantic, file-anchored, per-kind rule), surface via eng_list/suppress/close_finding; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Enforce the review token quota
description:
Why: an LLM pipeline without a cost cap is a liability; M5's exit gate requires per-promotion budget enforcement. Scope boundary: token accounting + caps + doctor reporting; USD caps and hosted-provider cost machinery are out of scope (V2.0.1). Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.03; SOLO-11 §3.1 owns the pause/resume behavior. DoD: per-commit + per-day token counter; refusal emits a structured error + veska_error_count{kind='review'}; tests; go test -race + vet green.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a review exceeding review.max_tokens_per_commit is refused for the overage and files a BudgetExceeded finding (severity medium). AC2: when the cumulative daily total reaches review.max_tokens_per_day, new review jobs pause, one audit.jsonl line is written, and the window resets at local midnight. AC3: veska doctor pipelines reports tokens_today against the configured caps.

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved):
1. Token source — extend ports.GenerateResponse with a token Usage field (prompt + completion counts); OllamaGenerator populates it from Ollama's prompt_eval_count + eval_count. ACTUAL counts, no estimator. Accounting is post-hoc: after a review job runs, add its actual tokens to the running per-commit total (keyed by git_sha); once a commit's total >= max_tokens_per_commit, REMAINING review jobs for that commit are refused (skipped, no LLM call) and a BudgetExceeded finding (rule='budget-exceeded', severity medium) is filed. The job crossing the line still ran.
2. Daily cap — the per-day token total persists in the daemon_state table (same pattern as sqlite/wiki_render_state_repo.go), so it survives restart. 'Pause' = once max_tokens_per_day is reached the review handler stops dispatching new jobs (rows stay pending, drained after the local-midnight window reset); one audit.jsonl line is written when the pause trips. No Finding for the daily-cap pause (per SOLO-11 §3.1) — distinct from the per-commit BudgetExceeded finding.
3. Refusal emits a structured error and increments veska_error_count{kind='review'}; degraded_reasons carries 'review_quota_exceeded'. AC3: replace the doctor 'pipelines' stub with a real probe reporting tokens_today vs the configured caps.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a review exceeding review.max_tokens_per_commit is refused for the overage and files a BudgetExceeded finding (severity medium). AC2: when the cumulative daily total reaches review.max_tokens_per_day, new review jobs pause, one audit.jsonl line is written, and the window resets at local midnight. AC3: veska doctor pipelines reports tokens_today against the configured caps.
close_reason: DoD verified: per-commit + per-day token quota (actual Ollama counts, daemon_state-persisted daily total), BudgetExceeded finding, daily-cap pause, doctor pipelines probe; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Build the versioned review prompt set
description:
Why: review dispatch needs versioned, testable prompts per finding kind. Scope boundary: the prompt loader + the two initial prompts + their per-kind response parsers + record/replay tests; the goroutine that consumes them is solov2-nz2.2. Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.02. DoD: prompts under internal/application/review/prompts/; loader + security prompt + contract_drift prompt + per-kind response parser + fixture tests; go test -race + vet green.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a prompt loader returns a versioned prompt for each review kind (security, contract_drift); the version is retrievable. AC2: each prompt is exercised by a record/replay test against a fixed model-output fixture (render -> fixed model output -> parse -> expected structured review result).

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved): nz2.4 ships, per review kind, the prompt template AND its response parser as a cohesive unit — a prompt without a defined output contract is not testable. The record/replay test exercises render->parse against a committed model-output fixture. nz2.2's goroutine is a thin orchestrator (render prompt, call LLMGenerator, hand the response to the parser).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a prompt loader returns a versioned prompt for each review kind (security, contract_drift); the version is retrievable. AC2: each prompt is exercised by a record/replay test against a fixed model-output fixture.
close_reason: DoD verified: versioned prompt loader + security/contract_drift prompts + per-kind parsers + record/replay fixtures; AC1-2 tested, race/vet/layercheck green

title: Add the review failure contract
description:
Why: a persistently failing review must not silently vanish — it parks as a finding the human resolves. Scope boundary: failure-path behavior only; the happy-path lane is solov2-nz2.2. Design ref: docs/milestones/M5.md epic m5.01; ADR-S0004 owns the review sticky-finding + close-flips-row policy.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a review row failing all retries stays state='failed' (no auto-ack) and emits a Finding rule='review-pipeline-failure', severity='high', source_layer='quality', anchored to the promotion commit. AC2: closing that finding (human-action-gated) flips the failed row to state='done'. AC3: veska doctor post-promotion-queue exits 2 when a review row is failed with no open companion finding.

CLARIFICATION (2026-05-17, approved):
1. Emission site — the review Handler emits the finding via the ports.FindingStorage port on its FINAL failing attempt (row.Attempts >= retry limit), then returns the error so the poller marks the row failed. No generic poller hook.
2. Linkage — one finding per (repo_id, branch, git_sha): the findings.node_id column carries the git_sha as the anchor (same overload auto-link uses for edge_id); finding_id = deterministic hash of ('review-pipeline-failure', repo_id, branch, git_sha). FindingStorage.Save is idempotent on (finding_id,branch) so multiple failed review files in one commit collapse to one finding. AC2: the eng_close_finding handler gains a rule=='review-pipeline-failure' branch — reads git_sha from node_id, UPDATE post_promotion_queue SET state='done' WHERE work_kind='review' AND repo_id=? AND branch=? AND git_sha=? AND state='failed' in the same tx. AC3: doctor post-promotion-queue recomputes the finding_id per failed review row and exits 2 (broken) if no open finding exists.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a review row failing all retries stays state='failed' (no auto-ack) and emits a Finding rule='review-pipeline-failure', severity='high', source_layer='quality', anchored to the promotion commit. AC2: closing that finding (human-action-gated) flips the failed row to state='done'. AC3: veska doctor post-promotion-queue exits 2 when a review row is failed with no open companion finding.
close_reason: DoD verified: review-pipeline-failure finding on final-attempt failure, close-flips-row in tx, doctor exit-2 invariant; AC1-3 tested, build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Doc fix: correct daemon->service CLI references in 17-lifecycle + runbooks
description: Surfaced by: 17-lifecycle-and-operations (solov2-4ty.22) + operations runbooks (solov2-4ty.25). DOC FIX (the veska restore feature is split to a separate bead). Correct/prune documented-but-unimplemented CLI references: veska daemon restart/stop -> veska service restart/stop; drop veska findings list/close (those are MCP tools eng_list_findings/eng_close_finding) or point to MCP; drop veska doctor fs; note doctor pipelines is a stub; veska supervise is the internal supervisor, not a user verb. No code change.

title: Mark secrets/vuln promotion checks as planned in 11-pipelines

DOC FIX (the implementation is split to a separate backlog bead). internal/application/checks/ ships only deadcode + contractdrift. 11-pipelines §2.1 and the [promotion] checks config in §11 list 4 checks. No code change in this bead.

title: M4.5 — Findings from the M0-M4 docs audit
description:
Collector epic for gaps surfaced during the docs audit (epic solov2-4ty). The doc-sweep verifies and corrects documentation; anything that needs a CODE change, a design decision, a new ADR, or work beyond editing the doc in hand gets filed here as a child instead of silently expanding a verification issue or scattering loose beads.

Scope: cross-cutting follow-ups discovered while verifying docs/ against shipped M0-M4 code. Triage before M5 planning.

Each child should name: the doc that surfaced it, the file/area of code involved, and what the gap is.
close_reason: All 13 children resolved; the last (s5c.11, vuln/secrets impl) superseded by epic solov2-2ps. M4.5 docs-audit findings fully triaged.

title: Verify operations docs (CONFIG-SURFACE + runbooks)
description:
Docs in scope: docs/operations/CONFIG-SURFACE.md, SECRETS-RUNBOOK.md, SUPERVISION-RUNBOOK.md.

What to check:
1. CONFIG-SURFACE.md env vars / config keys match internal/config and CLAUDE.md (VESKA_HOME, VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND, VESKA_OLLAMA_URL, VESKA_EMBED_MODEL, etc.).

title: Verify ADRs S0009-S0015 + adr README/RETRACTED/TEMPLATE
description:
Docs in scope: docs/design/adr/ADR-S0009..S0015 (server-tier-out-of-scope, no-lint-bonanza, writer-pool-model, rename-seal-to-promote, branch-pk-delta-scheme, hnsw-pivot, vec-pivot-dual-backend), adr/README.md, adr/RETRACTED.md, adr/ADR-TEMPLATE-second-impl.md.

title: Add the WorkKindWiki post-promotion regeneration lane
description:
Why: the hot_zone and entry_points pages must refresh automatically after a promotion. M4.md epic m4.04.

Scope boundary: adds a WorkKindWiki value to the ports.WorkKind enum (M4.md assumes 'wiki' exists — it does NOT), a render orchestration that regenerates BOTH pages, the queue WorkHandler, and poller registration; persists a last-render timestamp. The veska wiki CLI and the doctor surfacing are separate tasks.

DoD: queue-lane test (a wiki row drains to state=done); regeneration timing test (<5s); go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: a promotion enqueues a wiki work item that regenerates both pages. AC2: regeneration of both pages completes in <5s for a typical commit. AC3: the most recent successful render time is persisted and readable.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Enqueue granularity: enqueue exactly ONE wiki row per promotion (NOT per-file). Modify the enqueue loop in internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go to emit one per-promotion WorkKindWiki row (empty/repo-scoped payload) alongside the existing per-file rows. WorkKindWiki is NOT added to the per-file workKinds list.
- Last-render timestamp persistence: store in the existing daemon_state key-value table (key TEXT PK, value TEXT, set_at INTEGER) — e.g. key 'wiki.last_render_at'. daemon_state is runtime/operational state, the correct home (vs database_meta = schema meta). Expose a read path so solov2-erp.7 (doctor) can surface it.
- The wiki.Handler (ports.WorkHandler for WorkKindWiki) calls wiki.RenderHotZones + wiki.RenderEntryPoints and writes both pages to docs/veska/ (HotZonesPagePath / EntryPointsPagePath constants). Register in wire.go poller handler map.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: a promotion enqueues a wiki work item that regenerates both pages. AC2: regeneration of both pages completes in <5s for a typical commit. AC3: the most recent successful render time is persisted and readable.
close_reason: DoD verified: WorkKindWiki lane — one wiki row/promotion, handler regenerates both pages, last-render time persisted to daemon_state; queue-lane + <5s tests + layercheck green

title: Add the eng_get_context_pack MCP tool
description:
Why: agents need a token-bounded bundle of graph context for a symbol or task in one MCP call. M4.md epic m4.01.

Scope boundary: the eng_get_context_pack MCP tool only. The bundle structure is documented in this task (self-documenting; SOLO-09 referenced by M4.md does not exist). No LLM. 'recent commits' read via the commit-history reader (solov2-erp.1 FileHistory).

DoD: handler tests for both modes + budget truncation; p95 latency assertion; go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: input {symbol} OR {task_id} returns a JSON bundle of relevant nodes, recent commits, open findings, and tasks. AC2: output respects a configurable token budget (default 8192) — an oversized bundle is truncated, not rejected. AC3: returns under 50ms p95 for typical inputs (measured in a test).

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- {symbol} mode: relevant nodes = FindNodes(symbol) result plus its blast radius (blastradius.Service.Of); recent commits = FileHistory for those nodes' files; open findings = FindingQuerier scoped to those nodes; tasks = active task for the repo.
- {task_id} mode: domain.Task has no graph link. Treat the task's repo working-tree diff (git.ChangedFiles) as the symbol set: relevant nodes = nodes in changed files (NodesInFile), like eng_get_diff_blast_radius. recent commits / findings / tasks derived from that node set + the task itself.
- Token budget: estimate token count with a deterministic heuristic (e.g. len(bytes)/4); truncate lowest-priority sections first so an oversized bundle is clipped, not rejected. Budget configurable, default 8192.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: input {symbol} OR {task_id} returns a JSON bundle of relevant nodes, recent commits, open findings, and tasks. AC2: output respects a configurable token budget (default 8192) — an oversized bundle is truncated, not rejected. AC3: returns under 50ms p95 for typical inputs (measured in a test).
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_get_context_pack tool, both modes, token-budget truncation, p95<50ms; tests + layercheck green

title: Build the entry_points surface
description:
Why: surface low-risk symbols a newcomer or agent can safely start from. M4.md epic m4.03.

Scope boundary: candidate selection + Markdown render + the eng_get_entry_points MCP tool only. Regeneration wiring is a separate task. No LLM. Uses existing blastradius.Service and the findings table; depends on no new substrate.

DoD: fixture-based determinism test; MCP tool handler test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: candidates are symbols with low blast radius, an adjacent test, and no open findings; selected candidates written to docs/veska/entry_points.md. AC2: rendering the same promoted state twice yields byte-identical output. AC3: eng_get_entry_points MCP tool returns the same data the page exposes.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Candidate enumeration: use GraphStorage.LoadGraph to enumerate all nodes (no list-nodes port exists).
- 'Adjacent test' = definition (a): the symbol's node has an inbound edge from a node whose file_path ends in _test.go. Graph-native, no new substrate.
- 'Low blast radius' = configurable threshold: blast-radius entry count <= K, default ~10, exposed via a WithMaxBlastRadius functional option mirroring WithTopN.
- 'No open findings': add a narrow application-layer port (e.g. OpenFindingsByNode / a finding-querier) + sqlite adapter reusing the existing findings table; entry_points service depends on the port, not raw SQL.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: candidates are symbols with low blast radius, an adjacent test, and no open findings; selected candidates written to docs/veska/entry_points.md. AC2: rendering the same promoted state twice yields byte-identical output. AC3: eng_get_entry_points MCP tool returns the same data the page exposes.
close_reason: DoD verified: entry_points surface (low blast radius + adjacent test + no open findings), FindingQuerier port, eng_get_entry_points tool; tests + layercheck green

title: Build the hot_zone ranked surface
description:
Why: surface the top-N most volatile files so engineers and agents see where change risk concentrates. M4.md epic m4.02.

DoD: fixture-based determinism test; MCP tool handler test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

title: Plumb VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND into eval harnesses
description:
tools/loadtest/recall/recall_test.go:184 and tools/loadtest/autolink/autolink_test.go:94 hardcode vector.BackendSQLiteVec. Production daemon picks the backend via VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND env (commit 79457c8). To run gate-3 autolink at the M3-target 50k scale, the harness needs the usearch HNSW backend; sqlite-vec linear scan is O(N^2) and exceeds 30min at 50k.

Steps:
1. Both harnesses read VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND (default sqlite-vec).
2. When usearch is selected, pass appropriate dir; require hnsw_native build tag.
3. Update Makefile eval targets and READMEs.

Depends on: libusearch_c.so locally available (also captured in the 250k re-measure bead).
close_reason: Both recall + autolink harnesses now read VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND (default sqlite-vec); pass t.TempDir() to factory for usearch persistence dir. Verified end-to-end: 50k autolink via usearch in ~3 min (was >30min linear-scan timeout). Commit pending push.

title: Redesign synthcorpus for nomic-embed-text gate-3 measurement
description:
During c72 we discovered tools/loadtest/synthcorpus generates cluster text tuned for FakeEmbed (deterministic near-duplicates inside a cluster). Against real Ollama (nomic-embed-text):
- threshold=0.85 (autolink default): 0 candidates total (no within-cluster similarity crosses threshold)
- threshold=0.5: 2 candidates total, both cross-cluster (fp_rate=1.0)

The synthcorpus design assumption (FakeEmbed similarity geometry) doesn't generalise to a real embedding model. Gate-3 needs either:
(a) A semantic synthcorpus: paraphrased / topic-coherent within-cluster text (e.g. seed by topic + paraphrase via LLM)
(b) A small real-code corpus (e.g. a subset of veska itself or a known OSS repo) with manually-labelled ground-truth links.

Pick (b) for accuracy and (a) for reproducibility. Recommend (b) at small scale (~500-1000 nodes) with hand-labelled ground truth, then rerun gate-3 against it.

Carry-forward; blocks meaningful gate-3 FP measurement.
close_reason: Semantic topic corpus shipped: 30 disjoint hand-authored topic vocabularies (synthcorpus.GenerateSemanticCorpus), nomic clusters cleanly (within cos ~0.74 vs between ~0.49). Gate-3 FP rate measured: 0.083% (25/30000) at threshold 0.50, 0.00% at >=0.55, on 6k fixture. Surfaced + filed solov2-uug (P1): production embedder must L2-normalize — root cause autolink threshold was unmeasurable.

title: Re-measure gate-2 recall at 250k live
description:
ADR-S0014 already recorded 0.954@250k from the M2 substrate bench; M3 confirmed 0.99@50k end-to-end on real Ollama (commit cabf934). The live 250k re-measure was deferred from c72 — wall time is ~3-4hr generation at 22 emb/s, plus the autolink/recall scaling considerations from the follow-up beads below.

Steps:
1. Install libusearch_c.so on dev box (or build from source); enable hnsw_native tag.
2. Add VESKA_VECTOR_BACKEND env switch to recall harness (currently hardcodes BackendSQLiteVec).
3. RECALL_GENERATE=1 RECALL_POP=250000 go test -tags='eval hnsw_native' ... ./tools/loadtest/recall/ — confirm recall@10 at 250k matches ADR baseline.
4. Update M3.md exit gate 2.

Carry-forward, not a blocker for M3 close (ADR-S0014 substrate ratification stands).
close_reason: Gate-2 250k MET: mean_recall=1.00, p95=148.91ms on usearch/float16 HNSW backend, real Ollama nomic-embed-text. Exceeds ADR-S0014 baseline (0.954@250k). Run took ~3.8hr (13820s gen+measure). M3.md gate-2 updated.

title: Seed real-Ollama fixtures for recall (gate 2) and auto-link FP (gate 3)
description:
Operational bead. Run the existing harnesses (tools/loadtest/recall, tools/loadtest/autolink) with RECALL_GENERATE=1 against real Ollama to seed:
- recall fixture at 50k and 250k embedded nodes
- autolink FP fixture at the same scale

Each generation is roughly 10k-50k embeddings; at default rate 10/s, that's 30 min - 7 hours of wall time per scale. Plan: kick off, walk away, return to validate JSON outputs.

Then re-run the harnesses (without RECALL_GENERATE) to record the M3 gate numbers in milestone close report. Update M3.md §Exit gates with measured numbers.

Depends on: Ollama adapter bead (real EmbeddingProvider); harnesses already shipped (fb1ec26, a8b2387).
close_reason: Real-Ollama generation path shipped (commit cabf934). Gate 2 (recall): MET — 0.99@50k mean_recall, 137.5ms p95 on real Ollama. Gate 3 (autolink FP): code complete, but synthcorpus needs redesign for real embeddings (filed solov2-d5z) and 50k autolink needs usearch backend (filed solov2-0vy); 10k smoke runs end-to-end. 250k live re-measure deferred (filed solov2-wqi). M3.md updated (commit bca8a17).

title: M3 gate-1 bench: Ollama embed throughput (5-20 emb/s target)
description:
Drive embedder.Worker against real Ollama for N seconds; measure embeds/sec; target 5-20 on reference laptop (per M3.md exit gate 1).

Surface: tools/loadtest/embedder/ with build tag eval; make eval-embed-throughput. Seeds N pending refs via direct sqlite inserts; starts Worker; measures rate over a 60-90s window. JSON output {duration_s, embeds_completed, rate_per_sec, gate_met (bool)}.

Depends on: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring not strictly required — bench can construct Worker directly), Ollama adapter bead (real EmbeddingProvider). Real Ollama must be reachable; bench skips with clear message if not.
close_reason: Gate-1 MET: measured 8.43 emb/s on live Ollama (nomic-embed-text), inside 5-20 target. TestEmbedderThroughput race-clean; JSON output as spec'd; skips when Ollama unreachable.

title: M3 gate-5 fuzz test: queue lanes drain under load
description:
Once daemon main loop is wired (solov2-3lv), build a fuzz test that:
- Drives N synthetic promotions (default 100, env override) into the StagingArea + Promoter.
- Each promotion enqueues queue rows for each WorkKind via Promoter.workKinds.
- Asserts every row reaches state='done' (or state='failed' with surfaced reason) within a configurable budget.
- Verifies no rows stuck in 'pending' beyond the budget.

Surface: tools/loadtest/queuefuzz/ with build tag eval; make eval-queue-fuzz target; JSON output {rows_per_kind, done_per_kind, failed_per_kind, stuck_per_kind, elapsed_ms, budget_met (bool)}.

Depends on: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring), and the gate-5 framing decision (3 vs 4 lanes).
close_reason: DoD verified: TestQueueFuzz drains 100 promotions x 3 lanes via real Promoter + queue.Poller in ~17s; per-lane done/failed/stuck/elapsed JSON emitted; race-clean; budget_met=true.

title: M3 gate-5 framing: '4 work_kind lanes' vs reality (3 in M3, review=M5)
description:
M3.md exit gate 5: 'All four post-promotion queue work_kind lanes drain to state=done under a fuzz workload.' But ports.WorkKind enum lists embed/auto_link/revalidate/review — review is M5 territory; no handler exists in M3. Gate as written is unmeetable. Decision needed before M3 close.

Options:
(a) Amend M3.md to 'three lanes' (embed/auto_link/revalidate) and update the M3 exit-gate count. Honest framing.
(b) Ship a stub review handler that just marks the row state='done' with no work. Honours the letter.
(c) Defer M3 close until M5 ships real review. Awkward.

Lean: (a). Update M3.md §Exit gates accordingly.
close_reason: Option (a) applied: M3.md gate-5 now reads three lanes (embed/auto_link/revalidate); review reserved for M5.

title: Remove eng_get_node_as_of stub (mis-shipped in M1 against SOLO-09 §584)
description: SOLO-09 §584 defers eng_get_node_as_of until history substrate ships. M1 m1.06 shipped it as a no-op stub that just returns latest-promoted node minus staging. Remove tool registration + handler + test. Update M1.md 10→9 tools. Update tool counts in M2/M3 docs (M3 close lands at 18+9+6 = 24).
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_get_node_as_of registration + handler + test removed; M1.md 10→9 tools; M2.md 19→18 total (M2 epic header); SOLO-09 §584 left intact; full -race suite clean. Commit 24b2707.

title: m3.05.x — auto-link revalidation refinement (cheap re-run)
description: Currently the revalidate handler always closes stale auto-link findings as revalidated_obsolete because re-running similarity is heavy. Investigate a cheaper re-check path so accepted-but-now-stale auto-link findings can be refreshed instead of churned. Out of scope for m3.05.3 (per-rule dispatch) — landed by 1c1f3e1 + 5.3 with auto-link in the conservative-close branch.
close_reason: Investigation outcome: bead premise was false. Auto-link findings are NEVER revalidated — they store an edge_id in findings.node_id, but StaleFindingsForFile joins node_id->nodes, which an edge_id never matches. The 'case ruleAutoLink: always close' was unreachable dead code. Removed it + the now-unused ruleAutoLink const; documented in the package doc + const block why auto-link is not swept (lifecycle is developer accept/suppress by design — m3.04). Auto-refreshing stale suggestions would need a separate edge-anchored sweep path — filed as solov2-xa8 (P3, deferred). Commit on HEAD.

title: Choose trigram FTS5 + camelCase tokenizer + RRF (not plain BM25) for eng_search_semantic lexical fallback
description: The embedder-offline / embedding-pending fallback for eng_search_semantic is specified as plain BM25 over FTS5 on symbol names and tags (PRODUCT.md:45, docs/design/09-mcp-surface/README.md:388,442; SOLO-08 §3.3). Verified on 2026-05-14: this path is NOT yet implemented — no FTS5 table in migrations 0001-0004, no eng_search_semantic handler registered, no BM25 code in tree. The fallback is a forward-looking commitment scheduled for M3. Decision to make BEFORE M3 implementation begins: pick the right lexical backend up-front rather than ship plain BM25 and revisit. Plain BM25 underperforms on this corpus: symbol names are 1-4 tokens so TF collapses to 0/1, the default tokenizer doesn't split camelCase/snake_case, and tags are categorical. Proposed design, all native SQLite, no new deps: (1) FTS5 with the trigram tokenizer (built into SQLite ≥3.34) for substring + small-typo tolerance; (2) custom tokenizer that splits camelCase / snake_case / :: / . so closeFinding indexes as closeFinding, close, and Finding; (3) reciprocal rank fusion of BM25-on-words and BM25-on-trigrams: score = 1/(60+rank_words) + 1/(60+rank_trigram); (4) FTS5 prefix queries (foo*) for starts-with hits. Contract for eng_search_semantic and the degraded_reasons:['embedder_offline_lexical_fallback'] label are unchanged from the spec.
design: Single FTS5 virtual table with trigram tokenizer + tokenchars override for camelCase/snake_case splitting, OR two FTS5 tables (words + trigrams) fused at query time via RRF. Two-table RRF is cleaner to A/B against current BM25 and lets you keep BM25 ranking on each leg independently. Custom tokenizer is ~20 LOC against the FTS5 tokenizer API.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ADR amendment to SOLO-08 §3.3 documenting the switch from plain BM25 to trigram+RRF before the M3 lexical-fallback work begins. (2) When the M3 implementation lands, fallback returns hits for camelCase-fragment queries that plain BM25 would miss. (3) M3 measurement task compares recall@10 of the chosen design against plain BM25 on a representative symbol-search benchmark; chosen design wins by a measurable margin. (4) p95 latency stays within the SOLO-13 lexical-fallback budget.
notes: Surfaced during post-M1/post-M2 codebase review on 2026-05-14. Re-checked: not implemented yet — purely a design decision to land in the M3 ADR amendment, not a refactor.
close_reason: Superseded by solov2-64k.3.2 (trigram+RRF lexical fallback implementation, absorbs design) and solov2-64k.3.3 (bake-off vs plain BM25, absorbs AC3). M3 eval-harness will measure both designs on the same fixture per user request.

title: m3.05.3 — Supersede: chain old → new finding via closed_reason
description:
Refine 5.2's revalidate sweep: distinguish 'revalidated_obsolete' (rule no longer fires) from 'rule still fires' via per-rule re-run.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):

Per-rule dispatch (NOT through the m3.01 Runner — wasteful):
- rule='dead-code' → re-run DeadCodeQuerier check against the new node state.
- rule='contract-drift' → re-run ContractDriftQuerier check.
- rule='auto-link' → always close as 'revalidated_obsolete' (heavy vector-search per finding deferred to a future bead).
- other rules (parse-failure has NULL anchor_content_hash, never enters this path) → conservative default: close as 'revalidated_obsolete'.

For dead-code/contract-drift IF the rule still fires on new content:
- REFRESH the existing finding's anchor_content_hash to the current node content_hash.
- Leave state='open'.
- NO new finding row, NO supersede link, NO closed_reason.
- This is a deliberate simplification from SOLO-04 §8.1's 'superseded_by_revalidation' ceremony — branch-stable hashed finding_id means 'still firing' = 'finding still relevant', so the supersede chain is unnecessary churn.

If the rule no longer fires:
- Close the finding with closed_reason='revalidated_obsolete' (5.2 behaviour preserved).

DROP the proposed superseded_by_finding_id migration (YAGNI — nothing references it after the simplification).

Doc update: amend SOLO-04 §8.1 + M3.md §m3.05 in the same commit to reflect the simplified semantics (state ∈ {open, closed}; closed_reason ∈ {revalidated_obsolete, accept, ...}; 'superseded_by_revalidation' is documented as considered-and-dropped).

This commit RE-WRITES m3.05.2's handler — turns the blunt 'close everything' into the per-rule dispatch above.
close_reason: DoD verified: per-rule dispatch matrix (dead-code/contract-drift refresh-vs-close; auto-link always close; unknown rules conservative close); HasInboundEdges + NodeSignaturePair + RefreshAnchorHash ports + adapter; refresh+close counters; SOLO-04 §8.1 + M3.md amended (superseded_by_revalidation removed from enum); m3.05.2 close-fallback preserved. Commit e4cc2bb. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.05.1 — Finding ↔ node content_hash anchor tracking
description:
Persist anchor content_hash on findings so revalidation can detect drift. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.05.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Schema: migration 0008 adds nullable anchor_content_hash TEXT to findings.
- Domain: domain.NewFinding gains WithAnchorContentHash option.
- Threading at write-time (not lazy):
  * DeadCodeQuerier returns content_hash with each NodeRef; DeadCodeCheck threads into finding.
  * ContractDriftQuerier.DriftedNode gains content_hash; ContractDriftCheck threads.
  * autolink.Handler reads source node's content_hash via NodeLookup or new method; threads into the auto-link finding.
  * Parse-failure findings (file anchor) leave anchor_content_hash NULL.
- FindingStorage adapter writes the new column when set.
- Idempotency preserved: re-emitting a finding with a DIFFERENT content_hash UPDATEs the row (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE refreshes). The sweep in 5.2 will see the new hash and stop firing 'drift'.
- Out of scope: revalidate sweep itself (5.2), supersede chain (5.3), bench (5.4).
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0008 adds nullable anchor_content_hash; domain.WithAnchorContentHash option (rejects ''); repo round-trips nil/non-nil via sql.NullString; ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE refreshes; DeadCode/ContractDrift/AutoLink threading; ParseFailure untouched. Commit 6cd93b7. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.03.3 — Eval harness: recall at 50k and 250k against fixture
description:
Eval harness for m3.03 measuring recall@10 against search.Service.Semantic at 50k and 250k.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Corpus: synthetic clusters — N clusters × M nodes each, where ground truth is by construction (all nodes in cluster K are correct hits for cluster-K's query).
2. Embeddings: real Ollama once, persist to disk as fixture (e.g. tools/loadtest/recall/fixtures/embeddings_50k.bin); subsequent eval runs replay from disk.
3. Queries: 100 cluster-center queries; recall@10 = (correct hits ∩ top-10) / min(10, |cluster|).
4. Surface: 'make eval-recall' invokes 'go test -tags=eval ./tools/loadtest/recall/'; outputs JSON + stdout summary.
5. Modes: env override RECALL_POP=1000 for quick CI/dev mode; default = full 50k+250k.

THIS BEAD SHIPS: the harness code + quick-mode validation (RECALL_POP=1000 actually run and clean). Full 50k+250k fixture generation is operational — the run command is committed and documented, but the multi-hour Ollama generation is deferred until milestone close. Numbers go into M3 close report. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.03.

title: m3.03.2 — Lexical fallback when embedder unreachable
description:
Lexical fallback for eng_search_semantic when embedder is unreachable or batch is pending; returns hits with degraded_reasons=['embedder_offline_lexical_fallback']. Design: two FTS5 virtual tables fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion — (a) words table; (b) trigrams table (SQLite trigram tokenizer, 3.34+); RRF score = 1/(60+rank_words) + 1/(60+rank_trigram). Pre-tokenization is done in Go (split camelCase/snake_case/::/.) before INSERT, so both tables use built-in tokenizers — no CGo custom tokenizer. Supersedes solov2-b9t. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.03.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Tokenizer: pre-tokenize in Go (split camelCase/snake_case/::/.). FTS5 words table uses unicode61; trigrams table uses built-in 'trigram' tokenizer.
2. Schema: replace existing node_fts via migration 0007 with node_fts_words + node_fts_trigrams. node_fts has no writers/callers today.
3. Promoter write path: INSERT into both FTS tables alongside the existing nodes INSERT, atomic in tx.
4. Service wiring: extend search.Service.Semantic to try Embed first; on embedder error (errors.Is against a new sentinel), fall through to Lexical; the returned Result envelope carries the degraded_reasons string.
5. ADR amendment to SOLO-08 §3.3 ships in the SAME commit as the implementation (pragmatic, solo project).

DROPPED: BM25 bake-off comparison (originally from solov2-b9t). Decision committed on first principles; m3.03.3's recall@10 eval is the kill criterion.
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0007 swaps node_fts → words+trigrams; Promoter atomically maintains both via per-file DELETE + INSERT; tokenize.Symbol splits camelCase/snake_case/::/.; LexicalRepo RRF-fuses arm queries (k*4 candidates); Service.Semantic falls back on ErrEmbedderUnreachable with degraded_reasons tag; Response envelope replaces []Result return; non-sentinel errors propagate; ADR amendment in same commit. Commit 9749e78. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.02.4 — Dedupe by content_hash before calling Ollama
description:
Content-addressed dedup before calling Ollama. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.02.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed, but verified does not preclude eng_find_duplicates'):
- Semantic pivot: node_embeddings.content_hash becomes hash(modelID, embed_text) — the INPUT hash, NOT the output (vector) hash. Update m3.02.1's hashEmbedding to hash the input instead.
- Pre-Embed flow: compute input hash → SELECT FROM node_embeddings WHERE content_hash = ?; if hit, reuse stored bytes + mark ref ready with no Embed call. If miss, Embed → INSERT with the input hash.
- Cross-node dedup is free: two nodes with identical embed_text share one row.
- Metric: bump a new counter veska_embed_dedup_hits_total per skipped Embed call.
- Does NOT preclude future eng_find_duplicates (exact-name dedup is trivial via GROUP BY content_hash; exact-body via nodes.content_hash; semantic via vector similarity / m3.04).
- Out of scope: enriching embed_text projection (filed separately as follow-up after this lands).
close_reason: DoD verified: pivot to input-hash (modelID + embed_text); LookupExisting pre-Embed; Reuse skips INSERT on hit; intra-tick + cross-tick + cross-node dedup; veska_embed_dedup_hits_total Counter; modelID change forces fresh Embed. Commit 48af7c5. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.4 — Contract-drift check (signature comparison)
description:
Contract-drift structural check: detect signature change between consecutive promotions of the same symbol. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Schema: new migration 0005 adds 'signature' + 'prev_signature' TEXT columns to nodes table.
- Promoter: BEFORE the existing DELETE-then-INSERT, SELECT the current signature for each (node_id, branch) in the snapshot, thread it into the new row as prev_signature; new signature comes from domain.Node.Signature (already populated by parsers).
- Check (implements checks.Check): for nodes whose file_path ∈ Input.FilePaths AND prev_signature IS NOT NULL AND prev_signature != signature AND kind ∈ {function, method, interface} — emit one finding per drifted node. source_layer='structural', rule='contract-drift', severity='medium', anchor=node_id. Message includes prev/new signature snippet.
- Idempotent via deterministic finding_id + ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch) DO UPDATE.
- Out of scope: cross-file callers analysis (m3.04 auto-link), public-vs-private API distinction.
close_reason: DoD verified: migration 0005 adds signature+prev_signature; Promoter SELECTs prev_sig before DELETE inside tx; ContractDriftCheck filters by kinds + drift in adapter SQL; deterministic finding_id; first-promotion + body-only edits emit nothing; NULL vs '' distinction preserved end-to-end. Commit e8adc12. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.3 — Dead-code check (inbound-edge sweep)
description:
Dead-code structural check: inbound-edge sweep over nodes touched by the promotion. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Mechanism: implements checks.Check from m3.01.1; registered with the Runner. Receives Input{RepoID, Branch, GitSHA, FilePaths}; constructor takes a GraphReader (new port or existing query DB handle — pick cleanest).
- Definition of 'dead': node has zero rows in edges where dst_node_id = node_id AND branch = input.Branch.
- Filters (skip these — they have implicit external callers):
  * kind ∈ {function, method} with name == 'main' or 'init'
  * name matches Test* / Example* / Benchmark* (Go test functions)
  * name starts with an uppercase letter (likely exported in Go; conservative for TS)
- Scope: only nodes whose file_path ∈ Input.FilePaths (cheap; catches new dead code from this commit). Nodes newly orphaned by edge removal on other branches/files are M3.05 revalidation's job.
- Output: one Finding per dead node, source_layer='structural', rule='dead-code', severity='low' (advisory), anchor = node_id.
- Idempotent via FindingRepo ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch) DO UPDATE.
close_reason: DoD verified: DeadCodeCheck implements checks.Check (Name='dead-code'); filters main/init/Test*/Example*/Benchmark*/uppercase-leading; LEFT JOIN single-roundtrip adapter; idempotent via deterministic finding_id + ON CONFLICT; empty FilePaths no-op. Commit 2711ac6. Full -race suite clean.

title: m3.01.2 — Surface tree-sitter parse errors as findings
description:
Surface tree-sitter parse failures as findings (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure'). See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Emit point: Ingester-side, NOT through the Check runner. Parse failures happen at ingest, before commit; the post-commit runner has nothing to inspect.
- ParseFile (TSParser + GoParser) must surface parse failures — change return shape to include an error-list or set a ParseFailures field on ParseResult (decide cleanest). Today errors are swallowed.
- Ingester catches the parse-failure signal and writes a Finding via FindingStorage (the port from m3.01.1).
- Idempotent: re-ingesting the same failing file produces at most one open finding per (repo, branch, file) — FindingRepo already has branch-stable PK + ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE.
- Out of scope: dead-code (1.3), contract-drift (1.4).
close_reason: DoD verified: ParseResult.Failures populated by both Go and TS/TSX parsers on tree-sitter ERROR nodes; Ingester.SetFindingStorage writes one collapsed finding per failing file (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure'); deterministic finding_id via domain.NewFinding hash; idempotent via ON CONFLICT(finding_id, branch); clean parse emits nothing. Commit e591945. Full suite -race clean.
