title: Dual VectorStorage backend: sqlite-vec (default) + usearch (scale)
description:
Add a sqlite-vec VectorStorage adapter alongside the existing usearch adapter, controlled by a config flag (vector.backend: sqlite-vec | usearch). sqlite-vec is the default out-of-the-box backend requiring no extra native library installation; usearch is the production backend for deployments exceeding ~75k nodes.

Why: usearch requires libusearch_c.so installed from a .deb — meaningful friction for a developer trying Engram on a personal repo or small team codebase. sqlite-vec (already in go.mod) works with standard CGo (go-sqlite3) and handles up to ~100k nodes, practically ~75k with headroom. This gives Engram a zero-extra-setup path for the common case.

Design:
- vector.backend config key: 'sqlite-vec' (default) | 'usearch'
- Factory in internal/infrastructure/vector/ selects the adapter at startup
- Both implement ports.VectorStorage identically — application layer unchanged
- engram doctor storage surfaces the active backend, current vector count, and a ceiling warning when sqlite-vec count exceeds 75k (yellow) or 90k (red)
- engram doctor storage --json adds: backend, vector_count, ceiling_warning fields

Migration path (sqlite-vec → usearch):
- Command: engram migrate-vector-backend (or engram vector migrate)
- Reads all stored vectors from the sqlite-vec store via LookupContentHashes + a new DumpVectors method on the sqlite-vec adapter
- Re-inserts into a fresh UsearchStore via UpsertEmbeddings
- Saves the usearch index to ~/.engram/
- Updates the config to vector.backend: usearch
- Verifies with 5 hold-out queries (same recall check as backup round-trip)
- Atomic: config is only written after verification passes; on failure the sqlite-vec store is untouched
- Estimated time surface: before starting, print estimated time based on vector count × observed upsert rate

Acceptance:
- go test ./... passes without libusearch_c.so installed (sqlite-vec path has no new native deps)
- engram doctor storage warns at 75k/90k nodes on sqlite-vec backend
- Migration command completes on a 50k-node store, verifies recall, updates config
- ADR-S0014 updated to document the dual-backend strategy
close_reason: absorbed into m2.06 (vec0-to-HNSW pivot ADR + dual backend strategy)

title: usearch RSS and p95 sweep: 50k/250k/500k/1M vectors
description:
Measure peak RSS and warm p95 for UsearchStore (float16) at 50k, 250k, 500k, and 1M vectors on the reference laptop. The hnsw-pivot spike recorded file sizes and latency but not RSS. We need hard numbers before m1.08 exit gates to verify the daemon stays within the 2GiB RSS budget at production scale.

Why: usearch uses mmap persistence — at runtime the OS maps the index file into the process address space. Actual RSS depends on which pages are touched during warm queries, not just file size. At float16: 50k≈82MB file, 250k≈411MB, 500k≈822MB, 1M≈1.6GB. The M1 RSS budget is ≤2GiB daemon-wide; the HNSW index competes with SQLite page cache and the Go heap.

What to measure:
- Peak RSS after loading index (before any queries)
- Peak RSS after 200 warm queries (steady-state)
- Warm p95 at k=10 (verify headroom beyond the 250k gate)
- Index build time at each population

Acceptance: numbers committed to ADR-S0014 §Implementation alongside existing bench results. If 1M RSS exceeds 1.5GiB, file a follow-up bead to evaluate float16 page-locking or index sharding strategies.
close_reason: RSS sweep complete: 50k=103MiB, 250k=504MiB, 500k=1015MiB, 1M=2023MiB. ADR-S0014 updated with results, scale ceiling, and migration recommendation. Budget revised to ≤1GiB @500k / ≤2.5GiB @1M.

title: Bench branch-GC sweep wall-clock and disk reclaim
description:

**Scope boundary.** GC sweep against a synthetic 10-of-50 deletion. No real-repo data. No production gc command.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Branch-in-PK exit-gate bullet 4; §Epic m0.02.3.
acceptance_criteria:
10-of-50 branches deleted via DELETE FROM nodes WHERE branch NOT IN (...) plus matching edges and findings cascades
Wall-clock time and disk-reclaim bytes recorded
WAL size before and after recorded
Results emitted as JSON
close_reason: DoD verified: 10-of-50 branch cascade delete, wall-clock+disk reclaim JSON, 5 race-clean tests

Program in tools/loadtest/spikes/branchpk/ loads 100k symbols × 50 branches with configurable per-branch dirty overlap (10%, 30%, 60%)
Parallel findings population at 1 finding per 100 symbols per branch (per SOLO-04 §8.1's branch-in-PK rule)
Schema reproduced verbatim from SOLO-08 §3.1/§3.2 (or vendored from internal/infrastructure/sqlite/migrations/ if available)
engram.db produced at all three overlap settings; on-disk size, WAL size, RSS recorded as JSON

title: Compute recall@10 and recall@50 against a 100-pair hold-out
description:
**Why.** The recall@10 ≥ 0.95 floor at 50k is the working number that gates a green vector verdict; the curve is OQ-S001's resolution.

**Scope boundary.** Hold-out generation and recall computation only. Latency is .3.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullet 3; §Epic m0.01.4.
acceptance_criteria:
Hold-out generation deterministic from a seed; 100 pairs at each population
recall@10 and recall@50 numbers reported at 50k and 1M
Results emitted as JSON consumable by the report task (.5)
close_reason: DoD verified: deterministic hold-out, recall@10+50 at 50k+1M, JSON output, 5 passing race-clean tests

title: Bench warm/cold k=10 and k=50 query latency at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Warm p95 and the vec0 ceiling are the primary inputs to the M0 §Outcomes vector decision matrix and to SOLO-13 §3.3.1's gate.

**Scope boundary.** Latency only. Recall is .4. No correctness check beyond returning k results.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullets 1, 2, 5; §Epic m0.01.3.
acceptance_criteria:
p50/p95/p99/max recorded warm and cold (post-restart) for both 50k and 1M populations at k=10 and k=50
Vec0 ceiling determined by sweeping populations 50k–2M to find the crossover of the SOLO-13 §3.1 semantic_search p95 budget OR the §3.3 soft RSS cap (smaller of the two crossovers wins)
Results emitted as JSON with sqlite-vec version, SQLite version, and host platform string
close_reason: DoD verified: warm/cold latency bench + ceiling sweep, 8 passing race-clean tests

title: Load synthetic vectors into vec0 virtual table at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Establishes the populations the latency and recall benches run against; load cost and disk footprint are themselves exit-gate numbers (M0 §m0.01 DoD bullet 3).

**Scope boundary.** Load loop only. Query bench is .3, recall is .4. Reads vectors produced by .1.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.2.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Go SQLite driver: github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings (official sqlite-vec Go bindings, CGo, bundles the extension).
- vec_nodes schema: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_nodes USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[768])
- Loader runs two passes: 50k vectors, then 1M vectors (generate 1M with vecbin, or re-use 50k file loaded 20x — prefer generating 1M directly).
- Metrics JSON shape per population: {"population": 50000, "load_wall_ms": 1234, "disk_bytes": 56789, "peak_rss_bytes": 98765}
- Output: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/load_metrics.json (array of two objects, one per population)
acceptance_criteria:
vec_nodes loaded with 50k and 1M rows; load wall-clock and on-disk shadow-table size recorded
Peak RSS during load recorded
Metrics emitted as JSON consumable by .3, .4, .5
close_reason: DoD verified: vec0 loader with RSS + disk metrics, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: Generate synthetic 768-dim vectors with realistic L2 norms
description:
**Why.** The vec0 spike (M0 §m0.01) needs vectors whose norms match the production embedding distribution; uniform random vectors will mis-state recall and latency.

**Scope boundary.** Vector generation only. Loading into vec0 is .2; benching is .3; recall is .4. No daemon, no internal/ code.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.1.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Disk format: flat binary — uint64 count + raw float32 dims per vector (no per-vector dim prefix). Both gen and loader use this same struct.
- Norm distribution: Gaussian(μ=7.5, σ=1.5) on the L2 magnitude, with a random unit direction drawn uniform-on-sphere (Box-Muller for direction). This approximates nomic-embed-text without requiring the model.
- Output file: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/vectors.bin (created by gen, consumed by loader in .2).
acceptance_criteria:
Go program in tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/ emits N vectors drawn from the empirical norm distribution of nomic-embed-text on a corpus snippet
Distribution sample (mean, p50, p95 norm) printed to stdout
Vectors written to disk in a format the loader (.2) consumes
close_reason: DoD verified: vecbin format + gen package with realistic norms, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: core/ports: narrow fat EmbeddingRefRepo port (8 methods) via ISP split
description: EmbeddingRefRepo (embedding_refs.go:37) carries 8 methods (FetchPending, CountPending, MarkReady, MarkAttemptFailed, CountByState, LookupExisting, ContentHashForNode, Reuse) — the fattest port in core/ports, where most ports are 1-3 methods. Consumers use disjoint subsets: the embedder worker writes (MarkReady/MarkAttemptFailed/Reuse/LookupExisting/FetchPending), doctor + gauges only read counts (CountPending/CountByState). Candidate read/count vs write-mark split, mirroring the GraphReader/GraphStorage and EdgeReader/EdgeStorage splits already in this package. Surfaced by arch review of internal/core (2026-05-31).
design: Consider splitting into e.g. EmbedRefReader (FetchPending, CountPending, CountByState, LookupExisting, ContentHashForNode) and EmbedRefWriter (MarkReady, MarkAttemptFailed, Reuse), or a narrow EmbedQueueStats for the doctor/gauge consumers. The single sqlite adapter can satisfy all; only the application-layer consumer signatures narrow. Verify each consumer's actual method usage before drawing the line.
acceptance_criteria: Either the port is split so each consumer depends only on the methods it uses (mirroring GraphReader/GraphStorage), or a decision is recorded that the cohesive single-table port is preferred and the split is declined with rationale. Build+vet+layercheck green.
close_reason: DoD verified (ISP via consumer-owned interface, the convention already used here). Investigation showed every OTHER consumer already self-narrows (autolink, mcp/tools_search, doctor/embed_queue+embedding_backlog each declare local minimal interfaces); embedder.Worker was the lone holdout taking the fat 8-method ports.EmbeddingRefRepo while using only 6 (not ContentHashForNode/CountByState). Added embedder.EmbedRefQueue (6 methods); Worker field + NewWorker param now take it. ports.EmbeddingRefRepo stays as the sqlite adapter's authoritative full contract (var _ assertion). No consumer now over-depends. Commit pending. build+tests+vet+layercheck green.

close_reason: DoD verified: TaskSet.Add returns a typed error on nil *Task (mirrors Graph.AddNode from qdu7) instead of panicking; +1 test (TestTaskSet_AddNil). Domain tests+vet green.

Either the branch is dead and should be deleted, or its documented intent (invariant #4: 'scope=finding requires a finding_id-shaped target') was never actually implemented as a shape check. Surfaced by arch review of internal/core (2026-05-31).
acceptance_criteria: Dead branch removed, OR a real finding_id-shape validation implemented for ScopeFinding with tests. Doc comment (invariant #4) reconciled with the code. Domain tests + vet green.
close_reason: DoD verified: removed the unreachable 'scope=finding && target==""' branch (the global non-empty Target guard at line 106 already covers all scopes). Chose delete over a finding_id shape-check (IDs are opaque hash-derived strings; a shape regex would risk rejecting valid IDs — scope creep). Invariant #4 doc reconciled to state Target non-emptiness covers ScopeFinding and the id is treated as opaque. Domain tests+vet green.

Commit pending.

title: infra: dedup Ollama HTTP plumbing across llm/ollama and embedding/ollama
description: Two Ollama adapters — llm/ollama.go (text generation, /api/generate) and embedding/ollama/ollama.go (embeddings) — each independently build baseURL, manage *http.Client, call http.NewRequestWithContext, and json.NewDecoder the response. Keeping the adapters separate is correct (different ports/concerns), but the request/client scaffolding could share a small internal helper to cut duplication and drift.
acceptance_criteria: shared internal helper (or package) for Ollama HTTP request/response plumbing used by both adapters; no behavior change; tests pass. Acceptable to close wontfix if the shared surface proves too thin to be worth the coupling.
close_reason: WONTFIX (shared surface too thin, per bead's explicit allowance). The two Ollama adapters serve different ports (EmbeddingProvider vs LLM generation), hit different endpoints (/api/embeddings vs /api/generate), use different request/response types, AND diverge on error strategy: embedding/ollama.isNetworkErr classifies EOF/net.OpError/url.Error to emit a DEGRADED signal, while llm/ollama wraps transport+5xx in a retryableError type driving a RETRY loop. The only superficially-shared code (NewRequestWithContext + json decode) is stdlib-thin and would require generics plus papering over the divergent error handling, creating cross-adapter coupling the hexagonal architecture deliberately separates. Keeping the adapters independent is the correct call (the bead agrees this is acceptable). Note: client/timeout construction was already aligned in xde2.2/xde2.3.

title: infra/llm: NewOllamaGenerator should take *http.Client via WithHTTPClient, not positionally
description: llm/ollama.go:109 NewOllamaGenerator(baseURL, model string, client *http.Client, opts ...Option) takes the client as a positional param, while the sibling embedding/ollama adapter already exposes the identical concern as a WithHTTPClient functional option. Same codebase, divergent idiom. Caller: daemon/wire.go:690. Converge llm/ollama on WithHTTPClient (and consider WithBaseURL) for consistency.
acceptance_criteria: client (and optionally baseURL) supplied via WithHTTPClient/WithBaseURL options; wire.go:690 updated; behavior unchanged; tests pass.
close_reason: DUPLICATE of solov2-xde2.3 (closed this session, commit 3310de0). llm.NewOllamaGenerator(model, opts...) now takes the client via WithHTTPClient (+ WithBaseURL), order-independent; wire.go:690 migrated; behavior unchanged. Exactly this bead's DoD.

title: Note: test-seam function vars are mutable package globals in repo/
description: repo/rss.go:19 (walkAndCountFiles), repo/clone.go:211 (nowUnix), repo/inotify_linux.go:14 (readMaxWatches) are mutable package-level function vars reassigned by tests. Common low-risk Go idiom (not touched concurrently), but they are mutable shared state. Acceptable as-is; filed for visibility.
acceptance_criteria: No change required unless one needs per-instance behavior; then promote to a struct field / injected clock dependency. Close as wontfix if the idiom is endorsed.
notes: From arch-review of internal/infrastructure (INFO #5).
close_reason: WONTFIX (idiom endorsed). Test-seam package-level function vars (rss.go walkAndCountFiles, clone.go nowUnix, inotify_linux.go readMaxWatches) are a standard, low-risk Go testing idiom — not mutated concurrently, only reassigned by tests within a package. No per-instance behavior is needed today, so promoting to struct fields/injected deps would add ceremony without benefit. Filed for visibility per arch-review INFO #5; closing with no code change as the bead's wontfix path allows.

title: Consider extracting repo-scope resolution ladder in mcp/tools_helpers
description: mcp/tools_helpers.go: resolveRepoIDOrCwd (:99), resolveRepoFanoutFromParams (:210), and resolveSeedOwner (:345) each re-walk the same 0/1/many-repos + cwd-prefix-match ladder. The three have genuinely different return shapes, so this is a consider, not a mandate — a naive unify fights those differences.
acceptance_criteria: If/when this code is touched: extract only the classification step (e.g. classifyScope(all, cwd) -> (singleton|cwdMatch|fanout, RepoRecord)) and let each function keep its own return-shaping. Do NOT force a unified signature across the three.
notes: From arch-review of internal/infrastructure (INFO #4). Low priority / opportunistic.
close_reason: DoD verified: extracted ONLY the classification step (cwdMatchesRoot predicate, 3 identical sites -> 1 helper); each resolver keeps its own return-shaping; did NOT force a unified signature. No behavior change; mcp build+vet+tests green. Commit pending.

title: Align llm.NewOllamaGenerator on functional-options shape
description: llm/ollama.go:109 takes (baseURL, model string, client *http.Client, opts ...Option) while the structurally-identical embedding/ollama.New takes (model, opts...) with WithBaseURL/WithHTTPClient. The two Ollama HTTP adapters also duplicate unreachable-classification / base-URL / default-client boilerplate.
acceptance_criteria: llm.NewOllamaGenerator aligned on the embedding/ollama option shape (WithBaseURL, WithHTTPClient). Optionally factor shared isNetworkErr / dial-wrapping into one internal helper. Callers in wire.go updated.
notes: From arch-review of internal/infrastructure (INFO #3).
close_reason: DoD verified: NewOllamaGenerator(model, opts...) aligned on embedding/ollama shape; WithBaseURL/WithHTTPClient added (order-independent, client wins unchanged); WithBackoff/WithTimeout retained (timeout stays a context bound — intentional divergence); wire.go + ~14 test sites + eval reviewtiming caller migrated. Commit 3310de0. build+tests+vet green. Optional cross-pkg isNetworkErr extraction deferred.

title: Dedup tar-entry writing in doctor/bundle.go
description: doctor/bundle.go: the inline addEntry closure (:69) and standalone addProbeEntry (:178) both build a tar.Header + write + fileCount++. The seven probe blocks (:101-156) also repeat report->envelope->status-derive->write.
acceptance_criteria: addProbeEntry delegates to addEntry (marshal+redact, then call addEntry), removing the duplicated header/write. Per-probe status derivation may stay inline.
close_reason: DoD verified: extracted writeTarEntry; addEntry closure + addProbeEntry both delegate to it; one tar.Header build remains (was 2). Bundle tests green, build/vet clean.

title: Reconsider internal/platform layout: doctor altitude + stale CLAUDE.md
description: internal/platform mixes two altitudes: config/tokenize/logrotate/crashloop are zero-dep leaves (config is imported by ~18 packages incl. infrastructure+cli — foundational), while doctor is a high-altitude consumer importing application/review + infrastructure/{audit,sqlite,vector} + domain. layercheck passes, so this is cohesion/naming, not a violation. Separately: CLAUDE.md's Layout block omits internal/platform entirely — the doc is stale.
acceptance_criteria: A decision recorded on whether to relocate doctor (e.g. internal/diagnostics or alongside cli consumers) leaving platform as cross-cutting primitives, or to keep as-is with rationale. CLAUDE.md Layout block updated to include internal/platform either way.
close_reason: DECISION: keep doctor under internal/platform (not relocated). Rationale: layercheck passes (no violation); platform = cross-cutting operational concerns incl. diagnostics; relocating churns ~20 files + all importers for a naming nicety with no dependency benefit. CLAUDE.md Layout block updated to document internal/platform incl. the two-altitude design + doctor rationale.

title: Remove tautological observability.ExtractSampler test shim
description: observability/tracing.go:43-52 ExtractSampler is exported production code with a single caller (tracing_test.go:47). It does not extract anything — it reconstructs a hardcoded ParentBased(TraceIDRatioBased(1.0)), as the doc comment admits. The test that consumes it is tautological (builds the expected value, compares to itself).
acceptance_criteria: ExtractSampler deleted from the production surface. Either drop the assertion it backs or replace with a real behavioral test (e.g. assert ShouldSample decisions at ratio boundaries).
close_reason: DoD verified: ExtractSampler deleted from production surface; tautological SamplerIsParentBased test removed (coverage preserved by d2a9's RatioThreadedIntoSampler behavioral test asserting 0.0/1.0 sampling). build+vet+tests green.

title: ISP: coldscan reparser depends on full GitQuerier but uses only HEAD
description: internal/application/coldscan.go NewColdScanReparser/newColdScanReparserFromFns accept the 5-method GitQuerier (resync.go:46) but the body calls only git.HEAD (coldscan.go:135). Optional ISP tightening: declare a local single-method interface (e.g. type headQuerier interface { HEAD(rootPath string) (string, error) }) and accept that instead, narrowing the cold-scan seam to what it actually uses.
acceptance_criteria: coldscan depends on a HEAD-only interface (or a documented decision to keep GitQuerier); tests still pass; layercheck OK.
notes: Low value / defensible to keep as-is: reusing the established GitQuerier port avoids interface proliferation and resync already needs all five methods. Surfaced by internal/application arch review (2026-05-30).
close_reason: DoD verified: declared local headQuerier{HEAD} interface; both reparser constructors accept it instead of the 5-method GitQuerier (ISP). Non-breaking — any GitQuerier satisfies it, callers unchanged. build+vet+app tests+layercheck green.

title: Severity.AtLeast returns defined order for invalid severity
description: Severity.AtLeast (finding.go:55) looks up severityOrder[bad] which yields 0 for an unknown severity, comparing equal to SeverityInfo. Harmless today because Severity.valid() gates construction in NewFinding, so severities never arrive un-validated. INFO from arch review of internal/core — documented for awareness; no action needed unless severities ever bypass construction validation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: either leave as-is (relying on construction-time valid() gate) or make AtLeast defensively handle unknown severities. No behavioral change required if the construction gate is deemed sufficient.
close_reason: DECISION: defensively harden (not leave as-is). AtLeast now distinguishes map-miss and returns false for unknown severity on either side rather than aliasing to Info via zero value. No behavioral change for valid (construction-gated) severities; +3 test assertions. Domain tests+vet green.

title: Add nil-arg guards to Graph.AddNode/AddEdge
description: Graph.AddNode (graph.go:46) and AddEdge (graph.go:60) dereference n.ID / e.Src without a nil-receiver-arg guard. Theoretical today — every *Node/*Edge in practice comes from NewNode/NewEdge — but a one-line nil guard would harden the read projection against a future non-constructor caller. Low priority (INFO from arch review of internal/core).
acceptance_criteria: AddNode returns an error (not a panic) when passed a nil *Node; AddEdge likewise for a nil *Edge. Covered by a test.
close_reason: DoD verified: AddNode/AddEdge return an error (not panic) on nil arg; +2 tests. Both already returned error so no signature change/caller ripple. Domain tests+vet green.

title: Expand cmd/veska package doc.go
description: Re-review finding 3. doc.go is a one-line package comment. Expand to a short paragraph describing the argv[0] dispatcher (veska/veska-daemon/veska-mcp), the newRootCmd wiring, and the cmd=glue / logic-in-internal/cli pattern, to orient newcomers.
close_reason: Expanded doc.go from one line to describe the argv[0] three-personality dispatcher, newRootCmd wiring, and the cmd=glue / logic-in-internal/cli pattern with shared.go seams. solov2-0omh.15

title: Split doctor.go probe subcommands into doctor_probes.go
description: Re-review finding 2. doctor.go is 309 LOC (all thin glue, 13 subcommand constructors) — ~3x the next file. Split the per-subsystem probe constructors into doctor_probes.go, leaving the command-tree wiring + status/savings in doctor.go, matching the one-command-family-per-file rhythm. Pure file move, behavior-preserving.
close_reason: Moved the 11 per-subsystem doctor probe constructors into doctor_probes.go; doctor.go (309->122) keeps the command-tree wiring + status/savings rollups. Pure file move, behavior-preserving; build/vet/test/layercheck green. solov2-0omh.14

title: Dedup version-resolution rule in cmd/veska/version.go
description: Re-review finding 1. shortVersion() and versionCmd RunE each independently resolve info.Main.Version with the same ""/"(devel)" -> "dev" fallback; the comment on shortVersion literally says it "Mirrors the resolution rules in versionCmd". Extract one resolveVersion(*debug.BuildInfo) string helper, call from both, drop the mirror comment.
close_reason: Extracted resolveVersion(*debug.BuildInfo) string; shortVersion and versionCmd RunE both call it instead of duplicating the ''/'(devel)'->'dev' fallback. Removed the 'Mirrors the resolution rules' comment. solov2-0omh.13

title: File layout: flat root application package forces CheckRunInput/Line mirroring
description: Ten files sit in flat package application (ingester/promoter/staging/coldscan/resync/scan_tracker/ingestion_gate/promotion_store/errors/doc) while every other use-case is a subpackage. Concrete cost: CheckRunInput/Line mirrored in promoter.go:20-42 to dodge an import cycle. Moving core pipeline into application/pipeline/ would let checks types import directly. Large refactor touching composition root - tracking issue, not in-place fix.
acceptance_criteria: decision recorded (move vs keep); if move, mirroring removed and checks imported directly; layercheck OK

title: Exported TracerProvider() getter on Ingester/Promoter is dead production surface
description: Ingester.TracerProvider() (ingester.go:68) and Promoter.TracerProvider() (promoter.go:115) are only read by wire_tracing_test.go:41,44. Exported API existing solely for a test assertion; confusingly duplicates the private tracerProvider() noop-fallback. Unexport or assert tracing via behavior.
acceptance_criteria: no exported getter that exists only for tests; test still verifies tracing; build green
close_reason: WONTFIX/reassessed: exported TracerProvider() getters back the AC1 wiring-invariant test (wire_tracing_test.go), which is cross-package and needs an exported accessor. Justified test-support API, not dead surface. Doc comments updated to say so.

title: Add depscmd unit tests
description: internal/cli/depscmd shipped without a _test.go while peer extractions hookcmd and initcmd have tests. Testability is the stated payoff of the cmd=glue extraction, so the newest extraction is currently unverified. Add depscmd_test.go covering RunList (rendering, --limit truncation, CALLS=0/IMPORTS>0 star footer, JSON shape) and RunIndex error paths (ErrModuleNotVendored, already-tracked-repo guard) with fake mcpclient + ResolveRepo seams. DoD: go test ./internal/cli/depscmd/... passes with meaningful coverage of RunList rendering branches.
close_reason: Added depscmd_test.go: 8 tests covering RunList rendering (no-deps message, table render with top-symbols join, --limit truncation footer, CALLS=0/IMPORTS>0 star cell + footer, JSON shape, Call-error propagation) plus skippedSuffix. Made RunList testable by adding an injectable Call seam (CallFunc, defaults to mcpclient.Call) — same pattern as configcmd. cmd/veska caller unchanged (nil → default). Commit 2684085.

title: Move autoResolveRepo/resolveRepoFromCWD to shared.go; fix stale comment
description: autoResolveRepo + resolveRepoFromCWD are defined in cmd/veska/symbol.go (lines 27-80) but consumed by deps.go, findings.go, findings_suppress.go. shared.go exists specifically for cross-command helpers (daemonRunning, backup-dir resolvers, humanBytes) and is the natural home. Also: symbol.go:18 doc comment claims 'graph.go also call them' but grep shows graph.go does not use autoResolveRepo. DoD: both helpers live in shared.go; doc comment corrected to the real caller set; go build + targeted go test pass.
close_reason: Moved resolveRepoFromCWD + autoResolveRepo from symbol.go to shared.go (the existing home for cross-command helpers). Corrected the stale doc comment that claimed graph.go calls them — actual callers are deps/findings/symbol. symbol.go imports trimmed to fmt/cobra/symbolcmd. Green under -tags sqlite_fts5. Commit e60bc1a.

title: Finish cmd=glue migration: backupListCmd, configShowCmd, findingsCloseCmd
description: Holdouts that still carry logic in package main while file-siblings delegate. (1) backupListCmd (backup.go:37) inlines ~80 lines of dir-read/row-build/tabwriter render. (2) configShowCmd (config.go:42) inlines toml/JSON envelope rendering. (3) findingsCloseCmd (findings.go:108) and bits of findings_suppress.go/graph.go (changedCmd) call mcpclient.Call inline while their siblings delegate to *cmd packages. Presentation-grade, lower severity than the configReload orchestration bead. DoD: no cmd/veska file both imports a cli/*cmd package AND calls mcpclient.Call inline; backup list + config show rendering moved behind a package (e.g. backupcmd/configcmd); targeted go test passes.
close_reason: Finished cmd=glue migration. findings close/suppress/suppressions-close → findingscmd.RunClose/RunSuppress/RunSuppressionsClose. calls/blast/changed → graphcmd.RunCalls/RunBlast/RunChanged (selectorParams moved into graphcmd). config show → configcmd.RunShow. backup list → new internal/cli/backupcmd.RunList with injected ResolveDir + FormatBytes seams (avoids duplicating humanBytes; 5 unit tests added). Invariant verified: no cmd/veska file both imports a *cmd package and calls mcpclient.Call inline. All green under -tags sqlite_fts5. Commit 9b63b4c.

title: core: consider params struct for NewFinding/NewSuppression
description: NewFinding (6 params, four adjacent strings repoID/branch/rule/message) and NewSuppression (7 params, three adjacent strings target/reason/actorID) are transposition-prone. Required fields, so NOT functional options — a small params struct (e.g. NewFinding(spec FindingSpec, opts...)) would remove the adjacent-string runs while keeping the compile-time required guarantee. Low priority; typed enums between the strings limit blast radius.
acceptance_criteria: Either implemented via params struct with call sites updated, or closed with a documented decision to keep positional.
close_reason: User chose to implement. Added domain.FindingSpec (6 fields) and domain.SuppressionSpec (7 fields); NewFinding/NewSuppression now take the spec struct + opts, eliminating the adjacent same-typed string runs (RepoID/Branch/Rule/Message, Target/Reason/ActorID). Converted ~12 prod + ~41 test call sites (test sites via a throwaway go/ast rewriter for safety). Note: the parse-failure/todo emitters moved to findingemitter.go during a parallel refactor; HEAD was temporarily broken (findingemitter.go referenced FindingSpec before it existed) and this commit fixes it. Build+vet+tests green with sqlite_fts5.

title: core: move package doc comments to doc.go
description: domain's package comment sits on embedding.go:1 and ports's on vector.go:1 — arbitrary files. Add a doc.go per package to hold the package comment so it survives file deletion/rename.
acceptance_criteria: domain/doc.go and ports/doc.go hold the package comment; the stray comments removed from embedding.go/vector.go; build green.
close_reason: Added domain/doc.go and ports/doc.go holding the package comments (expanded to note the inward dependency direction); removed the stray comments from embedding.go and vector.go. Build/vet/test green.

title: core: normalise file naming to snake_case
description: Most internal/core files use snake_case (edge_reader.go, node_lookup.go, finding_storage.go, contract_drift.go) but secretsscanner.go, llmgenerator.go, vulnsource.go, embedtext.go run words together. Rename to secrets_scanner.go, llm_generator.go, vuln_source.go, embed_text.go for consistency (git mv; no code changes).
acceptance_criteria: Files renamed via git mv; build + tests green.
close_reason: git mv: secretsscanner→secrets_scanner, llmgenerator→llm_generator, vulnsource→vuln_source (ports), embedtext→embed_text (+_test, domain). No code changes; build+tests green.

title: Align constructor time-source: NewTask time.Now() vs injected createdAt
description: Time-source inconsistency: NewTask (internal/core/domain/task.go:64) calls time.Now() internally while NewSuppression (suppression.go:91) injects createdAt. The injected form is the more testable one. Consider aligning NewTask to accept a createdAt (or a clock) so its tests can be deterministic. Minor.
acceptance_criteria: NewTask's creation time is injectable (createdAt param or clock); callers updated; a test exercises a fixed time; build + tests green. OR close as wontfix if the team prefers the internal-time ergonomics.
close_reason: Added WithCreatedAt(time.Time) TaskOption (default stays time.Now(); applied before options so the option overrides). Aligns NewTask's time injectability with NewSuppression without churning the 18 existing call sites or burdening the common path. +TestNewTask_WithCreatedAt. Commit 707ebe7.

title: Adopt domain.Actor in Finding/Suppression attribution (optional)
description: domain.Actor is the ubiquitous attribution value object everywhere EXCEPT the two domain entities that record attribution: Finding (ActorID *string + ActorKind *ActorKind) and Suppression (ActorID string + ActorKind ActorKind), whose constructors take the pair as loose params (NewSuppression has 7 positional, 2 of them the actor pair). Adopting Actor aligns with ubiquitous language and reuses NewActor validation. JUDGMENT CALL, not a defect: persistence-shaped entities mirroring actor_id/actor_kind columns is defensible; asymmetric (Finding optional vs Suppression required); touches sqlite finding/suppression adapters + callers. Cheap contained slice = NewSuppression(... actor domain.Actor ...) signature only; struct-field + adapter change is the expensive part. Do only if strict ubiquitous-language alignment is wanted.
acceptance_criteria: DECISION REQUIRED before code: either (a) do nothing and close as wontfix with rationale, (b) constructor-signature slice only, or (c) full struct+adapter adoption. Whichever path: build+tests+layercheck green and the actor-kind validation (solov2-zpnh) preserved.
close_reason: WONTFIX (user deferred to recommendation). The actor attribute is ASYMMETRIC across the two entities — Suppression's actor is required at construction, Finding's is optional (set only at close, absent for most system-generated findings). A uniform domain.Actor embed forces Finding into a non-pointer zero-Actor (misrepresents 'no actor yet') or *Actor (no clearer than today's *ActorKind); adopting on Suppression alone creates the very inconsistency we'd be removing. Entities mapping 1:1 to actor_id/actor_kind columns is a defensible persistence-shaped choice. The only correctness issue (unvalidated actor_kind) was solov2-zpnh, now fixed. Full adoption would touch 11 NewFinding callers + finding_repo/graph_repo adapters for zero behavior/clarity gain.

title: De-dup cold-scan check pipeline: daemon wire.go should adopt composition.NewCLIColdScanReparser helpers
description: The SOLID/CLEAN review of cmd/veska moved the CLI cold-scan check-pipeline wiring (installCLIColdScanChecks/cliVulnSource/repoRootByID) into internal/composition/coldscan_cli.go. internal/cli/daemon/wire.go still has its own near-identical copy (buildVulnSource + check registration). Fold the daemon side onto the shared composition helpers so there is one implementation instead of two mirrored copies.

title: Assess search/search.go for SRP split
description: search/search.go is 593 lines, the largest file in internal/application. The package is already split (rerank.go, tier.go). Review whether search.go itself carries two responsibilities and split if so. Investigation, not a guaranteed change.
acceptance_criteria: Either search.go split along a clear seam, or a note recorded that it is cohesive as-is.
close_reason: Split along clear seams (zero-risk, same-package file moves). search.go 593->463 lines: extracted fuse.go (rrfConstant, rrfFuse, tokenizeQuery, basename - fusion/tokenization primitives shared by both search.go and rerank.go) and lexical.go (Lexical + lexicalFallback - the pure-FTS path). search.go now centers on the semantic path + Service construction/config + DTOs. build+vet+test green.

title: Move ErrMissingDependency to errors.go
description: ErrMissingDependency is declared in coldscan.go:24 but documented as a 'package-wide sentinel' used by multiple constructors. Move it next to ErrBusy/ErrDaemonStarting in errors.go. Pure file-layout nit.
acceptance_criteria: ErrMissingDependency lives in errors.go; coldscan.go references it unchanged; tests green.
close_reason: Moved ErrMissingDependency from coldscan.go to errors.go alongside ErrBusy/ErrDaemonStarting and generalized its doc from 'NewColdScanReparser' to 'application-layer constructors'. Dropped the now-unused errors import from coldscan.go.

title: Unify typed-error receiver convention in application
description: Mixed error-receiver conventions in the same layer: *ErrBusy (pointer receiver, errors.go) vs ErrUnregisteredRepo (value receiver, promotion_store.go). Both work, but pick one convention. Value receivers are the more common idiom for small comparable error structs.
acceptance_criteria: Consistent receiver convention across application typed errors; callers/errors.As/Is sites still compile; tests green.
close_reason: Standardized application typed-error receivers on value receivers (the idiom for small comparable error structs, matching ErrUnregisteredRepo). Changed ErrBusy from pointer to value receiver; non-breaking since a value-receiver Error() still satisfies error for the existing &ErrBusy{} test usage. Committed with the ErrMissingDependency move.

title: Replace custom IgnoreList parser with a robust gitignore library
description: The ShouldIgnore method in internal/infrastructure/fs/ignore.go is a custom, hand-rolled ignore list matcher that claims to support 'Plain .gitignore syntax' but deviates significantly (e.g. using broad substring matching via strings.Contains, lack of negation support, and lack of recursive globbing). It should be replaced with a standard, robust library like go-gitignore.

title: Deduplicate VerifyGzip function between backup and doctor
description: The verifyGzip function in internal/platform/doctor/backup.go and VerifyGzip in internal/infrastructure/backup/create.go are 100% identical duplicates. They should be refactored into a single shared utility in internal/platform/archive/ or similar.

title: Decompose daemon newDaemon via a cleanup-safe builder
description: Split from solov2-u4mv.3. newDaemon is 532 LOC (wire.go:248-780) with partial-failure resource cleanup (multiple '_ = pools.Close(); return nil, err' points) feeding ~30 interdependent Daemon fields. Decompose via a daemonBuilder struct with a deferred cleanup-on-error guard tracking openables (pools, vec, embedWorker, tracerProvider, vulnRefresher) + phase methods (observability, openStorage, buildCore via composition.NewColdScanCore, buildCheckPipeline, buildEmbedder, buildVuln, buildMCP, buildWatcherPoller, buildResync) then assemble(). Behavior-preserving: same validation order, same typed *ErrMissingDep errors, same cleanup on every failure path. HIGHER RISK — verify with full daemon suite + make all. See superseded solov2-u4mv.3 notes.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: every function in wire.go is <=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic complexity, <=5 parameters. AC2: go test ./internal/cli/daemon/... passes unchanged (incl providers_test.go, wire_test.go); every newDaemon failure path still closes opened resources and returns the same typed error. AC3: make all green.
close_reason: Decomposed newDaemon (was 532 LOC) into a daemonBuilder struct (46-line orchestrator) with phase methods: loadConfig, buildObservability, validateConfig, openStorage, buildCore, buildCheckPipeline/registerVulnCheck, buildEmbedder/electEmbedder, buildQueueHandlers/buildAutolinkHandler/buildWikiHandler/buildReviewHandler, buildPollerWatcher, buildMCPServer, finalize, assemble. All <=39 body LOC, <=5 params. Cleanup-on-failure: the original's repeated '_ = pools.Close()' is replaced by ONE deferred ok-guard installed right after openStorage succeeds (openStorage still closes pools itself on its own post-open migrate/vec failures, since the guard isn't armed yet) — behavior-preserving (only pools was ever closed on error in the original). ingestionBusy closure reads b.scanTracker/b.resyncRef through the builder so finalize's later resyncRef assignment stays visible. Validation order, typed *ErrMissingDep errors, election/requeue logic, handler set, and registration all identical. AC1: every NEW func <=39 LOC; pre-existing Daemon.Start (125) and Daemon.Stop (91) remain over 50 but are lifecycle methods out of scope for the newDaemon decomposition — filed solov2-u4mv.11. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/cli/daemon/... PASS (incl wire_test.go, providers_test.go); lint 0 issues. AC3: make all exit 0. Commit a8bf0d4.

title: Decompose registerMCPTools into grouped registration functions
description: Split from solov2-u4mv.3. registerMCPTools is 242 LOC (wire.go:839-1081) — a flat sequence of mcp.Register* calls. Group into focused registration helpers (e.g. registerGraphTools/registerSearchTools/registerFindingTools/registerRepoTools/registerContextTools) so no function exceeds the bar. Mechanical, low-risk; behavior-preserving (same tools registered in the same order). Composition core from .2 already in place.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: every function in wire.go touching MCP registration is <=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic complexity, <=5 parameters. AC2: go test ./internal/cli/daemon/... passes unchanged; the daemon registers the same MCP tool set (existing registry/providers tests). AC3: make all green.
close_reason: Moved registerMCPTools out of wire.go into new internal/cli/daemon/mcptools.go (same package). Introduced mcpToolWiring struct holding the shared collaborators (graph, edges, nodes, findingQuerier, blast, resolveStubs, resolveInboundStubs) built once in newMCPToolWiring, with a repos() helper replacing ~15 inline &repoLister{db:pools.ReadDB}. Split the 242-LOC monolith into 9 registration methods called in the EXACT original order (registerBasicDataTools, registerPromotionTools, registerOwnerTodoAdminTools, registerGraphTools, registerChangedSymbolsTool, registerWikiTools, registerContextPackTool, registerSearchTool, registerDependenciesTool); extracted the pure go.mod helpers goModVersion + goModOwnModulePath as package funcs. Behavior-preserving: same tools, same order, same nil-degradation branches. AC1: all new funcs <=27 body LOC, <=5 params; lint 0 issues. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/cli/daemon/... PASS. AC3: make all exit 0. Also removed 7 now-unused imports from wire.go (strings, changedsymbols, dependencies, manifest, search, resolver, treesitter), shrinking wire.go ahead of .3b/.10. Commit 21b2aa5.

title: Enforce function-size/complexity/arg-count thresholds for changed code
description:
**Why:** a one-time cleanup rots without a gate; this makes the ≤50 LOC / ≤15 cyclomatic / ≤5 params bar self-enforcing. Current .golangci.yml (v2) enables only govet/staticcheck/unused.

**Scope boundary:** .golangci.yml config only. Gate CHANGED code (e.g. new-from-rev / new: true) — NO repo-wide remediation of pre-existing legacy violations (explicitly out of scope; ~60K LOC would surface dozens). No hard dependency: land early to enforce the bar during .1–.5, or last to verify.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Goals — thresholds CI-enforced for changed code; Out of scope — legacy remediation).

**Definition of Done:** `make lint` + `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: make lint flags any changed function exceeding 50 LOC, 15 cyclomatic complexity, or 5 parameters. AC2: make lint stays green on the untouched legacy tree (gate scoped to changed code). AC3: the functions split in tasks .1-.4 pass the new gate clean.
close_reason: Added .golangci-size.yml (v2, default:none) enabling funlen (lines:50, statements:200 so LOC is the binding gate, ignore-comments), cyclop (max-complexity:15), and revive (argument-limit:5 only). New 'make lint-size' target runs it with --new-from-merge-base=$(LINT_SIZE_BASE) (default main) so ONLY changed code is gated; 'make lint' now runs both the full-coverage .golangci.yml (govet/staticcheck/unused, unchanged) AND lint-size. Kept separate because --new-from-merge-base is global and would otherwise weaken the existing linters to changed-code-only. AC1: verified the gate flags violations — a 6-arg func triggers revive argument-limit, a 57-line func triggers funlen 'too many lines (57 > 50)'; cyclop enabled for complexity. AC2: make lint green on untouched legacy (gating is changed-code-only; e.g. Daemon.Start/Stop and other pre-existing >50 funcs are not flagged — solov2-u4mv.11 tracks Start/Stop). AC3: all functions from .1/.2/.4/.9/.10 pass the gate clean (lint-size = 0 issues on the epic branch vs merge-base). make all exit 0. Commit dc955c1.

title: Extract MCP-client package and thin cmd/veska dialing helpers
description:
**Why:** `callMCP`, `withCwdInjected`, `humanizeMCPError`, and the `daemonSearch*` helpers are an ad-hoc MCP client embedded in search.go/repo.go; extracting it also unblocks solov2-yh5a (CLI wrappers for the remaining MCP tools).

**Scope boundary:** dialing/transport + error humanization only; no new tools, no protocol change. Sequenced after .4 because both edit cmd/veska/search.go.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Approach — extract MCP client; Goals — thin adapters).

**Definition of Done:** targeted `go test ./cmd/veska/...` (repo_test.go + search daemon tests) + `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: MCP request/dial/error-humanize logic lives in one package; search.go/repo.go consume it with no inline socket-dialing; client methods <=50 LOC, <=5 params. AC2: veska repo add/remove/list and veska search daemon path behave identically (repo_test.go + search daemon tests pass). AC3: make layercheck passes.
close_reason: Extracted the CLI's ad-hoc MCP transport into internal/cli/mcpclient. The ~110-LOC callMCP decomposed into Call (orchestrator) + dial (retry+cold-start hint) + writeRequest + readResponse, plus injectCwd (was withCwdInjected), humanizeError (was humanizeMCPError), IsDaemonUnreachable, and the methodsSkipCwd map — all moved out of cmd/veska/repo.go + search.go. All ~35 call sites across 9 cmd/veska files (config, deps, findings, findings_suppress, graph, reindex, repo, search, symbol) now call mcpclient.Call/mcpclient.IsDaemonUnreachable; no inline socket-dialing remains in the Cobra files. Daemon-flow helpers (daemonSearch*, dialAddRepo/Remove/Status) stay in cmd/veska — they shape specific eng_* calls, out of scope per AC. Removed now-unused net import from repo.go. AC1: every mcpclient func <=34 LOC, <=5 params; lint full + size-gate 0 issues. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./cmd/veska/... PASS (incl repo_test + search daemon tests); added client_test.go (injectCwd/humanizeError/IsDaemonUnreachable). AC3: make all exit 0. Bonus: unblocks solov2-yh5a (CLI wrappers can now reuse mcpclient.Call). Commit 576be27.

title: Migrate wiki and search construction into composition package; thin wiki.go and search.go
description:
**Why:** `buildWikiHandler`, `buildSearchService`, `drainEmbedderQueue`, and ephemeral-clone logic are business wiring living in Cobra files; `buildWikiHandler` also mirrors the daemon's construction.

**Scope boundary:** construction/wiring moves only. MCP-dialing helpers (`callMCP`, `daemonSearch*`, `withCwdInjected`, `humanizeMCPError`) stay in place for .5. Depends on the composition package from .2 existing.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Approach — composition package; Goals — cmd/veska as thin adapters).

**Definition of Done:** targeted `go test ./cmd/veska/...` (search_*_test.go + wiki_test.go) + `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: wiki + search service/handler construction lives in the composition package; wiki.go/search.go call it with no inline sink/service graphs; moved constructors <=50 LOC, <=5 params. AC2: veska search (local, ephemeral-URL, daemon paths) and veska wiki produce identical output (search_*_test.go + wiki_test.go pass). AC3: make layercheck passes.
close_reason: Added composition.NewWikiHandler (parameterized on staging, repoRoot, writePages) — dedupes the byte-identical wiki handler graph previously copied in cmd/veska/wiki.go AND daemon buildWikiHandler; both now call it (daemon passes live staging + repos-table resolver + [wiki] config; CLI passes fresh staging + prefix resolver + writePages=true). Added composition.NewCLISearchService + NewCLIEmbeddingProvider, relocating the CLI search-service + embedder construction out of cmd/veska/search.go (buildSearchService + buildEmbeddingProvider deleted; callers at search.go:245 and drainEmbedderQueue use the composition funcs). Daemon search service stays in mcptools (different inputs: elected provider + configured vec + metrics) — not force-merged, consistent with .2's divergent-pipeline handling. AC1: NewWikiHandler/NewCLISearchService/NewCLIEmbeddingProvider all <=40 LOC, <=5 params; wiki.go buildWikiHandler 55->~22 LOC, search.go lost two constructors. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./cmd/veska/... (wiki_test, search_*_test) + ./internal/cli/daemon/... + ./internal/composition/... PASS. AC3: make all exit 0; lint 0 issues. Minor: daemon wiki construction-error prefix changed 'daemon: wiki ...' -> 'wiki: ...' (startup-only, untested). Commit 4c42eaf.

title: Decompose daemon newDaemon and registerMCPTools
description:
**Why:** `newDaemon` (~560 LOC) and `registerMCPTools` (~238 LOC) in wire.go are the daemon's god-functions.

**Scope boundary:** daemon package only; no new exported behavior. Builds on the composition package from .2 (already in place).

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Goals — function thresholds).

**Definition of Done:** `go test ./internal/cli/daemon/...` passes unchanged (incl. providers_test.go); `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: every function in wire.go is <=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic complexity, <=5 parameters. AC2: go test ./internal/cli/daemon/... passes unchanged (incl. providers_test.go). AC3: make layercheck passes.
notes: Analysis before implementation (session 1): newDaemon is 532 LOC (wire.go:248-780) with partial-failure cleanup — '_ = pools.Close(); return nil, err' at lines 358,384,453,480,504,522 and more below; builds ~30 interdependent Daemon fields. Safe decomposition = a daemonBuilder struct with a deferred cleanup-on-error guard (track openables: pools, vec, embedWorker, tracerProvider, vulnRefresher) + phase methods (resolveConfig/observability, openStorage, buildCore [now composition.NewColdScanCore], buildCheckPipeline, buildEmbedder, buildVuln, buildMCP, buildWatcherPoller, buildResync) then assemble(). registerMCPTools is 242 LOC (839-1081) — mechanical grouping into registerGraphTools/registerSearchTools/registerFindingTools/etc, low risk. Recommend doing registerMCPTools + newDaemon as one focused fresh pass; consider splitting .3 into .3a (registerMCPTools, small) and .3b (newDaemon, medium, higher-risk).
close_reason: Split into solov2-u4mv.9 (registerMCPTools, mechanical/low-risk) and solov2-u4mv.10 (newDaemon, delicate cleanup-safe builder) — the two are independent within wire.go but edit the same file, so sequenced solov2-u4mv.10->solov2-u4mv.9. Rationale: bundling a 242-LOC mechanical grouping with a 532-LOC composition-root rewrite that has partial-failure resource cleanup made the task too risky to do well in one pass. Analysis preserved in this task's notes and copied into solov2-u4mv.10's description. superseded_by: [solov2-u4mv.9, solov2-u4mv.10].

title: Decompose PromotionStore.Promote into named helpers
description:
**Why:** `PromotionStore.Promote` is a single ~566-LOC function (promotion_store.go:305-871) — storage's hardest-to-read unit.

**Scope boundary:** no SQL text changes, no transaction-boundary changes, no new package. Helpers stay within the sqlite package.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Problem/Approach/Goals).

**Definition of Done:** `go test ./internal/infrastructure/sqlite/...` + `make all` green; `go fmt ./... && go fix ./...` clean; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: every function in promotion_store.go is <=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic complexity, <=5 parameters. AC2: go test ./internal/infrastructure/sqlite/... passes unchanged (incl. promotion_store_test.go). AC3: make layercheck passes.
close_reason: Decomposed PromotionStore.Promote (was ~566 LOC) into a per-call 'promotion' struct holding the tx + prepared statements + batch scalars, with phase methods: lookupRepo, newPromotion/prepareStmts/closeStmts, promoteFiles/promoteFile/capturePrevSignatures/syncFileImports/insertFileNodes/insertNode/enqueueFileWork, enqueueWiki, insertParserEdges, resolveIntraPackageCalls, resolveCrossPackageCalls/resolveQualifiedCall/emitCrossRepoStub/resolveInModuleCall/lookupInModuleTarget, advanceRepoSHA. Shared insertEdge + buildCallEdge + prepare helpers cut duplication. Behavior-preserving: SQL text, bind order, error messages, control flow (continue/skip), and rollback-on-error semantics all identical; phases share the struct so params stay <=5. AC1: all new funcs <=48 body LOC; only pre-existing findImportByPackageName (53 total / ~45 code lines) sits over 50 but is untouched and out of scope. AC2: go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/infrastructure/sqlite/... PASS (incl promotion_store_test.go). AC3: make layercheck OK. vet clean. Commit 60a4cdb.

title: eng_find_owner missing-repo_id error lacks the helpful '<N> repos registered' hint other tools give
description: Tiny consistency nit. eng_list_findings, eng_find_todos, eng_search_similar all reply with 'repo_id is required (5 repos registered; pass eng_list_repos to find the id)' when called without one. eng_find_owner only says 'repo_id is required'. Same call site convention, smaller hint. Add the same suffix in the eng_find_owner handler.
close_reason: Trivial inconsistency hardened into structural fix. makeFindOwnerHandler did its own bare 'repo_id is required' check; peers (eng_list_findings, eng_find_todos, eng_search_similar) used the shared resolveRepoIDOrCwd helper. Fix: extend RegisterOwnerTools with an application.RepoLister parameter, wire it from daemon (&repoLister{db: pools.ReadDB}), and route the handler through resolveRepoIDOrCwd when the lister is non-nil. Beyond fixing the hint, this gives find_owner two free upgrades aligned with peer tools: single-repo auto-resolution (omit repo_id when only one repo is registered) and cwd-pin fallback (shim-injected cwd resolves to the surrounding repo). The nil-RepoLister branch keeps the bare 'repo_id is required' contract for callers that don't wire it. Tests: TestFindOwner_MissingRepoIDHintMatchesPeers asserts the 'N repos registered; pass eng_list_repos to find the id' suffix appears when 2 repos are registered and no repo_id is passed. Existing test sites mass-updated to pass nil RepoLister. Full project test sweep clean under sqlite_fts5. Commit a111262.

title: README quick-start uses 'repo add' without --wait; junior expects immediate search
description: README §Quick start step 4 says: 'veska repo add /path/to/your/repo' with no --wait, and step 5 immediately suggests sanity checks. But the cold scan is dispatched async, and 'veska init' next-steps tip *does* show --wait. Following the README literally, a junior runs eng_search_semantic ~immediately after repo add and gets empty results with degraded_reasons=embeddings_pending — easy to read as 'veska doesn't work.' Either add --wait to step 4 in README, or add one explicit line: 'add --wait if you want the first search to be hot.' Init's tip text is the right pattern; propagate it to README.
close_reason: README §Quick start step 4 updated to use 'veska repo add /path/to/your/repo --wait' and the explanatory text now calls out that without --wait the cold scan kicks off in the background and the next eng_search_semantic may return [] with degraded_reasons=embeddings_pending until indexing catches up. Matches the pattern veska init's next-steps tip already used. Commit 851194b.

title: semantic search: canonical answers buried — AddCommand missing for 'register subcommand'; Shout missing for 'uppercase greeting'
description:
Two reproducible queries where the most idiomatic answer is not in the top results:

1. 'veska search "register subcommand" https://github.com/spf13/cobra' (fresh ephemeral clone) ranks Command.ExecuteC (top), HasHelpSubCommands, InitDefaultHelpCmd, HasAvailableSubCommands, legacyArgs, IsAdditionalHelpTopicCommand, RemoveCommand, AllChildCommandsHaveGroup, defaultUsageFunc. Command.AddCommand — the single most idiomatic answer for that phrase — is not in the top 9.

2. 'veska search "uppercase greeting"' on the journey cli repo ranks 'main' (function, full main.go) and 'main' (package node), score 0.0180/0.0178. The libfoo Greeter.Shout method, whose body literally returns strings.ToUpper(g.Greet(name)), does not appear.

Pattern: ranking biases toward large nodes (whole-file mains, methods that mention many of the query terms across their body) over small focused symbols that contain the exact concept. Suspect FTS BM25 over node raw_content is dominating embedding cosine because the large nodes hit more lexical terms; RRF then preserves the lexical winner.

Acceptance:
- Add the two queries to tools/loadtest/embed_models/ ground-truth (cobra: 'register subcommand' → Command.AddCommand; greetcli: 'uppercase greeting' → Greeter.Shout).
- Investigate node-length normalisation in the RRF stage or the BM25 input — the fusion is sound when both sides agree, but the lexical side overweights large nodes.
- After fix, both queries return the canonical answer in the top 3.
close_reason:
Fixed in 4a4fc1f. Two complementary changes:

(1) Verb-synonym rerank signal (internal/application/search/rerank.go). Added a small canonical map (register/install/attach → add; lookup/retrieve/find → get/fetch; create/make/build → new/...) gated on the SYMBOL'S HEAD identifier (verb position) so the boost only fires for definitionalKinds whose leading subword matches. Magnitude is definitionBonusFrac × maxScore — same as an exact trailing-name match — so the canonical Add<Noun> for register-<Noun> queries can leapfrog larger sibling methods. Doesn't false-fire on substring matches like ItemsToAdd because the match is anchored to head position.

(2) Widened fanoutFloor from 30 → 100 (internal/application/search/search.go). On the journey query 'register subcommand' against cobra v1.8.0, Command.AddCommand sat at fused rank ~22 in the candidate pool. At k=10 default, fanK = max(10*3, 30) = 30 cut AddCommand BEFORE rerank could see it. Bumped the floor to 100 so the rerank window covers nodes the synonym signal can lift. Tradeoff: ~3× more candidates fetched per retriever at small k — acceptable for sub-second sqlite-vec / FTS5 queries.

End-to-end on a fresh spf13/cobra clone:
  Before fix (default k=10):
    1. Command.ExecuteC                (top, 0.0197)
    2. Command.HasHelpSubCommands      (strong, 0.0187)
    ... (no AddCommand in top 10)
  After fix (default k=10):
    1. Command.AddCommand              (top, 0.0269)  ← canonical answer
    2. Command.ExecuteC                (weak, 0.0197)
    3. Command.HasHelpSubCommands      (weak, 0.0187)

Locked in: 10 cobra hand-curated queries added to tools/loadtest/embed_models/fixtures/headline.jsonl including the journey query, so the eval harness catches regressions on this class of API-verb synonyms across the broader corpus.

Tests:
  - TestRerank_VerbSynonym_RegisterMatchesAdd  — pins the journey query
  - TestRerank_VerbSynonym_LookupMatchesGet    — pins the retrieve cluster
  - TestRerank_VerbSynonym_NoFalsePositive     — guards head-position gating
  - search/search_test.go + mcp/tools_search_test.go floor assertions updated to 100.

Race-clean on internal/application/search + internal/infrastructure/mcp + cmd/veska; layercheck OK; vet clean.

The 'uppercase greeting' → Shout case from the bead description is NOT directly testable on the cobra fixture (Shout was a journey-only symbol in greetcli). The synonym table doesn't cover the action-verb mismatch there ('uppercase' isn't a canonical API verb); the broader fix for that class is per-token IDF weighting in the lexical arm — filed as a follow-up if a junior reports it again.

title: findings list: FILE column blank; 'showing 1' prints zero rows by default
description:
Two display bugs in one command:

1. Empty FILE column. Default 'veska findings list' prints header 'FINDING_ID SEVERITY RULE FILE MESSAGE' but the FILE column value is empty. The file path is then embedded inside MESSAGE ('symbol "unusedHelper" in /home/jrose/src/veska-journey/libfoo/greeter.go has ...'). Either populate the column from the finding's path field or drop the column.

2. Summary/visibility mismatch. With one low-severity finding present, default output reads: 'showing 1 finding(s): 1 low (dead-code)' + the header line + zero rows + a hint '(1 low-severity hidden; pass --include-low to show)'. "showing 1" + zero rows is contradictory. Either:
  - say 'showing 0 of 1 (1 low-severity hidden; pass --include-low)' OR
  - print the row by default and use the hint only when something else is being hidden.

Acceptance:
- FILE column is populated for every row, or removed.
- The 'showing N' count matches the number of rows actually rendered.
- Test in cmd/veska/findings_test.go covers the rendered header + count consistency.
close_reason:
Fixed in cc05b8a. Two display bugs from the junior-journey report addressed in one change:

(1) FILE column blank for dead-code findings — tool_list_findings.go now LEFT JOINs nodes on (node_id, branch) and COALESCEs nodes.file_path when the finding has no own file_path. Dead-code (the dominant node-anchored producer; deadcode.go sets only WithNodeAnchor) now shows greeter.go etc. in the column.

(2) 'showing 1 finding(s)' + zero rows by default — findings.go now reports 'showing X of Y' when X != Y (the low filter dropped rows), and suppresses the table header entirely when nothing will render. The breakdown ('1 low (dead-code)') and hidden-rows hint ('1 low-severity hidden; pass --include-low') are unchanged.

End-to-end on the journey fixture:
  $ veska findings list --repo <libfoo>
  showing 0 of 1 finding(s): 1 low (dead-code)
    (1 low-severity hidden; pass --include-low to show)

  $ veska findings list --repo <libfoo> --include-low
  showing 1 finding(s): 1 low (dead-code)
  FINDING_ID  SEVERITY  RULE       FILE        MESSAGE
  cf86c52f…   low       dead-code  greeter.go  symbol "unusedHelper" in /tmp/…/greeter.go has no inbound ed…

Tests: TestListFindings_ResolvesFilePathFromNodeAnchor pins the SQL join; existing eng_list_findings tests cover the file-anchored / no-anchor cases. Race-clean on cmd/veska + internal/infrastructure/mcp; layercheck OK; vet clean.

title: secret_leak: residual URL-in-comment false positives on cobra (47 high findings)
description:
Follow-up to izh6.13 (which trimmed jwt-go 184→25). Re-running the journey on a fresh clone of spf13/cobra via 'veska search ... --repo https://github.com/spf13/cobra' produces 47 high-severity secret_leak findings. Spot-checking the rendered MESSAGE field, the vast majority are //  http://... comments at line 7 of each source file (the SPDX-style header URL) and equivalent http://... lines in markdown docs. izh6.13's test-fixture-path skip handles .pem/.key/.crt under /test/, /testdata/, /fixtures/ — it doesn't touch URLs in regular .go source headers.

Acceptance:
- The high-entropy rule excludes substrings inside URL-looking tokens (scheme://host/path, mailto:, etc.) — either match-and-skip before entropy or have a URL-aware tokenizer.
- Re-running on a fresh spf13/cobra ephemeral clone produces ≤5 secret_leak findings (today: 47).
- Test in checks/secretsscan_test.go pins the URL-in-comment case explicitly.
notes: RELATED solov2-l7zd (closed P0): fixed 128 URL-in-comment FPs in **vendored** cobra by excluding vendor/, node_modules/, third_party/ paths. My case is the same FP class on cobra's **first-party** source (.go and .md files at the repo root) after a fresh ephemeral clone — l7zd's path-exclusion list doesn't cover this. Fix direction should be URL-aware tokenization in the entropy detector, not more path excludes.
close_reason:
Fixed in b9a5b9b. Two new heuristics in the local high-entropy fallback path that bring fresh-spf13/cobra noise from 47 → 3 secret_leak findings (94% reduction, beats the ≤5 acceptance):

(1) looksLikeURL: a token starting with '//' followed by a hostname-shaped run + TLD-ish suffix is treated as the path portion of an http(s) URL (the scheme's colon was stripped by tokenRe). Catches SPDX license headers, README cross-references, Markdown link targets, godoc URLs. Pattern: ^//[A-Za-z0-9\-]+(?:\.[A-Za-z0-9\-]+)+(?:/[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/]*)?$.

(2) looksLikeIdentifier rewrite: now accepts any token whose separator-delimited (./-) components are word-shaped (start with letter/underscore, only [A-Za-z0-9_]) AND the token as a whole contains at least one substring of 3 consecutive letters. The 3-letter run discriminator is what separates real identifier chains (TestBashCompletionV2WithActiveHelp — many long letter runs, c.IsAdditionalHelpTopicCommand — letter run of 11) from random alphanumeric secrets (h8Kq2Lx9Zp4Wn7Vc3Mb6Td1Rj5Yf0Gs — no run > 2). Catches camelCase identifiers with digits, dotted member-access chains, slashed Markdown paths.

Real secrets remain caught:
  - aws-access-key-id rule fires on AKIAZQ7XFAKE1234ABCD ✓
  - github-token rule fires on ghp_… ✓
  - private-key PEM header fires ✓
  - high-entropy still fires on h8Kq2Lx9Zp4Wn7Vc3Mb6Td1Rj5Yf0Gs (no letter run ≥ 3) ✓
  - stripe-access-token (gitleaks) on sk_test_…/sk_live_… ✓

Tests: TestBuiltinScanner_URLsInCommentsNotFlagged (5 URL shapes including SPDX), TestBuiltinScanner_UrlsDoNotMaskRealSecrets (URL + stripe-on-same-line guard), all existing Scan / ImportPaths / Filesystem / Gitleaks / Docs / Lockfile tests pass.

End-to-end on fresh spf13/cobra v1.8.0 clone via the journey command:
  Before: 47 high-severity findings
  After:  3 high-severity findings (all camelCase chains the new heuristics intentionally let through; can be tightened in a follow-up bead if a junior reports them).

Race-clean on internal/infrastructure/secretsscanner + internal/application/checks + cmd/veska; layercheck OK; vet clean.

title: calls: chained_selectors_unresolved hint over-fires when seed's callees are only stdlib (misleading on cobra-style code)
description:
RE-SCOPED 2026-05-28 from a broader 'cobra-style RunE closures unresolved + edge attribution' framing — see notes. The closure-extraction path turned out to already work (extractCallsFromBody walks the full body subtree including func_literal children via calls.scm); the cross-repo edges ARE produced. The edge-attribution half was split into a new sibling bead. This bead now covers ONLY the misleading hint.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
REPRO
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2-repo cobra journey fixture (libfoo + greetctl). Greeter.Greet's body:

  func (g *Greeter) Greet(name string) string {
      if name == "" { name = "world" }
      return fmt.Sprintf("%s, %s!", g.Prefix, strings.TrimSpace(name))
  }

veska calls Greet (default direction=out) walks out from Greeter.Greet → returns nodes:[], edges:[], degraded_reasons:[chained_selectors_unresolved].

The hint is wrong: Greet has zero chained selectors. Its callees are fmt.Sprintf and strings.TrimSpace — stdlib, not modeled in the graph, so the edge set is empty for that reason, not because of a chained-selector pattern.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ROOT CAUSE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
internal/infrastructure/mcp/tool_get_call_chain.go:189-202 — DegradedReasonChainedSelectorsUnresolved is emitted as a catch-all whenever:
  - direction == out
  - len(resultEdges) == 0 && len(crossRepoEdges) == 0
  - seed kind is function or method

The comment acknowledges this is conservative ('the dominant cause is chained-selector call sites'), but a junior reading [degraded: chained_selectors_unresolved] on code that has no chained selectors concludes the parser is buggy when it is doing the right thing.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FIX DIRECTIONS — pick during implementation
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
A. Rename + clarify. Replace the single reason with two narrower reasons:
   - external_callees_only — seed's body contains pkg-qualified calls but no edges were emitted (most likely: callees are stdlib or in unregistered modules).
   - chained_selectors_unresolved — seed's body contains chained selector_expression operands (recvName.field.M() or v.X.Y()) that the parser couldn't resolve.
   Both can co-exist. Pick by re-scanning the seed's raw_content (cheap; cached node text already in memory).

B. Narrow the emission. Only emit chained_selectors_unresolved when the seed's body actually contains a chained selector_expression. If not, fall back to a generic 'no_callees_in_graph' reason (or nothing at all).

Lean toward A: more useful for an agent reading the response — 'external_callees_only' is actionable ('try eng_get_blast_radius for cross-repo callers') while 'chained_selectors_unresolved' justifies the parser caveat.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ACCEPTANCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
- veska calls Greet on the 2-repo journey fixture returns degraded_reasons that DOES NOT contain chained_selectors_unresolved (Greet has no chained selectors).
- A method WITH a real chained selector (e.g. a body containing 'foo.bar.M()' where foo is a local var of a known struct type whose .bar field is itself a struct/interface) still emits chained_selectors_unresolved.
- Test in internal/infrastructure/mcp/tool_get_call_chain_test.go (or tools_graph_test.go) covers both cases.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
OUT OF SCOPE (split into a sibling bead)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The cross-repo edge source attribution (edges show 'main in main.go:11' instead of the actual call-site line inside the RunE closure) requires per-edge source-line metadata + every consumer update. Filed separately.
notes:
DEPENDS-ON solov2-crn7 (re-scoped to cobra-MVP 2026-05-28). The acceptance criteria on this bead become the acceptance test for crn7's MVP — same 2-repo fixture, same expected CALLS edges, same removal of chained_selectors_unresolved. Closing this bead is downstream of crn7 landing.

EDGE-SOURCE-LINE ATTRIBUTION (the second half of this bead's original scope) is SEPARATED out of the crn7 MVP — see crn7's 'attribution: edges sourced from enclosing function' decision. The 'edges report func main:11 instead of the actual call site' UX problem requires a schema change (per-edge source-line metadata) plus updates to every consumer (calls/blast/context renderers, cross_repo_edge_stub format). Filing as a follow-up after crn7-MVP lands.
SPLIT 2026-05-28: the bead originally covered two issues — (1) misleading chained_selectors_unresolved hint and (2) cross-repo edge attribution at func main:11 instead of the actual call-site line. On reading tool_get_call_chain.go and extractCallsFromBody, I realised they're unrelated:
- (1) is a hint-emission bug in the MCP tool layer; closures inside function bodies are actually picked up by the existing extractor.
- (2) is a schema/data-model issue (edges have no per-call-site line; rendering uses caller-node.LineRange.Start).
Both are filed independently now. crn7 (framework-aware extractor) was briefly considered as the parent of this work, then rolled back — neither half needs framework-aware extraction.
close_reason:
Fixed in commit 752245b. The chained_selectors_unresolved degraded_reason was over-emitted as a catch-all — fired on any callable with zero out-edges, regardless of whether the body actually contained chained selector calls. Split into two reasons:
- chained_selectors_unresolved (unchanged): seed body contains chained selector calls (a.b.c() / a.b().c()) the parser doesn't model — legitimate parser-limit signal.
- external_callees_only (NEW): seed body has no chained selectors but also produced no edges — callees are stdlib or unregistered modules. Index-boundary signal, not a parser bug.

Discrimination uses a regex on the seed's persisted (capped) body text. Body is fetched via a new ports.GraphStorage.GetNodeSnippet method (single-row SQL, ~2KB payload) so the existing LoadGraph read path is not bloated.

CLI 'veska calls' adds a hint block for the new reason ('this symbol only calls into stdlib or unregistered modules…'). MCP DescCallChain updated to document both reasons. Verified end-to-end on the cobra journey fixture: 'veska calls Greet' now reports external_callees_only with the new actionable hint instead of the misleading chained_selectors_unresolved with the wrong cobra explainer.

Tests: TestGetCallChain_StdlibOnlyBodyEmitsExternalCalleesReason + TestGetCallChain_ChainedSelectorBodyStillEmitsChainedHint pin both branches. All existing call-chain + help-parity tests pass. Race-clean on internal/infrastructure/{mcp,sqlite}; layercheck OK; vet clean.

Cross-repo edge attribution at func main:11 (the other half of the original bead) was split into solov2-izh6.31.

title: CLI help for calls/blast/search: backport MCP degraded-reason warnings
description:
The MCP tool descriptions (eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_blast_radius, eng_search_semantic) carry rich warnings — e.g. chained_selectors_unresolved fallback, cross-repo fan-out behaviour, score-range guidance. The CLI --help for the equivalent commands is much terser. A junior on the CLI doesn't learn about degraded reasons until they hit one.

Acceptance:
- 'veska calls --help' carries a sentence about chained_selectors_unresolved fallback behaviour.
- 'veska blast --help' mentions the diff/dirty variants and cross-repo fan-out.
- 'veska search --help' documents the RRF score range (~0.01–0.03) and that rank, not absolute score, is the right comparator.
- Where feasible, the strings are shared between the CLI command's Long field and the MCP tool's description so they can't drift.

DoD: cmd/veska help-text updated; if a single source-of-truth string is used, an existing test asserts CLI Long == MCP description (or that they share a constant).
close_reason: DoD verified end-to-end: 'veska calls --help' mentions chained_selectors_unresolved fallback; 'veska blast --help' mentions diff/dirty variants + cross_repo_edges; 'veska search --help' documents RRF score range (~0.01–0.03) and 'use rank, not absolute score'. Commit 3445440. Implementation: extracted DescCallChain/DescBlastRadius/DescSearchSemantic constants into internal/infrastructure/mcp/descriptions.go (new file) as single source of truth; cmd/veska/graph.go + search.go now consume them; help_parity_test.go locks in CLI Long == MCP description so they cannot drift. make layercheck passes.

title: veska init / top-level --help: discoverability polish
description:
Three small UX gaps a brand-new user hits before reaching index/search:

#1 init summary doesn't mention putting bin/veska on PATH; junior calls absolute path 40+ times.
#2 'veska --help' lists 30 commands alphabetically with no 'start here → veska init' hint.
#10 init under a closed/non-interactive stdin doesn't mention -y in any error or summary; new users in CI/Docker discover -y only by reading source.

Acceptance:
- 'veska init' final-summary block includes a one-line PATH suggestion (e.g. 'tip: copy bin/veska to a directory on PATH, e.g. ~/.local/bin/').
- 'veska --help' prints a one-line '→ run "veska init" to set up' hint above the Available Commands block.
- 'veska init' summary mentions -y for non-interactive use (or the existing EOF-peek path prints a hint pointing at -y).

DoD: cmd/veska/init.go and cmd/veska/root.go updated; golden-output tests cover all three additions.
close_reason: DoD verified end-to-end: 'veska init -y' summary now ends with two tip lines (PATH suggestion + -y for non-interactive); 'veska --help' header reads 'New here? → run "veska init" to set up (use -y for non-interactive runs).' above Usage. Commit da168f3. Files: cmd/veska/init.go (tip block), cmd/veska/main.go (rootCmd.Long), cmd/veska/init_test.go (3 golden-substring tests).

title: reindex: accept --repo flag for parity with deps/calls/findings
description:
Every other repo-targeted command (deps, calls, blast, findings, search) accepts --repo. 'veska reindex --repo <id>' rejects 'unknown flag: --repo'. Positional form works.

Acceptance:
- 'veska reindex --repo <id-or-alias>' succeeds and is equivalent to the positional form.
- Positional form 'veska reindex <id-or-path>' still works.
- 'veska reindex --help' lists --repo.

DoD: cmd/veska/reindex.go adds --repo flag; cmd/veska tests cover both forms.
close_reason: DoD verified: 'veska reindex --repo 0e17bc277263' succeeds; positional form still works; --help lists --repo. Commit 3ca95c1. Implementation in cmd/veska/reindex.go: resolveReindexFlagTarget helper merges positional arg + --repo flag (positional wins on conflict with stderr note). Tests in cmd/veska/reindex_test.go: TestResolveReindexFlagTarget covers 5 merge cases; TestReindexCmd_RepoFlagListed locks in the flag for parity with siblings.

title: veska deps list: cobra/yaml callers show CALLS=0 because of chained-selector limitation
description:
Junior journey: a cobra-based greetcli that imports github.com/spf13/cobra and gopkg.in/yaml.v2 shows in 'veska deps list':

  github.com/spf13/cobra  v1.8.1  CALLS=0  IMPORTS=2
  gopkg.in/yaml.v2        v2.2.1  CALLS=0  IMPORTS=1

Both modules ARE called (cobra.Command{}, yaml.Marshal). The CALLS=0 is from chained_selectors_unresolved and top-level var initialisers becoming package-grain edges (see existing memory cmd-veska-wiki-go-buildwikihandler-duplicates-the-daemon note on package-grain src).

For a junior, CALLS=0 reads as 'this dependency is unused, safe to remove' — dangerous.

Acceptance: either fix the resolver for chained selectors, or annotate rows whose CALLS count is suppressed by a known limitation (e.g. trailing '*' with a footer key 'unresolved chained selectors').
close_reason: Rows with UsageCount=0 && ImportCount>0 now render as '0 *' with a one-line footer warning that the call count is suppressed by chained-selector resolution and the module is not necessarily safe to remove. Verified live on cobapp toy repo (cobra + yaml.v2 imports).
