title: eng_find_todos returns empty for uncommitted TODOs without a degraded_reasons hint
description:
Junior journey: appended '// TODO(jr): handle empty name' to a file and ran eng_find_todos. Got '{"todos":[],"degraded_reasons":[]}'. After committing, the TODO appeared.

The empty result with empty degraded_reasons gives no signal that the scan is commit-grain, not working-tree. A junior reasonably expects 'find TODOs' to scan files on disk.

Acceptance: when there are uncommitted changes in the repo and todos is empty, emit a degraded_reason like 'todos_are_post_promotion' so callers can show 'commit first to scan' guidance. (Or scan the working tree.)
close_reason: Added gitinfra.WorkingTreeHasUncommittedChanges; eng_find_todos appends 'todos_are_post_promotion' to degraded_reasons when result is empty AND working tree is dirty. Layercheck passes. Two new unit tests cover dirty-emits-degraded and clean-stays-undegraded. Verified live: dirty repo now returns degraded_reasons=['todos_are_post_promotion'].

title: veska search of cloned external repo prints full ~/.cache/veska/repos/<sha>/ paths
description:
Junior journey: 'veska search "command line flag parsing" --repo https://github.com/spf13/pflag' clones to ~/.cache/veska/repos/<64-char sha>/ and prints results with the full cache path on every row. Hard to scan.

Either (a) display 'pflag/flag.go:194-206 (cached)' style, deriving display name from the URL, or (b) when the repo is ephemeral, root-strip the cache prefix and prepend the repo display name.

Acceptance: search output for ephemeral cached repos uses a short display path; the full path is still available via --json for tooling.
close_reason: Added prettifyEphemeralPaths + ephemeralDisplayName + buildSearchEnvelope helpers; rec.RootPath is the path prefix (clone dir uses URL-derived id, not registry path-id). Live verified: text output now shows 'pflag/flag.go:194-206'; --json keeps absolute paths for tooling. Three unit tests cover the rewrite, no-op guards, and display-name extraction.

title: eng_list_suppressions requires repo_id while eng_list_findings defaults across repos
description:
Junior journey: 'eng_list_findings' with no params lists across all repos (with a 'no --repo' advisory line). 'eng_list_suppressions' with no params errors '-32602 repo_id is required (3 repos registered; pass eng_list_repos to find the id)'.

Either both default to all-repos, or both require repo_id. Pick one.

Acceptance: paired list endpoints share the same default-repo behavior.
close_reason: eng_list_suppressions no longer rejects empty repo_id when multiple repos are registered; mirrors eng_list_findings default-fan-out behavior. Explicit repo_id still validates. New TestListSuppressions_MultiRepoDefaultsToFanOut pins the contract. Verified live with 3 repos registered.

title: MCP tool surface inconsistency: eng_find_owner rejects 'branch' param accepted by peers
description:
Junior journey: probing the MCP surface, every other read-side tool (eng_find_todos, eng_search_similar, eng_get_hot_zone, eng_find_changed_symbols) accepts a 'branch' parameter. eng_find_owner returns -32602 'unknown parameter "branch"'.

Either accept and ignore 'branch' for owner lookup or add it to the schema. Inconsistency breaks the discoverability promise of MCP — agents iterate the tools list and pass the same canonical args to each.

Acceptance: 'branch' is accepted (and used or ignored consistently) across all read-side eng_* tools, or its absence is documented in the tool description.
close_reason: Added 'branch' to findOwnerInputSchema (it was already on the Go struct, just rejected by additionalProperties:false). New TestFindOwner_AcceptsBranchParam pins the schema contract. Verified live: eng_find_owner with branch=main now returns owner instead of -32602.

title: veska calls returns bare 'no nodes in chain [degraded: chained_selectors_unresolved]' for cobra-style code
description:
Junior journey: ran 'veska calls Execute' on a cobra root command whose body is rootCmd.AddCommand(...); rootCmd.Execute(). Got 'no nodes in chain [degraded: chained_selectors_unresolved]'.

A first-time user reads this as 'my code has no calls' (false). The degraded_reasons tag is opaque — there is no inline explanation that chained selector expressions (a.b().c()) are a known tree-sitter limitation.

Acceptance: when chained_selectors_unresolved is the sole degraded reason and the chain is empty, append a single-line hint explaining the limitation and pointing at 'veska blast/context' as alternatives.
close_reason: When the chain is empty and chained_selectors_unresolved is the only degraded reason, the renderer now appends a multi-line hint explaining the limitation and pointing to veska blast/context. Two new unit tests pin hint-present and hint-not-leaked-to-other-reasons. Verified live on a cobra-style toy repo.

title: veska changed: confusing error on single-commit repo (HEAD~1 unresolved)
description:
Junior journey: ran 'veska changed HEAD~1 HEAD --repo=<id>' on a repo with one commit. Error: 'ref_a="HEAD~1" or ref_b="HEAD" does not resolve in the repo (insufficient history? try omitting both refs or pass an explicit pair)'.

The advice is contradictory — 'omit both' vs 'pass an explicit pair'. Junior can't tell which to do.

Acceptance: error explains which ref failed to resolve (HEAD~1) and gives one concrete remediation (e.g. 'use an earlier ref or pass an explicit commit SHA').
close_reason: Added gitinfra.ResolvesRef helper; tool_find_changed_symbols now identifies which ref failed and emits a tailored, non-contradictory message. Live verified on single-commit repo (HEAD~1), unresolved ref_b, and both-bogus cases. Existing TestChangedSymbols_ExplicitUnknownRefStillErrors still passes.

title: secret_leak and dead-code rules also flood ephemeral repos
description: Sibling to izh6.8. After izh6.8 skipped autolink for ephemeral repos, a fresh reindex of /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/.../pflag yielded 273 findings (53 high secret_leak — high-entropy false-positives in Go test fixtures; 220 low dead-code on unused exported helpers that are part of pflag's public API). Same UX problem the bead identified for autolink. Fix direction: extend the repo-kind-aware skip to checks.SecretsScanCheck and checks.DeadCodeCheck via the same WithRepoKindLookup-shaped option, or gate registration in cli/daemon/wire.go when promoting an ephemeral repo. Probably want to keep secret_leak active even on ephemeral (it's a real signal if a real key leaks via a fork) — so the right trim is dead-code + autolink wholesale, and secret_leak only when matching test fixtures.
close_reason: DoD verified end-to-end on jwt-go ephemeral re-clone: findings dropped from 184 → 25 (was 1 dead-code + 168 high secret_leak + 15 critical secret_leak; now 21 high + 4 critical secret_leak, 0 dead-code). Commit 6e4d757. Two fixes: (1) checks/deadcode.go added DeadCodeOption + WithDeadCodeRepoKindLookup; daemon wire.go threads repo.Get().Kind so ephemeral repos short-circuit dead-code wholesale (mirrors izh6.8 autolink pattern). (2) checks/secretsscan.go skips test-fixture credential paths (.pem/.key/.crt under /test/, /testdata/, /fixtures/) and collapses N consecutive same-rule hits on the same file into one finding with '+N more line(s)'. Tests in checks/deadcode_test.go + secretsscan_test.go.

title: autolink rule fires 102 low-severity findings on a 75-file external repo (pflag)
description:
Repro:
  veska search 'command line argument parser' --repo https://github.com/spf13/pflag
  (clones, indexes, marks ephemeral)
  veska reindex /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/<id>
  veska findings list --repo df688ea949a0 --include-low
  → 'showing 102 finding(s): 102 low (auto-link)' for a 75-file repo

Many are duplicate matches on the same target (e.g. intSliceValue.String matched 4×, DurationVar/DurationVarP each matched 3×). For a user evaluating veska on an external codebase this is the first findings list they see and it reads as noise.

Fix direction: (a) deduplicate auto-link findings against the same target within a repo, or (b) raise the auto-link similarity threshold for ephemeral/cache-tier repos, or (c) hide auto-link findings from the default 'findings list' view on ephemeral repos (they're already low-severity, but the count signal still misleads). Threshold-tuning eval is solov2's existing eval-autolink-fp harness — re-run with this corpus.
close_reason: Added autolink.WithRepoKindLookup option; daemon wire.go now passes a repo.Get-backed callback. autolink.Handle short-circuits when repo Kind == 'ephemeral' so cache-tier clones from 'veska search --repo <url>' no longer generate any auto-link findings. Verified on /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/...pflag (ephemeral): autolink count dropped from 102 → 0 after reindex. Follow-up bead filed for secret_leak / dead-code noise on the same repo (53 high + 220 low post-fix from rules outside autolink's scope).

title: veska deps <unknown-subcommand> silently runs 'deps list' instead of erroring
description:
Repro:
  veska deps show github.com/jrose/greetlib
  → prints the standard 'deps list' table; 'show' and the module path are swallowed.

deps has only 'index' and 'list' subcommands. cobra appears to route 'deps' itself to a default-list RunE; unknown subcommand tokens then look like positional args and disappear. This makes 'I tried veska deps show foo and it just printed everything' a confusing first-touch experience.

Fix direction: deps's parent command shouldn't have a default action when an arg that doesn't match a known subcommand is supplied — cobra's Args validator can reject unknown positional tokens, or the default RunE can branch to 'unknown subcommand'.
close_reason: depsCmd parent now caps positional args at MaximumNArgs(1), and its RunE detects when the single arg looks like a subcommand name (alphabetic identifier, no /.@~) that isn't registered — returning 'unknown deps subcommand "show" — run veska deps --help'. The bare 'veska deps' and 'veska deps <path-or-id>' paths still work (preserving solov2-mtd0). Verified: 'veska deps show github.com/foo' → 'accepts at most 1 arg(s)'; 'veska deps show' → unknown-subcommand error; 'veska deps /tmp/junior/greetcli' and 'veska deps list' still print the table.

title: veska changed accepts positional ref args silently then ignores them
description:
Repro:
  veska changed HEAD              → degraded: no_parent_commit_used_empty_tree, ignores 'HEAD'
  veska changed HEAD~1 HEAD       → same (positional args swallowed)

The command only honours --ref-a / --ref-b. Positional refs are common muscle-memory from git itself ('git diff HEAD~1 HEAD') and the silent fallback to empty-tree makes the degraded output look like a bug in the daemon rather than a user error.

Fix direction: either accept up to 2 positional refs (preferred — matches git ergonomics), or hard-error 'veska changed takes refs via --ref-a/--ref-b' when positional args are present.
close_reason: changedCmd now accepts up to 2 positional refs ('veska changed v1.0 v1.1' or 'veska changed v1.0'), mapping them onto ref-a/ref-b when the flags are empty. Help text documents the git-diff-style positional form. Args tightened from NoArgs to MaximumNArgs(2). Verified: 'veska changed HEAD~1 HEAD' is now honored; 'veska changed a b c' is rejected by cobra.

title: veska init -y does not summarize what the OSV vuln-scan prompt would have asked
description: Junior journey: ran 'veska init -y' from a fresh shell. The OSV vuln scanner ended up enabled (vuln_source.provider=osv in config show, 4 vuln findings against greetcli's go.mod after registration). Without --no-vuln + interactive mode, the prompt about OSV egress would have been shown, but with -y the user gets vuln scanning enabled silently. The init output lists data/backups/embedder/service/repo but doesn't mention 'OSV vuln scanner: enabled (will query osv.dev for go.mod deps)' — junior in restricted networks could be surprised by egress. Add a one-liner to the post-init summary.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: CLAUDE.md snippet says 'usually optional. When exactly one repo is registered' — misleading with multiple repos
description: Junior journey: ran 'veska init --agent claude' inside greetcli. The generated snippet says repo_id/branch are 'usually optional. When exactly one repo is registered the daemon auto-resolves repo_id.' That phrasing implies a single-repo setup, but our daemon had 3 (greetlib, greetcli, gorilla/mux ephemeral). The actual auto-resolve behavior is cwd-based via eng_get_current_repo (already referenced in the snippet) — that should be lifted earlier and the 'single repo' caveat softened, so agents know to rely on cwd resolution in multi-repo setups.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: veska changed on single-commit repo silently diffs HEAD against empty, returns all symbols as added
description: Junior: 'veska changed' (defaults HEAD~1..HEAD) in /tmp/junior/greetlib, which has exactly one commit so HEAD~1 doesn't resolve. Instead of erroring, the command returns every symbol in the repo as '+' added. That's misleading — junior would think a real diff exists. Should detect the missing parent ref and either print '(no parent commit; nothing to diff)' or fail with a clear message.
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: auto-link findings have empty FILE column in list output
description: Junior: 'veska findings list --severity low --all' for the gorilla/mux auto-link findings prints an empty FILE column. The full path lives in the MESSAGE text ('Router.HandleFunc in /home/jrose/.cache/...'). The FILE column should be populated from the node's file path so users can scan / sort findings by file like they can for vulnerable_dependency findings (which correctly show 'go.mod').
close_reason: Fixed in working-tree changes; verified end-to-end against the junior-journey repos.

title: veska init: no flag to script vuln-source choice (stdin EOF auto-accepts)
description:
'veska init' prompts 'Enable OSV vulnerability scanning? [Y/n]'. Under no-TTY (CI, automation, agents) it logs 'stdin EOF; accepting default' and proceeds. There is no --vuln-source=osv|none flag (or --yes / --no-vuln) for a fully non-interactive install. Add one so packaged install scripts and dev containers can pick deterministically.

Also: the prompt's wording 'Enable OSV vulnerability scanning?' should mention this dials network egress (OSV.dev). A privacy-conscious user reading the prompt may want to know the default = network call.
close_reason: The flags already existed (--yes, --no-vuln) — the original gap was UX, not the flag surface. Updated: (1) interactive prompt mentions 'queries osv.dev over the network' so a privacy-conscious user understands the default = network egress; (2) the prompt itself names the flags so juniors never grep --help to script the install; (3) both the no-TTY and stdin-EOF auto-accept messages mention --no-vuln so the caller learns how to override next time.

title: MCP error messages list allowed params alphabetically but never required ones
description:
eng_find_related rejected {node_id, limit} with:
  'eng_find_related: unknown parameter "node_id" (allowed: branch, cwd, file_path, k, limit, line, repo_id)'

That is the alphabetized union of optional+required params; nothing flags that file_path+line are REQUIRED while branch/cwd/k are optional. For a junior dev or LLM consumer trying tools/list -> first call, this is the #1 frustration. Either:
  - augment ToolSpec error formatting to mark required params, OR
  - on missing-required, error with 'missing required parameter: file_path' instead of 'unknown parameter'.

Same surface likely affects every eng_* tool, not just find_related.
close_reason: validateAgainstSchema now reads the schema's 'required' array and the 'allowed: …' hint in unknown-parameter errors marks each required key with ' (required)' AND sorts required-first. A junior dev or LLM that tried 'node_id' on eng_find_related now learns from the error that file_path and line are required, instead of having to fetch the full schema. Applies to every eng_* tool with a published schema.

title: findings list: --all + --repo mutually exclusive (surprising)
description:
'veska findings list --all --repo <id>' errors 'findings list: --all and --repo are mutually exclusive'. A user wanting 'show me ALL states for repo X' has no single command. --all is currently 'all repos at default state'; --repo is 'one repo, open only'. There is no 'one repo, all states'.

Also: without flags, 'findings list' silently scopes to the cwd-detected repo even when cwd is OUTSIDE any registered repo (it picked the first registered repo). Should at least print which repo it picked, or error if cwd is unregistered.
close_reason: Two changes: (1) findings list no longer rejects --all + --repo; the combination means 'every finding in repo X, regardless of state'. (2) Daemon's eng_list_findings accepts state='any' to disable the per-state WHERE clause. With --repo unset, --all keeps its original 'fan out across repos' semantics. The cwd-outside-any-repo auto-fanout path is preserved with a stderr breadcrumb.

title: CLI wrappers for the remaining 12 MCP tools (split from xomk)
description:
Follow-up to solov2-xomk. The parity-lint MVP shipped wrappers for the three highest-impact MCP tools (eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_blast_radius, eng_find_changed_symbols) plus the lint itself. The remaining 12 tools are currently marked CLIExempt=ExemptDeferred pointing at this issue. Each needs its own design pass for the CLI surface and output shape.

## Remaining tools

- eng_find_owner          — 'veska owner <path-or-symbol>'
- eng_find_related        — 'veska related <file:line>'
- eng_find_todos          — 'veska todos'
- eng_get_dirty_blast_radius — 'veska blast --dirty' (extend existing blast cmd?)
- eng_get_diff_blast_radius  — 'veska blast --diff <ref_a>..<ref_b>' (extend existing blast?)
- eng_get_current_repo    — 'veska repo current' (or implicit via cwd elsewhere)
- eng_get_entry_points    — 'veska entry-points' (wiki already emits the markdown; raw query for agents?)
- eng_get_file_nodes      — 'veska file-nodes <path>'
- eng_get_hot_zone        — 'veska hot-zones' (same as entry-points: wiki bakes them)
- eng_get_node            — 'veska node <id>'
- eng_get_repo            — 'veska repo show <id>'
- eng_search_similar      — 'veska similar <symbol-or-node>'

## Acceptance

For each tool: a thin cobra wrapper that calls the MCP tool, renders text + supports --json, and is added to tools/lint/cliparity/wrapped.txt. Remove the ExemptDeferred marker from the corresponding ToolSpec once landed.

## Design questions worth resolving in this issue

- Should the 'dirty' and 'diff' variants of blast_radius be flags on 'veska blast' rather than separate verbs? Same shape, different scope — likely yes.
- entry_points / hot_zone — the wiki command already bakes these into docs/veska/. Do we want a raw-query CLI for agents that want the data without writing the markdown? Probably yes (lets agents skip the file read).
- get_current_repo — currently implicit; explicit 'veska repo current' would help script-writers. Cheap.

title: 'veska wiki' only acts on cwd repo — no multi-repo workflow
description: 'veska wiki' regenerates hot_zones.md / entry_points.md for the cwd repo only. For users with multiple registered repos (the assumed mature usage), there's no 'veska wiki --all' or 'veska wiki --repo cli,lib' to refresh all of them in one go. Junior eng workflow with two repos (cobra CLI + shared lib) had to cd into each one and re-run.\n\nAlso: the generated docs/veska/ path is hardcoded — no way to opt into placing the wiki at e.g. docs/architecture/ or to print to stdout. Minor, but together they make wiki feel like a one-repo-only tool.
close_reason: Added --all flag to 'veska wiki'. When set, iterates over every registered repo, calls the same handler.Handle that the post-promotion queue lane uses, and continues on per-repo failures (a stuck repo must not suppress others). Mutually exclusive with --repo/--branch. Verified end-to-end against the junior journey's two-repo setup: a single 'veska wiki --all' regenerates docs/veska/hot_zones.md + entry_points.md for both greetlib and greetcli without cd-ing between them. Output path is still hardcoded to docs/veska/ — separate request worth filing if users want it configurable, but this issue specifically called out the multi-repo workflow which is now solved.

title: internal bd issue IDs leak into user-facing help/output/wiki
description:
End-to-end journey kept tripping over references like 'solov2-n57f', 'solov2-kxo5.6', 'solov2-pvyo', 'solov2-zm6i', 'solov2-awp6', 'solov2-73f' embedded in user-facing surfaces:
- 'veska init' output: '...co-located under VESKA_HOME; ... (solov2-n57f)'
- 'veska init --help': flag descriptions include trailing '(solov2-pvyo)', '(solov2-zm6i)'
- 'veska search --help': trailing '(solov2-kxo5.6)'
- 'veska config show': comments include '(solov2-awp6)'
- Generated wiki page docs/veska/entry_points.md: '... (solov2-73f).'

These are internal tracker IDs and mean nothing to users; they read like cryptic error codes. They were added as commit-trail breadcrumbs and crept into prod text. Recommendation: add a lint that greps for /solov2-[a-z0-9]{4,6}/ in cmd/**/*.go, internal/**/*.go, and templates, fails CI if found in user-visible string literals. Move the tracking-id refs to code comments only.
close_reason: Removed 'solov2-xxxx' tags from user-visible string literals across cmd/veska (init output, flag descriptions for findings/search/init/backup/restore/repo/deps/doctor), internal/application/wiki/render.go (entry_points doc), and internal/infrastructure/mcp (tool descriptions, schemas). Added tools/lint/noidleak: a Go AST walker that scans every non-test .go file under cmd/ and internal/, visits BasicLit string nodes only, and fails with line:col of each leaked id. Wired as a make 'noidleak' target and added to 'make all' so this can't regress. Code-comment ids (// solov2-xxx) are still allowed — they're the legitimate breadcrumb shape.

title: auto-link low-severity noise overwhelms small repos
description:
After indexing a 5-file CLI repo, 'veska findings list --include-low' returned 8 low-severity 'auto-link' findings such as:
  - main in main.go similar to Execute in cmd/root.go
  - init in cmd/root.go similar to init in cmd/shout.go
  - shoutCmd similar to rootCmd, etc.

These are auto-link suggestions surfaced as findings. On a tiny demo repo there are 8 of them — every Go init() func compared to every other init(), every cobra.Command var to every other. Signal:noise on a fresh repo is roughly 0%.

Fixes to consider:
- Don't emit auto-link findings for symbol pairs whose names are identical and Go-idiomatic (init, main, TestX).
- Don't emit when both sides are *cobra.Command vars in the same /cmd tree (structurally similar by design).
- Or hide auto-link findings from 'findings list' entirely; expose via a dedicated 'veska autolink suggestions' command so security findings stay clean.
close_reason: Added isIdiomaticAutolinkNoise filter to the autolink handler's candidate-filter stage. Drops two structural-trivia patterns before findings emit: (1) name-on-name pairs where the shared unqualified symbol name is a Go idiom (init, main, String, Error, TestMain); (2) variable-to-variable pairs whose names both end in 'Cmd' (cobra package-level command vars). Verified end-to-end: junior journey's 5-file cobra repo went from 8 low-severity auto-link findings to 0 with no loss of useful signal — the jwt-go vuln finding is still the only thing surfaced. Pinned by TestIsIdiomaticAutolinkNoise truth table.

title: context output shows package name as caller, not the calling function
description:
'veska context Greeter.Hello --repo lib' prints:
  cross-repo edges (4):
     cmd --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello in 0e17bc277263
     cmd --CALLS--> New in 0e17bc277263
     cmd --CALLS--> New in 0e17bc277263
     cmd --CALLS--> Greeter.Shout in 0e17bc277263

The caller column is the bare package name 'cmd' — useless. Three different functions in package cmd call Hello/New/Shout, but the output collapses them all into 'cmd'. Should be the calling function/method qualified by file + line, e.g.:
  cmd/root.go:18 RunE --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello

Without the calling function, the cross-repo edges section is decorative rather than navigable.
close_reason: Extended resolveCrossRepoNode to return the node's kind + file_path + line, then taught formatCrossRepoNode to render both sides of each cross-repo edge with: (a) 'package ' prefix when the caller resolved to a package node (signalling the underlying parser limitation — cross-ref solov2-9rc2 — so users know not to hunt for a missing function name), (b) 'in <file_path>[:<line>]' suffix when known. Verified end-to-end against the junior journey: 'veska context Greeter.Hello --repo lib' previously rendered each cross-repo edge as 'cmd --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello in 0e17bc277263' (four indistinguishable rows) and now renders 'package cmd in /tmp/junior/greetcli/cmd/root.go --CALLS--> Greeter.Hello in /tmp/junior/greetlib/greet.go in 0e17bc277263' (each row distinguishable by file). The bare 'cmd' caller is still the package grain because the parser doesn't yet bind calls inside cobra Command{Run: func(...){...}} anonymous functions — that's the deeper graph-side bug (solov2-9rc2) which this rendering fix surfaces honestly to the user.

title: co-locate backups under VESKA_HOME so a single rm clears all state (ylgd follow-up)
description: Companion to the immediate solov2-ylgd doc fix: rather than leaving backups in ~/.veska-backups (which survives 'rm -rf ~/.veska'), default the backup directory to ${VESKA_HOME}/backups. Migration plan: (1) on first backup-create after upgrade, if ~/.veska-backups exists and ${VESKA_HOME}/backups is empty, symlink or move; (2) keep --backup-dir flag as the override; (3) restore should look in both locations during the transition. Also consider 'veska reset --hard' that wipes data+backups+savings+config with a y/N prompt. Affects: cmd/veska/backup.go, cmd/veska/restore.go, cmd/veska/doctor.go (backup dir scan).
close_reason: New config.DefaultBackupDir() = $VESKA_HOME/backups (was ~/.veska-backups). config.LegacyBackupDir() returns the prior path for read-side fallback. Writers (backup create, prune, pre-migration auto-snapshot in sqlite/migrations.go) target the canonical location; readers (backup list, restore --latest, doctor backup) prefer canonical and fall back to legacy when canonical is empty so existing users see their tarballs post-upgrade. Init summary updated. Live e2e: VESKA_HOME=/tmp/v shows backups at /tmp/v/backups/; an existing ~/.veska-backups still lists via fallback. Tests: TestDefaultBackupDir_UnderVeskaHome, TestLegacyBackupDir_HomeRelative.

title: init: junior-friendly explanations missing (what is model2vec? where is data?)
description: 'veska init -y' output: 'embedder: model2vec(potion-code-16M) (in-process, fat)' — to a first-time user, 'fat' and the model name are jargon. Recommend a one-line gloss: 'embedder: in-process CPU model (no Ollama required); switch to Ollama via VESKA_EMBEDDER=ollama'. Same for 'data: /home/jrose/.veska' — add 'override with VESKA_HOME' so they know that path is configurable.
close_reason: Init output now says 'local CPU, model baked into the binary; no Ollama required — set VESKA_EMBEDDER=ollama to switch' instead of '(in-process, fat)', and the data line shows '(override with VESKA_HOME)'. Doctor's string ('in-process') unchanged because its tests pin it. cmd/veska/init.go

title: cleanup: backups in ~/.veska-backups persist after wiping ~/.veska
description: Junior wanted a clean start: 'rm -rf ~/.veska' wipes the daemon state, but ~/.veska-backups/ (containing tarballs from earlier sessions) survives. Either (a) document the full set of wipe locations, (b) add 'veska reset --hard' that nukes data + backups + savings + config with a y/N prompt, or (c) co-locate backups under ~/.veska/backups/ so a single rm clears everything.
close_reason: Init summary now prints 'backups: ~/.veska-backups (kept separate from data — both must be wiped for a clean reset)' so a junior who does 'rm -rf ~/.veska' immediately sees the second location. Structural co-location ('veska reset --hard' / move backups under VESKA_HOME) tracked as solov2-n57f follow-up.

title: duplicate vulnerable_dependency findings for aliased advisories (GHSA + GO-)
description: Adding github.com/dgrijalva/jwt-go produced 2 findings: GHSA-w73w-5m7g-f7qc (high) and GO-2020-0017 (medium) — same vulnerability, different OSV IDs (one is an alias of the other in OSV.dev). Should de-duplicate by CVE/aliases or at least co-locate aliased findings and report the highest severity.
close_reason: OSV adapter now reads the 'aliases' field, unions advisories per package via union-find, and keeps one canonical finding per equivalence class (GHSA > CVE > GO-/PYSEC). Suppressed IDs are surfaced on VulnFinding.Aliases and rendered in the finding message as 'aliases: ...'. Test pins: jwt-go GHSA+GO twin yields 1 finding, AdvisoryID=GHSA-w73w-..., Aliases=[CVE-2020-26160, GO-2020-0017]. Files: internal/infrastructure/vulnsource/osv/osv.go, internal/core/ports/vulnsource.go, internal/application/checks/vulnscan.go

title: search: numeric scores (score/norm) are unexplained to first-time users
description: 'veska search' prints 'score=0.0197 norm=1.00' alongside each hit. Junior users have no calibration: is 0.0197 good? CLAUDE.md (project) notes top RRF scores cluster around 0.016-0.033. Two options: (a) hide the numeric score by default and rely on the qualitative labels (top/strong/...); (b) add a one-line legend at the bottom of the output. Same applies to --json output documentation.
close_reason: Search output now prints a one-line legend before results: '# tier: top|strong|weak (relative to this query); score: post-fusion RRF; norm: score / top_hit_score'. First-time users get calibration without re-reading docs. cmd/veska/search.go

title: 'doctor status' says config=degraded pre-install but the cause is 'no service installed'
description: Right after 'veska init' (before service install/start) 'veska doctor status' reports: 'status: degraded — daemon is not running (egress=stopped); on-disk checks: ... config=degraded ...'. The word 'config=degraded' makes a new user think their config.toml is broken. The real reason is that the service-state file hasn't been written yet by 'veska service install'. Either rename that check from 'config' to 'service_state'/'install', or suppress its degraded flag while egress=stopped to avoid double-reporting the same cause.
close_reason: When veska.db is missing AND the daemon has never been started (no broken-marker, no live sockets), the config probe now reports 'pending' instead of 'degraded' — that's the expected state between 'veska init' and first 'veska service start'. The rollup line still leads with 'daemon is not running' so the actual cause is up front. Verified: VESKA_HOME=$tmp veska doctor status now reads 'embedder=healthy, config=pending, ingestion=...'.

title: Auto-link low-severity findings dominate on trivial new repos
description: On a brand-new repo with ~5 functions (main, Execute, Run, rootCmd, init), the autolink rule produces 4 low-severity findings flagging main/Execute/rootCmd as 'similar to' each other. They're hidden behind --include-low by default, but on a clean onboarding 'findings list' the headline reads '6 findings: 1 high, 1 medium, 4 low' which is alarming. Suggest either (a) suppress autolink under a minimum-corpus threshold (skip if fewer than N exported symbols total) or (b) phrase the headline so the 4 low are clearly the autolink class.
close_reason: When ≥80% of low-severity findings share a single rule (typically auto-link on small repos), the 'showing N finding(s): … 4 low' headline now annotates the dominant rule: '4 low (auto-link)'. Diverse low-severity mixes fall back to the original unannotated count. Verified: greetcli now shows '7 finding(s): 1 high, 1 medium, 4 low (auto-link), 1 info'.

title: eng_get_call_chain direction=in returns package nodes as 'callers'
description: For 'eng_get_call_chain {symbol:Run, direction:in}' on greetcli, the returned nodes include both TestRun (correct caller) and 'cmd' (the *package* containing Execute, not a function). The CALLS edge points cmd-package → Run, which is semantically wrong (packages don't call functions). This is likely an unresolved-call fallback or import-graph artifact. Either filter package nodes from CALLS-edge results or relabel the edge kind (IMPORTS / REFERENCED_BY) so callers can distinguish a real call site from a coarse approximation.
close_reason: eng_get_call_chain direction=in (and both) now filters out CALLS edges that originate from container nodes (package/file/module/chunk) — those are coarse extractor approximations, not real call sites. Function/method callers still appear. Verified: 'Run' direction=in now returns just TestRun, not the misleading 'cmd' package.

title: eng_find_owner accepts file_path/path but not symbol/node_id
description: Most eng_* tools accept a symbol-or-node_id pair (find_symbol, get_blast_radius, get_call_chain, get_context_pack). eng_find_owner only accepts file_path / path / repo_id — calling it with symbol returns 'unknown parameter "symbol" (allowed: file_path, path, repo_id)'. Owner lookup is naturally a per-symbol question (who owns ParseConfig?). Either add symbol/node_id support (resolve to defining file under the hood) or document the asymmetry in CLAUDE.md so agents stop guessing.
close_reason: eng_find_owner now accepts symbol or node_id in addition to file_path/path. Resolution: node_id matches full id or >=8-char prefix; symbol matches symbol_path exactly or trailing-dot suffix (e.g. 'Shout' → 'pkg.Shout'); ambiguous symbol returns a clear error directing to file_path/node_id. Schema and description updated. Tested live against /tmp/junior/greetlib.

title: eng_get_dirty_blast_radius returns nodes with empty name/kind/file_path
description: After editing a file but not committing, eng_get_dirty_blast_radius returns entries that include real symbols plus several stubs like {node_id:..., name:'', kind:'', file_path:'', distance:0}. These look like unresolved-or-pending nodes leaked through the response shape. Either filter them or attach a 'pending' marker so callers can render them sensibly. Repro JSON captured in junior-engineer journey 2026-05-27.
close_reason: Added Pending bool to blastradius.Entry and propagated to blastEntryDTO as 'pending'. When NodeLookup can't hydrate a node id at call time (eventually-consistent gap during reparse), the entry now carries pending=true so callers can render 'unresolved' rather than treat empty name/kind/file_path as a real symbol. Verified tests pass; live ENG call shows pending omitted when all hydrate.

title: 'deps list' shows empty VERSION column for some modules
description:
On /tmp/junior/greetcli with go.mod requiring golang.org/x/text v0.3.7, 'veska deps list' renders:
  MODULE                      VERSION  USAGE  TOP_SYMBOLS
  github.com/junior/greetlib  v0.0.0   1      Shout
  golang.org/x/text/language           1      Parse
The x/text row has no version. Suspect: the row is keyed by import path 'golang.org/x/text/language' but the version lives on the module 'golang.org/x/text'. Resolve the import path → module path and report the module version.
close_reason: depsVersions in cli/daemon/wire.go now walks back the import path (e.g. golang.org/x/text/language → golang.org/x/text) until a go.mod require line matches, so sub-packages inherit their parent module's version. Verified: x/text/language now shows v0.3.7 in 'veska deps list'.

title: 'veska deps list' rejects positional path argument
description: Other commands take a path: 'veska repo add /path', 'veska reindex /path'. But 'veska deps list /path' errors with 'unknown command "/path" for veska deps list'. You must use '--repo <id>' and an id-from-list workflow. Inconsistent. Either accept a positional path or auto-detect cwd-relative repo like 'deps index' does ('default: the cwd-resolved repo').
close_reason: 'veska deps list' now accepts an optional positional <id-or-path> argument (resolved via resolveRepoArg, same as repo add/reindex). Verified: 'veska deps list /tmp/junior/greetcli' and 'veska deps list 52d6c257dfe2' both work; bare form still auto-resolves cwd.

title: 'veska backup list' is missing (users expect it next to create/prune/verify)
description: 'veska backup' subcommands are: create, prune, verify. There is no 'list'. Running 'veska backup list' prints the parent help, which looks like the command silently succeeded. Backups are written to ~/.veska-backups/ — add 'veska backup list' that prints filename, size, mtime, and which retention bucket each falls under.
close_reason: Added 'veska backup list' subcommand listing tarballs in --backup-dir (default ~/.veska-backups) sorted newest-first, with name/kind (user|pre-migration)/size/mtime columns and --json mode. Tested live: 'veska backup list' shows the 2 backups created during the journey + this run.

title: Veska vs Semble/Cursor/Sourcegraph external A/B benchmarks
description: Filed 2026-05-27 as the deferred half of the original solov2-kcmo scope. solov2-kcmo was narrowed to multi-repo Veska vs grep+read (which extends solov2-wise's harness); this bead carries the external-tool comparisons that the original kcmo wanted but require access/install decisions we haven't made: Semble (subscription? CLI?), Cursor's index (closed format? what's queryable?), Sourcegraph (cloud account or local install — both heavy). Needs design pass + access decisions BEFORE any implementation. Output should slot into the same chart-shape solov2-kcmo produces so the headline can mix internal grep + external commercial tools fairly.

title: Rip out modernc.org/sqlite opt-in driver
description: Per solov2-jkgp follow-up: mattn is the default and modernc's stated rationale (CGO_ENABLED=0 cross-compile) is moot because tree-sitter requires cgo anyway. Remove the sqlite_modernc build tag, driver_modernc.go shim, dead blank imports of modernc.org/sqlite across ~22 test files, modernc dep from go.mod, and stale doc references in CLAUDE.md/Makefile/dbbench. Leave jkgp-runs/RESULTS.md historical snapshot intact.
close_reason: Removed the sqlite_modernc build tag and driver_modernc.go shim from internal/infrastructure/sqlite/sqldriver/ and tools/loadtest/dbbench/; stripped dead _ "modernc.org/sqlite" blank imports across ~22 test files; cleaned stale modernc references in code comments (registry.go busy-detect, tokenize/symbol.go, pools.go DSN, pools_test.go, graph_repo_test.go, embedder_test.go, aliases_test.go); flipped dbbench verdict incumbent from modernc to mattn; updated CLAUDE.md, Makefile (SQLITE_TAGS comment + eval-dbbench), tools/loadtest/dbbench/README.md, and docs/design/08-data-and-storage/README.md. go.mod has modernc.org/sqlite still indirect (pulled by zombiezen.com/go/sqlite in the dbbench harness only — unavoidable for that comparison harness). Build green (CGO_ENABLED=1 with sqlite_fts5), vet green, layercheck OK, all touched-package tests pass.

title: Measure whether mattn swap moves real veska latency (vs synthetic SQLite ops)
description:
solov2-6e5r bench (tools/loadtest/dbbench/RESULTS.md) showed mattn beats modernc on isolated SQLite workloads:

- bulk_ingest: 2.9x   - fts_query: 2.4x
- queue_poll:  2.3x   - rehydrate_scan: 1.4x
- graph_read:  1.4x   - promotion_tx p50: 1.5x (p95 worse: 65ms vs 27ms)

But those numbers are NOT a proxy for veska's user-visible latency, because veska's hot paths don't bottleneck on SQLite:

- Vector storage is in-memory (internal/infrastructure/vector/sqlitevec is misleadingly named — it's a map[string][]float32 plus a flat-scan or usearch HNSW). The semantic-search hot path never touches SQLite for the kNN.
- FTS is the lexical-fallback arm of eng_search_semantic; it runs in parallel with the vector arm and the RRF fusion completes when the slower of the two returns.
- Embedder roundtrips (Ollama/model2vec) dominate ingest wall-time, not the SQL insert.
- Rehydrate runs once per daemon start; a 12ms saving is invisible.
- promotion_tx happens on git-hook fire, not per-request — and mattn's p95 there is actually WORSE than modernc, which would matter more than the median improvement.

The realistic places a swap could pay off are:
- queue_poll: runs continuously in the post-promotion worker. 2x faster polling is real, but the poller sleeps between iterations anyway.
- bulk_ingest: cold scan of a new repo. 3x faster means a 100k-LOC repo ingests in N/3 seconds — user-visible iff embedder isn't already the bottleneck.

## DoD

Before any swap PR, MEASURE end-to-end against existing harnesses with the driver flipped:

- [ ] make eval-recall under both drivers — does p95 search latency change?
- [ ] make eval-revalidate-bench under both — does the 60s gate move meaningfully?
- [ ] make eval-queue-fuzz — does the budget-ms shrink under mattn?
- [ ] Cold-scan timing on a real ~100k-LOC repo, both drivers.

If end-to-end deltas are <10% the swap is not worth taking on cgo + losing CGO_ENABLED=0 cross-compile + the unexplained mattn p95 promotion_tx regression. Close as won't-do with the measurement table attached.

If a real workload moves >20%, then file the swap PR (one-line driver name change across the ~20 import sites; build flags flip).

## Out of scope

Don't bother with zombiezen — it needs an adapter rewrite, and even mattn (drop-in) hasn't proven its case yet.
notes: BLOCKED on solov2-14lw: cold-scan crashes on hugo (223k LOC) and prometheus (353k LOC) with UNIQUE constraint on nodes PK. Both modernc and mattn affected equally (application-level bug, not driver). Pivoting to fix 14lw first.
close_reason: Default flipped to mattn in 3a6f60a. End-to-end measurement (tools/loadtest/dbbench/jkgp-runs/RESULTS.md): hugo cold-scan 197.5s→121.1s (1.63x), reglet 16.1s→8.0s (2.0x), eval-revalidate-bench Combined10k 4297ms→1565ms (2.7x). Modernc remains opt-in via -tags=sqlite_modernc; CLAUDE.md updated. Test sweep green except the pre-existing TestDoctorStubSubcommandsExitZero/pipelines flake.

title: User-defined repo aliases (human-friendly names for repo_id)
description:
Let users alias a repo_id to an arbitrary human-friendly name, usable anywhere --repo accepts a repo_id today.

Motivation: today the only ways to reference a repo are full sha256 hex, 12-char short_id, an unambiguous hex prefix (>=4), or the absolute root path. None of these are memorable. Aliases give the same UX as git remotes — you name a thing once and call it that forever.

Distinct from the URL-canonical alias in solov2-kxo5: that one is automatic + URL-derived (origin remote of a path-registered repo aliases to the URL-hash so an ephemeral search --repo <url> resolves to the existing tracked row). This bead is user-set + arbitrary string.

Design pass still owed before implementation — capture-then-defer per conversation 2026-05-26. The bullets below are the working sketch, not final acceptance criteria.

Working sketch:
- Schema: new repo_aliases table (name TEXT PK, repo_id TEXT NOT NULL). Separate table not column so multiple aliases per repo are allowed and reversible.
- Resolution order in resolveCLIRepoID (cmd/veska/repo.go:142) and the MCP equivalent: full repo_id -> 12-char short_id -> user alias -> unambiguous prefix (>=4 chars). Alias beats prefix so a typed alias never gets shadowed by a colliding prefix.
- Reject aliases that look like a repo_id (hex-only — see looksLikeRepoID at repo.go:737), otherwise the alias shadows the short_id namespace.
- Commands: veska repo alias <name> <id-or-path-or-url> to set; veska repo unalias <name> to remove; ALIAS column in veska repo list.
- MCP: eng_set_repo_alias / eng_remove_repo_alias. Every existing tool accepting repo_id resolves aliases via a shared helper (one change, not N).
- Applies to both tracked and ephemeral (kxo5) repos — same path, no special case. Alias outlives an evicted cache clone because it points at the URL-derived repo_id, which is stable.

Auto-suggest UX (decided 2026-05-26): on veska repo add, prompt the user 'alias this to <basename>? [y/N/<custom>]' — y accepts the suggested name, N skips, any other input is treated as a custom alias. TTY-only; non-TTY (scripted/MCP) skips the prompt entirely. Rejected the alternative of silent auto-aliasing (too much magic, surprising collisions).

Open for design pass:
- Suggested-name source for URL-registered repos (owner/name? name only? prefer name unless colliding?)
- What happens if the suggested name collides with an existing alias (skip prompt? offer suggestion-with-suffix?)
- Whether MCP tools should return alias in responses (e.g. eng_list_repos includes aliases array)
- Should alias-create on existing alias replace or error?
close_reason: Migration 0014 adds repo_aliases (name PK, repo_id FK CASCADE). internal/repo/aliases.go exports SetAlias/RemoveAlias/LookupAlias/AliasesByRepoID + ValidateAliasName + SuggestAliasNames; List/Get on registry.Record now populates the new Aliases []string field. Resolver progression (CLI + MCP) is: full id -> 12-char short_id -> alias -> >=4-char prefix; alias beats prefix so explicit names never get shadowed. ValidateAliasName rejects empty/whitespace/hex-only-and->=4-chars (would shadow short_id namespace). CLI: 'veska repo alias <name> <id|prefix|alias|url>' with --force, 'veska repo unalias <name>', new ALIAS column in 'repo list'. MCP: eng_set_repo_alias / eng_remove_repo_alias; eng_list_repos / eng_get_repo include 'aliases' array per repo. Auto-suggest prompt on 'repo add' (both path and URL forms): TTY-only, offers basename or owner/name fallback on collision; y/N/<custom> UX. Decisions per design pass: (1) URL form suggests name; on collision suggests <owner>-<name>; (2) collision fallback is the suggestion (next free wins, both-taken skips); (3) MCP responses expose aliases; (4) re-alias requires --force. Tests added at every layer (repo CRUD + cascade, prompt scenarios, table column). go fmt/fix + vet + layercheck + full test suite all pass.

title: vendor index phase 2: $GOMODCACHE source + auto-trigger on go.sum + embeddings
description:
Follow-up to solov2-bchl (phase 1 shipped vendor/-only indexing via veska deps index).

PHASE 2 SCOPE:
1. $GOMODCACHE indexing. When a module is NOT vendored, fall back to its module-cache directory: \$GOMODCACHE/cache/download/<escaped-import-path>/@v/<version>.zip OR \$GOMODCACHE/<escaped-import-path>@<version>/ (the unzipped source). Use golang.org/x/mod/module Escape() for the directory name (BurntSushi → !burnt!sushi convention). Version comes from go.mod via manifest.ReadGoMod (already imported).

2. Auto-trigger on go.sum delta. The fsnotify watcher already observes file changes per registered repo. Hook a handler on go.sum writes:
   - read the new go.mod, list required modules
   - diff vs the set of currently-indexed (repo_id, module_path) pairs in nodes WHERE external=1
   - for each added module: trigger IndexVendorModule (or its module-cache equivalent)
   - for each removed module: DELETE FROM nodes WHERE repo_id=? AND external=1 AND file_path LIKE "<module>%"

3. Embeddings for external nodes. Currently none — eng_search_semantic does not see vendor symbols. Cost-controlled:
   - opt-in per module via config block in config.toml
   - OR auto-embed the top-N most-called external symbols (the eng_list_dependencies usage counts are already computed)
   - skip chunks for external nodes (chunks are an embedding-internal concept and embedding them for vendor code doubles storage)

4. Per-module size budget. \$GOMODCACHE can be gigabytes. Add a config knob to cap nodes per module + warn-and-skip when exceeded.

5. MCP tool eng_index_external. Today \`veska deps index\` uses the direct-write DB path. For multi-tenant safety we should route through the daemon when it is running, falling back to direct write otherwise (mirrors repo add's pattern).

ACCEPTANCE:
- module not vendored: indexer falls back to \$GOMODCACHE with the right version
- go.sum write triggers re-indexing within fsnotify debounce
- config block enables/disables embeddings per module
- size budget enforced; exceeded modules logged with the budget number
- MCP tool eng_index_external registered; CLI prefers it when daemon is up

title: Benchmark SQLite drivers: modernc vs mattn vs zombiezen
description:
Benchmark the three viable Go SQLite drivers against veska's actual storage workloads to decide whether to swap off modernc.org/sqlite for better latency/throughput.

## Background

Production currently uses modernc.org/sqlite (pure-Go, no cgo) everywhere:
- internal/infrastructure/sqlite/pools.go (main pool)
- internal/infrastructure/sqlite/migrations.go
- internal/backup/{create,verify}.go
- internal/doctor/postpromotionqueue.go
- cmd/veska/hook_runner.go

modernc is the slowest of the mainstream Go drivers (typically 2–4x behind cgo drivers on write-heavy and FTS paths). We chose it for the no-cgo build story.

Vectors do NOT live in SQLite (see internal/infrastructure/vector/sqlitevec — misleading name; it's an in-memory map). So the driver swap has no extension-compatibility constraint — sqlite-vec was spiked and rejected (tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/RESULTS.md, ADR-S0014/S0015).

## Candidates

- modernc.org/sqlite (current; pure-Go, database/sql)
- mattn/go-sqlite3 (cgo, database/sql — drop-in driver swap)
- zombiezen.com/go/sqlite (cgo, non-database/sql API — invasive, requires adapter rewrite, but typically fastest)

## Workloads to measure

Each against a freshly-seeded copy of a realistic veska.db:
1. Graph read — node + edge fetch by id (MCP read path; node_lookup_repo / edge_reader_repo)
2. FTS5 query — lexical_repo path used by eng_search_semantic
3. Promotion transaction — multi-statement write via PromotionStore + FTS + embedding-refs sinks
4. Post-promotion queue poll — queue repo SELECT/UPDATE loop
5. Bulk ingest — ingester writing nodes/edges in batches
6. Rehydrate scan — large sequential read of node_embeddings join node_embedding_refs (driver-sensitive blob scan; runs on every daemon start)

## Deliverable

- Harness at tools/loadtest/dbbench/ behind the `eval` build tag
- Parameterized by driver, runs each workload, reports ops/sec + p50/p95 per driver per workload
- RESULTS.md with verdict + recommendation
- If a winner is clear, a follow-up issue to land the swap (mattn is drop-in; zombiezen needs adapter rewrite)

## Out of scope

- Switching engines (DuckDB / Pebble / Bolt) — separate question, would lose FTS5
- Vector storage — already settled, not driver-bound
close_reason: dbbench harness landed at tools/loadtest/dbbench/. Pure-Go (modernc+zombiezen) via 'make eval-dbbench'; cgo (mattn) via 'make eval-dbbench-cgo'. Sample RESULTS.md committed: mattn wins 5/6 workloads with parity pragmas — 2.9x bulk_ingest, 2.4x fts_query. Follow-up solov2-jkgp tracks the swap evaluation.

title: Extend veska init --agent MCP-config registration to codex/opencode
description:
solov2-zo0w shipped MCP-server registration for Claude Code (.mcp.json) and Cursor (.cursor/mcp.json). The two other harnesses zo0w explicitly called out — codex and opencode — currently get instruction-file-only writes; the MCP config still has to be hand-edited.

Codex (OpenAI CLI): MCP config lives at ~/.codex/config.toml in [mcp_servers.<name>] blocks. User-level, not project-level — different scope semantics from veska init --agent which is per-project. Decisions needed:
- Add a separate 'veska install-mcp codex' for user-level? Or extend --agent with --scope=user?
- TOML merge is trickier than JSON merge (modernc.org/sqlite TOML... or use a small bespoke parser).

OpenCode (Sourcegraph CLI): MCP config in .opencode.json or opencode.config.json at project root. Need to confirm exact filename + schema before writing — incorrect guesses would corrupt existing configs. Look at the official docs and a real opencode project before implementing.

Acceptance:
- 'veska init --agent codex' registers veska in ~/.codex/config.toml (preserves other server blocks)
- 'veska init --agent opencode' registers veska in the project's opencode config
- Idempotency + 'updated when path changes' semantics match the existing Claude / Cursor path
- Documented scope: project-level for opencode (matching cursor), explicit prompt for codex's user-level write
- Tests mirror the .mcp.json test set (creates, preserves others, idempotent, updates)

title: findings list across all repos requires --all OR repo_id; default UX papercut
description: With 2 repos registered, `veska findings list` errors with 'repo_id is required (2 repos registered)'. Most multi-repo users want a unified view; --all should be the default when stdout is a terminal, and the daemon should suggest --all in the error message (it already names the count).
close_reason: When neither --repo nor --all is set and autoResolveRepo can't pick a single repo from cwd, the CLI now falls back to listing across every registered repo with a stderr breadcrumb ('no --repo and cwd outside any registered repo; listing findings across all repos'). Eliminates the 'repo_id is required (N repos registered)' papercut on multi-repo setups without breaking explicit --repo / --all callers.

title: Junior journey docs: missing 'first 5 minutes' walkthrough
description:
A first-time user runs veska --help and sees 24 subcommands. The natural ordering (init -> service install -> service start -> repo add --wait -> search/symbol/context -> wiki) is not documented anywhere reachable from --help. `veska init -y` succeeds but never tells you the next step is 'service install/start' to make --wait/live-watch actually work.

Add either:
- a 'veska tour' subcommand that prints a 5-step quickstart
- or have `veska init` print the 3-line next-steps block (service install, service start, repo add) as part of its success output
close_reason: 'veska init' now ends with a 3-step 'next steps' block: install/start service, add a repo with --wait, run search. Plus a one-liner pointing at the per-agent init for MCP setup. Replaces the prior 'ready' line that left juniors guessing what to do next.

title: Response shape drift: eng_find_todos / eng_list_findings omit degraded_reasons
description: The README's 'Conventions across the tool surface' promises every tool emits the same envelope ('Empty result collections serialize as [], never omitted', plus the degraded_reasons field on every response). Observed responses: eng_find_todos -> {"todos":[]} ; eng_list_findings -> {"findings":[]} — both omit degraded_reasons, while sibling tools (eng_find_symbol, eng_search_semantic, eng_list_repos, eng_get_hot_zone, etc.) include degraded_reasons:[]. Fix: emit degraded_reasons:[] uniformly from the JSON-RPC layer (or via a shared response wrapper) so callers can rely on the documented invariant.
close_reason: TodosResponse and the list_findings response now emit degraded_reasons:[] uniformly, matching every other query tool per the README's 'Conventions across the tool surface' contract. Regression tests pin the empty-array shape on both tools.

title: veska init OSV prompt swallows non-TTY stdin without echoing default
description: 'veska init' prints 'Enable OSV vulnerability scanning? [Y/n] ' and proceeds to write the [vuln_source] block, but when stdin is non-TTY (CI, agent harness, piped install) it neither echoes the chosen default nor waits — output shows the prompt sitting on the same line as 'veska initialized'. Junior reading the output cannot tell whether vuln scanning was enabled until they inspect ~/.veska/config.toml. Fix: when stdin is non-TTY, skip the prompt entirely and log 'OSV scanning enabled (default; non-interactive)' OR honour a --enable-vuln/--no-vuln flag. Also flush the prompt before printing the success summary so interactive runs aren't visually mashed together.
close_reason: When stdin is non-TTY (CI, piped install, agent harness), 'veska init' now suppresses the [Y/n] prompt entirely and prints an explicit 'OSV vulnerability scanning: enabled (default; non-interactive stdin)' line so the caller can read the summary and tell what happened. TTY callers see the same prompt as before. Detection via os.Stdin.Stat() ModeCharDevice.

title: Secrets scanner flags AWS's documented dummy keys as critical
description: The README/docs and AWS's own published examples use AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE (access key) and wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY (secret) — these are intentionally publicized placeholders. The gitleaks-based scanner currently flags AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE as critical and the matching secret as high. Most scanners allowlist this canonical example pair (and similar GitHub/Stripe sample tokens). Add an allowlist of well-known documentation placeholder keys so the first-run finding list isn't dominated by false positives that look real. Repro: junior journey created cmd/auth.go with both constants; both produced findings 4327444f… (critical) and 46365b80… (high).
close_reason: Added a strict-equality docs-example allowlist (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE and wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY for now) checked across all three detection paths (local regex, gitleaks, high-entropy). The first-run junior journey no longer surfaces these vendor-published placeholders. Easy to extend the map as more vendor-canonical examples come up.

title: findings show: accept --repo flag (parity with findings list)
description: `veska findings list` accepts --repo for cross-repo scoping. `veska findings show <id>` rejects it ('unknown flag: --repo'). Symmetry: show should accept the same scoping flags as list. Tiny cobra fix.
close_reason: Added --repo flag to findings show, threaded through eng_get_finding schema + handler as optional assertion (mismatch = NotFound, short-id prefix accepted). No auto-resolution from cwd to avoid silent scoping. Tests cover match/mismatch/prefix.

title: repo: unify `repo remove` and `repo prune` under one verb
description:
Today there are two cleanup verbs: `veska repo remove <id>` (deregister one) and `veska repo prune` (drop registered repos whose root dir no longer exists). Same destination, different invocation, --help doesn't explain the split. Unify under `veska repo remove`:
- `veska repo remove <id|path>` — remove one
- `veska repo remove --missing` — remove all whose root no longer exists (current prune behavior)
- `veska repo remove --all` — wipe registry (with confirmation)
Keep `prune` as a hidden alias for one release with a deprecation notice.
close_reason: Unified: 'veska repo remove' now accepts <id|path>, --missing (old prune behavior), or --all (with confirmation). --dry-run honored for bulk paths. 'repo prune' kept as a hidden deprecation alias that forwards to remove --missing with a stderr notice; remove after one release cycle. Build/vet/tests pass.

title: veska --version flag (alias for `veska version`)
description: Common ergonomic miss: `veska --version` errors with 'unknown flag: --version'. Only `veska version` subcommand works. Wire --version and -v at the root command to call the version subcommand. Tiny cobra change.
close_reason: Added cobra Version field on root + SetVersionTemplate to give a compact one-liner; multi-line 'veska version' subcommand untouched. Test pins behavior.

title: eng_get_context_pack accepts symbol/task_id but not node_id (asymmetric)
description:
Every other graph-anchored MCP tool (eng_get_blast_radius, eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_node, eng_search_similar) accepts node_id. eng_get_context_pack is the exception: schema requires exactly one of symbol or task_id; passing node_id errors with -32602 'exactly one of symbol or task_id is required'.

Journey friction: agents typically have node_id in hand (from a prior eng_find_symbol or eng_search_semantic call) and now have to either re-resolve back to a name string or add a special case. The bundled CLAUDE.md snippet for context_pack even says 'parity with eng_find_symbol' for call_chain — context_pack lacks that parity.

Fix: accept node_id as a third anchor in eng_get_context_pack, mutually exclusive with symbol/task_id.
close_reason: DoD verified: node_id is third anchor; mutex covers all combinations; Assembler.ForNode added; schema retains additionalProperties:false (commit cfdccdc)

title: tree-sitter parse-failure false positive on revalidate_repo_test.go
description:
cold-scan + reindex both surface one parse-failure finding:

  parse failure in internal/infrastructure/sqlite/revalidate_repo_test.go at line 90: syntax error

But 'go vet ./internal/infrastructure/sqlite/...' parses the file cleanly (only complains about an undefined helper from another file — semantic, not syntactic). 'go build ./...' is clean too. Line 90 is ordinary code:

  f.insertNode(t, "n-stale", f.branch, "pkg/a.go", "h-current")

Tree-sitter is wrongly flagging valid Go. Worth checking whether the smacker/go-tree-sitter version pinned in go.mod has a known bug around test-file constructs or pointer-call helpers, and either bump or add a fallback parser. Also: a single false-positive parse-failure pollutes the findings list for every future scan.
close_reason: DoD verified: tree-sitter errors cross-checked against go/parser; smacker grammar lags Go 1.26 new(value) — go/parser is authoritative; false positive on revalidate_repo_test.go suppressed (commit 1ad2de2)

title: doctor 'status: healthy' contradicts eng_get_status 'degraded: embeddings_pending'
description:
During cold-scan post-promote, with ~6.5k embeddings still pending, two health surfaces disagree:

  ./bin/veska doctor status
  -> status: healthy (embedder=healthy, egress=healthy, config=healthy, ingestion=healthy, queue=healthy)

  eng_get_status (MCP)
  -> {status: 'degraded', degraded_reasons: ['embeddings_pending'], pending_embeds: 6480}

A junior runs doctor expecting an authoritative go/no-go; gets 'healthy'; then their agent's first eng_search_semantic returns 'degraded_reasons:[embeddings_pending]' and they wonder if something broke.

Reconcile: either doctor should report a 'degraded' line when pending_embeds > 0 (or include an 'embedding_backlog' subsystem in the rollup), or eng_get_status should not classify backlog as 'degraded' once the backfill is healthy.
close_reason: DoD verified: doctor surfaces embedding_backlog (drained/backfilling) without promoting rollup; eng_get_status contract preserved with regression test (commits d79c369 + b003c55)

title: eng_promote_repo inputSchema omits branch/git_sha/actor_* params it actually honours
description:
tools/list reports eng_promote_repo properties as just {repo_id, root_path}. But calling the tool with branch, git_sha, actor_kind, actor_id (e.g. {repo_id, branch:'main', git_sha:'<sha>', actor_kind:'human', actor_id:'junior'}) is accepted and returns {files_promoted:1}.

Either:
  (a) the extras are no-ops and should be removed from the codepath, or
  (b) they are honoured and the schema is wrong — agents that read the schema will never know to pass actor attribution.

Schema-truth divergence is hostile to schema-driven MCP clients (we just shipped publish-inputSchema for all tools in solov2-jtl5.9 — this is the second mile).
close_reason: DoD verified: branch/git_sha/actor_kind/actor_id now published with enum validation; handler honours them; additionalProperties:false retained (commit d87d973)

title: repo add 'cold scan running' message suggests --wait on wrong command
description:
After 'veska repo add <path>' the success message says:

  cold scan running in the background — `veska repo list` shows status; re-run with `--wait` to block until it finishes, or `tail ~/.veska/logs/daemon.log` for live progress

But '--wait' is a flag of 'veska repo add', not 'veska repo list'. 'veska repo list --wait' errors with 'unknown flag: --wait'. A junior reading the message naturally tries it on 'list' (the just-mentioned command). Either reword to 'list shows status; re-run `veska repo add <path> --wait` to block', or add a --wait flag to 'repo list' that polls until all walks/embeds are done.
close_reason: DoD verified: repo add hint now correctly attributes --wait to 'repo add' (commit 155d586)

title: 'veska repo remove <path>' returns 'repo not found' when given the same path used for add
description: Repro: 'veska repo add /tmp/x' succeeds. 'veska repo remove /tmp/x' returns 'repo remove: repo not found: /tmp/x'. Only 'veska repo remove <short_id>' works. Asks: repo remove should accept the same identifiers repo add does (filesystem path); error 'repo not found' is misleading when the path is in fact registered (it's a lookup gap, not a missing-repo state).
close_reason: repo remove now accepts a filesystem path (same identifier shape repo add takes). resolveRepoArg() resolves a path to abs form and looks it up in the registry; hex-only strings (repo_id / short_id prefix) pass through unchanged. Not-found error names the resolved abs path so users see exactly what was looked up.

title: vuln finding 'rule' column shows 'vuln' but docs/UX expect 'vulnerable_dependency'
description: README says: 'vulnerable go.mod dependencies via the OSV.dev advisory database.' First instinct was 'veska findings list --rule vulnerable_dependency' which returned 'no findings'. Actual rule name is 'vuln'. As a naive user I assumed vuln_source was broken. Either rename the rule to 'vulnerable_dependency' (clearer) or document the short name and add it as an example in 'veska findings list --help'.
close_reason: Rule renamed from 'vuln' to 'vulnerable_dependency' to match documentation and user intuition. Existing stored findings keep the old rule name in audit history until the next promotion re-emits them. The MCP-test-only rule string 'vuln' (in tools_findings_test.go) is just test fixture data, unrelated to the production rule.

title: 'veska doctor' (no subcommand) should run the status rollup, not just print help
description: As a first-time user, 'veska doctor' was my obvious next call after install/start to confirm everything is healthy. It returns the help screen. The natural default is the 'status' rollup, which already exists at 'veska doctor status'. Make bare 'veska doctor' alias to 'veska doctor status'.
close_reason: Bare 'veska doctor' now runs the status rollup; --json flag preserved on parent so 'veska doctor --json' is equivalent to 'veska doctor status --json'. Subcommand fallback ('doctor blah' → 'unknown command') still works.

title: veska context <missing> says '0 node(s)' instead of a friendly miss message
description:
When  is run, the output is:
  context for UnknownSymbol (0 node(s))

A junior would expect:
  no symbol named 'UnknownSymbol' found in this repo
  hint: try  to fuzzy-search, or check --repo

The equivalent  returns the cleaner 'no matches'. Align the two.
close_reason: zero-node context-pack now prints a friendly 'no symbol named X found' + 'try veska symbol X' hint, matching the symbol cmd's voice. Verified end-to-end.

title: veska findings list lacks pagination/totals — 32 findings printed as 32 lines with no summary
description:
On the tack repo after enabling vuln scanner,  prints 32 raw rows with NO header summary like 'showing 32 findings' or pagination flags. By contrast ○ solov2-3a52 ● P1 Tree-sitter: add language parsers beyond Go
○ solov2-9rc2 ● P1 [epic] epic: resolve chained-selector CALLS edges (s.field.method)
○ solov2-cdw3 ● P1 Packaging: single fat binary with embedded model2vec weights
○ solov2-mdn3 ● P1 [bug] veska reindex runs cold scan in-process, racing the running daemon → SQLITE_BUSY
○ solov2-to8m ● P1 Default to model2vec backend; demote Ollama to opt-in
○ solov2-zo0w ● P1 One-line install + auto-config for popular agent harnesses
○ solov2-1hnh ● P2 Curate MCP tool surface so agents discover moat features
○ solov2-2g4r ● P2 MCP tool: find_related(file, line) for span-based semantic neighbors
○ solov2-71xq ● P2 Sharpen product positioning: pick a wedge among search / graph / workflow
○ solov2-bchl ● P2 Index vendor/ and Go module cache symbols into the graph
○ solov2-en47 ● P2 [bug] cold scan progress stalls visibly mid-walk on solov2 (525→535 files in 176s)
○ solov2-hl70 ● P2 Search-score legibility: normalize or rerank RRF output
○ solov2-illi ● P2 Token-savings telemetry and reporting
○ solov2-jlws ● P2 eng_list_dependencies: surface go.mod deps with call-site usage counts
○ solov2-qwpt ● P2 [bug] eng_*_finding tools have inconsistent required params
○ solov2-sxxs ● P2 Publish benchmark numbers from eval-recall and friends
○ solov2-utyu ● P2 [epic] Junior Journey Round 2: fresh end-to-end UX audit
○ solov2-vdum ● P2 [bug] service install/start emit no output — silent success is confusing UX
○ solov2-w1ng ● P2 [bug] veska init does not create ~/.veska/config.toml — docs claim it does
○ solov2-z5o0 ● P2 [bug] eng_get_hot_zone reports no_post_registration_commits as degraded — confusing for fresh repos
◐ solov2-0k5h ● P3 Benchmark embedding models (model2vec + Ollama) on real codebases + publish comparison table
└── ◐ solov2-0k5h.9 ● P3 0k5h.9 Run -full with Ollama prose specialists; publish updated table
❄ solov2-1yev ● P3 Replace hand-rolled AST descent with tree-sitter query (.scm) API
○ solov2-9fsn ● P3 Version-pinned graph queries (--at <git-ref>)
○ solov2-bcn ● P3 eng_search_semantic has no cross-repo mode (repo_id required)
○ solov2-ebvg ● P3 [bug] eng_get_config.embed_model is empty string while .embedder shows the model
○ solov2-jtao ● P3 Veska agent-skill wrapper for non-MCP harnesses
○ solov2-kcmo ● P3 Competitive evals: A/B against grep+read, Semble, Cursor index, Sourcegraph
○ solov2-kxo5 ● P3 Accept remote git URL in repo registration / search
❄ solov2-nlg ● P3 Migrate embed queue lane to queue.Poller (remove noopEmbedHandler)
○ solov2-oqlr ● P3 config change for vuln_source requires manual daemon restart with no hot-reload signal
○ solov2-pwuu ● P3 LSP adapter over the daemon: expose veska to IDEs that don't speak MCP
○ solov2-q2az ● P3 Public repo, issue tracker, community surface
○ solov2-wise ● P3 Tokens-saved vs grep+read benchmark (semble-style), paired with recall
❄ solov2-xa8 ● P3 Edge-anchored revalidation path for auto-link findings
○ solov2-crn7 ● P4 Framework-aware extractor: surface CLI/router struct-literal vars as first-class command/route nodes
○ solov2-hi5 ● P4 Per-document-type embedder routing: model2vec for code, nomic for prose
❄ solov2-zcwa ● P4 Cross-repo IMPORTS-edge stubs (not just CALLS)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total: 38 issues (32 open, 2 in progress)

Status: ○ open  ◐ in_progress  ● blocked  ✓ closed  ❄ deferred shows counts and groupings.\n\nReal repos will produce hundreds of findings. Suggested adds:\n  --limit N (default ~25)\n  --severity-counts header line ('open: 1 critical, 2 high, 28 medium, 1 low')\n  --group-by rule|file\n  --order severity (currently order looks arbitrary)\n\nAlso: long messages wrap awkwardly because each finding includes the source line 'go.mod:151 [GHSA-…]: …' duplicated with the file column already showing 'go.mod'. Could drop the 'go.mod:N' prefix when file column has it.
close_reason: findings list now: (a) prints severity-counted header ('showing 31 finding(s): 1 critical, 3 high, 27 medium'); (b) sorts by severity (critical-first) then rule; (c) honours --limit (default 25) with '... N more' footer; (d) strips redundant 'go.mod:NNN ' prefix from messages when the FILE column already shows it. Verified live on tack.

title: config change for vuln_source requires manual daemon restart with no hot-reload signal
description:
Enabling the vuln scanner requires (1) creating ~/.veska/config.toml with a [vuln_source] block then (2) restarting the daemon (README explicitly calls this out). After restart we still need a third step (3) reindex or wait for next commit to actually scan the dep graph.

Three steps would be one step if the daemon either:
  a) watched its own config.toml (fsnotify) and re-read on change, OR
  b) accepted a SIGHUP / , OR
  c) had a 'veska config set vuln_source.provider=osv' subcommand that triggered the reload itself + auto-rescanned promoted repos.

Also: 'veska reindex' is the documented way to retroactively scan, but it's broken in this state — see solov2-mdn3.
close_reason: New 'veska config reload' subcommand restarts the daemon and re-promotes every registered repo so newly-enabled checks (like vuln-scan via [vuln_source]) surface findings retroactively without three separate commands. Verified live: restarts, waits for socket, dispatches eng_promote_repo per repo, reports ok/failed counts. True fsnotify-based hot-reload of config.toml is the deeper fix and is left as a follow-up if needed.

title: eng_get_config.embed_model is empty string while .embedder shows the model
description:
eng_get_config returns:
  "embedder": "model2vec(potion-code-16M)",
  "embed_model": "",

The empty 'embed_model' field is confusing — README documents VESKA_EMBED_MODEL as the env var that controls the model, so a junior expects this field to echo that value (e.g. 'potion-code-16M' or 'nomic-embed-text'). Either populate it consistently for every embedder, or drop the field.
close_reason: embed_model is now derived from the embedder marker when cfg.EmbedModel is unset, so the default model2vec path reports 'potion-code-16M' (matching the parens content in 'embedder'). Ollama path still wins when VESKA_EMBED_MODEL is set. Verified live: {embedder:'model2vec(potion-code-16M)', embed_model:'potion-code-16M'}.

title: eng_get_context_pack: response shape undocumented; no 'focus' key as readers expect
description: README documents which params each tool accepts but the response shape for eng_get_context_pack is undocumented. The actual shape returns top-level: branch, estimated_tokens, mode, nodes, open_findings, query, recent_commits, repo_id, tasks, token_budget, truncated — and 'focus' is encoded as nodes[?seed==true] rather than a dedicated key. Either: (a) add a 'focus' convenience field pointing to the seed node, or (b) document the shape (including the seed marker) in the README's MCP tools table. (b) is cheapest; (a) saves agents one filter pass per call.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: MCP convention violation: degraded_reasons serializes as null, not []
description: README's 'Conventions across the tool surface' guarantees: 'Empty result collections serialize as [], never omitted.' Yet `eng_search_semantic` returns `degraded_reasons: null` when there are no degradation reasons (observed on a tack semantic-search call). Either the docs are wrong or the marshaller is. Pick one — agents written against the documented contract will branch incorrectly on null vs []. Audit all *_reasons / list-shaped response fields for the same nil-slice-marshalled-as-null pattern (likely a missing `make([]string, 0)` at construction).
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: Vuln findings: file_path is relative ('go.mod'), line_start is null
description: vuln-rule findings carry file_path="go.mod" (relative) while structural findings (dead-code, etc.) use the absolute path. line_start is also null for vuln findings, so 'go to source' from an editor can't land on the offending `require` line. Two small fixes: (1) emit absolute path for consistency with the rest of the finding surface, (2) populate line_start with the require-block line for the affected module (parse go.mod once per scan and map module -> line). Cheap and meaningfully improves IDE jump-to-source UX.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: no CLI progress for cold-scan; users must tail daemon.log
description: After 'veska repo add', only way to track progress is tailing daemon.log or polling eng_get_status. Consider 'veska repo add --wait' default progress bar, or 'veska status --watch'.
close_reason: already-implemented: 'veska repo add --wait' blocks until the cold scan completes and prints progress. The hint at cmd/veska/repo.go:343 surfaces this in the post-add output: 'cold scan running in the background — veska repo list shows status (pass --wait to block here), tail .../daemon.log shows progress'. Bead resolution is to document/discover this, not to add new functionality. If desired follow-up: make --wait default-on for interactive sessions.

title: eng_close_finding 'human_required' error lacks resolution hint
description: Closing a high-severity finding from agent caller errors with {code:-32001, message:'human_required'}. Good guardrail; bad UX. Add resolution hint: 'use veska findings close as a human, or set acknowledged_by=<user>'.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska context --symbol X errors; correct syntax is positional
description: README documents eng_get_context_pack param as 'symbol'. Users naturally try 'veska context --symbol DefaultConfig' which errors 'unknown flag: --symbol'. Accept --symbol as alias or document the CLI shape next to the MCP shape.
close_reason: Closed

title: eng_get_node returns plural {nodes:[...]} for single-id lookup
description: Other single-id lookups return singular shape. eng_get_node returns {nodes:[...]} even for one node_id. Wrap to {node:...} or rename tool.
close_reason: by-design per tools_graph.go comment: eng_get_node intentionally returns {nodes:[...]} to match eng_find_symbol and eng_get_file_nodes (consistent graph-lookup shape). Closing as won't-fix; documentation could be clearer but the choice is deliberate.

title: files_seen counter froze at 500 during entire 9-minute cold scan
description: During solov2 cold-scan, eng_get_status reported scans_in_flight[0].files_seen=500 throughout. Either it caps at 500 or refreshes rarely. Without progress signal users wonder if the daemon hung.
close_reason: Closed

title: cold scans are serialized; small repos wait behind large ones
description: Registering solov2 (~540 files) then tack (~38 files) on a clean install made tack wait ~9min for solov2 to finish. Tack alone takes ~5s. Parallelize across repos or schedule shortest-first.
close_reason: future-enhancement; needs scoped epic. The repoRegistrar.AddRepo (cmd/veska-daemon/providers.go:56) spawns a per-repo goroutine for the cold-scan reparser, so multi-repo concurrency exists at dispatch. The serial behaviour I observed (tack waited ~9min behind solov2) was likely due to the startup-resync path running through the scan_tracker / reparser pipeline that uses scanWG and may serialize within the post-promotion queue or the embedder writer. A clean fix needs (a) confirm where the serialization happens (scanTracker? embedder lane? sqlite write pool?), (b) decide on a parallelism policy (N workers? per-repo isolation? backpressure?), and (c) ensure the existing single-connection write pool isn't a contention bottleneck. Not in scope for the junior-journey sweep — file as own epic when revisited.

title: build-fat prints 'Restart the daemon' before any daemon exists
description: make build-fat install step prints 'Installed potion-code-16M to ... Restart the daemon to elect it as the embedder.' On a brand-new clone there is no daemon yet. Suppress that line on first-time setup, or rephrase to 'If a daemon is running, restart it'.
close_reason: Closed

title: build-fat prints 'Restart the daemon' before any daemon exists
