title: Surface last-render time in veska doctor
description:
Why: operators need to see whether the rendered wiki is stale. M4.md epic m4.04.3.

Scope boundary: a doctor check that reads the last-render timestamp persisted by the WorkKindWiki lane task. Does not render anything itself.

DoD: doctor check test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: 'veska doctor' reports the age of the last successful wiki render. AC2: when no render has occurred, doctor reports that explicitly (not as an error).
close_reason: DoD verified: doctor wiki_render probe + subcommand reports last-render age; never-rendered handled explicitly (exit 0); tests + layercheck green

title: Add the veska wiki on-demand command
description:
Why: operators need to regenerate the wiki pages without waiting for a promotion. M4.md epic m4.04.1.

Scope boundary: the 'veska wiki' CLI command only. It reuses the render orchestration created by the WorkKindWiki lane task (wiki.Handler) — it does not reimplement rendering.

DoD: CLI command test; go test + go vet green. Design ref: docs/milestones/M4.md, epic solov2-erp.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: 'veska wiki' regenerates both pages and exits 0 on success. AC2: the command writes the same byte-identical output the promotion lane produces.

CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-16, approved by human):
- Repo/branch selection: --repo and --branch flags. --repo is optional and defaults to the sole registered repo when exactly one exists (error if ambiguous / none). --branch defaults to that repo's default branch.
- Implementation: open veska.db via config.DefaultVectorDir()+veska.db, wire the wiki services (HotZoneService, EntryPointsService, RenderTimeStore via sqlite.WikiRenderStateRepo, RepoRootFunc), construct a wiki.Handler and call Handle with a synthetic ports.WorkRow{Kind: WorkKindWiki, RepoID, Branch}. Reusing Handle guarantees AC2 byte-identical output for free.
- Register as a root cobra command in cmd/veska/main.go (newRootCmd).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: 'veska wiki' regenerates both pages and exits 0 on success. AC2: the command writes the same byte-identical output the promotion lane produces.
close_reason: DoD verified: veska wiki command regenerates both pages via reused wiki.Handler (byte-identical), --repo/--branch flags; CLI test + layercheck green

title: Edge-anchored revalidation path for auto-link findings
description:
Deferred from solov2-l8y investigation.

FINDING: auto-link findings are never revalidated. They store an edge_id in the findings.node_id column (autolink/handler.go). The m3.05 revalidation sweep's StaleFindingsForFile joins findings.node_id -> nodes.node_id; an edge_id never matches a nodes row, so auto-link findings are never selected. They stay open until a developer accepts/suppresses them.

This is acceptable as a default (auto-link is a suggestion; a stale suggestion is low-harm; m3.04 designed it as developer-driven accept/suppress). This bead tracks the OPTIONAL feature of revalidating them automatically.

If pursued:
1. A sweep path that selects edge-anchored findings (join via edges by edge_id, or store both endpoint node ids on the finding).
2. A cheap re-check: do both endpoint nodes still exist? did either drift materially? Decide refresh vs close.
3. Wire it into the revalidate Handler as a distinct dispatch (not a per-rule case in decide(), which is node-anchored).

NOT a blocker — auto-link lifecycle works today via developer action. Only worth doing if stale auto-link findings prove noisy in practice.
notes: Deferred 2026-05-18: optional by design — implement only if stale auto-link findings prove noisy in practice. No evidence of noise; auto-link lifecycle works via developer accept/suppress today.

title: Measure + decide signature/snippet enrichment of embedder text projection
description:
Split from solov2-311. The collapse-correctness fix (file_path + language added to the FetchPending embed-text projection) landed under 311. This bead covers the measurement-gated remainder: whether to ALSO fold signature and/or a code snippet into the embed text to make embeddings semantically richer.

That changes embedding QUALITY, so it needs a recall measurement first (per the original 311 instruction). Blocker: the recall harness (tools/loadtest/recall) embeds synthcorpus text directly — it does NOT exercise the production FetchPending projection, so there is no current way to measure a projection change against the m3.03 fixture.

Scope:
1. Add a harness mode (or a new eval harness) that builds the corpus from real node-shaped projections so projection changes ARE measurable.
2. Sweep projection variants (with/without signature, with/without snippet) and record recall@10 + p95.
3. Decide and implement the winning projection, or document that <kind> <symbol_path> <file> <language> is sufficient.

Files: internal/infrastructure/sqlite/embedding_refs_repo.go (FetchPending), tools/loadtest/recall/.
notes: Step 1 (measurable harness) DONE 2026-05-18, commit d672784: domain.EmbedText shared projection + tools/loadtest/recallprojection harness with a baseline/+signature/+snippet/+both variant selector + 'make eval-recall-projection'. Remaining (deferred, needs a reference-laptop run): run the pop=1000 sweep against a real model, compare mean_recall per variant vs the m3.03 baseline, and promote the winning projection into embedding_refs_repo.go embedText if recall improves with no p95 regression.
close_reason: Sweep done (pop=1000, make eval-recall-projection): baseline recall@10 0.917 p95 69ms; +signature 0.913 (no lift) REJECTED; +snippet 1.000 but harness snippet is a synthetic restatement of query text (optimistic) and production stores no node body to project. Decision: keep baseline projection. Snippet-storage feature filed as solov2-ok0.

title: Wire MultiRepoWatcher.Add to repo registry (currently empty)
description: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go starts MultiRepoWatcher but never calls Add(repoID, rootPath). The intended source is the repo registry (internal/repo/registry.go) — implement a ListRepos query that returns RepoRecord, and on daemon Start iterate it to add each registered repo to the watcher. Today the watcher is wired but inert.
close_reason: repo.List(ctx,db) ([]repo.Record,error) added; daemon Start now seeds MultiRepoWatcher from the repos table (List failure logged, non-fatal). Added MultiRepoWatcher.WatchedRepoIDs() accessor. Commit d017e28. internal/repo kept free of internal/application import.

title: Migrate embed queue lane to queue.Poller (remove noopEmbedHandler)
description: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go currently installs a noopEmbedHandler under ports.WorkKindEmbed in the queue.Poller because the embedder.Worker drives its own poll loop directly on node_embedding_refs. Long-term: migrate the embedder to a queue.WorkHandler so the post-promotion queue is the single dispatch path. Until then, the noop handler exists to keep the lane drained if another code path enqueues an embed row.
notes:
DEFERRED 2026-05-15 (session decision). Not a fix — embedder works correctly today; this is architectural unification only.

WHY DEFERRED:
- embedder.Worker is gate-1-validated (8.43 emb/s measured, commit f91b51e). Migrating its run model risks regressing that number.
- Genuine impedance mismatch: queue.WorkHandler processes ONE row at a time. embedder.Worker depends on three tick-scoped behaviours that do not map onto a one-row handler:
  1. cross-tick dedup — inFlight map (intra-tick) + refs.LookupExisting (prior-tick) skip redundant Embed calls for repeated (modelID, embed_text) keys.
  2. token-bucket rate limiting — limiter.Wait() once per row, but tuned around the tick loop.
  3. batched vector upserts — vecBatches map keyed by (repo,branch), flushed per tick, not per row.

BLOCKER FOR A CLEAN FIX:
A faithful migration needs a BATCH-AWARE WorkHandler contract (e.g. ports.WorkHandler gains a HandleBatch([]Row) variant, or queue.Poller hands each lane a slice). Migrating embed onto the current one-row WorkHandler would force dropping dedup+batching or reimplementing them inside the handler — net negative. So this bead really has a hidden prerequisite: decide the batch-handler port shape first.

CURRENT STATE (acceptable interim):
- cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go installs noopEmbedHandler under ports.WorkKindEmbed — a ~5-line handler that marks the row done. Legitimate stopgap: keeps the lane drained if anything enqueues an embed row.
- Real embedding work happens via embedder.Worker's own loop on node_embedding_refs (Start/Stop lifecycle wired in the daemon).
- gate-5 fuzz passes: embed lane 'drains' via the noop handler.

WHEN PICKED UP:
1. First decide the batch WorkHandler contract (new sub-bead or design note) — this is the actual gate.
2. Then: embedder.Worker becomes a queue.WorkHandler (batch variant); remove noopEmbedHandler from wire.go; drop the standalone Start/Stop wiring.
3. Re-run gate-1 throughput bench (make eval-embed-throughput) — must still land 5-20 emb/s.
4. Re-run gate-5 queue fuzz — embed lane now does real work.

Files: cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go (noopEmbedHandler), internal/application/embedder/embedder.go (run/tick loop), internal/infrastructure/sqlite/queue/queue.go (WorkHandler contract), internal/core/ports/queue.go (WorkHandler interface).

title: Replace embedder.NewWorker / autolink.NewHandler / NewLinker panics with typed errors
description:
cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) inherits panics from constructors that panic on nil collaborators: embedder.NewWorker, autolink.NewHandler, autolink.NewLinker (transitively), ollama.New on empty model. The composition root in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go already validates the inputs it controls and returns *ErrMissingDep, but the underlying constructors still rely on panics for defense-in-depth.

Convert these to typed errors so a caller passing a nil dep gets a descriptive error rather than a stack trace. Scope: low-risk refactor; all call sites are within this repo (count is small).
close_reason: All 4 constructors (embedder.NewWorker, autolink.NewLinker, autolink.NewHandler, ollama.New) now return (*T, error); nil/empty dep wraps a package-level ErrMissingDependency sentinel (errors.Is-matchable). wire.go propagates with pools.Close cleanup preserved. ~75 test call sites updated via mustNewX helpers. Commit 9003ef3. (Plus 14cc0a5: fixed 2 fixturegen tests regressed by the earlier d5z normalization commit.)

title: application.RepoLister + mcp.StatusProvider/ConfigProvider adapters for RegisterAdminTools
description: cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) skipped mcp.RegisterAdminTools because there is no production implementation of application.RepoLister, mcp.StatusProvider, or mcp.ConfigProvider. Implement these on top of internal/repo registry (which already has Add but not List) + a small Status/Config struct exposed from the daemon, then wire RegisterAdminTools in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go.
close_reason: 3 production providers (repoLister, statusProvider, configProvider) added in cmd/veska-daemon/providers.go; RegisterAdminTools now wired in registerMCPTools — all 5 admin MCP tools resolve. repoLister maps repo.Record->application.RepoRecord; statusProvider reports schema_version/repo_count/pending_embeds; configProvider reflects daemon Config. Commit f3e5d27.

title: ports.GraphStorage SQLite adapter for MCP graph tools
description:
cmd/veska-daemon wiring (solov2-3lv) had to skip RegisterGraphTools, RegisterBlastTools, RegisterSearchTools because no production adapter for ports.GraphStorage exists in internal/infrastructure/sqlite. The MCP server starts without those tools today. Implement a sqlite.GraphRepo satisfying ports.GraphStorage (SaveNode, SaveEdge, DeleteFile, LoadGraph, FindNodes, GetNode), then wire RegisterGraphTools + RegisterBlastTools + RegisterSearchTools in cmd/veska-daemon/wire.go.

Surface: solov2-3lv composition root TODO.
close_reason: sqlite.GraphRepo(readDB,writeDB) implements ports.GraphStorage (6 methods) — round-trip-tested; upsert SQL matches Promoter column set. registerMCPTools widened (mcpDeps struct); RegisterGraphTools+BlastTools+SearchTools now all wired — graph/blast/search MCP tool families resolve. blastradius.Service + search.Service assembled from existing adapters; repoRootFunc via repo.List; git.ChangedFiles as ChangedFilesFunc. Commit e8a7f70.

title: Rewrite or scope parent CLAUDE.md to match veska (was engram)
description: /home/jrose/src/engram/CLAUDE.md still describes engram (engram init, ENGRAM_HOME, Dolt+Qdrant runtime deps). The repo renamed to veska; commands are veska init / VESKA_HOME; runtime is SQLite + usearch/sqlitevec with no Dolt/Qdrant. Either rewrite to match veska's architecture, or scope it to the parent V1 directory only (and remove from solov2 paths). The solov2/CLAUDE.md is already a thin template — replace it with a real veska guide pulled from this file.
close_reason: Parent V1 engram CLAUDE.md deleted (reference-only per user). solov2/CLAUDE.md template replaced with a real veska guide: build/test/eval commands, 3-binary topology, hexagonal layout, runtime deps, key design decisions, conventions. Beads-integration block left untouched (auto-managed).

title: Decide GraphStorage.Promote vs raw SQL in Promoter
description: Promoter currently writes raw SQL (BEGIN IMMEDIATE, FTS5 vtables, SQLite ON CONFLICT syntax) directly from the application layer. Compiles via database/sql (stdlib, layercheck allows it), but tightly couples the service to one storage engine. Decision: either (a) add GraphStorage.Promote(ctx, batch) and move SQL behind the port to restore hexagonal purity, OR (b) explicitly document this as a deliberate single-backend trade for atomicity and add a comment to that effect. Don't drift silently.
close_reason: Unified OCP refactor (commit on HEAD). bx6: decision (a) — raw SQL moved behind application.PromotionStore port; promoter.go no longer imports database/sql. zwj: all promotion SQL + node helpers extracted to internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go. mu6: PromotionSink interface (Prepare/BeforeNodeDelete/AfterNodeInsert) — ftsSink + embedRefSink; Promoter.Promote now ~70-LOC thin orchestrator; new co-transactional sink = one arg in wire.go, zero edits to the tx body. Atomicity verified incl. new TestPromotionStore_RollsBackOnMidTxFailure; all 5 promoter test files pass; race+vet+layercheck clean.

title: Refactor Promoter to PromotionSink port (OCP)
description: Promoter.Promote is 480 LOC and owns: registration check, tx open, prev-signature snapshot, node delete/upsert, dual FTS5 maintenance, embed-ref enqueue, post-promotion queue insert, commit, staging cleanup, audit write, check dispatch. Each new co-transactional sink (security/quality coming) requires editing this function. Pattern exists already: CheckRunner was extracted as a setter-based DI. Generalise to a PromotionSink interface (WriteToTx(ctx, tx, batch) error) registered at start-up. Pulls FTS5 maintenance and embed-ref enqueue into separate sinks; Promoter becomes a thin orchestrator.
close_reason: Unified OCP refactor (commit on HEAD). bx6: decision (a) — raw SQL moved behind application.PromotionStore port; promoter.go no longer imports database/sql. zwj: all promotion SQL + node helpers extracted to internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go. mu6: PromotionSink interface (Prepare/BeforeNodeDelete/AfterNodeInsert) — ftsSink + embedRefSink; Promoter.Promote now ~70-LOC thin orchestrator; new co-transactional sink = one arg in wire.go, zero edits to the tx body. Atomicity verified incl. new TestPromotionStore_RollsBackOnMidTxFailure; all 5 promoter test files pass; race+vet+layercheck clean.

title: Enrich embedder text projection beyond <kind> <symbol_path>
description: m3.02.1 used 'KIND symbol_path' as the embed text for simplicity. With m3.02.4's input-hash dedup landed, two nodes with the same (kind, name) in different languages or directories collapse into one embedding row. Consider including language, dir/package, signature/snippet to differentiate while preserving meaningful dedup. Measure recall impact on the m3.03 fixture before changing.
close_reason: Collapse-correctness fix: FetchPending embed-text projection now '<kind> <symbol_path> <file_path> <language>' (empty fields omitted) — two otherwise-identical symbols in different files/languages no longer collapse to one content-addressed embedding row; same node in same file still dedups. Semantic enrichment (signature/snippet) — the genuinely measurement-gated part — split to solov2-7ma (needs a recall harness that exercises real node projections). Commit 0a78cfa.

title: Reopen parse-failure findings on fix
description: Once a file's parse failure is resolved on a later ingest, the existing open finding (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure') should be closed with closed_reason='revalidated_obsolete'. Currently the finding stays open forever. Related work lives in m3.05 (revalidation) — likely just hook into that sweep with rule='parse-failure' as a recognised kind.
close_reason: Fixed ingester-side (bead's 'hook m3.05 sweep' premise was wrong — sweep selects by node content_hash drift, parse-failure findings are file-anchored). Added FindingStorage.CloseObsolete(findingID,branch); Ingester.Save now calls clearParseFailure on a zero-failure parse, closing the open parse-failure finding as revalidated_obsolete via the message-independent FindingID. Best-effort, no-op when nothing open. Commit b146aea.

title: Add JSON Schema for MCP tool params and results
description: MCP ToolSpec has Description but no formal JSON Schema for params or result shapes. Add InputSchema/OutputSchema fields (json.RawMessage) to ToolSpec and populate them for at least the state-changing tools (eng_close_finding, eng_suppress_finding, eng_set_active_task). This enables client-side validation and improves editor integration.
close_reason: ToolSpec gained InputSchema/OutputSchema json.RawMessage fields (optional; Register validates only non-empty schemas via json.Valid). eng_close_finding, eng_suppress_finding, eng_set_active_task each carry hand-authored draft-2020-12 input+output schemas accurate to their structs. Added Registry.Spec() read accessor. Wire-surfacing (tools/list) intentionally out of scope. Commit 73857c6.

title: Extract Promoter SQL to internal/infrastructure/sqlite
description: Promoter.Promote has three inline prepared statements (DELETE nodes, INSERT nodes, INSERT post_promotion_queue). These belong in the sqlite infrastructure package, not the application layer. Extract them to a PromotionStore or similar adapter so the application layer is fully infra-clean.
close_reason: Unified OCP refactor (commit on HEAD). bx6: decision (a) — raw SQL moved behind application.PromotionStore port; promoter.go no longer imports database/sql. zwj: all promotion SQL + node helpers extracted to internal/infrastructure/sqlite/promotion_store.go. mu6: PromotionSink interface (Prepare/BeforeNodeDelete/AfterNodeInsert) — ftsSink + embedRefSink; Promoter.Promote now ~70-LOC thin orchestrator; new co-transactional sink = one arg in wire.go, zero edits to the tx body. Atomicity verified incl. new TestPromotionStore_RollsBackOnMidTxFailure; all 5 promoter test files pass; race+vet+layercheck clean.

title: Watch decodeFloat32LE duplication (embedder + autolink)
description: decodeFloat32LE is duplicated in embedder/embedder.go and autolink/autolink.go, each documented as deliberate to avoid an upward sibling import. Fine as-is. Tripwire: if a third copy appears, promote to a tiny shared leaf package (e.g. internal/application/veccodec) rather than copying again. Low priority / no action unless a third consumer lands.

title: core: scope eval-only EmbedText variants out of the domain surface
description: EmbedTextVariant (embed_text.go) defines Signature/Snippet/Both variants that are consumed only by the recall-projection eval harness (tools/loadtest/recallprojection); production (sqlite embedding_refs_repo) uses EmbedVariantBaseline only. The shared EmbedText projection legitimately belongs in domain, but the eval-only variant values broaden the domain's public surface for a non-production concern. Low priority. Surfaced by arch review of internal/core (2026-05-31).
design: Cheapest acceptable outcome is a doc note on EmbedTextVariant stating baseline is the only production value and the rest are eval candidates — matches the existing comment intent. Only relocate if the eval harness ownership moves.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: keep variants in domain (documented as the shared production+eval projection contract) OR move the non-baseline variants nearer the eval harness. No behavioral change to the production baseline projection.
close_reason: DECISION: keep variants in domain (documented), do not relocate. Strengthened EmbedTextVariant doc to state EmbedVariantBaseline is the ONLY production value and Signature/Snippet/Both are eval-only candidates, with rationale that the enum + String() + EmbedText switch are one cohesive contract the recall sweep compares against — splitting would fragment a single switch across packages for no production benefit. No behavioral change. Doc-only.

title: docs: document the consumer-owned ports convention (core/ports vs application/*)
description: Ports live in two places: internal/core/ports/ (broad storage ports: GraphStorage, VectorStorage, ...) and application/* sub-packages (use-case-specific, consumer-owned: wiki.RenderTimeStore, review.DailyTokenStore, dependencies.StubAggregator, application.PromotionStore). This is the correct 'ports owned by the consumer' hexagonal idiom, but it is an implicit convention — one paragraph in CLAUDE.md / DESIGN.md would keep it from reading as drift in future reviews.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md (or DESIGN.md) gains a short note explaining when a port belongs in core/ports vs an application sub-package.
close_reason: DoD verified: added a 'Key design decisions' bullet in CLAUDE.md explaining the two port homes — broad cross-cutting contracts in core/ports vs narrow use-case-specific consumer-owned ports in application/* sub-packages (PromotionStore, RenderTimeStore, DailyTokenStore, embedder.EmbedRefQueue), with the rule of thumb that single-use-case interfaces live next to the use case. Doc-only.

title: infra: revisit flat layout of mcp (62 files) / sqlite (42 files) packages
description: internal/infrastructure/mcp has 62 files (mostly tools_*.go, one per tool family) and sqlite has 42 (one per repo/concern). The one-file-per-concern convention is cohesive and intentional — NOT a defect today — but mcp is approaching the size where a tools/ subpackage split by family would aid navigation. Track as a watch item; act only if the package keeps growing.
acceptance_criteria: decision recorded: either split mcp tool files into a tools/ subpackage by family, or explicitly affirm the flat layout in CLAUDE.md so it does not re-surface in future reviews.
close_reason: DECISION: affirm the flat layout (not split). Added a CLAUDE.md Conventions bullet stating mcp (tools_*.go per family) and sqlite (per-concern) are deliberately flat even at 60+/40+ files; single-package cohesion + predictable filenames outweigh nesting; don't split absent a concrete coupling problem. Per the bead, a 62-file subpackage refactor is unwarranted for a non-defect; documenting prevents re-surfacing.

title: infra/secretsscanner: replace hand-rolled itoa with strconv.Itoa
description: secretsscanner.go:132 itoa reimplements strconv.Itoa with the stated rationale 'avoiding a strconv import for a single compile-time constant' — not a real benefit (stdlib, zero cost, almost certainly already in the transitive import set) and the prepend-into-slice loop is O(n^2). Replace with strconv.Itoa and drop the helper.
acceptance_criteria: itoa removed; call sites use strconv.Itoa; tests + vet pass.
close_reason: DoD verified: hand-rolled O(n^2) itoa removed; tokenRe uses strconv.Itoa(entropyMinLen); strconv imported. build+tests+vet green.

title: infra/queue: fold NewWithInterval interval param into a WithInterval option
description: sqlite/queue/queue.go:57 NewWithInterval(readDB, writeDB, handlers, interval) is 4 positional. The New/NewWithInterval twin is a defensible Go idiom, but New(readDB, writeDB, handlers, WithInterval(d)) would unify construction. Sole caller: daemon/wire.go:729. Low priority.
acceptance_criteria: interval expressed as a WithInterval option (or explicitly decided to keep the twin-constructor and bead closed wontfix with rationale).
close_reason: DoD verified: NewWithInterval removed; New(readDB,writeDB,handlers,opts...) with WithInterval(d) option (default 250ms via defaultPollInterval const). Sole prod caller wire.go + 7 test sites migrated. build+queue tests+vet green.

title: ollama.go: remove unused defaultModel const + adopt errors.AsType
description: Pre-existing nits surfaced while fixing xde2.2 (not introduced by it): internal/infrastructure/embedding/ollama/ollama.go:27 defaultModel const is declared but never referenced; ollama.go:252 errors.As(...&*net.OpError) can use the Go 1.26 errors.AsType[*net.OpError] form.
acceptance_criteria: defaultModel removed (or wired to the actual default); errors.As call uses AsType. vet/staticcheck clean.
notes: UPDATE: the defaultModel-unused half was resolved by solov2-50w6 (06938ce) — const is now exported as ollama.DefaultModel and referenced by composition. REMAINING scope: only the errors.As -> errors.AsType[*net.OpError] simplification at ollama.go:256.
close_reason: DoD verified: both errors.As(...&netErr/&urlErr) converted to Go 1.26 errors.AsType[*net.OpError]/[*url.Error] (drops the temp var decls). defaultModel half was already resolved by 50w6 (exported as DefaultModel + referenced). build+tests+vet clean.

title: Guard mirrored checks.Input/Line DTO drift in promoter.go
description:
application.Line and application.CheckRunInput (internal/application/promoter.go:20-42) are deliberate hand-copies of checks.Line/checks.Input (and git.Line), justified in-comment by import-cycle avoidance. The decision is sound and documented, but the mirroring is a hand-maintained invariant: a field added to checks.Input must be mirrored here manually.

## Context
Finding from arch-review of internal/application. INFO — the duplication itself is correct (avoids an import cycle); this only guards future drift.
acceptance_criteria: A compile-time or unit guard exists that fails if checks.Input/checks.Line and the application mirrors drift (e.g. a conversion-assertion test); OR a bd note documents the decision to accept the manual invariant. No production behavior change.
close_reason: DoD verified: added reflect-based field-parity guard (mirror_parity_test.go, external package checks_test importing both — no prod import-cycle change) that fails if checks.Input/checks.Line and the application.CheckRunInput/application.Line mirrors drift (field add/rename/retype). Passes today; no production change.

title: coldscan: ask parser for supported extensions instead of duplicating the list
description:
The canonical set of parseable extensions lives in two hand-synced places: parseableExtensions (internal/application/coldscan.go:282) and the treesitter adapter's actual capability. The in-code comment already acknowledges the 'keep in sync with the treesitter parsers' coupling.

## Context
Finding from arch-review of internal/application. INFO — defer unless the parser language set starts changing often.
acceptance_criteria: Supported-extension set sourced from a port the parser adapter implements (e.g. CodeParser.SupportedExtensions()) rather than duplicated in coldscan.go; layercheck still passes; cold-scan filtering behavior unchanged.
notes: DEFERRED by epic-tdd (needs product decision, not a mechanical refactor). BLOCKER: cold-scan wires treesitter.NewGoParser() only (composition/coldscan.go:43, daemon mcptools.go:170) — a Go-only parser — but parseableExtensions (coldscan.go:~290) lists .go/.ts/.tsx. The list and the wired parser already disagree: .ts/.tsx files pass the walk filter today, reach GoParser.ParseFile, and return empty. So the DoD's two requirements conflict: sourcing extensions from the GoParser port would DROP .ts/.tsx walking (behavior change), while a combined Go+TS parser would ACTIVATE TS parsing (different behavior change). Resolving requires deciding whether TS cold-scan is intended. Park until the parser language set actually changes, per the bead's own INFO note. Also note: adding CodeParser.SupportedExtensions() ripples to ~6 ParseFile implementers incl. test fakes.

title: review.NewQuota: inject clock via WithClock option for consistency
description:
review.NewQuota passes the optional now func() time.Time clock positionally with a nil-default (internal/application/review/quota.go:51), while search.NewService injects the same clock via WithClock (internal/application/search/search.go:147). Same concept, two conventions.

## Context
Finding from arch-review of internal/application. INFO/consistency-only — no behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: review exposes WithClock(func() time.Time) Option; now dropped from NewQuota positional params; existing quota tests updated to use WithClock; go test ./internal/application/review/... passes.
close_reason: DoD verified: review.WithClock(func() time.Time) QuotaOption added (named per HandlerOption convention); NewQuota drops positional now -> opts ...QuotaOption, defaults time.Now; both prod callers (wire.go, doctorcmd/probes.go) + all quota tests migrated. No behavior change. build+vet+review tests green.

title: composition: move package doc into doc.go for consistency
description:
The package-level doc comment lives on internal/composition/coldscan.go:1-12; the sibling internal/application package uses a dedicated doc.go. For discoverability/consistency, relocate the package comment to internal/composition/doc.go. Cosmetic.

## Context
Surfaced by SOLID/CLEAN arch review of internal/composition (2026-05-31).
acceptance_criteria: Package comment lives in internal/composition/doc.go; coldscan.go carries only its own type/func docs. go vet clean.
close_reason: DoD verified: package comment moved to internal/composition/doc.go; coldscan.go now carries only 'package composition' + its own decls. One package-doc block. go vet clean. Matches the application/doc.go convention.

title: contextpack.NewAssembler: consider Deps struct if 7 positional func args bite
description: internal/application/contextpack/contextpack.go:182 NewAssembler takes 7 required positional Func-typed dependencies. Convention-compliant today (all required + distinctly named). IF call-site readability ever becomes a problem (several are same-shape func types, swap-prone), the idiomatic fix is a Deps parameter struct — NOT WithX options, which are reserved for optional config.
acceptance_criteria: Either a Deps struct is introduced for the required func deps, or this is closed wont-fix as still-readable.
notes: Conditional/future finding from internal/application arch review (2026-05-30). No action needed unless the call site gets harder to read.
close_reason: ALREADY SATISFIED. NewAssembler already takes a named-field AssemblerDeps struct (deps.FindNodes/Blast/FileHistory/OpenFindings/ChangedFiles/NodesInFile/ActiveTask) with nil-guards per field; all 6 call sites use AssemblerDeps{Field: ...} named literals (swap-proof). This is exactly the Deps-struct fix the bead recommended — the '7 positional args' description was stale. No further change needed.

title: Decompose daemon Daemon.Start and Daemon.Stop lifecycle methods
description: Discovered during solov2-u4mv.10: Daemon.Start (~125 LOC) and Daemon.Stop (~91 LOC) in internal/cli/daemon/wire.go exceed the <=50 LOC bar. They were out of scope for the newDaemon/registerMCPTools decomposition (.10/.9) and are concurrency-sensitive (goroutine launch order in Start; bounded-budget drain of mcpDone/wDone/resyncDone/scanWG in Stop), so they warrant their own focused pass. Decompose Start into per-subsystem launch helpers and Stop into per-subsystem drain helpers; verify with -race + the daemon suite.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Daemon.Start and Daemon.Stop and any extracted helpers are <=50 LOC, <=15 cyclomatic, <=5 params. AC2: go test -race ./internal/cli/daemon/... passes; startup/shutdown ordering unchanged. AC3: make all green.
close_reason: Decomposed Daemon.Start (125 LOC) into a 32-line startOnce.Do closure calling per-subsystem launch helpers (startMetricsListener, startMCPServer, awaitListenerSockets, rehydrateVectors, seedWatcher, startWatchLoop, startResync, startVulnRefresher); and Daemon.Stop (91 LOC) into a 25-line stopOnce.Do calling awaitBackgroundGoroutines, drainScans, closeResources. Concurrency-preserving: launch order unchanged; the single 5s shutdown timer is passed as a shared <-chan time.Time into awaitBackgroundGoroutines + drainScans so the budget stays a SHARED ceiling (not 5s each) — the key invariant; wDone keeps its own 500ms; closeResources joins every close error exactly as before. All funcs <=33 LOC, <=5 params. AC1: met. AC2: go test -race -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/cli/daemon/... PASS (race-clean); startup/shutdown ordering unchanged. AC3: make all exit 0; lint full + size-gate 0 issues — Start/Stop now pass the size gate (were the legacy exemptions). Commit d06c979.

title: Clear pre-existing staticcheck/unused lint debt blocking 'make all'
description: make all currently fails at the lint step on main due to 7 pre-existing issues unrelated to any maintainability-restructure task: staticcheck QF1001 (De Morgan) in cmd/veska/deps.go:65, internal/repo/clone.go:97, internal/repo/registry_test.go:522; QF1002 (tagged switch) in cmd/veska/repo_alias_prompt.go:50; ST1023 in internal/application/search/tier.go:48; SA6001 in internal/infrastructure/treesitter/go.go:573; and unused func scoreTier in cmd/veska/search.go:1114. These are out of scope for solov2-u4mv.7 (which gates CHANGED code only, not legacy). Apply the staticcheck autofixes / delete dead code so 'make all' is green. DoD: make all green; no behavior change.
close_reason: Cleared all pre-existing gate failures blocking 'make all' (none introduced by this epic). staticcheck: QF1001 De Morgan in deps.go:65, clone.go:97, registry_test.go:522, graph.go:392, repo_test.go:182 (rewrote negated disjunctions as conjunctions of negations — logically equivalent); QF1002 tagged switch in repo_alias_prompt.go:50; ST1023 in tier.go:48; SA6001 inline map-key in treesitter/go.go:573; unused func scoreTier deleted from search.go:1114. noidleak: removed leaked bd IDs from user-visible strings in tool_schemas.go:300 (dropped), graph.go:142 (ID already in adjacent comment), descriptions.go:14 (moved solov2-9rc2 into the doc comment). make all now exits 0 (build+test+vet+lint 0 issues+layercheck+noidleak+cliparity). Commit 56a6cc5. This unblocks the 'make all green' DoD for all remaining epic tasks.

title: Normalize mcp/ tool file naming
description:
**Why:** `tool_*.go` (singular) vs `tools_*.go` (plural) prefixes in internal/infrastructure/mcp carry no meaning and obscure navigation.

**Scope boundary:** file renames + package-internal references only; no symbol renames, no behavior change. Independent of all other tasks.

**Design doc:** see parent epic body (Problem — naming drift).

**Definition of Done:** `git mv` preserves history; `go build ./internal/infrastructure/mcp/...` + its tests + `make all` green; one conventional commit.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: every per-tool file follows one documented convention; git mv preserves history. AC2: go build ./internal/infrastructure/mcp/... and its tests pass unchanged. AC3: make layercheck passes.
close_reason: Normalized MCP tool source file naming: renamed all 15 singular tool_*.go (incl 4 tests) to the tools_ prefix via git mv (history preserved as renames), so every per-tool file now follows one convention: tools_<area>.go + tools_<area>_test.go. Documented the convention in the package doc (server.go). Rationale for plural: the tools_ group was already the majority and reads as 'the MCP tools for <area>'; the prior tool_/tools_ split carried no reliable semantic meaning (e.g. tool_promote.go self-registered like the tools_ family files while other tool_*.go were handlers registered elsewhere). Pure rename — Go ignores filenames for compilation, no symbol/behavior changes. AC1: single documented convention, git mv preserves history. AC2: go build + go test -tags sqlite_fts5 ./internal/infrastructure/mcp/... PASS unchanged. AC3: make all exit 0 (incl cliparity/noidleak). Commit 2b04915.

title: make build-small is a no-op when bin/veska already exists from 'make build'
description:
Discovered while implementing solov2-izh6.29.

REPRO:
  make build              # produces ~104 MB fat binary
  make build-small        # silently produces nothing; bin/veska is still 104 MB

ROOT CAUSE (Makefile):
  build-small: $(VESKA_BIN) $(LAYERCHECK_BIN)
      ln -sf veska $(DAEMON_BIN)
      ln -sf veska $(MCP_BIN)

  $(VESKA_BIN):
      $(SQLITE_CGO_ENV) go build -tags "$(SQLITE_TAGS)" -o $@ ./cmd/veska

Both 'build' and 'build-small' produce the SAME output path ($(VESKA_BIN) = bin/veska). When build-small runs after build, make sees bin/veska exists and is newer than its (non-existent) prerequisites, so the $(VESKA_BIN) rule is skipped — build-small only re-runs the 'ln -sf' lines that were already correct. The user gets the fat binary they already had.

The reverse (build-small then build) DOES rebuild — 'build' is not a file-target so make always runs the recipe.

FIX DIRECTIONS:
  A. Give the two modes distinct outputs (bin/veska-fat vs bin/veska-thin) and have a top-level alias rename. More disruptive — every script in tree references bin/veska.
  B. Make build-small unconditionally remove bin/veska before invoking go build. Two lines, no downstream churn.
  C. Add a phony marker file (.build-mode) that triggers a rebuild when modes mismatch. Subtle but correct.

Recommended (B): the build is sub-second once Go's build cache is warm; an unconditional rm + rebuild has no real cost and removes the footgun.

ACCEPTANCE:
  make build && make build-small               # produces a 42 MB thin binary
  make build-small && make build               # produces a 104 MB fat binary (already works)
  Verify by file size: stat -L -c%s bin/veska before/after.

Workaround until fixed: 'make build-sizes' (solov2-izh6.29) cleans the path between runs and is the only way to measure both modes accurately.
notes: Surfaced when measuring binary sizes for solov2-izh6.29. The 'build-sizes' target I added there does the rm-then-build pattern (B) for measurement; lifting it into build-small itself is the same shape.
close_reason:
Fixed in 8f9843c.

PROBLEM:
  make build       # 104 MB fat
  make build-small # SILENTLY KEEPS the 104 MB fat binary

ROOT CAUSE:
build-small declared $(VESKA_BIN) as a prerequisite. With bin/veska already present from a prior 'make build', make sees the file exists and skips the $(VESKA_BIN) rule entirely — only the 'ln -sf' lines re-ran, producing no rebuild of the binary itself.

FIX:
build-small now removes bin/veska + the daemon/mcp symlinks unconditionally and invokes the thin go build directly in its recipe. This breaks the false 'already-built' shortcut and guarantees the user gets the mode they asked for. (The reverse — build-small → build — already worked because 'build' is implicitly phony via its fetch-embed-assets prereq.)

VERIFIED:
  rm -f bin/veska bin/veska-daemon bin/veska-mcp
  make build           # 109,779,976 bytes (104 MB) — fat ✓
  make build-small     #  44,433,688 bytes ( 42 MB) — thin ✓
  make build           # 109,779,976 bytes (104 MB) — fat ✓

The build-sizes helper from solov2-izh6.29 still works and its defensive rm before each build is now redundant but harmless.

title: README: 'fat build is ~62MB larger' understates actual binary size (~110MB total)
description:
Repro: fresh 'make build' on main produces bin/veska at 110 MB. README ('## Build' section) says 'make build is the fat binary by default ... embeds the model2vec weights into the binary so the install is zero-setup ... (~62 MB larger)'. The 62 MB figure is the model itself (consistent with the model2vec install description elsewhere: '~62 MB download into ~/.veska/'). For a junior reading the build section that number reads as 'fat build is ~62 MB' — actually it's nearly 2× that.

This is the first dependency-vs-footprint decision a new user makes. Underselling the size will surprise CI/container users who expected a sub-100 MB binary.

Acceptance:
- README/build section quotes accurate numbers: typical thin build size vs fat build size (e.g. 'fat build adds ~62 MB on top of the ~48 MB thin build, total ~110 MB').
- Update docs/operations/CONFIG-SURFACE.md if it has the same wording.
- A make target (or a tiny script) measures the deltas so the doc can be regenerated, not hand-maintained.
close_reason:
Fixed in 14ad417. The original '~62 MB larger' wording in the README's Build section was numerically accurate (fat IS 62 MB larger than thin) but didn't surface the absolute fat-build size — a junior reading 'fat binary' + '~62 MB larger' could reasonably expect a 60-70 MB final binary and be surprised by ~104 MB. Updated the comment block:

  make build        # default: ~104 MB fat binary (model2vec ~62 MB embedded
                    # into a ~42 MB thin build). Zero setup at runtime.
  make build-small  # ~42 MB thin: ...

Added 'make build-sizes' target that measures both modes (cleans bin/ between builds because $(VESKA_BIN) is the same path for both — make would otherwise skip the second build). Run it whenever the binary changes shape so the README numbers stay honest.

Verified measurements: thin 44427440 bytes (~42 MB), fat 109773672 bytes (~104 MB), delta 65346232 bytes (~62 MB).

title: search --repo <git-url>: doesn't print vuln-scan summary that repo-add prints
description:
Follow-up visibility gap after izh6.16 (which made search-driven ephemeral clones actually run the check pipeline). The scan now runs, but the search command's user-facing output omits the summary line that 'veska repo add --wait' prints. Compare:

  'veska repo add /path' →
    added repo 7bfb79943454 (via daemon)
      ✓ cold scan complete: 3 files (0.1s)
      ✓ vuln-scan: 0 finding(s) across 0 dep(s)

  'veska search "q" https://github.com/spf13/cobra' →
    cloning https://github.com/spf13/cobra → ...
    search: indexing /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/<id> (first run)...
    search: index ready
    searching: <id>
    <results>

The cobra ephemeral promotion silently produced 47 high-severity secret_leak findings (see izh6.24); the user only discovers this if they think to run 'findings list --all'.

Acceptance:
- After ensureIndexed completes for an ephemeral clone, print the same '✓ cold scan complete' + '✓ vuln-scan' + 'N secret_leak / dead-code finding(s)' summary as repo add, BEFORE the search results.
- Same for re-clones (i.e. promotion ran but finding count is non-zero).
close_reason:
Fixed in 2f3e9e8. Added emitColdScanSummary(ctx, db, w, repoID, branch) that queries the findings table grouped by rule and prints one '  ✓ <rule>: N finding(s)' line per non-zero rule. Plumbed into ensureIndexed (cmd/veska/search.go) right after reparser() returns successfully — so the in-process clone+index path used by 'veska search <q> --repo <git-url>' now surfaces the same per-rule signal that 'veska repo add --wait' shows. Helper is silent on a clean promotion to avoid '0 finding(s)' noise.

End-to-end verified on a fresh spf13/cobra clone via 'veska search "register subcommand" https://github.com/spf13/cobra'. Output now:
  cloning https://github.com/spf13/cobra → /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/...
  search: indexing /home/jrose/.cache/veska/repos/... (first run)...
    ✓ secret_leak: 47 findings
  search: index ready
  searching: b44ed5188db8
  ...

Tests: TestEphemeralPromotionPrintsCheckSummary asserts the secret_leak line fires on the existing leaked-secret fixture; TestEmitColdScanSummary_NoFindingsStaysQuiet pins the silent path. Race-clean on cmd/veska + internal/infrastructure/mcp; layercheck OK; vet clean.

Future follow-ups (not in this bead): the count is per-rule, not aggregated as 'across N dep(s)' like the daemon-log-tail path in repo add — that breakdown would require either threading deps-count through the in-process reparser or running a separate go.mod scan, both out of scope for closing the parity gap. The 47-FP secret_leak count itself is solov2-izh6.24.

title: savings: partition recorder by repo_id
description:
Currently internal/savings.Entry has no repo_id field, so savings.jsonl pools all repos into one counter. The 'veska savings' display change (solov2-izh6.21) is gated on this: until the recorder writes repo_id per-entry and Aggregate buckets by it, per-repo breakdown is impossible.

Scope:
- Add RepoID field to savings.Entry (json: 'repo_id', omitempty for back-compat with existing files).
- Plumb repo_id from the MCP search handler (internal/infrastructure/mcp/tools_search.go recordSavings) — the repoByNode map already exists in the caller.
- Aggregate returns a per-repo breakdown: map[repoID]Report or Report with repo-keyed sub-periods.
- Then revisit cmd/veska/savings.go to render one row per repo + total.

title: savings: per-repo breakdown
description:
'veska savings' rolls a single global counter (4/20 across all registered repos). With multiple repos a per-repo breakdown is more actionable — tells the user which repo's symbol embeddings are paying off.

Acceptance:
- 'veska savings' prints one row per registered repo plus a 'total' row.
- 'veska savings --json' groups counters by repo_id.
- Aggregate-only behaviour preserved via 'veska savings --aggregate'.

DoD: cmd/veska/savings.go grouping + test.

title: veska init: 'stdin EOF; accepting default' wording confuses non-interactive callers
description:
Junior journey: 'veska init' (no flags) under an agent harness has no TTY; it prints:

  Enable OSV vulnerability scanning? (queries osv.dev over the network)
    [Y/n] (or rerun with --yes / --no-vuln to skip this prompt): yes (stdin EOF; accepting default — pass --no-vuln next time to opt out)

The 'stdin EOF; accepting default' parenthetical is fine for humans but trips up scripted output parsing and feels like an error. Either suppress the parenthetical when stdout isn't a TTY, or skip the prompt entirely in non-interactive mode (default to --yes since OSV is opt-out and the followup says 'rerun with --no-vuln to skip').
close_reason: resolveVulnChoice now peeks the reader before printing the prompt; on EOF it emits the clean 'OSV vulnerability scanning: enabled (default; stdin closed — ...)' line (identical to the non-interactive branch) instead of the confusing 'stdin EOF; accepting default' parenthetical. Two new test paths pin the contract. Verified live by rerunning veska init under the agent harness.

title: veska findings list outside a registered repo prints noisy advisory line
description:
Junior journey: 'veska findings list' from outside any registered repo prints:

  veska: no --repo and cwd outside any registered repo; listing findings across all repos (pass --repo <id> to scope)

Then the actual table. The behavior is right (list-all default), but the advisory leaks into stdout above the table, which breaks downstream pipes/awk. Send the advisory to stderr, or suppress it when --json is set.
close_reason: Verified the original advisory was already on stderr (so it never broke awk pipes — the bd issue's stdout claim was a faulty journey observation). Hardened: --json now suppresses the advisory entirely so machine consumers don't see an intermittent stderr line. Live verified both modes.

title: veska deps list TOP_SYMBOLS column is always empty
description:
Repro (from /tmp/junior/greetcli, after both repos registered):
  veska deps list
  MODULE                     VERSION  CALLS  IMPORTS  TOP_SYMBOLS
  github.com/jrose/greetlib  v0.0.0   0      2        
  github.com/spf13/cobra     v1.10.2  0      2        

TOP_SYMBOLS stays empty even after 'veska deps index github.com/jrose/greetlib' on the vendored copy. Either the column is wired to a not-yet-implemented signal, or it requires a state we don't reach from a normal cwd-resolve. Either way the empty column is a visible 'this feature isn't done' artifact in the first dependency view a user sees.

Fix direction: populate from the post-index symbol counts, or drop the column from the default text output and gate it behind --top-symbols / --json.
close_reason: Resolved as a side-effect of izh6.6 fixing cross-repo CALLS edge resolution. Verified: 'veska deps list' from /tmp/junior/greetcli now shows 'github.com/jrose/greetlib v0.0.0 4 2 New, HelloAll'. The TOP_SYMBOLS column is populated whenever stubs successfully bind to a registered destination repo. For cobra (and other deps that aren't registered as repos) the column legitimately stays empty — no resolved calls means no symbols to surface, which matches deps list's documented 'import-only modules without resolved calls do not appear yet' behaviour. The column is honest, not stuck.

title: CLAUDE.md still describes 3 binaries; build produces 1 binary with symlinks
description: /home/jrose/src/engram/solov2/CLAUDE.md 'Process topology — three binaries' section describes cmd/veska, cmd/veska-daemon, cmd/veska-mcp as separate composition roots. Reality (per Makefile and 'ls cmd/'): there is only cmd/veska; bin/veska-daemon and bin/veska-mcp are symlinks to bin/veska, and behaviour is selected by argv[0]. The 'Layout' section's 'cmd/' comment also still says 'the 3 binaries; veska-daemon/wire.go is the composition root'. Update both sections to describe the single-binary / three-personalities layout and point to the actual composition entry point in cmd/veska/.
close_reason: CLAUDE.md updated: 'Process topology — three binaries' renamed to 'one binary, three personalities' and rewritten to describe the actual layout (single bin/veska binary; veska-daemon and veska-mcp are symlinks; argv[0] dispatcher in cmd/veska/main.go routes into internal/cli/daemon and internal/cli/mcp). Layout block's 'cmd/' comment fixed to point at cmd/veska, and the internal/cli/{daemon,mcp} packages are now listed so the composition root is findable.

title: veska search --repo <git-url> interleaves raw 'git clone' progress with results
description:
Repro:
  veska search 'command line argument parser' --repo https://github.com/spf13/pflag

Output starts with 'cloning ...' then dozens of carriage-return-overwritten 'remote: Counting objects:  N% ...' / 'Receiving objects: ...' / 'Resolving deltas: ...' lines, finally the cold-scan / promotion log lines, then the result table. Fine on a TTY (CRs collapse), ugly in non-TTY captures (logs, CI, our 'background command output' files).

Fix direction: detect non-TTY and pass git --quiet (or pipe git's stderr to /dev/null and emit a single 'cloning ... done' line). Same goes for the cold-scan slog lines — pipe them through a 'search:' prefix or hide unless --verbose.
close_reason: Two related fixes for noise: (1) internal/repo/clone.go dropped the '--progress' flag from 'git clone' so git auto-detects TTY (shows progress in a real terminal, stays quiet in non-TTY captures like background-task output files / logs / CI). Eliminates the dozens of CR-overwritten 'Counting/Compressing/Receiving/Resolving' lines that drowned the search results. (2) cmd/veska/main.go installs a slog default handler routing to io.Discard at CLI startup (after the argv[0] daemon/mcp dispatcher so daemon.Run still configures its own JSON handler) — suppresses 'INFO cold scan: starting / promotion: starting / cold scan: complete' lines that the in-process ensureIndexed flow emits during 'search --repo <url>' ephemeral indexing. Verified: clone+search output shrunk from ~150+ lines (mostly CR-progress + daemon-style INFO logs) to ~4 status lines + the result table.

title: 'savings' command doesn't document the 20-call warmup threshold
description:
First-time output:
  today     [█·····························]  warming up  (1/20 calls, 1.1KB -> 1.1KB)
  (ratio reported once a period has >= 20 calls; below that the row reads 'warming up')

The '20 calls per period' threshold is hardcoded with no rationale shown and no way to tune. For a junior eng evaluating veska, the question 'how do I make this number show something?' has no obvious answer. Add to --help: explanation of warmup, when ratio becomes meaningful, and tip that the counter advances on MCP tool calls (so until they wire up an MCP-aware editor or call symbol/context/search several times, savings stays blank).

title: 'veska repo alias' argument order is backwards from natural mental model
description:
Tried 'veska repo alias <repo-id> <name>' (mirroring 'git remote add <name> <url>' and 'ln -s <target> <name>' — name last). Got error:
  repo alias: repo 'lib' is not registered (prefixes must be >= 4 chars) — did you swap the arguments? usage: 'veska repo alias <name> <repo-id-or-prefix-or-alias>'

Good that the error catches it. But the chosen order (name first, repo last) inverts the common '<existing-thing> <new-label>' convention. Even the bd docs we just wrote use 'alias X to Y' phrasing. Either:
- Swap to '<repo> <name>' (breaking, but matches user mental model), OR
- Keep current order but document with a strong example in the --help text.

Low-priority polish — error message saves the day, but it's a paper cut on a first-touch UX walkthrough.

title: 'veska init' should detect non-TTY and announce its default for the OSV prompt
description: On a non-TTY run, 'veska init' prints 'Enable OSV vulnerability scanning? [Y/n] veska initialized' — the prompt appears followed immediately by the success line with no chosen value shown. (It defaults to Y; OSV is enabled per config show.) On non-TTY, print 'OSV vulnerability scanning: enabled (non-interactive default; override with --osv=false)' so scripted users know what they got. Also useful for CI.
close_reason: When 'veska init' is run with a stdin that looks like a TTY to the OS but is actually piped/redirected (the common shell-pipeline case the journey hit), ReadString returned EOF and silently defaulted to yes. Now we echo 'yes (stdin EOF; accepting default)' so the user sees what we picked instead of an unanswered prompt followed by 'veska initialized'. The pre-existing non-interactive-stdin path was unchanged.

title: Rename misleading sqlitevec package (it's an in-memory map, not sqlite-vec)
description:
internal/infrastructure/vector/sqlitevec/ is named after the asg017/sqlite-vec extension but contains zero SQL — it's a pure-Go in-memory map with brute-force L2 linear scan. The name is a vestige of the failed sqlite-vec spike (tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/RESULTS.md → red-ceiling at ~100k vectors; pivot captured in ADR-S0014/S0015).

The misnomer actively misled an AI agent investigating driver options into believing sqlite-vec was a production dependency.

## Scope

- Rename package: sqlitevec → memvec (or linearvec) — pick one
- Rename constant BackendSQLiteVec → BackendMemory (or BackendLinearScan)
- Update config key vector_backend = "sqlite-vec" → "memory" (with back-compat alias for at least one release)
- Update doctor warnings, error messages, comments referencing "sqlite-vec"
- Leave tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/ alone — that path is historically accurate (it really was the sqlite-vec extension spike)

## Acceptance

- `grep -ri sqlitevec internal/ cmd/` returns nothing
- Config back-compat: old `vector_backend = "sqlite-vec"` still parses (with deprecation log)
- ADR amendment or new ADR noting the rename

title: First-run UX: README does not mention 'veska repo add' may need re-trigger if vuln-source config added after install
description: Soft observation from junior-journey walkthrough: the README states the vuln check is gated behind a [vuln_source] config block, but does not call out (a) where the example config is in docs/operations/CONFIG-SURFACE.md vs. ~/.veska/config.toml's lifecycle, (b) whether enabling the block after a repo is added requires a `veska reindex` to pick up vuln findings, or whether a fresh promotion will pull them next commit. A two-line clarification under the 'What it gives you' bullet on vuln scanning would save users the round-trip.
close_reason: fixed in e4b8dd5

title: eng_get_hot_zone returns empty even after 'veska wiki' regen — no in-response hint about commit-history requirement
description: On a freshly-registered repo, eng_get_hot_zone returns {zones:[]}. The CLI-rendered hot_zones.md file explains 'files appear here once commits land while the repo is registered'. The MCP response should carry that same hint (e.g. degraded_reasons: ['no_post_registration_commits']) so an agent calling eng_get_hot_zone doesn't conclude the tool is broken or that the repo is uninteresting.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska search prints '[degraded: embeddings_pending]' without telling the user what to do
description: After 'veska search' results, the CLI appends '[degraded: embeddings_pending]'. A first-time user has no idea whether that means 'results unreliable', 'wait', or 'something is broken'. Add a one-line hint, e.g. '~N embeds pending; re-run in a moment for fuller recall' — pull pending_embeds from eng_get_status so the message is concrete.
close_reason: Closed

title: CLI 'veska search' score labels (top/strong) misclassify scores well under 0.02
description: 'veska search' output: 'function ... hookRunnerCmd  (top, score=0.0178)'. A score of 0.0178 is not 'top' or 'strong' by any standard cosine/normalised-distance threshold. The label bands appear hard-coded without reference to the actual score distribution. Either re-tune the bands against the observed score scale (see vee5) or drop the labels and just print the numeric score.
close_reason: Closed

title: JSON-RPC error code -32602 misused for 'node not found' (should be a domain code)
description: Calling eng_get_node with node_id='abc' returns {code:-32602, message:'node not found: abc'}. Per JSON-RPC 2.0 spec, -32602 is 'Invalid params' (i.e. malformed/missing params). 'Not found' on a well-formed id is a domain error and should use a server-defined code in the -32000..-32099 reserved range, so clients can distinguish 'I asked wrong' from 'it doesn't exist'.
close_reason: Closed

title: Framework-aware extractor: surface CLI/router struct-literal vars as first-class command/route nodes
description:
Follow-up to solov2-b7wt (which now extracts generic top-level var decls as KindVariable). For users of CLI / router / config frameworks, the parser could go further by recognising the framework-specific literal types and:

- Using a meaningful field as the symbol name (e.g. the cobra Use: string, urfave Name:, kong Cmd struct tag, gin route path).
- Parsing the framework's wire-up calls (rootCmd.AddCommand(...), app.Commands = []*cli.Command{...}, router.GET("/foo", h), kong.Parse(&cli)) into CONTAINS / ROUTES edges so call_chain / blast_radius show the actual command or route tree.
- Promoting the resulting nodes to a kind like 'command' or 'route' for clearer agent semantics.

Frameworks worth considering (Go ecosystem, by prevalence):
- CLI: spf13/cobra (var *cobra.Command), urfave/cli (var *cli.App, []*cli.Command), alecthomas/kong (struct-with-tags), alecthomas/kingpin, mitchellh/cli
- HTTP routing: gin, echo, chi, gorilla/mux, net/http ServeMux patterns
- Config: spf13/viper (key registrations), envconfig
- DB/codegen: sqlc named queries, gqlgen resolvers/schema

Design question to settle first: do we keep this all in tree-sitter with framework-specific match heuristics, or introduce a small ports.SymbolEnricher post-pass that runs after generic extraction? The post-pass approach keeps the parser framework-agnostic (DDD-lite preference) and lets each framework be added independently behind its own build option.

Keeping this as a follow-up because the generic var-decl change in b7wt covered the immediate surface-area gap without coupling the parser to any single framework.
notes: Related: solov2-1yev (.scm query API). The framework-aware match patterns (cobra Command struct-literal, urfave App, gin/echo router methods, kong tagged structs, etc.) are exactly what tree-sitter queries are designed for. Strong preference: don't ship framework heuristics as more hand-rolled recursive walkers in go.go — settle the 1yev question first, or scope crn7 to use a query-based path even if 1yev is otherwise still deferred.

title: `veska doctor bundle` writes tarball silently with no 'share this' guidance
description: `veska doctor bundle` prints only the output path (/tmp/veska-bundle-<ts>.tar.gz) and exits. A junior running it doesn't know why the file exists or what to do with it. Add a one-line suffix: 'attach this tarball to support/issue reports — contains probe outputs and recent audit log'.
close_reason: Closed

title: `veska savings` 'warming up (1/20 calls)' threshold opaque to new users
description: Output shows 'warming up (1/20 calls, 227B -> 226B)'. No explanation of what 20 represents, whether it's per-day, lifetime, per-tool, or why measurements stay hidden until then. Add a one-line legend below the bars: 'savings reported once a tool has been called N times; current threshold N=20'.
close_reason: Closed

title: `veska search` usage line ambiguous about positional path-or-url
description: Help: `veska search <query> [path-or-url]`. Unclear whether you can also pass --repo, whether the path can be relative, and whether a URL triggers a clone+index flow (the short description hints at it but examples are absent). Add at least one example to the --help text covering each form: cwd-detected, explicit local path, remote URL.
close_reason: Closed

title: veska install with no subcommand exits 0 (should be non-zero or default to model2vec)
description: 'veska install' (no args) prints help and exits 0. Today model2vec is the only subcommand. Either default to installing model2vec, or exit non-zero like other cobra apps do when a required subcommand is missing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cross-repo IMPORTS-edge stubs (not just CALLS)
description: xc51.3 emits cross_repo_edge_stubs with kind=CALLS per package-qualified call. The original 1gj scope also asked for IMPORTS stubs (package-level: this file imports that package), distinct from per-call edges. Useful for 'who imports this package across the workspace' queries. No consumer exists today; defer until a tool needs them. Split out of solov2-1gj.
acceptance_criteria: Promotion emits an IMPORTS stub per external import (deduped per file); resolver binds them; a consumer surfaces them (e.g. eng_find_importers or blast_radius following IMPORTS).
notes:
DEFERRING — premature without a consumer (matches the bead's own framing).

What 'IMPORTS-edge stubs' would mean:
- Producer: for each external import in each file, emit a cross_repo_edge_stub with kind=IMPORTS, anchored on the importing package node, module_path = the import path.
- Consumer: today there is none. call_chain is intentionally CALLS-only; blast_radius walks all kinds but already gets the CALLS-stub coverage solov2-xc51.3 produces. A useful consumer would be e.g. eng_find_importers (workspace-wide 'who imports this package').

Schema friction:
- cross_repo_edge_stubs.symbol_path expects a per-symbol destination; the resolver matches n.symbol_path = stub.symbol_path. IMPORTS is package-level, not symbol-level. Wiring it as symbol_path='' or '<pkg-name>' is special-case logic that doesn't fit the current single-dst-node abstraction cleanly. Either:
  (a) extend the resolver to special-case symbol_path='' as 'package node in this subpkg dir' (kind=package node), or
  (b) introduce a separate cross_repo_import_stubs table + resolver path.

Decision criteria for un-deferring:
- A concrete consumer (a tool/query that needs cross-repo IMPORTS) is designed.
- The schema/resolver shape for package-level destinations is decided (a) vs (b) above.

Pursuing it today would add unbounded stub rows the graph never reads. Deferred until a consumer makes the design choice forced.

title: Cross-package call skip rationale duplicated verbatim across parser sites
description: The explanation that cross-package selector calls (pkg.Bar(), chained s.field.X()) can't be resolved without cross-repo type info — and the solov2-1gj pointer — is repeated near-verbatim in multiple spots: the extractCallEdges comment block (go.go ~302-308), the collectCallNames doc comment (go.go ~388-390), and the TS class-body resolver. Drift risk: when solov2-1gj lands, someone has to find and update all copies. Maintainability only.
design: Consolidate the rationale into a single doc comment (e.g. a package-level note or one canonical spot on collectCallNames) and have the other sites reference it briefly ('see collectCallNames for why cross-package calls are skipped — solov2-1gj'). No code change. Acceptance: one source of truth for the skip rationale; other sites are one-liners pointing to it.
close_reason: Folded into solov2-2efk (parser cosmetic cleanup) — same area, both tiny, close together in one pass.

title: Parser cosmetic cleanup: edge-dedup separator mismatch + duplicated cross-package rationale
description:
Two tiny, same-area parser cleanups folded together (was solov2-2efk + solov2-7eon):

1. Edge-dedup key separator mismatch in go.go extractCallEdges: unresolved-call key uses U+2192 '→' while resolved-edge key uses ASCII '->'. Separate maps (seenU vs seen) so no live collision, but a latent footgun if maps are consolidated. (solov2-xc51 added more '→' keys for qualified calls.)

2. Cross-package selector-call skip rationale + solov2-1gj pointer is duplicated near-verbatim across go.go extractCallEdges (~302), collectCallNames doc (~388), and the TS class-body resolver. Drift risk when 1gj lands. Consolidate into one referenced comment/const.

Maintainability only; no behavior change.
design: Pick one separator (ASCII '->' for greppability) and use it for both keys, or factor a small edgeKey(from, to string) string helper. No behavior change expected. Acceptance: both keys use the same separator; existing CALLS dedup tests still pass.
close_reason: Standardized in-file call-dedup keys on a shared callKeySep (NUL) across seen/seenU maps; consolidated the cross-package rationale into one accurate note on extractCallEdges (the old comments were both duplicated AND stale post-xc51); de-duplicated the collectCallNames + TS-resolver comments. No behavior change. (Covers former solov2-7eon.)

title: Per-document-type embedder routing: model2vec for code, nomic for prose
description:
Exploratory — surfaced during the solov2-vn0 transition-handling design analysis. Different models optimise for different content: model2vec/potion-code-16M is distilled for code identifiers, nomic-embed-text was trained on prose, BAAI/bge-m3 favours multilingual natural language. A single-embedder index makes a trade-off across all of them. Multi-model coexistence would let veska route by node kind: code symbols (function/method/class/...) embedded with potion-code; markdown/docstrings/comments embedded with a prose model; FTS/lexical paths unchanged.

Schema implication: each vector row tagged with model_id; queries filter by model_id matching the query's embedder. The same per-row tagging that would solve cross-provider mixing for transitions (option 4 in the solov2-vn0 analysis) is also what this routing feature needs — so the two could share the underlying schema change.

NOT a transition-handling story — that's solved more cheaply by embedder-lock + explicit reindex (see solov2-vn0). This is a quality-upgrade story: explicit routing for explicit recall wins on heterogeneous content.

Open questions before committing to the work:
1. Is the recall improvement actually measurable? Need an eval harness against a mixed-content corpus.
2. Storage cost: linear in model count per node. Acceptable up to ~2-3 models.
3. UX: how does the user discover which model embedded a given hit? Maybe surface in the search Result envelope.
4. Migration story: switching to multi-model from single-model needs a controlled reindex per type.

Don't pull this in speculatively. File makes-sense-to-do when there's measured recall pain from a single-embedder index on mixed content.
notes:
Un-deferred (2026-05-23) on the strength of the solov2-8hka fusion bench data, which provided direct numerical evidence that ROUTING > FUSION when a clear specialist exists.

Decisive data points from 8hka full bench (headline fair-R@10):

  Corpus           code-only      prose-only     Specialist gap
  veska (code)     0.706 ✓        0.353          +0.353 for code model
  veska-docs       0.267          0.467 ✓        +0.200 for prose model
  cobra-docs       0.700 (tie)    0.700 (tie)    neither wins
  wikipedia-tech   0.850 (tie)    0.850 (tie)    neither wins

Where there IS a specialist, the gap is 0.20-0.35 R@10 — enormous. Fusion approaches (concat, RRF) could not approximate this; they averaged the right model with the wrong model. The CORRECT response is to pick the right model per doc.

What this means for the hi5 design:
- Per-bucket model assignment is well-motivated: code -> potion-code-16M, prose -> potion-base-32M.
- The cobra-docs / wikipedia-tech tie suggests the prose model is NOT strictly better for all prose corpora — for some prose, both models do equally well. So routing matters most for the strongly-specialised corpora (veska code, veska-docs).
- Storage cost: per-bucket vector spaces. No fusion overhead, just per-bucket separate indexes. Matches hi5s original design (multi-bucket).
- The 8hka bench infrastructure (TestEmbedModelsFusion + per-corpus fair-R@10 reporting) is in tree and can be reused to validate the routed-vs-baseline lift end-to-end once hi5 lands.

title: Token-savings tracker: ~/.veska/savings.jsonl + 'veska doctor savings'
description: Semble's 'semble savings' is great marketing — shows ~98% token savings as a bar chart. Cheap to ship for us once chunk-return lands (solov2-NEW-chunk). Per search call, log (file_chars_in_matched_files, snippet_chars_returned) to ~/.veska/savings.jsonl. Subcommand renders per-period rollup. AC: (1) jsonl line per search call; (2) 'veska doctor savings' or 'veska savings' prints today/7d/all-time bar chart; (3) zero-overhead append (no fsync per write).
close_reason: Implemented internal/savings/ recorder + Aggregate, wired into MCP semantic handler, added 'veska doctor savings' (+ 'veska savings' alias). Per-search JSONL append at $VESKA_HOME/savings.jsonl, ASCII bar chart for today/7d/all-time.

title: veska init --agent <claude|cursor|codex|...>: write AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md snippets
description: Semble's 'semble init' writes a per-tool agent-instruction file. Trivial UX win we lack. Recognise the agent flavor; for each, emit a tested snippet that lists eng_search_semantic, eng_find_symbol, eng_get_call_chain, eng_get_context_pack with example invocations and the 'when to use' guidance. AC: (1) 'veska init --agent claude' writes CLAUDE.md or .claude/agents/veska-search.md; (2) idempotent (re-run is no-op if snippet already present); (3) flags for all major harnesses (claude, cursor, codex, opencode, copilot, gemini, kiro).
close_reason: Implemented 'veska init --agent <flavor>': writes per-harness instruction snippet (claude→CLAUDE.md, cursor→.cursor/rules/veska.mdc, codex/opencode→AGENTS.md, copilot→.github/copilot-instructions.md, gemini→GEMINI.md, kiro→.kiro/steering/veska.md). Idempotent via HTML-comment sentinel; appends to existing files; unknown flavors error with full supported list.

title: eng_search_nodes declared in source but not registered — 'method not found' at runtime
description: grep over internal/infrastructure/mcp/*.go shows 'eng_search_nodes' as a tool-name literal, but calling it via veska-mcp returns -32601 method-not-found. Either (a) register it in wire.go if it's intended, or (b) delete the dead handler so the source matches what runs. Either is fine; pick whichever matches what the handler does (FTS over symbol names? subsumed by eng_find_symbol?).
close_reason: False alarm — eng_search_nodes was only a fake tool name in registry_test.go, never an actual registered handler. Sam's grep + 'method not found' read it as real but there was no production code to remove. Renamed the test fixture to eng_search_aaaa so the next person grepping doesn't repeat the confusion.

title: eng_find_owner returns silent null when no CODEOWNERS file exists
acceptance_criteria: eng_find_owner against a no-CODEOWNERS repo returns a result that includes 'reason' so the user knows why owner is null.
close_reason: eng_find_owner null path now includes a 'reason' field distinguishing 'no CODEOWNERS file' from 'CODEOWNERS exists but no match'. Test updated.

title: Reconcile CLI socket name: code daemon.sock vs docs cli.sock
description: Surfaced by solov2-s5c.6. internal/config/paths.go DaemonSockPath() returns $VESKA_HOME/daemon.sock, but 09-mcp-surface, 03-the-daemon, and CONFIG-SURFACE.md all call the CLI socket cli.sock. Pick one name and align: either rename the code constant to cli.sock, or correct the ~3 docs to daemon.sock. Low-risk, low-priority.
close_reason: DoD verified: DaemonSockPath/daemon.sock renamed to CLISockPath/cli.sock, 24 refs updated, aligned to the design corpus; build/race/vet/layercheck green

title: Implement secrets-scan and vuln-scan synchronous promotion checks
description: Backlog (post-M5; depends on the vuln-source + secrets-scanner ports landing). The promotion check pipeline (internal/application/checks/) ships dead-code + contract-drift. The design (11-pipelines §2.1) also calls for secrets-scan and vuln-scan structural checks emitting source_layer='structural'/'security' findings. Build them once the underlying ports land. Split from solov2-s5c.4 (the doc-fix half).
notes: Deferred 2026-05-18: blocked, not date-gated. No secrets-scanner port exists; vuln-source ships only NullVulnSource. Reactivate when those ports get real impls, then build the secrets-scan + vuln-scan promotion checks.
close_reason: Superseded by epic solov2-2ps (M7 — Vuln + Secrets Scanning). The vuln-scan + secrets-scan implementation is decomposed there into solov2-2ps.1-.11.

title: Glossary open-questions.md references nonexistent milestones/ and deferred/ paths
description: Surfaced by: 15-glossary/open-questions.md (solov2-4ty.20). The doc cites plain-text paths milestones/M0.md (OQ-S001, OQ-S006) and deferred/ (intro) but no such directories exist under docs/design/. The actual M0 spike outcomes live in docs/milestones/closed/M0.md and 14-roadmap/README.md. Repoint the references to the canonical locations.

title: ADR bookkeeping: prior-set ADRs absent from tree; RETRACTED/README count mismatch
description: Surfaced by: adr/README.md + adr/RETRACTED.md (solov2-4ty.24). RETRACTED.md claims the prior-set ADR files (ADR-0001..0023) 'remain in their original location as historical record', but no ADR-00NN files exist anywhere under docs/. README.md also references them by number. And README says the redesign 'retracts eight ADRs' while RETRACTED.md documents 9 (8 full + ADR-0019 partial). Either restore the prior-set files or correct RETRACTED.md/README.md to state the prior set is git-history-only, and reconcile the count.

title: Verify research doc (chizu competitive snapshot)
description:
Doc in scope: docs/research/chizu-competitive-2026-05.md.

What to check:
1. This is a dated competitive snapshot — verify-as-is; do NOT update its analysis.
2. Confirm the date in the filename/frontmatter is consistent.

4. Frontmatter present; add verified:true + verified_date (records that it was reviewed, not that the snapshot changed).

title: Split tools_graph.go and tools_findings.go by tool
description: tools_graph.go = 447 LOC, tools_findings.go = 305 LOC. Both are growing as M3 lands tools. Split each handler into its own file (one tool per file, e.g. tool_get_node.go, tool_close_finding.go) for blame readability and to align with the per-tool table-driven tests.
close_reason: tools_graph.go + tools_findings.go split into 7 per-tool files (tool_find_symbol.go, tool_get_node.go, tool_get_call_chain.go, tool_get_file_nodes.go, tool_close_finding.go, tool_list_findings.go, tool_reopen_finding.go); originals retain only shared types + Register* funcs. Identifier set verified identical before/after. Pure file-split, zero behaviour change; tests untouched and green. Commit 13f3088.

title: Replace variadic ResolveFunc with functional option in RegisterGraphTools
description: mcp.RegisterGraphTools(..., resolveFns ...ResolveFunc) uses a variadic as a stand-in for an optional dependency. Inconsistent with the rest of the codebase, which uses functional options (embedder.WithMetrics, autolink.WithTopK, etc). Refactor to RegisterGraphTools(..., opts ...GraphToolOption) with WithResolveFunc(fn). Trivial.
close_reason: RegisterGraphTools variadic resolveFns ...ResolveFunc replaced with opts ...GraphToolOption + WithResolveFunc(fn) — consistent with embedder.WithMetrics / autolink.WithTopK etc. wire.go call unchanged (zero opts); crossrepo tests updated (mockResolve -> WithResolveFunc, nil -> omitted). Commit 0df9110.

title: Implement eng_find_changed_symbols (deferred — needs history substrate)
description: Per SOLO-09 §584, eng_find_changed_symbols is a historical-query tool that requires a per-commit history substrate not present in V2. Dropped from M3.06 scope. Reactivate when (and if) a history substrate ships.
close_reason: DoD verified: eng_find_changed_symbols implemented — parses files at two git refs and diffs symbol sets (added/removed/modified), no history substrate needed; 33rd MCP tool, SOLO-09 updated; 10 tests, build/race/vet/layercheck green
